# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Create/Import/Export / Import From OneClick/Profile Builder – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Create/Import/Export / Import From OneClick/Profile Builder

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Create-Import-Export-Import-From-OneClick-Profile-Builder/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



======================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

May 22, 2023 22:24

Introduction
------------

Accounts are used for the Mail section of Inbox. You can easily import and export your accounts via CSV format or import them directly from Profile Builder to Inbox.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can add many individual accounts or your main catchall/forwarded accounts.
*   Some email providers require you to use an App Password to access IMAP. We support OAuth2 for providers such as Gmail, AOL, Yahoo, Outlook, Hotmail, and AT&T. You can use your regular/standard password with OAuth2.
*   The setup for all providers is different, so we have created separate guides for various Providers. The guides are linked below.
    *   [Gmail](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13320383595671)
        
    *   [iCloud](https://aycd.io/blog/How-Setup-IMAP-Apple-iCloud-Email-Accounts)
        
    *   [Yahoo](https://aycd.io/blog/How-Setup-IMAP-yahoo-Email-mail-Accounts)
        
    *   [AOL](https://aycd.io/blog/How-Setup-IMAP-aol-Email-mail-accounts)
        

Create (Basic Auth)
-------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Click on the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Create** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457369520407)

3\. Select the **Provider** from the drop-down menu and click **Next**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31947616206999)

4\. By default, accounts are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your account created into a unique category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457369521815)

5\. Specify the login type to use for this account. This section of the guide covers Basic login type.   
6\. Specify the **Email/Username** for your account.

7\. Specify the **App Password** for your account. Some email companies require an app password to connect to the IMAP server using the basic login type.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31947616210839)

8\. Optionally, you can specify an **IMAP Proxy** to use when connecting to the mail provider. This is only recommended on AOL and Yahoo accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457361596567)

9\. Optionally, you can exclude the account from mail syncing / displaying emails by enabling the **Exclude from Emails View** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457361597975)

10\. After configuring the Create Mail Account dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/26941129386519)

11\. Inbox will verify whether it can access the account and display a status message. If the Status shows **Good**, your accounts are set up correctly. If the Status shows **Authentication Failed**, please ensure your login details are correct. 

Create (OAuth2)
---------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Click on the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Create** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28641959913495)

3\. Select the **Provider** from the drop-down menu and click **Next**. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25630528158359)

4\. By default, accounts are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your account created in a unique category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457361599639)

5\. Specify the **Login Type** to use for this account. This section of the guide covers the OAuth 2 login type. AOL, AT&T, Gmail, Hotmail/Outlook, and Yahoo support OAuth2.  
6\. Specify the **Email/Username** for your account.

7\. Specify the **Password** for your account. When connecting via OAuth2, you need to use your regular/normal password.

8\. If your account has 2FA enabled, input the **2FA Secret Key,** and Inbox will automatically enter 2FA codes.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31947616211735)

9\. Specify a **Recovery Email** for the account. If you do not already have a recovery email added to your account, you should still specify one here. Inbox will automatically add the recovery email if Microsoft requires you to add one during login or when unlocking your account.

10\. Optionally, you can specify a **Recovery Code** for the account. This code will be saved to this field after running the Generate Recovery Code tool.

11\. Optionally, you can specify a **Phone Number** for the account. You can use the phone number when using account tools with the SMS via email feature.

12\. After you use the OAuth2 Automatic/Manual Login Tool and your account is logged in, the **Refresh Token** will populate. You can use the Refresh Token to automatically log in to the account on other devices through Inbox.

13\. If you purchase your accounts from a third-party provider, they may have provided you with a Client ID / Client Secret key. You can input those in their respective fields.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31947616212503)  
14\. Specify an **IMAP Proxy** you want to use when syncing mail and viewing your emails. This is only recommended on AOL and Yahoo accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/26963331327127)  
15\. Specify a **Browser Proxy** you want to use when logging into/managing your Outlook/Hotmail account. We recommend using a Browser proxy when logging into many accounts.

16\. You can specify which browser you want to use with the **Browser Type** dropdown. In most cases, you should use **Camo Chrome**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31947661569047)

17\. Optionally, you can exclude the account from mail syncing / displaying emails by enabling the **Exclude from Emails View** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/26941161718423)

18\. Optionally, you can specify which email your account forwards to, and which forwarding method it uses (Standard or Rules). This information will be filled out if you run one of the Outlook forwarding tools in Inbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31947661569431)

19\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. Your account will be added to the tableview.

20\. You can log in to your accounts by selecting them, clicking the **Tools** button (or right-clicking the account(s)), **Tools,** and then selecting **OAuth2 Automatic Login**. You should choose the **OAuth2 Manual Login** option if your account requires information we don't automatically support during the login phase.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/26941129394199)

Import (CSV)
------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Click on the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Import** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457369527703)

3\. By default, accounts are created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your accounts created into a unique or existing category.

4\. Click the blue spreadsheet icon. The file explorer will open, and you can select the CSV file you want to import. You can create a copy of the example format by manually creating one account and following the instructions below to export the account.

5\. After configuring the Import Items dialog, click the **Import** button to add the accounts to Inbox.

Import From OneClick CSV
------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Click the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Import** button, and click the **OneClick Account CSV** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457361602455)

3\. A dialog will appear. In this dialog, you can select the CSV that contains your Gmail accounts from OneClick. 

4\. Click the **Import** button after selecting your accounts to import them to Inbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31948061125527)

Import From OneClick
--------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Click the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Import** button, and click the **OneClick Gmail Accounts** button. Your browser session data is automatically imported to Inbox when using the OneClick Gmail Accounts option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457361604759)

3\. A dialog will appear. In this dialog, you can select the Gmail accounts you saved in the Tasks section of OneClick. 

4\. Click the **Import** button after selecting your accounts to import them to Inbox. 

Import From Profile Builder
---------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Click the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Import** button, and click the **Profile Builder Accounts** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457361606423)

3\. A dialog will appear. In this dialog, you can select the Outlook/Hotmail accounts you saved in the **Account Credentials** section of Profile Builder. 

4\. Click the **Import** button after selecting your accounts to import them to Inbox.

Export
------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to export, then click the **File** button at the top left of the view and the **Export** button. Click the **CSV** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25631347578519)

3\. Click the **Yes** button to export the accounts you selected.

4\. Click the **Yes** button in the Completed—Export Items dialog to view the exported accounts file location. The file explorer will open with your exported CSV file.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14775125110551)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - iCloud - Create / Generate / Import / Delete / Export – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - iCloud - Create / Generate / Import / Delete / Export

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

October 08, 2025 21:44

Introduction
------------

Emails are integral to your setup, whether for accounts or used as the mail address during checkout. iClouds are especially useful as they are widely accepted and not blocked. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   In order to use the iCloud generator, your account must be an iCloud+ Account. To check whether or not your account is an iCloud+ account, you can sign in here: [https://www.icloud.com/settings/](https://www.icloud.com/settings/)
    
*   You can create a maximum of 750 iCloud Hide My Email addresses per account, and most accounts are capped at generating 5 per hour. 
*   iCloud+ plans start at $0.99/month; the difference between plans does not determine the number of emails that can be generated.
*   [You can learn how to create an iCloud app password by clicking here](https://aycd.io/blog/How-Setup-IMAP-Apple-iCloud-Email-Accounts)
    .
*   The account needs an active iCloud email address. Please sign in [here](https://www.icloud.com/mail/)
     to check if you have an iCloud email address. If you don’t have an iCloud email account, [this guide](https://support.apple.com/guide/icloud/create-a-primary-icloudcom-email-address-mmdd8d1c5c/icloud)
     will show you how to set one up.    
    Please note that differentiating between an iCloud email address and an Apple ID is essential.   
    The iCloud email address is [aycdtest@icloud.com](mailto:aycdtest@icloud.com)
    , and the Apple ID on the top right is [Jake@aycd.com](mailto:Jake@aycd.com)
      
    ![2Icloud AppleID.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35504929612439)

Tool Hyperlinks
---------------

*   You can click the text below to go directly to the section of this guide for that specific tool.
*   [Adding iCloud+ Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export#01HCG6AKZF618QRFP2B2M48A34)
    
*   [Generating iCloud Hide My Email (HME) Addresses](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export#01HGHADWEF5DFW5X9H0SV4PV9M)
    
*   [Importing iCloud Hide My Email (HME) Addresses](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export#h_01K733MAZPQYTKMA592ZM0KV83)
    
*   [Deleting iCloud Hide My Email (HME) Addresses](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export#h_01K7339Q6J0JJVJNANMRC3GRH9)
    
*   [Exporting/Viewing Generated iCloud Hide My Email (HME) Addresses](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export#h_01KJXFXGSYBQT7S22FHZDS6Y6W)
    

Adding iCloud+ Account
----------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Click on the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Create** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35504921492759)

3\. Select the **Provider** from the drop-down menu and click **Next**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35504921493015)

4\. By default, accounts are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your account created in a unique category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35504929612951)

5\. Specify the login type to use for this account. iCloud uses basic auth.  
6\. Specify the **Email/Username** for your account.

7\. Specify the **App Password** for your account. iCloud requires you to use an app password to connect to the IMAP server using the basic login type.

8\. If your account has 2FA enabled, input the **2FA Secret Key,** and Inbox will automatically enter 2FA codes.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35504929613079)

9\. Optionally, you can specify an **IMAP Proxy** to use when connecting to the mail provider.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35504929613335)

10\. Specify a **Browser Proxy** you want to use when logging into/managing your Outlook/Hotmail account. We recommend using a Browser proxy when logging into many accounts.

11\. You can specify which browser you want to use with the **Browser Type** dropdown. In most cases, you should use **Camo Chrome**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35504921494039)

12\. Optionally, you can exclude the account from mail syncing / displaying emails by enabling the **Exclude from Emails View** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35506963570071)

13\. Optionally, you can specify which email your account forwards to, and which forwarding method it uses (Standard or Rules). This information will be filled out if you run one of the iCloud forwarding tools in Inbox. For iCloud, if the forwarding email is specified here, you do not need to add the forwarding email to a Mail Task Template

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35506963570711)

14\. If you already have your iCloud Hide My Email (HME) addresses saved, you can input them into the **Aliases** field. Your generated/imported iCloud Hide My Email (HME) addresses are saved in the Aliases section.   
![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35506952112023)

Generating iCloud Hide My Email (HME) Addresses
-----------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to use the iCloud Generate HME tool, then click the Tools button and select the **iCloud Generate HME** tool.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35506952113047)

3\. Specify a **Label** for the generated HME addresses.

4\. Optionally, you can add a **Note** for the generated HME addresses.

5\. Specify the **Quantity of Emails** to generate.

6\. You can add a **Delay** between generating each HME address. We recommend using a minimum delay of 12 minutes to prevent iCloud+ account locks.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35506963576855)

7\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to generate. Once they have been successfully generated, the status will show **Generated XXX HME Addresses**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35506952116503)

Importing iCloud Hide My Email (HME) Addresses
----------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to use the iCloud Delete HME tool, then click the Tools button and select the **iCloud Import HME** tool.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35507194244887)

3\. Specify the **iCloud Website Country** to use.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35507194245783)

4\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to import. Once they have successfully imported, the status will show **Imported XXX HME Addresses**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35507188496791)

Deleting iCloud Hide My Email (HME) Addresses
---------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to use the iCloud Delete HME tool, then click the Tools button and select the **iCloud Delete HME** tool.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35507194246167)

3\. Specify the **iCloud Website Country** to use. 

4\. Select the **iCloud Hide My Email Addresses to Delete**. You cannot reactivate deleted Hide My Email (HME) addresses.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35507194246551)

5\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to import. Once they have successfully imported, the status will show **Deleted XXX HME Addresses**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/35507188499735)

Exporting/Viewing generated alias emails
----------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Right-click the iCloud+ account on which you want to export the aliases, then click the Tools button and select the **Export Aliases to CSV** tool.

![icloudexport1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38812875014935)

3\. To view the alias within the program, right-click the task, click **Edit**, and **click** Aliases.

![icloudexport222.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38812875018007)

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28227969764375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Schedule-Tools-Review-Scheduled-Tools)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27040329117847-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Unlock-Account)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate AOL Accounts – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate AOL Accounts

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29071604718615-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-AOL-Accounts/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==========================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

January 08, 2025 18:12

Introduction
------------

You can generate AOL accounts with Inbox. After generating, you can use the various AOL tools offered within Inbox!

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To log into your AOL accounts, you need to add your email credentials to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to add your AOL accounts to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   To view/sync emails in Inbox, you must add an app password to your AOL credentials.
*   You can configure how many AOL browsers run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools > AOL Tools. 

AOL Account Generation
----------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**

2\. Click on the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Generate** button. The generate dialog will appear.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29071604658583)

3\. Select the type of mail account to create with the **Account Type** dropdown.  
4\. By default, accounts are generated in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to generate your account into a unique category.

5\. Specify a **Domain** to use for the account.

6\. Specify the **Quantity** of accounts to create.

7\. If you want the accounts to automatically generate after configuring the dialog, enable the **Automatically start 'Generate New Account' tool** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29071604670871)

8\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

9\. The mass edit dialog will automatically open. You can add additional information to your tasks before proceeding with generation.

10\. By default, accounts are generated in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to generate your account into a unique category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29071604672407)

11\. Specify a **Password** to use for the account. You can leave this blank, and Inbox will automatically generate a random, unique password. 

12\. Specify an **App/IMAP Password** to use for the account. Providers using the Basic login type must use an app password to view emails within Inbox.

13\. Specify **Recovery Emails** to use with your accounts. If AOL requires you to add one during login or unlocking your account, Inbox will automatically add the recovery email.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29071604677143)14\. Specify an **IMAP Proxy** you want to use when syncing mail and viewing your emails. We recommend using an IMAP proxy when logging into many accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29071616689175)

15\. Specify a **Browser Proxy** you want to use when logging into/managing your AOL account. We recommend using a Browser proxy when logging into many accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29071616691735)

16\. Optionally, you can exclude the account from mail syncing / displaying emails by enabling the **Exclude from Emails View** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29071616697751)

17\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. Your accounts will be added to the tableview.

18\. Select the newly created account tasks, click the **Tools** button, select the **AOL Generate New Account** option, and click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29071604690711)

19\. If you want emails to populate in the emails tab after your account(s) is generated and logged in, enable the **Sync mail after login** checkbox.

20\. If you want to force re-login into the credentials before generating your account(s), enable the **Force re-login credentials** checkbox.

21\. Before generating accounts, activity is randomly generated (through warmup) to increase trust in the generated account. Enable the **Skip warmup** checkbox to skip the warmup process.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29071616701079)

22\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29071604698647)

23\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used if your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow or other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, you need to select it with the **Edit** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29765992810263)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   You must also assign the phone number that correlates with each task to the task. You can do this by selecting your task, right-clicking> Edit > Edit, going to the Credentials section, and inputting the phone number.
*   After creating your Mail Task Template, you need to select it with the **Edit** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29765992811031)

24\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if AOL forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29071616712599)

25\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the Manual option. Click here to learn how to configure AutoSolve with Inbox.

26\. Specify the **Maximum Solve Attempts** before failing the task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29071616714391)

27\. After you finish configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your accounts will attempt to generate. Once your accounts have finished generating, the browser status will show **Account Created**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29071616715927)

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - OAuth2 Automatic / Manual Login (Gmail/Outlook/Hotmail/Yahoo/AOL)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/25709284534295-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-OAuth2-Automatic-Manual-Login-Gmail-Outlook-Hotmail-Yahoo-AOL)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Yahoo Accounts – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Yahoo Accounts

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29062200244503-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Yahoo-Accounts/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

January 08, 2025 13:27

Introduction
------------

You can generate Yahoo accounts with Inbox. After generating, you can use the various Yahoo tools offered within Inbox!

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To log into your Yahoo accounts, you need to add your email credentials to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to add your Yahoo accounts to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   To view/sync emails in Inbox, you can run the OAuth2 Automatic Login tool or you can specify an App Password in the account credential.
*   You can configure how many Yahoo browsers run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools > Yahoo Tools. 

Yahoo Account Generation
------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**

2\. Click on the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Generate** button. The generate dialog will appear.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29062200231063)

3\. Select the type of mail account to create with the **Account Type** dropdown.  
4\. By default, accounts are generated in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to generate your account into a unique category.

5\. Specify a **Domain** to use for the account.

6\. Specify the **Quantity** of accounts to create.

7\. If you want the accounts to automatically generate after configuring the dialog, enable the **Automatically start 'Generate New Account' tool** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29062175210263)

8\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

9\. The mass edit dialog will automatically open. You can add additional information to your tasks before proceeding with generation.

10\. By default, accounts are generated in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to generate your account into a unique category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29062200233367)

11\. Specify a **Password** to use for the account. You can leave this blank, and Inbox will automatically generate a random, unique password. 

12\. Specify an **App/IMAP Password** to use for the account. Providers using the Basic login type must use an app password to view emails within Inbox.

13\. Specify **Recovery Emails** to use with your accounts. If Yahoo requires you to add one during login or unlocking your account, Inbox will automatically add the recovery email.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29062175211159)

14\. Specify an **IMAP Proxy** you want to use when syncing mail and viewing your emails. We recommend using an IMAP proxy when logging into many accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29062175212055)

15\. Specify a **Browser Proxy** you want to use when logging into/managing your Yahoo account. We recommend using a Browser proxy when logging into many accounts.

*   You can specify which browser you want to use with the **Browser Type** dropdown. In most cases, you should use Camo Chrome.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31948336253207)

16\. Optionally, you can exclude the account from mail syncing / displaying emails by enabling the **Exclude from Emails View** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29062175213463)

17\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. Your accounts will be added to the tableview.

18\. Select the newly created account tasks, click the **Tools** button, select the **Yahoo Generate New Account** option, and click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29062200238487)

19\. If you want emails to populate in the emails tab after your account(s) is generated and logged in, enable the **Sync mail after login** checkbox.

20\. If you want to force re-login into the credentials before generating your account(s), enable the **Force re-login credentials** checkbox.

21\. Before generating accounts, activity is randomly generated (through warmup) to increase trust in the generated account. Enable the **Skip warmup** checkbox to skip the warmup process.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29062200240151)

22\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29063979163543)

23\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used if your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow or other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, you need to select it with the **Edit** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29765993450519)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   You must also assign the phone number that correlates with each task to the task. You can do this by selecting your task, right-clicking> Edit > Edit, going to the Credentials section, and inputting the phone number.
*   After creating your Mail Task Template, you need to select it with the **Edit** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29766009004439)

24\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Yahoo forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29063979168791)

25\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the Manual option. Click here to learn how to configure AutoSolve with Inbox.

26\. Specify the **Maximum Solve Attempts** before failing the task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29063970461207)

27\. After you finish configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your accounts will attempt to generate. Once your accounts have finished generating, the browser status will show **Account Created**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29063970462615)

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Yahoo Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29062200244503-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Yahoo-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Microsoft (Outlook/Hotmail) Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27311400040727-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Microsoft-Outlook-Hotmail-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28227969764375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Schedule-Tools-Review-Scheduled-Tools)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27040329117847-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Unlock-Account)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook - Change / Verify/Update Recovery Email](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27032547222551-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Change-Verify-Update-Recovery-Email)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Enable Email Forwarding / Disable Email Forwarding / Check Email Forwarding](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/26963363437719-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Enable-Email-Forwarding-Disable-Email-Forwarding-Check-Email-Forwarding)

# Profile Builder - Profiles - Create / Generate / Import / Export (Format / Custom CSV) Profiles – AYCD

Profile Builder - Profiles - Create / Generate / Import / Export (Format / Custom CSV) Profiles

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13258919736343-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Create-Generate-Import-Export-Format-Custom-CSV-Profiles/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

March 21, 2023 15:36

Introduction
------------

You can manually add profiles to Profile Builder or use the import feature to import profiles in bulk. The Generate profiles feature can create random profiles with fake address data. You can export your profiles from Profile Builder in lots of different formats. You can export them one by one or select multiples. Profiles are the starting point of numerous features throughout Profile Builder and contain tools to help you build the most complete profiles.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   The data in all Toolbox programs is secured and encrypted automatically. Given the sensitive nature of Billing and Credit Card information, you can further secure data in Profile Builder with a [Password Lock](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7446003478039)
    .
*   Use categories to simplify organization. Group by bot, website, address, and more.

Create Profiles
---------------

1\. Navigate to the Profiles section in Profile Builder under Profiles. When you hover over it, the Profiles section icon looks like a book with a person on it.

2\. Click on **File** and then **Create**. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39296429095959)

3\. This will open the New Profile dialog. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39296454118423)

3\. By default, profiles are created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Profile created into a unique category.

4\. Specify a unique **Profile Name** for this profile. **Size** and **Profile Group** are optional fields that will only export with a format if the exported format supports it. You can use the checkbox for **Same Billing and Shipping Address** to avoid having to type the same details in both sections. 

![Create-Profile-1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13650167418391)

5\. Fill out the remaining Profile details like Name, Email, Address, and Payment Card details.

6\. After adding your profile details, you can randomize or jig certain information with the Jig button at the bottom of the Billing and Shipping Columns. You can expand the Advanced Jig view to have more control over the quick jigging options, and if you want to revert to the original information, press the **Reset** button.

![Create-Profile-2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13650167441303)

7\. After you have finished configuring the profile, you can save it using the **Save** button at the bottom right of the dialog. If you are editing a profile or want to create multiple copies of it, you can use the **Save As** button to create a copy of your current profile.

![Create-Profile-5.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13651197129751)

Generate Profiles
-----------------

1\. Navigate to the Profiles section in Profile Builder under Profiles. When you hover over it, the section has an icon that looks like a book with a person on it, titled **Profiles**.

2\. Click on **File** and then **Generate**. This will open the Generate Profiles dialog. The Generate profiles feature can create random profiles with fake address data.

3\. By default, profiles are created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Profile created into a unique category.

4\. Specify a **Name** for your profiles.

5. Specify the **Quantity** of profiles to generate.

6\. Specify the **Gender Preference** of the generated profile's first name.

7\. Optionally, you can specify a **U.S. Postal Code** to generate address data for.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31926175299607)

8\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. Your profiles will be saved to the category specified in the Generate Profiles dialog.

Import Profiles
---------------

1\. Navigate to the Profiles section in Profile Builder under Profiles. When you hover over it, the section has an icon that looks like a book with a person on it, titled **Profiles**.

2\. Click on **File** and then **Import**. This will open the Import Profiles dialog. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39296454125591)

3\. Select the format you want to import with.

*   If you want to use our CSV format, you can create a profile manually and export it using File> Export to get our format.
*   When you are ready to import the profiles, click the blue page icon to the right of the text area and select the File. If you are importing the AYCD CSV, select the **CSV/Google Sheet/Form** format.
*   Optionally, you can copy paste the data from the file in the Copy/Paste section.
*   Optionally, you can provide a download URL for the file(s) in the From URLs section.

3\. Click the **Import** button to add your profiles to **Profile Builder**. ![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39296429102743)

4\. If an asterisk is next to the format you want to use, you will use our **AutoImport** feature. This should be compatible with the majority of formats; however, it is possible that some values may not transfer over correctly. Ensure you inspect your profiles after importing to verify that all fields are filled and correct. ![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13259160787607)

5\. Profile Builder will import your profiles to the category name corresponding to your selected format. 

Now you have added profiles in Profile Builder. You can now completely customize your profile setup.

Export Profiles (Format)
------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Profiles section in Profile Builder under Profiles. When you hover over it, the section has an icon that looks like a book with a person on it, titled **Profiles**.

2\. Click on **File,** then **Export** and then click the **Format** button. This will open the Export format dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39296454132887)

4\. Within the dialog, you can select the bot format(s) you wish to export the profiles. For example, in the screenshot below, I have decided to export the profile in AYCD and CSV/Google Sheet/Form format.

5\. Within the dialog, you can enable the **Send to Discord** checkbox to send the file to a Discord channel via a webhook. For this feature to work, ensure you have a webhook added to Settings > General. 

6\. The checkbox **Shuffle Profiles** randomizes the order in which your profiles are. This is useful if your bots create tasks based on the order of the profiles, as it means that each profile has an equal chance of being used, rather than the first few being consistently used.

7\. Within the dialog, you can export your profiles to different files with the **Export as multiple files** checkbox. Then you can input how many profiles you want in each file in the **Amount of** **Profiles Per File** field.

8\. After configuring how you wish the profiles to be exported, click the **Export** button. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39296429108375)

9\. You will then have the option to open the file location of the exported file. Click **Yes**, and your Finder/Windows Explorer window will open with the file(s) within. 

Export Profiles (Custom CSV)
----------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Profiles section in Profile Builder under Profiles. When you hover over it, the section has an icon that looks like a book with a person on it, titled **Profiles**.

2\. Click on **File,** then **Export** and then click the **Custom CSV** button. This will open the Export Custom CSV dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39296454139031)

3\. Select the Fields To Export that you want included in the exported CSV.

*   You can select any fields you want to export.
*   You can create presets by selecting the fields you want to export and clicking the Save Selected Fields As Preset button.
*   You can clear your selected fields by clicking the Clear Selection button.
*   You can remove saved presets by selecting the preset and clicking the Remove Selected Preset button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39296454141463)  
4\. After configuring the dialog, click the Export button.

Finding Previous Exports
------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Profiles section in Profile Builder under Profiles. When you hover over it, the section has an icon that looks like a book with a person on it, titled **Profiles**.

2\. Click on **Options** and then **Open Exports Folder**. This will open a Finder/Windows Explorer window where all the exports are located.   
![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39296454146199)

3\. Each folder is named after the bots format. You can use this to find any old exports you may need.

*   [Profile Builder - Profiles - Edit, Basic Jig, & Mass Edit](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13264022167703-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Edit-Basic-Jig-Mass-Edit)

# Profile Builder - Profiles - ACO Slot Form / CSV Format – AYCD

Profile Builder - Profiles - ACO Slot Form / CSV Format

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13265031173143-Profile-Builder-Profiles-ACO-Slot-Form-CSV-Format/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



============================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/1500080569202/crw1.png)

**Crewie**

March 21, 2023 18:22

Introduction
------------

This form is meant for those who run slots for drops. When accepting slots, you can customize the form as much as possible; however, Profile Builder will only pick up the Profile fields from the form. Please ensure you do not delete any preset fields on the form. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Ensure you do not delete any of the preset fields.
*   This is an example of the [Microsoft Forms Template](https://forms.office.com/Pages/ShareFormPage.aspx?id=DQSIkWdsW0yxEjajBLZtrQAAAAAAAAAAAAN__qKSfrxUOEU1UVVaS0FSSk8yTFNRVVFKWEtaWjM4Ty4u&sharetoken=lICvIw7lpZBkWWHxhU6c)
    .

Microsoft Forms/Excel
---------------------

1\. Create a copy of the [Microsoft Forms Template](https://forms.office.com/Pages/ShareFormPage.aspx?id=DQSIkWdsW0yxEjajBLZtrQAAAAAAAAAAAAN__qKSfrxUOEU1UVVaS0FSSk8yTFNRVVFKWEtaWjM4Ty4u&sharetoken=lICvIw7lpZBkWWHxhU6c)
. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13665550339479)

2\. Once you finish collecting entries, click the **Open in Excel** button to download the Excel file.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13665566992791)

3\. Navigate to the Profiles section of Profile Builder, click the **File** button, and then click the **Import** button.

4\. Select the **Microsoft Forms** option.

5\. Click the blue file icon and then select your Excel file.

6\. Click the **Import** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13665668136599)

7\. After clicking Import, there will be a prompt. This will state how many profiles were successfully imported. Your profiles will be imported to a category corresponding to your selected format. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13666711338263)

8\. You can also import using the Drag and Drop method. Navigate to the Profiles tab in Profile Builder, find the forms file, click on it, and then drag it to Profile Builder.  
![2023-03-28_17_09_59-Google.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13427495644311)  

2\. On the drop-down, click Microsoft Form and click the **Save** button.

3\. After clicking **Import/Save**, there will be a prompt. This will state how many profiles were successfully imported. Your profiles will be imported to a category corresponding to your selected format. 

![2023-03-28_17_22_35-Completed_-_Import_Profiles.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13427863000471)

# Profile Builder - Profiles - Tools - AI Address Jig – AYCD

Profile Builder - Profiles - Tools - AI Address Jig

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16039185026583-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Tools-AI-Address-Jig/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

July 14, 2023 21:35

Introduction
------------

You can utilize the AYCD ChatGPT AI to create jigs for your address. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can use ChatGPT directly within Profile Builder to generate address jigs. AYCD includes 100,000 tokens per month for free for AI usage.
*   You can create your own Jig Rules Configuration if you want to use specific jigging techniques. You can also create different Jig Rules Configurations for each of your addresses.
*   To improve accuracy, you can provide an example of your desired address jig output in the Jig Rules Configuration dialog.

AI Address Jig
--------------

1\. Navigate to the Profiles section in Profile Builder under Profiles. This section has an icon that looks like a book with a person on it titled **Profiles** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Profiles you want to jig your address for in the table.

3\. Click on the Tools button at the top right of the view or right-click on the table and click on **Tools**. This will bring up the **Tools** dialog for Profiles.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39330173746711)

4\. Click the dropdown and choose **AI Address Jig.**

5\. Click **Start**. The AI Address Jig dialog will open. 

6\. By default, Profiles are created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Profiles created into a unique category.

7\. Input the actual address you want to create jigs for into the **Original Address** field. This field supports Address Lines 1, 2, and 3.

8\. Click the **Jig Billing Address** checkbox to replace your billing address with the jigged address.

9\. Click the **Jig Shipping Address** checkbox to replace your shipping address with the jigged address.

10\. Select which Jig Rules Configuration you want to use in the **Jig Rules Configuration** section. You can use our default configuration or create your own.

11\. Create your own Jig Rules Configurations with the **Add** button. To improve accuracy, you can provide an example of your desired address jig output in the Jig Rules Configuration dialog.

12\. You can edit your Jig Rules Configurations with the **Edit** button.

13\. You can remove your Jig Rules Configurations with the **Remove** button. You can save multiple configurations for each of your addresses.

14\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Jig** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16054668039959)

15\. The Jig Address Confirmation dialog will show you the estimated cost of the operation. AYCD includes 100,000 tokens per month for free for AI usage. If you use all of your balance, you can add your own OpenAI API key in Settings | ChatGPT Service.

16\. Once you have confirmed the dialog is correct, click the **Proceed** button. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16074678439703)

17\. The completed dialog confirms your jigs were successfully generated, as well as the total cost of the operation.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16041695048215)

18\. Your new jigged Profiles can be found in the All category or the category specified in the previous dialog.

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Microsoft (Outlook/Hotmail) Accounts – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Microsoft (Outlook/Hotmail) Accounts

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27311400040727-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Microsoft-Outlook-Hotmail-Accounts/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

October 24, 2024 20:01

Introduction
------------

You can generate Outlook/Hotmail accounts with Inbox. After generating, you can use the various Outlook tools offered within Inbox!

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To log into your Outlook/Hotmail accounts, you need to add your email credentials to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to add your Outlook accounts to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   Microsoft prompts for various forms of verification during login. Inbox can automatically handle TOTP 2FA and recovery email codes. You can add your TOTP 2FA Secret Key and recovery email to your credentials.  
*   You can configure how many OAuth2 browsers run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools. 

Outlook / Hotmail Account Generation
------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**

2\. Click on the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Generate** button. The generate dialog will appear.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27313397332119)

3\. By default, accounts are generated in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to generate your account into a unique category.

4\. Specify a **Domain** to use for the account. You can choose between Outlook.com and Hotmail.com

5\. Specify the **Quantity** of accounts to create.

6\. If you want accounts to automatically generate after configuring the dialog, enable the **Automatically start 'Generate New Account' tool** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27313397334935)

7\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

8\. The mass edit dialog will automatically open. You can add additional information to your tasks before proceeding with generation.

9\. By default, accounts are generated in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to generate your account into a unique category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27320303831447)

10\. Specify a **Password** to use for the account. You can leave this blank, and Inbox will automatically generate a random, unique password. 

11\. Specify **Recovery Emails** to use with your accounts. Inbox will automatically add the recovery email if Microsoft requires you to add one during login or unlocking your account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27320261782807)

12\. Specify an **IMAP Proxy** you want to use when syncing mail and viewing your emails. We recommend using an IMAP proxy when logging into many accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27320303832471)

13\. Specify a **Browser Proxy** you want to use when logging into/managing your Outlook/Hotmail account. We recommend using a Browser proxy when logging into many accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27320261783191)

14\. Optionally, you can exclude the account from mail syncing / displaying emails by enabling the **Exclude from Emails View** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27320303833111)

15\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. Your accounts will be added to the tableview.

16\. Select the newly created account tasks, click the **Tools** button, select the **Outlook Generate New Account** option, and click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27320261784087)

17\. If you want emails to populate in the emails tab after your account(s) is generated and logged in, enable the **Sync mail after login** checkbox.

18\. If you want to force re-login into the credentials before generating your account(s), enable the **Force re-login credentials** checkbox.

19\. Before generating accounts, activity is randomly generated (through warmup) to increase trust in the generated account. If you want to skip the warmup process, enable the **Skip warmup** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27320303836567)

20\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27320303837079)

21\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used if your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow or other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, you need to select it with the **Edit** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29766044558487)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   You must also assign the phone number that correlates with each task to the task. You can do this by selecting your task, right-clicking> Edit > Edit, going to the Credentials section, and inputting the phone number.
*   After creating your Mail Task Template, you need to select it with the **Edit** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29766044561303)

22\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Outlook forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27320422110743)

22\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to manually solve captchas, you can select the **Manual** option. [Click here to learn how to configure AutoSolve with Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27372634713111)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27373352996119)

23\. After you finish configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your accounts will attempt to generate. If you are not using a third-party captcha provider, you must manually solve the captcha in the browser when prompted. Once your accounts have finished generating, the browser status will show **Account Created**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27322134768407)

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27040329117847-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Unlock-Account)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook - Change / Verify/Update Recovery Email](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27032547222551-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Change-Verify-Update-Recovery-Email)

# Profile Builder - Profiles - Expressions & Expression Templates – AYCD

Profile Builder - Profiles - Expressions & Expression Templates

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058115713-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Expressions-Expression-Templates/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

October 22, 2020 18:34

Introduction
------------

Expressions allow you to have more control over the types of jigs you use when editing profiles, creating profiles from Virtual Cards, or even creating new ones. Expression Templates allow you to create a reusable template rather than typing out your Expressions each time.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can use Expressions from the Mass Edit/Jig dialog, the Create New Profile dialog, and when importing a CSV.
*   You can test out your expressions inside Profile Builder by clicking Available Expressions near the bottom of the dialog. There is a test expression field that allows you to test the result of your expressions.

Using Expressions with Mass Edit
--------------------------------

#### %%**:**

*   The %% expression will allow you to write a percent symbol into a field as we use % for expressions. If you just type one % it will not show up.  
    ![__.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360095492154)  
     

#### %c\[Length\]%:

*   The %c\[Length\]% expression will allow you to insert a specified number of random characters into the field you are using. This is great for adding letter jigs before or after an address or for anywhere you want to use a set of random characters.  
    ![_c3_.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360097796993)  
     

#### %c\[Min-Max\]%:

*   The %c\[Min-Max\]% expression will allow you to insert a random number of characters between the specified range you have provided. This is great if you have a number of profiles and you want to vary the jig of letters before an address between 1 letter and 3 letters.  
    ![_c1-3_.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360095494394)  
     

#### %c\[Comma Separated Words\]%:

*   The %c\[Comma Separated Words\]% expression will allow you to insert a random word from a set you specify into a profile field.   
    ![_croad-street_.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360097801533)

#### %fname% and %lname%:

*   The %fname% and %lname%: expressions will allow you to insert a random first name or last name into any field you specify. This is great for generating names of profiles or adding a name to an email address. Normally these expressions will generate a different name in each place on the billing but will become even more powerful when paired with the [%var.\[variable name\]=\[expression\]% and %var.\[variable name\]%](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058115713-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Expressions-Expression-Templates#h_01EN99GZ6P18W98DAQDTGDHRRS)
     expressions as they allow you to use the same generated name in multiple fields such as a name and email field.  
    ![_fname_.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360095500434)  
    ![_lname_.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360095500614)  
     

#### %ffname% and %mfname%

*   The %ffname% and %mfname% expressions will allow you to insert a randomly generated first name that is commonly associated with its respective gender into any field you specify. Using the %ffname% expression will generate a randomly generated female first name, and using the %mfname% expression will generate a randomly generated male first name.  
    ![_mfname_.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/5416427624343)  
    ![_ffname_.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/5416490707991)

#### %n\[Digits\]%:

*   The %n\[Digits\]% expression is used to specify a random number of digits to be generated in the field you specify. This is great for generating a random Phone Number for the digits that follow a specific area code or for unit numbers.  
    ![_n_.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360095502214)

#### %n\[Min\]-\[Max\]%:

*   The %n\[Min\]-\[Max\]% expression is used to specify a random number in between the min and max values you provide. This can be useful for setting a random unit number or for anywhere you want a random number within a specific range.  
    ![_n222-444_.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360097809113)  
     

#### %n\[Comma Separated Numbers\]%:

*   The %n\[Comma Separated Numbers\]% expression will allow you to insert a random number from the ones you specify in your profile field.   
    ![_n14-9000_.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360095506134)  
     

#### %n\[Starting Number\]++%:

*   The %n\[Starting Number\]++% expression allows you to insert a starting number that is increased by for each profile created. This is great for numbering your profiles when you are creating them or for use in other fields where you want the numbers to be used in succession.   
    ![_n2_.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360095511574)

#### %var.\[variable name\]=\[expression\]% and %var.\[variablename\]%:

*   The %var.\[variable name\]=\[expression\]% and %var.\[variablename\]%: expressions are used in conjunction with one another and with other expressions. They allow you to bind the value of an expression to a variable using %var.\[variable name\]=\[expression\]%. Then later, you can reuse the result of that expression by using %var.\[variablename\]% in another field. This is great as instead of getting two separate values when you try to use %fname% in two places, you can instead do %var.name=fname% in the name column and then reuse that value by doing %var.name%  
    ![variablename.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360097819653)

Expressions
-----------

1\. Navigate over to the Expression Templates section of Profile Builder in Profiles. This section has the book icon titled Expression Templates when you hover over it.

2\. To create an Expression Template click the **File** button in the top-right, then click **Create**.

3\. In the New Expression Template dialog click the box next to the field you wish to edit, and then input the Expression. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39325607502103)

4\. Once you have finished editing your template, you need to name it; simply input the name into the Template Name field. After you have named the template, click **Save**.

5\. Your Expression Template will be saved and available for you. You can Import, Export, and Edit Expression Templates from the **File** and **Edit** buttons. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39325613691415)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - OAuth2 Automatic / Manual Login (Gmail/Outlook/Hotmail/Yahoo/AOL) – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - OAuth2 Automatic / Manual Login (Gmail/Outlook/Hotmail/Yahoo/AOL)

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/25709284534295-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-OAuth2-Automatic-Manual-Login-Gmail-Outlook-Hotmail-Yahoo-AOL/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")





![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

August 16, 2024 22:07

Introduction
------------

Some providers, such as Hotmail/Outlook/Yahoo/AOL, require you to log in to your accounts via OAuth2. You can use automatic or manual login options to access your email credentials, view your emails, use mail scrapers, and more.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To login to your Outlook/Hotmail accounts, you need to add your email credentials to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to do this.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   Microsoft prompts for various forms of verification during login. Inbox can automatically handle TOTP 2FA and recovery email codes. You can add your TOTP 2FA Secret Key and recovery email to your credentials.  
*   We recommend using automatic login initially. You can use manual login if some of your accounts encounter additional security measures that Inbox does not automatically handle.
*   You can configure how many OAuth2 browsers run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools. 

OAuth2 Automatic Login
----------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to automatically login to. Then click the Tools button and select OAuth2 Automatic Login.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457900265111)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457900268055)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457900269463)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457891316503)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Outlook forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457891318423)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. [Click here and navigate to the Captcha Services section of the Inbox Settings guide to learn how to configure automatic captcha solving](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457900278551)

8\. If you want to automatically sync mail after login, enable the **Sync mail after login** checkbox.

9\. If you want to force re-login into the credentials before running the OAuth2 Automatic Login tool, enable the **Force re-login credentials** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457891321623)

10\. You can automatically move accounts Inbox successfully logged in to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37457900283415)

11\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button.  Your accounts will attempt to login. When they successfully log in, the **Browser Status** shows "**Logged In**" or "**Already Logged In**," and the IMAP Status shows "**Good**."

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37458051615639)

Manual Login
------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to automatically login to. Then click the Tools button and select OAuth2 Manual Login.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37458051618967)

3\. If you want to automatically sync mail after login, enable the **Sync mail after login** checkbox.

4\. If you want to force re-login into the credentials before running the OAuth2 Manual Login tool, enable the **Force re-login credentials** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27012141165847)

5\. A browser will open, allowing you to manually input your account login details and handle any additional security questions. When they successfully log in, the **Browser Status** shows "**Logged In**" or "**Already Logged In**," and the IMAP Status shows "**Good**."

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37458051615639)

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Enable Email Forwarding / Disable Email Forwarding / Check Email Forwarding](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/26963363437719-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Enable-Email-Forwarding-Disable-Email-Forwarding-Check-Email-Forwarding)
