# Crypton (BETA) - Getting Started Guide – AYCD

Crypton (BETA) - Getting Started Guide

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



===========================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

July 06, 2022 20:28

Crypton is a new unified Cryptocurrency tool by AYCD. It enables you to perform functions like generating wallets, sending crypto and NFTs, dispersing crypto across multiple wallets, viewing and managing transactions, NFTs, and more. _Crypton currently supports Ethereum and Solana wallets_.

* * *

Disclaimers
===========

*   Never give your private key to anyone. We will never ask for your private key when working with you. Under no circumstance should you expose your private key to anyone else.
*   AYCD or any of our representatives do not endorse or recommend that any cryptocurrency or NFT should be bought, sold, or held by you. You should conduct your own due diligence before making any investment decisions.
*   AYCD or any of our representatives will never offer you deals or opportunities involving cryptocurrencies or NFTs.
*   All representatives of AYCD have the support team or higher role in our server. Never accept help or support from anyone other than AYCD support representatives.
*   Crypton stores all data locally with secure encryption. It is highly recommended to also add a Password Lock to have another layer of encryption and security.
*   Ensure your computer is up to date and you are not installing unnecessary or suspicious programs. Please maintain an active and up to date antivirus on all devices you use our products on to provide a secure environment.

[Wallets](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMGB73S71H31WHGW706T8S)
 | [Transactions](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMGHY0F30WWX4Y98XBWGPX)
 | [NFTs](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMH85S4SJQT7642VJ65Q2N)
 | [Prices](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMHGAZCYDPDS1PZYHA6BKV)
 | [RPCs](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMHPMDJJ0NWGT0Z3Z8EHKQ)
 | [Settings](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMHZCDFZ9TY7CZC5WY2VFA)
 | [Additional Resources](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMJTNBESX8F5JT9QVX2H8S)

### Wallets

The wallets view allows you to add or generate Ethereum and Solana wallets, see detailed balances for wallets and tokens, send and disperse crypto across multiple wallets, and sync enabled features of your wallets used throughout Crypton.

[Adding Wallets](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMM3Y1EE8PJGXGYVBZFRZB)
 | [Features](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMM9CPFAQXCTK59RTRYCVV)
 | [Syncing](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMMFJC7DYZWYV1CQPA461R)
 | [Balances](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMMMF0WW2X8AJHN4MC5QTA)
 | [Generating Wallets](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMMW5HT0BJS8GMJHXWVFGZ)
 | [Sending Crypto](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMNQT5W4F6T1HCARGY5V4S)

#### Adding Wallets

Crypton supports adding wallets either by their Public Key, which allows view-only access to the wallet or by the Private Key, which allows you to send Crypto or NFTs and perform on-chain operations from that wallet. There are a few ways to add wallets.

[Manually Creating](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#WalletsManuallyCreating)
 | [Importing Wallets](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#WalletsImporting)
 | [Exporting Wallets](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#WalletsExporting)

**Manually Creating**

1.  In the Wallets view, click on File > Create, select your wallet type from the dropdown, and then click Next.  
    ![Crypton-Wallets-Manually-1.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7363912548375/Crypton-Wallets-Manually-1.gif)
2.  The Add Wallet dialog will open with a few options to configure:  
    _Category_: The Category the wallet will be in after it gets added to Crypton.  
    _Name_: This is the display name used in Crypton to help you identify the wallet.  
    _Public or Private Key_: This is the key for the wallet. The Public Key gives you view-only access to the wallet, and the Private Key gives you full access to send Crypto, NFTs, and other on-chain operations.  
    _Notes_: This allows you to create a note with information you want to save alongside this wallet.  
    _Features_: These are the features you want to enable for the wallet. They include the ability to store transaction history, show NFTs, allow you to access the breakdown of tokens held by the wallet, and the ability to receive notifications for wallet events.  
    ![Crypton-Wallets-Manually-2-1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7408292434199/Crypton-Wallets-Manually-2-1.png)
3.  After you have configured the wallet, click Save, and Crypton will begin to sync the balance and data for enabled features. Clicking on the spinner will reveal a tooltip with what is currently being synced.  
    ![Crypton-Wallets-Manually-4.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7363982750487/Crypton-Wallets-Manually-4.gif)

**Importing Wallets**

If there are multiple wallets that you want to add to Crypton, rather than adding them manually one at a time, you can use the [Crypton Wallet CSV Import Format](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/12Y0DAkfIq9gA8xfcDbULw3Exfu6G5IsB0W04Prk7fGA/export?format=csv)
 to add and configure the features of each wallet quickly. _We recommend downloading the CSV Format and editing the file locally rather than online with Google Drive. It is important to be extremely careful when handling Private Keys and where you store them in an unencrypted form._ 

1.  Click on the link to start downloading the [Import Format as a CSV](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/12Y0DAkfIq9gA8xfcDbULw3Exfu6G5IsB0W04Prk7fGA/export?format=csv)
    .
2.  Once the download is complete, open the file and fill out the rows for your wallets.
3.  Back in Crypton in the Wallets view, click on File > Import > Find the CSV file, and this will import all of the wallets on your CSV into Crypton.  
    ![CRP-Wallet-Import.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7361388381847/CRP-Wallet-Import.gif)

Once you have successfully imported your wallets, we recommend permanently deleting the file if it contains any Private Keys so that you do not have them sitting in a plain text file.

**Exporting Wallets**

You can use the export feature if you want to export your wallets from Crypton. Please remember that exporting your wallets will create a plaintext unencrypted CSV file with your wallet's private keys. Be mindful of how you send exported files and where you store them.

1.  In the Wallets view, click on File > Export. Review the prompt and make a selection based on whether you want Private Keys exported or whether you want only Public keys exported. After that, select Proceed.![Crypton-Wallets-Exporting-2.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7410396631191/Crypton-Wallets-Exporting-2.gif)

#### Features

Whether you are adding or importing your wallets into Crypton, you can enable different features on each wallet that will extend the functionality of a wallet throughout Crypton. This includes the ability to search through your wallet's previous transactions, view and send held NFTs, and view your wallet's Token balance. These features can be enabled when adding or later when you edit the wallet.

*   _Enable Transaction History_: This allows Crypton to sync all of the transactions made by a wallet to the Transaction History tab based on the wallet type allowing you to search and explore the wallet's transactions. Enabling this will also ensure that pending transactions save within Crypton after completion. Without this feature enabled, Pending Transactions will be removed from Crypton when it is closed.
*   _Enable NFTs_: This allows NFTs held by this wallet to sync into Cryptons NFTs view. From that view, you can search, filter, and hide any NFT. If this wallet has private key level access, it will allow you to send and disperse NFTs to different wallets.
*   _Enable Tokens_: This allows all Token balances on a wallet to sync into Crypton. When enabled, it allows you to see the various Tokens held, their amounts, and their balance.
*   _Enable Notifications_: This allows Crypton to send Notifications for any events on the wallet detected while Crypton is open. In order for Notifications to send, you must also add an AYCD or Discord Webhook URL to Settings > Notifications. 

#### Syncing

Wallets in Crypton will automatically sync during startup or when a transaction is detected on the wallet, but these are the only times an automatic sync will occur for wallet features. If you are using Crypton and want to ensure you see the most accurate information, you can Sync your wallets in one of the following ways:

*   _Individual Wallets_: You can Sync individual wallets from the Wallets view by clicking the Sync icon in the Actions column. This will bring up the Sync Wallet dialog with the enabled features for you to select and Sync.  
    ![Crypton-Wallets-Sync-1.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7364704673815/Crypton-Wallets-Sync-1.gif)
*   _Multiple Wallets_: To Sync multiple wallets, select them and go to Options > Sync > Selected. This will bring up the Sync Wallet dialog with the enabled features for all selected wallets for you to select and Sync.  
    ![Crypton-Wallets-Sync-2.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7364704682903/Crypton-Wallets-Sync-2.gif)

#### Balances

When you add a Wallet to Crypton, it will sync the wallet balance and display it under the Balance column of the table. The balance will show in two amounts; the amount of ETH or SOL depending on your wallet type and the value in your Global Currency Type set in Settings > User Interface. 

**Tokens**

If you have the Tokens feature enabled for a wallet, you can view additional details on the wallet's holdings of ERC20 or SPL Tokens. To view a more detailed balance from the Wallets:

1.  Click on the wallet icon under the Actions column. This will Sync the Balance before showing the detailed balance of your wallet's holdings.

![Crypton-Wallets-Token-Balance-1.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7364693840663/Crypton-Wallets-Token-Balance-1.gif)

#### Generating Wallets

If you do not yet have a wallet or need to create additional wallets for your use case, you can quickly generate up to 1,000 wallets at once from within Crypton. To do this:

1.  In the Wallets view, click on Wallet Tools > Generate Wallets. Select the Wallet Type of either Ethereum or Solana and click Next.  
    ![Crypton-Wallets-Generate-1.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7362375060119/Crypton-Wallets-Generate-1.gif)
2.  This will open the Generate Wallets dialog with a few options:  
    _Category_: This will allow you to specify a category for the generated wallets to appear in.  
    _Name_: This is the display name used in Crypton to help identify the wallet. It will sequence wallet names from 0 to the amount of wallets you are generating in a "-#" format.  
    _Amount_: The amount of wallets you want to generate.  
    _Notes_: This allows you to create a note with information you want to save alongside the generated wallets.  
    _Features_: These are the features you want to enable for the wallet. They include the ability to sync transaction history, show NFTs, and allow you to access the breakdown of tokens held by the wallet. These can always be modified after generation.  
    ![Crypton-Wallets-Generate-2-1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7410574152983/Crypton-Wallets-Generate-2-1.png)
3.  After you have filled out the options for generating your wallets, click Generate.  
    ![Crypton-Wallets-Generate-3.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7362386742167/Crypton-Wallets-Generate-3.gif)

#### Sending Crypto

Crypton enables you to send funds between wallets, disperse funds to multiple wallets, manage transfers, and track the status of uncompleted transfers, all without having to leave Crypton. To send Crypto, you will need private key level access to the wallet you are sending from.

[Send Crypto](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#WalletsSendCrypto)
 | [Disperse Crypto](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#WalletsDisperseCrypto)

**Send Crypto**

Using Crypton, you can easily send funds between wallets of the same type and create multiple transfers from the same dialog. In order to initiate a transfer, the wallet you are sending from must be in Crypton and be a private key level wallet.

1.  From the Wallets view, there are two ways to initiate a transfer. You can find the wallet you are sending from and use the Send Crypto button in the Actions column, or you can use Wallet Tools > Send Crypto.  
    ![Crypton-Wallets-SendCrypto-1.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7364815111575/Crypton-Wallets-SendCrypto-1.gif)
2.  This will open up the Send Crypto dialog, where you can completely configure your transfers. _Based on the Wallet Type, your dialog may look slightly different due to how Ethereum and Solana fees work._  
    _From_: This is the wallet you are using to send funds from. You cannot specify a public key level wallet here.  
    _To_: This is the wallet you will be sending funds to; this can be a wallet of any access level. If you are sending funds to another wallet in Crypton, you can select it from the dropdown. If you are sending to an external wallet, you can paste the wallet address into the field.  
    _Amount_: This will be the amount you are sending. You can enter a value in either field, and it will convert it to show you the amount in the opposite field.  
    _Add Transfer_: This will create another transfer card for you to create multiple transfers simultaneously.  
    _Max Priority Fee (ETH Only)_: This "tip" goes directly to the miners and incentives them to prioritize your transaction.  
    _Max Fee (ETH Only)_: This is the most you will pay for a transaction and is calculated from the Base Fee + Priority Fee. _You can see the current transfer rates in the top right of Crypton_.  
    _Low, Medium, and High (ETH Only)_: These buttons will adjust the fee fields with the suggested amounts depending on your desired transaction speed.  
    ![Crypton-Wallets-SendCrypto-2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7364932283927/Crypton-Wallets-SendCrypto-2.png)
3.  Once you have completed all of the fields in the dialog for your transfer, you can press Verify. This allows Crypton to verify the transfers and will highlight each transaction with a color indicating its status: Green for valid, Yellow to caution you and display any warnings about the transfer, or Red if it is invalid and why. Verify also enables the More Details button for Green and Yellow transfers, which shows additional details on the wallet balances and fees.
4.  Once all transfers have received a green status, the Submit button will be available. We recommend reviewing each of your transactions again before clicking Submit. After this, there will be one last confirmation dialog before submitting your transfers with the default transaction delay set in Settings > Network. You can view and track each transaction's status from the Pendings Transactions tab.  
    ![Crypton-Wallets-SendCrypto-ETH2.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7365485132183/Crypton-Wallets-SendCrypto-ETH2.gif)

**Disperse Crypto**

The Disperse Crypto feature allows you to create transfers to disperse funds from one wallet to multiple wallets equally. As with Send Crypto, the From wallet used to initiate the transfer must have Private Key level access and enough funds to cover the transactions you are trying to create.

1.  In order to start the process, in the Wallets view, click on Wallet Tools > Disperse Crypto. Select the Wallet Type and click Next.
2.  This will open the Disperse Crypto dialog, which allows you to configure multiple transfers to disperse funds sent from one wallet evenly across multiple receiving wallets.  
    _From Wallet_: This is the wallet with the funds you will be dispersing.  
    _To Wallets_: These are the wallets you want to receive the funds sent with. You can select specific wallets inside a category by expanding it or select all of the wallets within a category in one click by selecting the category itself.  
    _Send Amount_: This is the amount before fees that will be withdrawn from the From Wallet to be dispersed across the To Wallets. Crypton will split this amount evenly between all of the To wallets you have selected and display that amount in the Receive Amount fields.  
    _Receive Amount_: This is the amount that each To Wallet will receive. This amount gets multiplied by the number of To Wallets you have selected to get the total amount of funds that will be sent and to display in the Send Amount fields.
3.  Once you have made your selections, click Next. This will open the Send Crypto dialog with each of your transfers prefilled based on the selections you made. Here you can add or remove transfers and review them before clicking Verify. If you are dispersing Ethereum, you must set the fees before submitting your transfers.
4.  After submitting your transfers, there will be one last confirmation dialog before submitting your transfers with the default transaction delay set in Settings > Network. You can view and track each transaction's status from the Pendings Transactions tab.  
    ![Crypton-Wallets-Disperse-ETH.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7366063086743/Crypton-Wallets-Disperse-ETH.gif)

### Transactions

The Transaction view allows you to track the status of transactions created in Crypton and, if enabled, browse your Solana and Ethereum transaction history under their respective tabs.

[Pending Transactions](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QN9MCP47V05JZVPVBBG2CS)
 | [Transaction History](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QN9XC647V2H70GKD09VXS0)

#### Pending Transactions

After you initiate a transaction in Crypton, either by sending crypto or an NFT, you will see it in the Pending Transactions tab as scheduled with the default transaction delay. Here you can monitor its status, cancel it, or in the event of an Ethereum transaction, speed it up by providing higher fee amounts.

[Viewing Transaction Status](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#TransactionsViewStatus)
 | [Canceling Transactions](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#TransactionsCancel)
 | [Speed Up Transactions (ETH Only)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#TransactionsSpeedUp)

**Viewing Transaction Status**  
After you initiate a transfer, you can monitor its status or review transactions completed while Crypton was open. The possible statuses for a transaction are:

*   _Scheduled_: This status shows with a countdown for the default transaction delay and indicates the transaction has not been submitted to the blockchain by Crypton yet.
*   Processing: This status indicates the transaction was submitted to the network by Crypton and is waiting for the required amount of network confirmations to be completed. 
*   Failed: This status indicates the transaction was unsuccessful. You can double-click on this status to show additional details on the failed transaction.
*   Completed: This indicates the transaction was successful and has received the required amount of network confirmations. 
*   _Canceled:_ This status will show on a transaction when you cancel it from within Crypton.

**Canceling Transactions**

If you have started a transfer of Crypto or an NFT and no longer want to proceed with it, if it still has a Scheduled status, it has not been submitted to the network by Crypton yet and can be canceled immediately. To do that:

1.  From the Pending Transactions table, select the transactions you want to cancel. You can hold Ctrl and click on table items to multi-select them.
2.  Go to Options > Cancel > Selected or All if you want to cancel all Scheduled transactions.
3.  Confirm that you want to cancel the transactions by pressing Yes and this will cancel the scheduled transactions.  
    ![Crypton-Transactions-Cancel-SOL.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7366129065879/Crypton-Transactions-Cancel-SOL.gif)

In the case of Ethereum transactions, you may be able to cancel them after they are pending; however, there is no guarantee that a transaction that is already submitted will be successfully canceled. To cancel a Processing Ethereum transaction:

1.  Select the Ethereum transactions you want to cancel. You can hold Ctrl and click on table items to multi-select them. Go to Options > Cancel > Selected or All.
2.  A prompt notifying you it was already submitted to the Network and asking if you'd still like to cancel this will appear.
3.  If you proceed, it will display the Cancel Transactions dialog where you can specify a new Max Priority Fee and Max Fee to use to cancel the transactions. This must be higher than the amount used to submit the original transaction.
4.  Once you have set those amounts, click Verify and Submit.  
    ![Crypton-Transactions-Cancel-ETH.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7366469052055/Crypton-Transactions-Cancel-ETH.gif)

**Speed Up Transactions (ETH Only)**

After submitting an Ethereum transaction, you can increase its priority by using the Speed Up function. Speed Up allows you to override an existing transaction with a new one containing a higher priority and higher max fee, thus increasing its priority on the network. To speed up a transaction:

1.  Select the Ethereum transactions you want to speed up. You can hold Ctrl and click on table items to multi-select them. Go to Options > Speed Up > Selected or All.
2.  The Speed Up Transactions dialog will open, asking you to specify the new fee amounts for the transaction. These must be higher than the original transaction amounts.
3.  Once you have specified the amounts, click Verify, and then Speed Up. This will override the existing transactions with the new fee amounts.

#### Transaction History

The Transaction history is split between two tabs; Ethereum Transaction History and Solana Transaction History. These tables will display the complete transaction history for wallets with the feature enabled.

[Enabling Transaction History](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMM9CPFAQXCTK59RTRYCVV)
 | [Viewing & Filters](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#TransactionsViewFilters)
 | [Searching Transactions](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#TransactionsSearching)

**Viewing & Filters**

When you open the transaction history tab, it will show a table with all of your transactions. Here it will display information like the Transaction Hash, the wallets used in the transaction, the amount sent, fees, and the completion date. By default, this table shows only Normal Transactions. However, if you want to see your NFT/Token based transactions, you can use the Filter By option to flip between Normal and NFT/Token.

**Searching Transactions**

If you want to find a specific transaction or a collection of transactions in your wallet's history, you can use the Advanced Search function to search by the Transaction Hash, To and From Wallets, and for transactions between specific dates.

### NFTs

The NFTs view allows you to see all of the NFTs and their properties for any wallet with the NFTs feature enabled. For wallets that you have private key level access to, you can perform on chain operations like sending and dispersing NFTs between wallets.

[Enable NFTs](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QMM9CPFAQXCTK59RTRYCVV)
 | [Viewing NFTs](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QNAYXTBQPXSDPRYWX9B118)
 | [Sending NFTs](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G7QNBAGZK3MQ88JPXWVDZJ68)

#### Viewing NFTs

With the NFTs feature enabled on your wallet, you should see your NFTs in this view split into the same category as in your Wallets view. From here, we can do things like Previewing the NFT to see its properties or rarety data, hiding NFTs from the view, and searching for specific collections or wallets using the advanced search.

[Previewing NFTs](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#NFTPreview)
 | [Hiding NFTs](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#NFTHiding)
 | [Searching NFTs](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#NFTSearching)

**Previewing NFTs**

If there is an NFT that you want to see additional details on, you can view it either by double-clicking on the NFT or right-clicking on it and clicking Show Details. A dialog with a bigger version of the NFT, the description, properties, additional links, and any available rarity information will appear.  
![Crypton-NFTs-ShowDetails.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7411767670295/Crypton-NFTs-ShowDetails.gif)

**Hiding NFTs**

If there is an NFT that you don't want to see or an NFT you don't want to be displayed for any reason, you can hide it. To hide the NFT:

1.  Select the NFTs you want to hide, right-click and press hide.   
    ![Crypton-NFTs-Hiding.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7411162067479/Crypton-NFTs-Hiding.gif)

Once you have hidden an NFT, you can use the Filter by Hidden option to see your hidden NFTs and show them again by right-clicking > Show.

**Searching NFTs**

There are different options for searching and filtering your NFTs. You can use the Filter By option, which will allow you to sort them by token type or allow you to show any hidden NFTs. If you want to be more specific, you can search through your NFTs using the search field with the following parameters:

*   Public Wallet Address
*   Project Name
*   Project Description
*   Symbol
*   Token Address
*   Properties/Metadata
*   Image URL

#### Sending NFTs

With Crypton, you can send your NFTs between wallets, disperse NFTs across multiple wallets, and easily manage all of those transfers from one place. To send NFTs, you will need private key level access to the wallet the NFTs are on.

[Send NFTs](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#NFTSending)
 | [Disperse NFTs](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7277779060119-Crypton-BETA-Getting-Started-Guide#NFTDisperse)

**Send NFTs**

To quickly see the NFTs eligible for sending, you can switch the Filter By to Owned. This will leave only wallets that you have Private Key level access to in view.

1.  From the NFTs view, you can use Ctrl + Click to select one or more NFTs to send. These can be sent all to one address, or you can send each selected NFT to a separate address. Click NFT Tools > Send NFTs > Selected.
2.  If you want to send all the selected NFTs to one address, you can enter that To Address to prefill that field in the next dialog and then click Next. If you want to send each NFT to a different wallet, click Next to skip this step.
3.  This will open the Send NFTs dialog. You can set or change the wallet you send each NFT to and preview the Gas Limit for each NFT. If you send Ethereum NFTs, you will also be able to set the Max Priority Fee and Max Fee to be applied to each transfer.
4.  Once you have completed all of the fields in the dialog, you can press Verify. This allows Crypton to verify the transfers of your NFTs and highlight each transaction with a color indicating its status: Green for valid, Yellow to caution you and display any warnings about the transfer, or Red if it is invalid and why. Verify also enables the More Details button for Green and Yellow transfers, which shows additional details on the wallet balances and fees.
5.  Once they all have a green status, the Submit button will be available. We recommend reviewing each of the NFT sends one more before clicking Submit. After this, there will be one last confirmation dialog before submitting your transfers with the default transaction delay set in Settings > Network. You can view and track each transaction's status from the Pendings Transactions tab.

**Disperse NFTs**

The Disperse NFTs feature gives you the unique ability to quickly create many transactions at once that will split your NFTs as evenly as it can across the number of wallets you select. This is useful if you require a certain token in a wallet to participate in a future mint. To quickly see the NFTs eligible for sending, you can switch the Filter By to Owned. This will leave only wallets that you have Private Key level access to in view.

1.  From the NFTs view, you can use Ctrl + Click to select more than one NFT that you want to be dispersed. Click NFT Tools > Disperse NFTs > Selected.
2.  This will open the Disperse NFTs dialog, where you can select the To Wallets that will receive your selected NFTs. You can select specific wallets inside a category by expanding it or select all of the wallets within a category by selecting the category itself. Towards the bottom of this dialog, you can see how many NFTs will be received by how many wallets. Once you are satisfied, click Next.
3.  If you have selected more Wallets than NFTs, a dialog will notify you of the number of wallets that will receive an NFT, and you can either continue or go back to modify the number of selected wallets.
4.  After you have proceeded, the Send NFTs dialog will appear with the To Wallets prefilled based on your selected wallets. If you want, you can change the wallet you send each NFT to and preview the Gas Limit for each NFT. If you send Ethereum NFTs, you will also be able to set the Max Priority Fee and Max Fee to be applied to each transfer.
5.  Once you have completed all of the fields in the dialog, you can press Verify. This allows Crypton to verify the transfers of your NFTs and highlight each transaction with a color indicating its status: Green for valid, Yellow to caution you and display any warnings about the transfer, or Red if it is invalid and why. Verify also enables the More Details button for Green and Yellow transfers, which shows additional details on the wallet balances and fees.
6.  Once they all have a green status, the Submit button will be available. We recommend reviewing each of the NFT sends one more before clicking Submit. After this, there will be one last confirmation dialog before submitting your transfers with the default transaction delay set in Settings > Network. You can view and track each transaction's status from the Pendings Transactions tab.

### Prices

The Prices view shows a list of values for some common Crypto, and Fiat currencies relative to your Global Currency Type that you can set in Settings > User Interface > Global Currency Type. In addition to the currencies found here, you can manually add Ethereum and Solana tokens using their Contract address in order to track their prices here.

1.  Click Create and select a wallet type.
2.  Paste the Contract Address for the token you are trying to add and click Save.
3.  If it is a valid address, it will add it to the table and update it every 60 seconds.  
    ![Prices.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/7410961301271/Prices.gif)

Any wallet with the Tokens feature enabled will automatically add the rate to the Prices table.

### RPCs

The RPCs view allows you to add different RPCs to Crypton and benchmark them to see information like their latest block, Protocol Version, and benchmark speeds. You can also override the default RPCs with one of your own. To add an RPC:

1.  Click Create, and the Add RPCs dialog will open. Here you can set the type of RPC (ETH or SOL), a category where your RPC will be saved, and the URLs of the RPCs that you want to add to Crypton.
2.  After you have entered all of the URLs, click on Save. Crypton will benchmark them to make sure they are valid before adding them to the table.

If you want Crypton to use your own RPC, you can override Crypton's RPC by right-clicking on your RPC and clicking Set as Default RPC. To reset the default RPC, go to Settings > Network > Delete the value there and click Save.

### Settings

In the Settings view, you can configure features used across Crypton, such as enabling notifications, modifying the global currency, changing your RPC URLs, enabling the Password Lock, and more.

#### Password Lock

You can read about enabling the Password lock here.

#### Network

*   _Etherscan.io API Key_: This will allow you to set your own Etherscan API Key, which may reduce your likelihood of being rate limited when fetching NFTs or updating Ethereum wallet data.
*   _Ethereum and Solana RPC URLs_: These fields allow you to override the default RPC used in Crypton. We recommend creating your own RPC with services like Alchemy to reduce rate limits.
*   _Transaction Delay in Seconds_: This is the delay that will be used for all Crypto and NFT transactions before they are submitted to the network. This is to allow for off-chain canceling of transactions within Crypton.
*   _Ethereum RPC Max Batch Size_: This is the maximum batch size for Ethereum RPC requests. This value depends on the RPC you are using, as each RPC has its own maximum batch size. If you provide a value that is larger than what your RPC supports, all RPC requests will fail.

#### Notifications

*   _AYCD/Discord Webhook_: This is the Webhook URL that Crypton will use to notify you in Discord based on the Notification Events you have enabled. You will also need to Enable Notifications on each individual wallet in order to receive notifications.

#### User Interface

*   _Global Currency Type_: This currency will be used across the program to display the value of cryptocurrencies, tokens, and other fiat currencies relative to itself.
*   _Open Approved external links without asking for confirmation_: This allows you to instantly open links you click in the program without opening a confirmation dialog displaying the link to the website you are clicking.
*   _Allow autoplay preview for GIF NFTs_: This will allow gifs to autoplay as you scroll through the NFTs view. This may impact the performance and cause higher RAM usage.
*   _Allow autoplay preview for MP4 NFTs_: This will allow mp4s to autoplay as you scroll through the NFTs view. This may impact the performance and cause higher RAM usage.

### Additional Resources

Coming soon...

# AutoSolve AI - Getting Started Guide – AYCD

AutoSolve AI - Getting Started Guide

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4418658160791-AutoSolve-AI-Getting-Started-Guide/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=========================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

January 10, 2022 23:41

### What is AutoSolve AI?

AutoSolve AI is a new backup solving method for your one-click accounts and will allow AutoSolve to solve captchas automatically using AI right in your browser. In addition to the OneClick browser, we are extending the power of AutoSolve AI to Chromium-based browsers such as Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge through the [AutoSolve AI Extension](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4418385024023)
.

_You need both a subscription for the AYCD Toolbox and AutoSolve before you will be able to purchase and use AutoSolve AI._

### Setting up AutoSolve AI

1.  **Once you purchase AutoSolve AI, restart OneClick** and go into the AutoSolve section.
2.  Under AutoSolve you should see AI Solve at the top of the table in the middle of this section. You will also see it in the status bar along the bottom, showing **Companion/AI Status.  
    ![Picture1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/4944795260567/Picture1.png)  
    **
3.  Click on the selected checkbox to add it as a backup service, then start a OneClick account with AutoSolve and let AI Solve get to work solving the captcha challenges you get!  
    ![Picture2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/4944808709399/Picture2.png)

### Settings

Once AutoSolve AI is enabled, you will have some additional settings under AutoSolve Settings.  
![Picture3.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/4944748598423/Picture3.png)

**Max Requests Per Challenge For AI Solve:** This will determine the maximum number of requests that will be used to solve a Captcha before displaying Manual Solve Required.

**Enable AI Solve when testing accounts for one-clicks:** This toggle will enable you to use AI Solve to complete captchas you may get when testing your accounts for one-clicks.

# AutoSolve AI - AutoSolve AI Keys – AYCD

AutoSolve AI - AutoSolve AI Keys

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/8547342184471-AutoSolve-AI-AutoSolve-AI-Keys/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

August 31, 2022 08:08

Introduction
------------

AutoSolve AI Keys allow you to utilize the power of AutoSolve AI directly within bots that support it rather than using it through OneClick. This feature simplifies your setup for supported bots by no longer requiring an Access Token and API Key in your bot, instead just requiring the AutoSolve AI Key generated from within OneClick. While it can make setup easier, there are different rate limits that apply when using it. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   **We recommend using AutoSolve AI through OneClick, as OneClick is optimized for AutoSolve AI and is not subject to the same rate limits.** You can read more about AutoSolve AI [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4418658160791)
    . 
*   In order to generate AutoSolve AI Keys, you will need to have a Toolbox, AutoSolve, and AutoSolve AI subscription.
*   Have OneClick 2.6.7 or newer in order to generate a key.

Accessing your AutoSolve AI Key in OneClick.
--------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the AutoSolve section of OneClick. 

2\. At the top, click on **View AutoSolve AI Key**.

3\. This will open the dialog where you can copy your AutoSolve AI Key to your clipboard, reset the key, and sync the key. Clicking Reset Key will open your default browser and load the AutoSolve AI Dashboard where you can reset the key._  
![2023-05-04_20_28_08-Settings.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14368907565719)  
_

4\. Read through the dialog and click Copy AutoSolve AI Key when you are ready.  
  

Finding your AutoSolve AI Key on the AutoSolve AI Dashboard
-----------------------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the AutoSolve AI section of the Dashboard. 

![2023-05-04_20_55_13-Account___AYCD.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14369934033943)

2\. On the Dashboard you can Copy AI Key, find the link to the  Getting Started Guide, and Reset your AI Key.

Using your AutoSolve AI Key
---------------------------

1\. Once you have found your AutoSolve AI Key, using either of the two methods above, click Copy AutoSolve AI Key.

2\. The bot you are using should have a place for your AutoSolve AI Key. Paste the copied key into that field and save. _If you are using the AutoSolve AI Key, ensure that you remove your Access Token and API Key from that bot so that the two systems are not conflicting_.  
![MEKpreme.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/8547497033367/MEKpreme.png)

# OneClick - Traffic - Search History - View / Export – AYCD

OneClick - Traffic - Search History - View / Export

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/25147315832343-OneClick-Traffic-Search-History-View-Export/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

July 24, 2024 22:56

Introduction
------------

You can view search history related to your Traffic Campaigns to have a better understanding of if your query’s are finding your target domain. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You need to have tasks created and farming for Search History to populate. [Click here to learn how to create tasks](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16222455171479)
    .
*   You need to create Campaign(s) for Search History to populate. [Click here to learn how to create Campaign(s).](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/21313462266647)
    

View Search History
-------------------

1\. Navigate to the Search History section in Traffic. This section has an icon resembling a clock titled **Search History** when you hover over it.

2\. You can view your Search History in the tableview. An example of the Search History section can be found in the image below.

**![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25147330679191)**

3\. You can double-click a query to view additional details such as:

*   Title: The title of the webpage that was viewed.
*   URL: The URL of the webpage that was viewed.
*   Page: Which page the result was on. 
*   Index: Which spot on the page the result was on.
*   Match: If your query found the intended URL when performing a search.
*   Clicked: If the webpage was clicked on.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25148861246615)

4\. Click the **Export** button to export the additional details for a specific query related to Search History.

Export Search History
---------------------

1\. Navigate to the Search History section in Traffic. This section has an icon resembling a clock titled **Search History** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Search History you want to export. Click the **Export** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25164888390423)

3\. A dialog will appear asking if you want to proceed with the export. Click the **Yes** button to proceed.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25164702784535)

4\. A dialog will appear asking if you want to open the exported file's location. Click the **Yes** button to view the file.

**![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25164894779543)**

# OneClick - AutoSolve - Using 3rd Party Services – AYCD

OneClick - AutoSolve - Using 3rd Party Services

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13322756914327-OneClick-AutoSolve-Using-3rd-Party-Services/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

March 23, 2023 20:14

Introduction
------------

For users who do not have a subscription to AutoSolve AI, there is an alternative option available to solve captcha challenges through the use of third-party solvers.

Adding Third Party Solvers
--------------------------

![mstsc_vpBqhZ3bgf.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13322643477527)

1\. Navigate to the AutoSolve tab within OneClick. This section has a cube icon titled **Services** when you hover over it.

2\. Click on the **Create Third Party API Key** on the middle bottom of the screen.

3\. Select the Third Party Solving Service that is being added from the drop down.

4\. Enter a **Nickname** that will help identify the third party API key.

5\. Enter an **API key** that the third party provides. [This guide contains instructions on how to find 3rd party API keys on some providers](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043745313-AutoSolve-Third-Party-Solver-Setup)

6\. Click **Create** to finalize adding the new Third Party API Key.

Configuring Third Party Services
--------------------------------

![UF5bnlcSmI.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13672988757783)

1.  To connect to a Third Party Solver after it's been added, select the service from the left hand column and click **Connect**.

2\. After the service has been enabled, it can be dragged around to re-arrange its priority. 

3\. To disconnect from a Third Party Solver uncheck the **Enabled** box that corresponds to the service in the center column.

# OneClick - Traffic - Campaigns - Tools - Create / View / Export Traffic Report – AYCD

OneClick - Traffic - Campaigns - Tools - Create / View / Export Traffic Report

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/24751247410583-OneClick-Traffic-Campaigns-Tools-Create-View-Export-Traffic-Report/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

July 09, 2024 09:39

Introduction
------------

You can create reports on your Traffic Campaigns to view a summary of data related to your campaigns, such as Domain Clicks, Query Clicks, Page URLs, and Result Types.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You need to have tasks created to view reports. [Click here to learn how to create tasks](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16222455171479)
    .
*   You need to create Traffic Campaigns to view reports. [Click here to learn how to create Traffic Campaigns.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/21313462266647)
    

Create Report
-------------

1\. Navigate to the Campaigns section in Traffic. This section has an icon with a magnifying glass in a book. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24768486018583)

2\. Select the Traffic Campaign(s) for which you want to create a report, then click the Tools button and press **Create Report**. Alternatively, you can right-click your selected tasks, click **Tools**, and then **Create Report**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24768486027799)3\. The new Traffic Report dialog will appear. By default, Traffic Reports are created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Traffic Report created into a unique category. 

4\. Specify a **Name** for the Report.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24768486031383)

5\. Select the **Report Type** you want to create.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24768495084183)

6\. Select the **Campaigns** that will be included in the Report.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24768622786839)

7\. When you have finished configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

View Report
-----------

1\. Navigate to the **Reports** section in Traffic. This section has an icon that looks like a document. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24769883554583)

2\. Select the **Report** you want to view. Double-click the Report to open the Traffic Reports dialog. Depending on the report type, various data will be shown. You can find examples of each Report Type below!

*   Query Click Report

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24769875734039)

*   Domain Click Report

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24769883564183)

*   Page URL Report

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24769875740439)

*   Result Type Report

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24769883571351)

Export Report
-------------

1\. Navigate to the **Reports** section in Traffic. This section has an icon that looks like a document. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24769883554583)

2\. Select the **Report(s)** you want to export.

3\. Click the **File** button, then click **Export**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24769883575319)

4\. A dialog will appear confirming that you want to export the Reports. Click **Yes** to proceed.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24769862467095)

5\. A dialog will appear confirming that you want to open the exported file's location. Click **Yes** to proceed.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24769929523735)

6\. You can view the CSV file with a CSV editor of your choice, such as Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, or Rons Editor.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24769883583127)

# How to Cancel your AYCD Subscription – AYCD

How to Cancel your AYCD Subscription

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038113374-How-to-Cancel-your-AYCD-Subscription/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==========================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

November 08, 2019 04:55

Your AYCD account gives you access to all of the products within our Toolbox. With a subscription to our Toolbox you get access to:

*   OneClick - Farming to increase the trust of your Google Accounts.
*   Spoof - Generate unique browsers, giving you the upper hand on popular releases!
*   Profile Converter - A Profile manager that serves as a hub for all of your billings allowing export into over 100+ different bot formats. Profile Converter is also perfect for slot management.
*   Resell Manager - Easily manage and track sales as well as bot keys and logins with our secure vault.
*   Generator - Automatically generate Data Center servers and proxies with providers like AWS and GCP. 

To learn more about what the Toolbox offers please [click here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038114094)
.

When you cancel and unsubscribe from your subscription you will lose access to all of the services within the toolbox and the end of your term. Once you cancel the only way to reactivate your subscription is to purchase a new one or contact our support team.

1.  Login to your AYCD account on [aycd.io/account](https://aycd.io/account#subscriptions)
     with your Email and Password.
2.  Once logged into your account go to the Billing option at the bottom of the Account menu.
3.  Find the subscription that you wish to cancel and use the action button beside the corresponding subscription in order to cancel it.
4.  Once you hit cancel and confirm that you wish to cancel the subscription you will be done. The subscription will cancel at the end of your term.

![Account_Billing.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500002050242/Account_Billing.png)

![mceclip0.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500002109161/mceclip0.png)

# OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Add / Import / Export Accounts – AYCD

OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Add / Import / Export Accounts

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16222455171479-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Add-Import-Export-Accounts/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

July 22, 2023 23:41

Introduction
------------

You can manage your accounts with OneClick. You can farm activity on your accounts, utilize our many valuable Tools, and use them to solve various captcha types through AutoSolve. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You should use one unique ISP/DC proxy per Account. Learn more about adding and assigning proxies to Accounts [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039319653)
    .
*   If your Account has a security answer, recovery email, and/or a 2FA secret key enabled, you should provide those details when adding your accounts. OneClick will use these details to fully automate the login process.

Browser Types
-------------

**Camo Chrome**

*   If your objective is to keep your accounts alive and in good standing with Google, you should use Camo Chrome.
*   Camo Chrome is essential for using the [Traffic & SEO](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/21313462266647)
     modules.
*   Camo Chrome is our in-house anti-detect browser. It gives your tasks a unique fingerprint, even from the same device, reducing the risk of account bans and detections.
*   Camo Chrome can solve cf / reCaptcha challenges with AutoSolve and farming accounts/solvers.
*   Camo Chrome does not support the majority of account tools in OneClick.
*   Camo Chrome supports visibly seeing what your tasks are doing by clicking the eye on the task to show the browser.

**![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25737450934423)**

**AYCD Browser**

*   AYCD Browser can solve most captcha challenges with AutoSolve and farming accounts/solvers. 
*   AYCD Browser is very lightweight compared to the other browsers. You can run, on average, 3-4 AYCD Browsers for every 1 Chrome/Camo Chrome browser.
*   AYCD Browser supports all account tools, such as forwarding, enabling 2FA, changing passwords/recovery emails, and more.
*   AYCD Browser supports visibly seeing what your tasks are doing by clicking the eye on the task to show the browser.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25737450933143)

**Chrome**

*   Chrome can solve cf / reCaptcha challenges with AutoSolve and farming accounts/solvers.
*   Chrome is identical to using Chrome manually on your computer. We do not alter the fingerprint for each Chrome task. 
*   Chrome does not support the majority of account tools in OneClick.
*   Chrome supports visibly seeing what your tasks are doing by clicking the eye on the task to show the browser.

**![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25737450933655)**

Add Account
-----------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts/Solvers section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a play button titled **Account/Solvers** when you hover over it.

2\. Click on **File**, then click the **Add Account** button. This will open the **New Account** dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/21367618266007)

3\. By default, Accounts are created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Accounts created into a unique category.

4\. If you want to add any notes to be stored with the account, you can include them here.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/21367604145687)

5\. Input your Account's Email address into the **Email** field.

6\. Input your Account's Password into the **Password** field.

7\. If your Account has a security answer assigned to it, input the answer to the **Security Answer** field.

8\. If your Account has a recovery email assigned to it, input the email to the **Recovery Email** field.

9\. If your account has a 2FA Secret Key assigned to it, input the key to the **2FA Secret** field.

10\. For the majority of use cases, you should leave the **Login Endpoint** dropdown set to **Default**. If your account does not have Gmail enabled, you should use the google.com login method. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25737445131415)

11\. Specify the Browser Type to use in the **Browser Type** dropdown. You can learn more about browser types at the beginning of this guide. You can choose **AYCD Browser, Camo Chrome,** or **Chrome Browser**. You can see the supported captchas and modes in the **Supported Features** section.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/21367618292247)

12\. You can configure which browser storage type to use with the **Browser Data Storage Type** dropdown.

*   **Lightweight** uses the least data and requires much less storage space. We have not noticed any downsides to using the lightweight option.
*   **Standard** mimics the browser data directory of a standard browser. It uses more data and requires more available storage.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092521432983)

13\. The History section contains your Account Action History, where important events that happen during farming or when using tools are logged to your accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/21367604165783)

14\. Optionally, you can configure a custom farming schedule with the **Custom Farming Schedule** section. 

15\. Click the **Farming Scripts Override** dropdown to enable/disable specific farming scripts.

16\. Click the **reCaptcha AI Solve Speed Override** dropdown to edit the solving speed for the account/solver. We recommend leaving this value on Normal for Accounts. High solve speeds can cause accounts to be banned.

17\. You can enable the **Activate Data Saver While Farming** checkbox to disable images from loading to help save proxy data on metered plans.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092505178391)

18\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Create** button.

19\. Your newly created Account will appear in the table view. You can farm your Accounts with OneClick. You can learn more about farming activity on your accounts [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173)
. You can use your Accounts with AutoSolve. You can learn more about how to use AutoSolve [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
.

Import Accounts
---------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts/Solvers section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a play button titled **Account/Solvers** when you hover over it.

2\. Click on **File**, then click the **Import** button. This will open the **Import Accounts** dialog. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16222924192407)

3\. When you are ready to import your Accounts, click the blue page icon to the right of the text area and select the file in windows explorer/mac finder.

4\. You can click the Copy/Paste button to input your Accounts via OneClick's copy-paste format. The format is email:::password:::securityanswer:::proxy

5\. When you have finished configuring the dialog, click the **Import** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16222881116055)

6\. Your newly imported Account(s) will appear in the table view. You can farm your Accounts with OneClick. You can learn more about farming activity [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173)
. You can use your Accounts with AutoSolve. You can learn more about how to use AutoSolve [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
.

Export Accounts
---------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts/Solvers section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a play button titled **Account/Solvers** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Accounts you want to export, then click the **File** button, then click the **Export** button. This will open the **Export Accounts** dialog. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16222884496919)

3\. The Accounts Export dialog will display which Accounts/Solvers you are exporting. Once you have confirmed the dialog looks correct, press the **Export** button.

4\. Your accounts will be exported via CSV format. Windows Explorer/Mac Finder will automatically open the folder where your Accounts/Solver CSV is located.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16222932910103)

*   [OneClick - Account Ratings](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5751258079895-OneClick-Account-Ratings)

*   [OneClick - Testing ReCaptcha](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038828114-OneClick-Testing-ReCaptcha)

# OneClick - Accounts - How Do I Get One-Clicks? – AYCD

OneClick - Accounts - How Do I Get One-Clicks?

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14342654550039-OneClick-Accounts-How-Do-I-Get-One-Clicks/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



===========================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 03, 2023 21:24

Introduction
------------

You can use OneClick to farm activity on your Accounts/Solvers, which contributes to them getting one-click reCaptcha solves.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You should farm your Account/Solver(s) with default settings in OneClick.
*   You should use one unique ISP proxy per Account/Solver.
*   You should use the Sleep Accounts at Night setting. This should be set to the region that matches your device's time zone.
*   For reCaptcha, you should test for one-clicks every three to five days. You should solve the captcha challenge if you do not get a one-click solve. Successfully solving a reCaptcha v2 challenge helps prove you are not a robot. If you have [AutoSolve AI](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/captchapass?referralCode=ZENDESK)
    , you can turn on **Automatically test reCaptcha while farming** checkbox in **Settings - AutoSolve | AI**.
*   You should [add 2FA](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360048175033)
     to your Accounts. This contributes to a high trust score.

How long does it take to get one-clicks?
----------------------------------------

1\. The time it takes to achieve OneClick status can vary based on a number of factors, such as:

*   The age of your Gmail account.
*   The trust of your account. You can increase trust by farming Gmails with OneClick.
*   The IP address (proxy) you are farming activity and solving captchas on.

2\. Don’t be concerned if you aren’t getting one-clicks immediately. Continue to farm for up to three weeks. If, after three weeks, you still cannot achieve one-clicks, consider switching a % of your Accounts/Solvers to a different proxy. 

3\. To test if your proxies work well for reCaptcha, you can test your proxies with the reCaptcha v3 Score test. You can read [this guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039319653)
 to learn more about adding proxies to the OneClick Proxy Tester.

4\. Navigate to the Proxies section in OneClick.

5\. Click the **Options** button and then click **Test Proxies**.

6\. Select the **ReCaptcha v3 Score** test in the dropdown menu. 

7\. In the Page Load (ms) column, you will be given a score of 0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, or 0.9. 

![TestingProxiesGif.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14487311794199)

Maintaining one-clicks
----------------------

1\. Once you achieve one-clicks on your Accounts/Solvers, you should continue to farm activity and follow the Setup & Recommendations above.

2\. Continuing to farm after achieving one-clicks can result in more consecutive reCaptcha solves during hyped releases.

Silent Account Flags
--------------------

1\. Through testing, we have found that a lot of older accounts that have been having trouble getting one-clicks are likely to have been silently flagged by Google. This issue is most common with older accounts because they almost always do not have a very active history, could be stolen, or are already silently flagged. 

2\. A few criteria must be met to know if an Account is silently flagged.

*   You cannot achieve one-clicks.
*   You follow proper farming procedures, such as the procedures detailed in this guide.
*   You have a reCaptcha v3 score of 0.7 or 0.9.
*   You have been farming for over a month.
*   You consistently test for reCaptchas and solve a challenge when needed.
*   You tried switching to a new farming proxy.
*   The account is older than six months.

3\. The account is likely silently flagged if all the above criteria are met. 

_While these older accounts may be silently flagged, it does not mean that all older accounts cannot get one-clicks or are just not trusted at all. There are still numerous users that can get one-clicks on their older accounts. It is just far more likely that older accounts may have something wrong with them when compared to newer accounts._

### Articles in this section

*   [OneClick - Getting Started Guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173-OneClick-Getting-Started-Guide)

# Table Selections – AYCD

Table Selections

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/30236359926167-Table-Selections/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

February 25, 2025 21:13

Introduction
------------

There are a few different ways to select items in a table across our programs. From manually to using shortcuts. Shortcuts are very helpful for selecting multiple accounts when running tools or performing other actions in bulk.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You must have data available in a table in order to select or edit it. 

Manually
--------

You can left-click an item in a table to select and deselect one item at a time.

![2025-02-25_15-54-25 (1).gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30236390440343)

You can use the checkboxes in the table to select multiple items at a time.

![2025-02-25_15-56-48 (2).gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30236390443159)

You can use the checkbox in the header at the top of the table to select all of the items in the table.

![2025-02-25_15-58-41 (1).gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30236359916951)

Shortcuts (Windows)
-------------------

You can use CTRL + Left Click to select items one at a time, adding them to the selection or clicking an item in the selection to deselect it.

![2025-02-25_16-03-35 (1).gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30236359917847)

You can use Shift + Left Click to select all of the items between your first and second clicks. You can click further down to add new items to the selection.

![2025-02-25_16-08-35 (1).gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30236390445847)

You can use CTRL + A to select all of the items in the table.

![2025-02-25_16-11-39 (1).gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30236359919767)

Shortcuts (MacOS)
-----------------

You can use Command + Left Click to select items one at a time, adding them to the selection or clicking an item in the selection to deselect it.

![2025-02-25_16-03-35 (1).gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30236359917847)

You can use Shift + Left Click to select all of the items between your first and second clicks. You can click further down to add new items to the selection.

![2025-02-25_16-08-35 (1).gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30236390445847)

You can use Command + A to select all of the items in the table.

![2025-02-25_16-11-39 (1).gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30236359919767)

# What is AYCD Resell Pass? – AYCD

What is AYCD Resell Pass?

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038114094-What-is-AYCD-Resell-Pass/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



===================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

November 08, 2019 05:22

Resell Pass (Formally AYCD Toolbox) is one of our base subscriptions giving you access to the industry's most powerful set of tools. Our tools ensure everything from setting up for drops to managing your mail is fast and easy. Resell Pass includes OneClick, Profile Builder, Inbox, Location Spoof, Server Generator, and Crypton.

OneClick

*   Generate one-clicks and maintain quality Google accounts.
*   Automate and manage your mail forwarding settings.
*   Randomized Google account activity keeping your accounts full with humanlike interactions.
*   Manage Account Security and Recovery for hundreds of accounts utilizing built-in tools.
*   Run Unlimited Accounts allowing OneClick to schedule and manage your accounts.

Profile Builder

*   Create, Import, and Export profiles to over 100 of your favorite applications.
*   Manage all of your Virtual Credit Cards (VCCs) utilizing tools to generate and delete VCCs.
*   Mass Edit and Jig your profiles using expressions and AI to create 1000s of unique profiles.
*   Generate, Update, and Reset your accounts for over 50 supported websites.
*   Tuner is the home of your Nike SNKRS accounts, managing them utilizing built-in tools.

Inbox

*   View Emails from any provider supporting IMAP and utilize tools to unsubscribe or delete emails.
*   Use Scraper Templates to pull data from your emails and easily aggregate it.
*   Actively monitor your accounts for incoming emails and automatically scrape new data.
*   Send Scraped Data to Inventory to use our shipment tracking tools and access profit/loss.
*   Create tasks to request Phone Numbers and SMS OTP codes from various providers.

Server Generator

*   Create servers with the ability to make and reuse images makes scaling up your setups a breeze.
*   Import and destroy existing instances from your accounts, ensuring you always know what servers are running.
*   Generate proxies from various cloud-based providers in seconds.
*   Generate Akami cookies for use across various sites with ease.
*   Scrape Shopify variants quality with the ability to specify an export format.

Crypton

*   Generate wallets on ETH and SOL.
*   Quickly send and disperse Crypto or NFTs to one or many wallets.
*   View multiple wallet balances from one page.
*   Monitor your wallet's activity (Crypto and NFT transactions) via Discord notifications.
*   View your NFT collection across all your wallets. Easily access helpful links and view rarity information in one place.

Location Spoof

*   Spoof your location for multiple iOS devices.
*   Create location markers to ensure you can quickly switch your device's location.
