# Generator - Server/Proxy Provider Guide – AYCD

Generator - Server/Proxy Provider Guide

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

December 02, 2019 22:22

The purpose of this guide is to walk you through some of the setup required on your end with your [Amazon Web Services](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_659f3ce8-3aa4-40be-ba47-a44980901079)
 (AWS) account, your [Google Cloud Platform](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_3b6b135c-02c4-491c-ba53-baec9a58548f)
 (GCP) account, your [Microsoft Azure](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_01EXN14HE6KW4FQ3DCBD3F8DGN)
 Account, your [Digital Ocean account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_baea3bd1-ad02-4c56-9f45-f28fd7eaa42d)
, your [Linode account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_cdaf7c7b-f6fc-4220-a569-b0c733db178f)
 or your [Upcloud Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_04ecf5cf-31e0-4e0c-8273-3bffb5f77eac)
 as well as some information on how to use Generator. This should be the first thing you read before using the application as there is some setup required on your part to get things started. Below I will get into a bit of the prep and explain what is required in order to ensure each account is set up and ready to go. 

 [AWS](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_659f3ce8-3aa4-40be-ba47-a44980901079)
 | [GCP](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_3b6b135c-02c4-491c-ba53-baec9a58548f)
 | [Azure](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_01EXN14HE6KW4FQ3DCBD3F8DGN)
 | [Digital Ocean](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_baea3bd1-ad02-4c56-9f45-f28fd7eaa42d)
 | [Linode](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_cdaf7c7b-f6fc-4220-a569-b0c733db178f)
 | [Upcloud](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_04ecf5cf-31e0-4e0c-8273-3bffb5f77eac)

#### **Amazon Web Services (AWS)**

If you do not already have an account with AWS you are going to need to [create one](https://portal.aws.amazon.com/billing/signup#/start)
. AWS is great because they give you 750hrs per month of free t2 micro server usage. After signing up for an account and following the steps to authenticate your account you will need to [sign in](https://console.aws.amazon.com/)
. Once you are signed in you will be greeted with their main console screen. Before we can go into the Generator there are a couple of things that we need. First, we need to [visit here](http://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/v2/home)
 then head over to the Security Credentials section which is found under your AWS **Profile name**. 

![mceclip0.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360053325373/mceclip0.png)

Once you get into your **Security Credentials** screen you will then have to head on down and click on the **Access Keys (access key ID and secret access key)** tab which will open up to the screen pictured below. Once you have this tab open you will need to press the **Create New Access Key**

  
![mceclip1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360053325393/mceclip1.png)

Once you have pressed create new access key a prompt will be created where you will be able to see your **secret key, access key ID and download a copy of it all**. Ensure you keep the downloaded file in a safe place because if it is lost you will not be able to retrieve your secret access key again.

![mceclip2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360053325413/mceclip2.png)

Once you have them created you will be able to go over to Generator and under the **Credentials** view you will click on **Create**, from the dialog select AWS**.** This will create a prompt where you will be able to set a nickname for these credentials, you will then be asked to input your **Access Key** and **Secret Access Key** and then you can press save.

![2020-12-12_22_15_22-Generator_x_Crewie_v2.0.0_EAP_Build_14.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000375841/2020-12-12_22_15_22-Generator_x_Crewie_v2.0.0_EAP_Build_14.png)

With all that information saved you will then be able to start using Generator to create servers and proxies for you. 

#### **Google Cloud Platform (GCP)**

Google Cloud Platform tends to be a little easier to deal with when it comes to setting up the application. First off you will need to head over to [Google Clouds Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/)
 once you have created your account or simply signed in please take the time to ensure you have the correct project selected. If you do not have a project please create one now.

[Creating a Service Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_01F2VS816S2MCJTC2Q9R61SG4F)
 | [Creating a Private Key](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553-Generator-Server-Proxy-Provider-Guide#h_01F2VSAG39N5FSNXEF9F25PPVA)

#### Creating a Service Account

If this is a completely new GCP account, you will need to perform some additional steps. You will first need to head over to the side menu on the left-hand side. After opening the side menu, you will need to go down to **Compute Engine** and then click on **VM instances**. After clicking on that you will need to select your project from the drop-down menu and then you will need to create and attach a billing profile in order to enable it. After you have done that you will be ready to complete the next steps.

![mceclip5.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360053325453/mceclip5.png)

With the correct project selected you can click this link to head over to the page required to [Create a Service Account](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials/serviceaccountkey)
 ![mceclip4.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360052435014/mceclip4.png)

When you get to this screen, you will need to create a **Service Account.**  If you do not already have one, you will need to input the Service Account name, description and give the account 'Owner' permissions. 

![Create_Service_account.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500008649481/Create_Service_account.png)

Once you have inputted the Service account name and description click Create.  
![Create_Service_account_2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500008649561/Create_Service_account_2.png)

You will then need to select a role, click Basic and then select Owner.  
![Create_Service_account_3.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500008436722/Create_Service_account_3.png)

Click Continue after giving the Service account the role. 

![Create_Service_account_4.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500008436762/Create_Service_account_4.png)

Click Done to create the service account.

![Create_Service_account_5.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500008436842/Create_Service_account_5.png)

#### Creating a Private Key

Once you have set up a service account you can now create the private key. In the Service accounts section click the respective email of the service account.

![Create_Service_account_6.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500008649901/Create_Service_account_6.png)

Within the service account section click the header KEYS, and in the Key section, click ADD KEY. 

![Create_Service_account_7.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500008650501/Create_Service_account_7.png)

In the dialog ensure the file type is JSON and then click CREATE in the bottom right. As soon as you click CREATE the .json file will download. Store it in a secure place as it will need to be on the same machine as Generator.

![Create_Service_account_8.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500008437602/Create_Service_account_8.png)

With this file downloaded you can head over to Generator where you will need to go to the Credentials view, click Create, then from the dialog select GCP, and click Next. After pressing next a prompt will appear asking you to set an account name and the file path to the JSON file that Google had you download when you pressed Create. After that press save.  
![Google_GCP.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360099243254/Google_GCP.png)

With the credentials saved you will be now able to create servers and proxies using GCP and the provided service account. 

#### **Azure**

Microsoft Azure, makes use of Microsoft Accounts in order to use their service. If you do not already have a Microsoft Account you will need to create one. Additionally, If you have not already set up a Microsoft Azure account once you sign in you will have to create your Azure Account. **_Azure heavily limits Trial Accounts, so to generate larger amounts of servers you will have to use a Pay As You Go account._**

![CreateAccountAzure.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360103287253/CreateAccountAzure.png)

Once you create your account and verify it using your Phone Number and Credit Card your account will be set up and you can access the [Portal Home](https://portal.azure.com/#home)
. Once you have reached the Portal Home you can use the Search Bar at the top to search for App registrations.

![AppRegSearch.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360103287273/AppRegSearch.png)

Once in App registrations click on New Registration near the top.

![NewRegistrationButton.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360103287293/NewRegistrationButton.png)

After clicking on that, you will reach the Register an Application screen where you will need to pick a name that is easy for you to identify. This can be anything and can always be changed later. In addition under **Supported Account Types** make sure you select the third option **Accounts in any organizational directory (Any Azure AD directory - Multitenant) and personal Microsoft accounts (e.g. Skype, Xbox)** and then click on Register at the bottom.

![RegistrationDetails.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360103287373/RegistrationDetails.png)

After you click on register it will take you to your Registered App. Right away under essentials, you will find your **Application (Client) ID**, and your **Directory (tenant) ID**. You can copy these into Generator under Credentials now. You will need to set a Name that is easy to Identify inside of Generator and then copy and Paste your Application ID and Directory ID into the specified places in Generator.

![AppAndDirectoryID.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360103287653/AppAndDirectoryID.png)

![CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.32.18.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/8853535373207/CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.32.18.png)

Lastly, in order to create Azure Proxies, you need to create a Client Secret to do that, from the same screen click on Certificates and Secrets on the left-hand side.

![CertAndSecrets.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360103287693/CertAndSecrets.png)

From there click on New Client Secret, and in the dialog that appears leave the description empty and switch the expiry to the amount of time you want the Client secret to be active for, then click on Add.

![CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.35.02.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/8853648106903/CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.35.02.png)

After you have created your Secret Key, copy the value field and add it to Generator. **It is important to do this now as once you leave this page you will no longer be able to copy this key again and you will have to create a new one.**

![CopyValueToClipboard.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500002292441/CopyValueToClipboard.png)

![CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.39.23.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/8853711842455/CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.39.23.png)

Now before you can generate proxies you need to give yourself permissions to do so. In order to do that Click on Home in the top left and then Subscriptions. 

![HomeSubScriptions.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360103287733/HomeSubScriptions.png)

From here Click on your subscription name.

![ClickSubscriptionName.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500002232662/ClickSubscriptionName.png)

Then click on Access Control (IAM) > Add > and then Add Role Assignment. Then in the following page click on the Owner role and then click next. 

![CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.41.05.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/8853807045015/CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.41.05.png)  
  
![CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.43.28.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/8853825647639/CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.43.28.png)

In the next page click Select members, and search for the application you created earlier, it will be using the same name, click Select and then Next or Review + assign.  
![CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.47.53.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/8853856715671/CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.47.53.png)  
You will be redirected to the Access control (IAM) page, the notifications tab will inform you whether the Role assignment was successful or not.   
![CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.50.24.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/8853949545623/CleanShot_2022-09-13_at_22.50.24.png)

After you have that setup you can begin to generate proxies using Microsoft Azure!

#### **Digital Ocean**

Digital Ocean is great and easy to set up as well. First off if you don’t already have an account you’ll need to head over to [Digital Oceans Website](https://www.digitalocean.com/)
 and create one. If you already have one simply login and use this link to go to the [API section](https://cloud.digitalocean.com/account/api/tokens)
 or navigate to it using the menu on the left.

![mceclip8.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360053325513/mceclip8.png)

Once here, press on the button Generate Token.

 ![mceclip9.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360053325533/mceclip9.png)

Here you will simply need to enter a name for your token, ensure that you have checked off the **Write** permission once you have entered a name press Generate Token.

![DO1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360101471293/DO1.png)  
Within the Generator navigate to the Credentials view, click Create, then from the dialog select Digital Ocean, and click Next. After pressing next a prompt will appear asking you to input the credential's name and your personal access token. After that press save.

![DO2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000375881/DO2.png)

Once you have clicked save you will be able to use Digital Ocean to create proxies.  

#### **Linode**

Linode can be a bit trickier to find where to create your API token for. First off, as always if you do not already have an account you’ll need to head over to [Linodes website](https://login.linode.com/signup)
 and create one. You’ll need to verify the email on the account as well as link a billing right away before you can use the account. If you already have an account you’ll need to go ahead and login. Once logged in you can either [Click Here](https://cloud.linode.com/profile/tokens)
 to take you to the API Token section under my profile or navigate there manually by clicking on your profile name. 

![2021-08-08_21_18_49-Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/4405901592343/2021-08-08_21_18_49-Window.png)

Once in My Profile click on API tokens, then click on Add a Personal Access Token, and then you will be able to create a token.

![mceclip12.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360053325573/mceclip12.png)

Here you will need to create your token name, and select the **Select All** under read/write access. After that click submit and you will be met with a screen showing you your Personal Access Token.   
![mceclip13.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360053325593/mceclip13.png)

![mceclip14.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360053325613/mceclip14.png)

You’ll need to save this token so that you can add it to Generator. Ensure you copy and paste it either to a file, or directly into Generator. Within the Generator navigate to the Credentials view, click Create, then from the dialog select Linode, and click Next. After pressing next a prompt will appear asking you to set the credential's name and your personal access token. After that press save.

![Linode.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000371802/Linode.png)

#### **UpC****loud**

UpCloud is very easy and simple to setup. Please note, in order to use Upcloud in the Generator, Upcloud requires you to deposit at least $10 into your account. 

First, create an account on [**UpCloud**](https://upcloud.com/signup/?promo=Y2YES4)
. The credentials you used to create an account will be used in the credentials section in Generator. Once you have created your account and deposited $10 navigate to your account [overview](https://hub.upcloud.com/account/overview)
. Under the Permissions tile on your account allow API Key connections from All addresses and then click Save.  
![2020-12-12_21_55_06-UpCloud___Account.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000371642/2020-12-12_21_55_06-UpCloud___Account.png)

After that, open Generator. Navigate to the Credentials view, click Create, then from the dialog select UpCloud, and click Next. After pressing next a prompt will appear asking you to input the credential's name, the account username, and the account password. After that press save.  
![UP.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000371902/UP.png)

Once you have added the credentials you will be able to create proxies using UpCloud!

#  What Is An Addon Session / How Do I Run AYCD On Two Computers At The Same Time? – AYCD

What Is An Addon Session / How Do I Run AYCD On Two Computers At The Same Time?

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038644273--What-Is-An-Addon-Session-How-Do-I-Run-AYCD-On-Two-Computers-At-The-Same-Time/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

November 08, 2019 06:28

The AYCD Toolbox is the most powerful set of tools in the industry. Our tools ensure that setup is fast and easy and proves itself invaluable to you when it comes to drops.

An addon session is a subscription from AYCD currently priced at $15/month for Toolbox subscribers or $12/month for AYCD Pro subscribers and requires you to have an active subscription for either Toolbox or Pro. The addon session allows you to log into AYCD Toolbox on two separate devices without being logged out of one. This is extremely useful for those that wish to split their running accounts between multiple devices or farm their accounts on a server while using Profile Converter or Generator locally.

For members of Pro or with an AutoSolve subscription, you will also receive 4 additional AutoSolve Bot API Keys and 4 additional AutoSolve Companion Keys.

To purchase an addon session you must:

1.  [Login](https://aycd.io/account)
     to your account (You must have an active subscription for Toolbox or Pro).
2.  Click on the [Addons](https://aycd.io/account#addons)
     tab from the menu bar.
3.  Complete the checkout process. 

Once you have checked out you will be able to login to the Toolbox from two different places with the same account without one computer being logged out. 

![Addon_Session.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500020021541/Addon_Session.png)

# OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Add Solver – AYCD

OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Add Solver

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16217942144279-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Add-Solver/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

July 22, 2023 17:02

Introduction
------------

You can create Solvers to use to solve various captcha types. Solvers are unique browser sessions **that do not require** a Gmail account to be logged in to them. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can use Solvers for reCaptcha, Cloudflare Turnstile, GeeTest, DataDome Slider, and other captcha types!
*   After farming Solvers for a few weeks, they will work great for reCaptcha challenges.
*   You should use one unique ISSolverroxy per Solver. You can learn more about adding and assigning proxies to Solvers [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039319653)
    .

Browser Types
-------------

**AYCD Browser**

*   AYCD Browser can solve most captcha challenges with AutoSolve and farming accounts/solvers. 
*   AYCD Browser is very lightweight compared to the other browsers. You can run, on average, 3-4 AYCD Browsers for every 1 Chrome/Camo Chrome browser.
*   AYCD Browser supports all account tools, such as forwarding, enabling 2FA, changing passwords/recovery emails, and more.
*   AYCD Browser supports visibly seeing what your tasks are doing by clicking the eye on the task to show the browser.

![aycd-browsers.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27924900247319)

**Chrome**

*   Chrome can solve cf / reCaptcha challenges with AutoSolve and farming accounts/solvers.
*   Chrome is identical to using Chrome manually on your computer. We do not alter the fingerprint for each Chrome task. 
*   Chrome does not support the majority of account tools in OneClick.
*   Chrome supports visibly seeing what your tasks are doing by clicking the eye on the task to show the browser.

![chrome-browser.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27924909665815)

**Camo Chrome**

*   Camo Chrome is essential for using the [Traffic & SEO](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/21313462266647)
     modules.
*   Camo Chrome is our in-house anti-detect browser. It gives your tasks a unique fingerprint, even from the same device, reducing the risk of account bans and detections.
*   Camo Chrome can solve cf / reCaptcha challenges with AutoSolve and farming accounts/solvers.
*   Camo Chrome does not support the majority of account tools in OneClick.
*   Camo Chrome supports visibly seeing what your tasks are doing by clicking the eye on the task to show the browser.

![camo-chrome.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27924909666327)

**Chrome / Camo Chrome Headless**

*   Headless browsers are more lightweight compared to their non-headless partners. You can run 2-3 headless browsers for every one non-headless browser.
*   Headless browsers can solve cf / reCaptcha challenges with AutoSolve and farming solvers.
*   Headless browsers do not support visibly seeing what your tasks are doing.

![headless.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27924900253975)

Add Solver
----------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts/Solvers section in OneClick under Tasks. When you hover over it, the section icon looks like a play button titled **Account/Solver**.

2\. Click on **File**, then click the **Add Solver** button. The Add Solvers Dialog will appear.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16218467862423)

3\. By default, Solvers are created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Solvers created into a unique category.

4\. Specify a **Name** for your Solvers.

5\. Specify the **Quantity** of Solvers you want to create.

6\. Specify the **Proxy Category** you want to use with your Solvers.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/21367597084823)

7\. Specify which **Browser Type** to use for your Solvers. Currently, AYCD and Chrome Browsers are supported. Each Solver Type is optimized for different captcha versions. You can see which captcha versions are supported in the **Supported Features** section and in the first section of this guide.

![aycd-browsers.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27924900247319)

8\. You can configure which browser storage type to use with the **Browser Data Storage Type** dropdown.

*   **Lightweight** uses the least data and requires much less storage space. We have not noticed any downsides to using the lightweight option.
*   **Standard** mimics the browser data directory of a standard browser. It uses more data and requires more available storage.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092574812055)

9\. Optionally, you can configure a custom farming schedule with the **Custom Farming Schedule** section.

10\. Click the **Farming Scripts Override** dropdown to enable/disable specific farming scripts.

11\. Click the **reCaptcha AI Solve Speed Override** dropdown if you want to edit the solving speed for the solver. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/22945469181847)

12\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Create** button.

13\. Your newly created Solvers will appear in the table view. You can use your Solvers with AutoSolve. You can learn more about how to use AutoSolve [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16219505613207)

# Toolbox Installer Error 1625 – AYCD

Toolbox Installer Error 1625

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039254514-Toolbox-Installer-Error-1625/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==========================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

December 01, 2019 21:53

Error #1625 is a Windows error that comes up during installation if a security policy on the computer has been enabled. You can adjust the setting to allow you to install but you will have to login as an Administrator to make the changes. Note, **you must restart your computer/server BEFORE following the steps below.** 

Please ensure before using the below steps that you right-click on the application and run as administrator first.  
![mceclip0.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360053725234/mceclip0.png)

To check:

*   Click on **Start**, type **Control Panel** and press **Enter**
*   Click on **User Accounts**. Under your name, it would indicate if you are an Administrator. If you are, proceed with the steps below, otherwise, you may have to let another user with Administrator rights to give you access to proceed.

Here’s how to fix the error:
----------------------------

1.  1.  Click on **Start** and type **gpedit.msc** then press **Enter**
    2.  Select **Administrative Templates** under **Computer Configuration**
        
        [![](https://www.sync.com/help/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/1603AdminTemplate.png)](https://www.sync.com/help/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/1603AdminTemplate.png)
        
    3.  Double-click on **Windows Components**
        
        [![](https://www.sync.com/help/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/1603WinComponents.png)](https://www.sync.com/help/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/1603WinComponents.png)
        
    4.  Double-click on **Windows Installer**
        
        [![](https://www.sync.com/help/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/1603WinInstaller.png)](https://www.sync.com/help/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/1603WinInstaller.png)
        
    5.  Go to **Turn off Windows Installer** and double-click on it to change the current state. Enabled means installation is restricted, resulting in Error #1625.[![](https://www.sync.com/help/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/1625WinInstaller-1.png)](https://www.sync.com/help/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/1625WinInstaller-1.png)
        
    6.  Select **Enabled** and click **Apply,** then set **Disable Windows Installer** to **NEVER** then click **OK** to save the changes.
        
        ![unknown__6_.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360052868274/unknown__6_.png)
        
        Click **OK** on the error prompt and retry installing.

# How to use Location Spoof (iOS Only) – AYCD

How to use Location Spoof (iOS Only)

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041659653-How-to-use-Location-Spoof-iOS-Only/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



========================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

January 09, 2020 12:27

Introduction
------------

You can use Location Spoof to change your iOS device's location to anywhere in the world. This can allow you to access geo-located events and releases, waitlists, games, location-based apps and services, and more.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To use Location Spoof, you need to have an iOS device with iOS 9 or newer. It is best not to use beta builds of iOS, as they are likely to have issues working.
*   If you are using a Windows Computer, you need to have iTunes installed. If you do not have iTunes installed, you can do so using the link here.
*   When you launch Location Spoof on Windows, it will request administrator rights to launch a console window to connect to your iOS devices and install the necessary drivers. Please leave this console window open while using Location Spoof.
*   Do not connect your iOS device to your computer until the console window has finished installing the necessary drivers. Otherwise, Location Spoof may not work.
*   The computer you are using to spoof your location must be a local device in front of you. It cannot be a server or a computer that you connect to remotely since you will need to plug your iOS device directly into the computer running Spoof.

Location Spoof on Windows
-------------------------

1.  First, ensure that your iOS device is not connected to your computer. Having it connected when first opening Location Spoof can cause It to not work properly. 

2\. Launch Location Spoof. When you launch it for the first time there will be a few dialogs that appear. If you do not have Python 3 installed from the Microsoft Store, a browser tab will automatically open, prompting you to install it.

![Download_Python3.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167495698583)

3\. Once you have finished installing it, click Yes on the Python 3 Install dialog, which should close it.

![Phython_3_Install.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167495706391)

4\. The next dialog notifies you that the Tunnel Service is starting up. You can close this dialog but do not plug in your iOS device until you see the console window has opened and finished starting. During this process, a window may appear requesting administrator permissions, make sure you click Allow access.

![Tunnel_Service.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167516106647)

5\. Once the Console window has appeared and finished starting, you can minimize it to your taskbar. 

![Ready_Console_Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167516116759)

6\. You can now plug your iOS device into your computer. Ensure it is unlocked when you plug it in. You should see it appear on the left side of Location Spoof. If you are on iOS 16 or newer, then you may need to manually enable Developer Mode on your iOS device. If you do, it will show you an error next to your device in Location Spoof. 

![spoofing_errors.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167495737751)

7\. To enable Developer Mode go to Settings on the device. Scroll down to Privacy & Security, then go down to Developer Mode. Click on that and Enable Developer Mode. It will prompt you to restart your device.

![EnableDeveloperModeiOS.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/9468922719639)

8 After you restart, you will receive a confirmation prompt to confirm that you want to Turn On Developer Mode before being asked to enter your passcode.

9\. You will be ready to proceed once you see your device show up in Spoof without any errors.

![no_errors.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167516138007)

10\. Search Google Maps for the location you want to Spoof your iOS device to. You can search by the location name, street address, city, or even search using coordinates. 

![Searching_Google_Maps.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167516143127)

11\. Once you have a location set in the search bar, select a device on Location Spoof, then click the Spoof Device button. Location Spoof will begin working to Spoof your location.

![Spoof_Location_Working.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167516150295)

![Spoofed_Location_iOS.JPG](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167516174231)

12\. To stop your device from Spoofing, select it from the left-hand side and click the Stop Spoofing button. It may take a few minutes for your device location to reset, however you can restart your iOS device if you want to speed this up.

![Stop_Spoofing_iOS.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167562894231)

Location Spoof on macOS
-----------------------

_Not all of the steps shown may appear as below, but you will need to trust your computer on your iPhone, allowing Location Spoof to access your iPhone and vice versa._

1\. Launch Location Spoof. When you launch it, a dialog will ask for your macOS login password. This is the password you enter when unlocking your computer. This allows Location Spoof to utilize Developer Tools on your iOS device. Enter your computer password and click Proceed.

![macos_login_password.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167516190359)

2\. Plug your iOS device into your computer and unlock it. If you do not see it appear in Location Spoof, you may need to go to Finder and find your device on the left-hand side. 

3\. After clicking on the device, an image like the one below should show. You can click Trust to connect the computer to your iOS device.

![mceclip1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360091627974)

4\. After hitting Trust, you will need to fill in your iOS device password through a prompt asking you to enter your Device Passcode to Trust This Computer. Your device should then show in Location Spoof. If your iOS device shows up in Location Spoof with an error icon, you will need to enable Developer Mode on your iOS device. 

![spoofing_errors.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167495737751)

5\. To enable Developer Mode go to Settings on the device. Scroll down to Privacy & Security, then go down to Developer Mode. Click on that and Enable Developer Mode. It will prompt you to restart your device.

![EnableDeveloperModeiOS.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/9468922719639)

6\. After you restart, you will receive a confirmation prompt to confirm that you want to Turn On Developer Mode before being asked to enter your passcode.

7\. You will be ready to proceed once you see your device show up in Spoof without any errors.

![no_errors.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167516138007)

8\. Search Google Maps for the location you want to Spoof your iOS device to. You can search by the location name, street address, city, or even search using coordinates. 

![Searching_Google_Maps.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167516143127)

9\. Once you have a location set in the search bar, select a device on Location Spoof, then click the Spoof Device button. Location Spoof will begin working to Spoof your location.

![Spoof_Location_Working.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167516150295)

![Spoofed_Location_iOS.JPG](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167516174231)

10\. To stop your device from Spoofing, select it from the left-hand side and click the Stop Spoofing button. It may take a few minutes for your device location to reset, however you can restart your iOS device if you want to speed this up.

![Stop_Spoofing_iOS.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24167562894231)

Troubleshooting Location Spoof
------------------------------

*   (Windows) If you have Python 3.12 Installed and Location Spoof is still prompting you to install it, you may need to close out of the Toolbox and right-click to run it as an Administrator, then launch Location Spoof.
*   The most common error occurs when you have not yet trusted the computer you are connected to. The first thing you can do to resolve this is to open up iTunes or Finder and ensure that your device shows up there. This can help prompt the trust message to get your computer and device trusted.
*   If you are using iOS 16.1.1 on an iPhone 14 then you will need to enable Airplane Mode in order to successfully Spoof your iOS device. You will also want to re-enable Wifi and Bluetooth.  
    ![iOSSpoof14.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/10336541901207)
*   If you have tried all of the above steps, try to clear the trusted devices. You can do this while the iPhone is plugged into your computer by going to Settings on your iPhone, and there is a Developer Category. Inside of that, at the top, there is a Clear Trusted Computers. After you clear trusted computers, you will need to restart the device and connect your device to Location Spoof again.   
    ![mceclip3.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360096360073)![mceclip4.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360094091474)

### Articles in this section

*   [How to use Location Spoof (iOS Only)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041659653-How-to-use-Location-Spoof-iOS-Only)
    

Was this article helpful?

[](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041659653-How-to-use-Location-Spoof-iOS-Only# "Yes")
[](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041659653-How-to-use-Location-Spoof-iOS-Only# "No")

# AutoSolve - Recommendations and Best Practices – AYCD

AutoSolve - Recommendations and Best Practices

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847-AutoSolve-Recommendations-and-Best-Practices/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=============================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

February 19, 2022 21:20

### What is AutoSolve?

AutoSolve is a service that enabled the process of connecting Supported Bots with OneClick. While AutoSolve on its own does not enable the Automatic solving of captcha challenges since its inception we have integrated backup solving solutions such as AutoSolve AI, Speech to Text solving, or by using Third Party Providers such as 2Captcha, AntiCaptcha, and CapMonster.

* * *

Never used AutoSolve before? Read our [Getting Started Guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
.  
The best way to find out if your bot Supports AutoSolve is to look for their AutoSolve section or reach out to a member of their team to find out. _We have [a list of some supported bots](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043237394)
, but this list does not include every program that supports AutoSolve_

[AutoSolve Crash Course](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847-AutoSolve-Recommendations-and-Best-Practices#h_01G0N6HKHRAPZHWZ8Z1KW6WVTR)
 | [Challenge Types & Recommendations](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847-AutoSolve-Recommendations-and-Best-Practices#h_01G0N6MMJ4NXAZMBGF72QZ37E9)

### AutoSolve Crash Course

This section of the guide is designed as a mini crash course to walk you through the very basics of enabling and using AutoSolve. For a more in-depth look and understanding, it is recommended that you review the [AutoSolve Getting Started Guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
.

[Enabling AutoSolve](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847-AutoSolve-Recommendations-and-Best-Practices#h_01G0N6J9Z4SAWWHDN091HG9JC9)
 | [Connecting Bots](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847-AutoSolve-Recommendations-and-Best-Practices#h_01G0N6JGV68MDSEDYAYHP09HS4)
 | [AutoSolve Settings](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847-AutoSolve-Recommendations-and-Best-Practices#h_01G0N6JS4QNEY28S0ZDMNH3NKY)
 | [Using AutoSolve](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847-AutoSolve-Recommendations-and-Best-Practices#h_01G0N6JZ5Z2W3JE3CXF9W5WC1E)

#### Enabling AutoSolve

AutoSolve is accessed through OneClick. As a result please ensure that you have both a Toolbox and AutoSolve subscription before trying to enable AutoSolve. 

1.  Open OneClick from the AYCD Toolbox
2.  Click on the Settings tab at the top of OneClick.
3.  Click on the toggle next to Enable AutoSolve and click on Save.
4.  Clicking on Save will cause the program to restart and once it opens the AutoSolve tab will be available.

#### Connecting Bots

AutoSolve is used in order to send Captcha challenges from your bot tasks to OneClick giving you access to the different solving methods that we support. We have a list of supported bots along with some setup guides that you can find here, however, the process for all bots should be similar to the one described below.

1.  Go to the [AutoSolve Dashboard](https://aycd.io/account#autosolve)
     using the link provided, or by navigating there from the account dashboard.
2.  Create an AutoSolve Bot API Key with a unique name. We recommend using the name of the bot you are creating the API Key for. It is important that you use a unique Bot API Key for each instance of a bot you are using. Using the same API Key in multiple places will cause issues with receiving solves from OneClick.
3.  Find your bots AutoSolve configuration section. This is usually in Settings, but some bots may have these settings in a different place. Use the Copy Access Token button on the AutoSolve Dashboard and provide that in the Access Token section of your bot. Then Copy the Bot API Key you made and provide that to your bot as well and ensure the settings are saved.
4.  You can generally confirm that your bot is connected to AutoSolve by creating a test task with fake billing and running it on a store that receives a captcha. You can also review the Connections tab of AutoSolve to see if your Bots API Key is active.

#### AutoSolve Settings

Under the AutoSolve tab, there are a few different views that provide some additional information on AutoSolve as well as some settings that you can configure.

*   **Services:** The first tab is used to enable services for AutoSolve such as AI Solve, 2Captcha, or others. It will also allow you to copy your Access Token, or already created API Keys on the right. 
*   **Tasks:** The second tab is used to see your incoming Captcha Requests. This section will allow you to see things like the API Key used, which Service solved the Captcha, the Solve Time, and more.
*   **Connections:** The third tab allows you to see which API Keys are connected to AutoSolve. This section is great for confirming whether a bot you are using is successfully connected to AutoSolve.
*   **Errors:** This tab is used for diagnosis when there is an error.
*   **Events:** This tab is used for the diagnosis of any connection related issues.
*   **Solver Info:** This tab is used to display the Captcha Type each Service supports.
*   **Routes:** This tab is used to create Routes that allow you to direct requests to certain categories based on set conditions.
*   **Settings:** This tab is where you can configure some of the settings we have for AutoSolve. While AutoSolve is set up to run out of the box some more advanced users may way to adjust things like the maximum amount of solves for a local solver, or the limit of local solvers that can run at the same time.

#### Using AutoSolve

Since our recommendations for using AutoSolve and Best Practices on the different Captcha Types are listed below, this section will just contain a quick overview of using AutoSolve on ReCaptcha V2 Challenges using Local Solvers.

1.  Open OneClick and ensure you have your Accounts added to the Accounts View.
2.  For request types that recommend Empty Solvers you can create those in the Accounts view > File > Add Solver > Specify a Category, Solver Name, Quantity of Solvers, and a Proxy Category and click save.
3.  When you are ready to Start AutoSolve click on the Accounts you want to use by clicking and holding down Shift then going to Options > Start AutoSolve > Selected or All depending on if you just want to use the selected accounts or all the accounts in a category.
4.  After you make the selections in the following Dialog your accounts will go to Waiting to Solve and show AutoSolve Connected. At the bottom of OneClick, you should see the AutoSolve Status show Connected in green and you are now ready to start your bots tasks.

### Challenge Types & Recommendations

This section of the guide will review our recommended setup and configuration for the different types of Captchas we support. _Your setup may vary based on the services you have available and your computer's available CPU and RAM. Additionally, rate-limiting from the Captcha Services can always be a factor so it is best to set a Max Solvers Per Local Solver to limit the number of solves on a particular proxy._

[reCaptcha v2](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847-AutoSolve-Recommendations-and-Best-Practices#h_01G0N6R0K23BXB9MC7MB3T168F)
 | [reCaptcha v3](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847-AutoSolve-Recommendations-and-Best-Practices#h_01G0N6RA5KH5RENVQG9XPD95B2)
 | [GeeTest](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847-AutoSolve-Recommendations-and-Best-Practices#h_01G0N6RR0YMPQAECFYEM009SE7)
 

#### reCaptcha v2

There are two types of reCaptcha V2 challenges. Checkbox, which will display a checkbox for you to click before determining if it will show you a challenge, and Invisible, where the challenge is in the background and you will only be shown the captcha if you are deemed to not be trusted enough by the reCaptcha system. There are a variety of ways that you can set up and use AutoSolve in order to solve these types of Captchas.   
![reCaptcha.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/5461877371543)

**Recommended Setup (Trusted Accounts + AutoSolve AI)**

Since reCaptcha is a Google service the best way to solve these types of challenges will be having trusted accounts that are receiving one-clicks and utilizing a backup solving service such as AutoSolve AI or Google Speech as your first line of defense when you do not get a one-click. In order to not burn out your accounts, it is best to limit the number of solves per account using the _Max Solvers per Local Solver_ to 10-30 solves and have other accounts scheduled to take their place when those limits are hit. The number of solves will vary based on the trust of your account so keep that in mind. You can also use Automatically stop a solver when Manual Solve Required (MSR). This will ensure that if you do get a solver that is struggling with a fading captcha and reaches the default 60 request limit for AutoSolve AI, instead of going to MSR, it will stop the solver and move on to one of the scheduled ones.  
**Your Services Tab should have Local Solvers as Primary, and AutoSolve AI as Active.** 

**![LocalAndAI.PNG](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/12313526314135)  
Your AutoSolve Settings Tab under reCaptchaV2 should look like this.  
![PresetConfig.PNG](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/12313560118039)  
**

**Alternative Setup (Empty Solvers + AutoSolve AI)**

If the Google Accounts you do have are not trusted, or if you don't have any at all then you will want to create Solvers which are Accountless Browsers that are used only for AutoSolve, paired with AI this will be much faster during high traffic drops than other third party services. You can also use Automatically stop a solver when Manual Solve Required (MSR). This will ensure that if you do get a solver that is struggling with a fading captcha and reaches the default 60 request limit for AutoSolve AI, instead of going to MSR, it will stop the solver and move on to one of the scheduled ones.  
**Your Services Tab should have Local Solvers as Primary, and AutoSolve AI as Active.**   
![LocalAndAI.PNG](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/12313526314135)  
**Your AutoSolve Settings Tab under reCaptchaV2 should look like this.**  
![SolverPresetConfig.PNG](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/12313597335191)

#### reCaptcha v3

reCaptcha v3 is a completely invisible challenge designed to gauge your likelihood of being an abusive user of the website. The system will take in factors like your browser, IP, and other factors and then return a score based on that assessment. There is no visible challenge like with reCaptcha v2, but some sites that use reCaptcha v3 include Yeezy Supply and Adidas.

**Recommended Setup (High Trust Score Accounts + Static ISP Proxies)**

Since reCaptcha v3 releases tend to be very request heavy, having a good amount of accounts with a high trust scores will result in the best results when paired with good quality proxies. Since V3 drops tend to be request heavy it is good to rest your accounts for 12 hours after a drop before you resume farming and to have sets of accounts you rotate between using on the releases so that you are not using the same set of accounts every release. In order to reduce the strain on your accounts, it is best to limit the number of solves per account to 60-80 and have other accounts scheduled to take their place when those limits are hit.  
![LocalSolverOnlyPrimary.PNG](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/12313648565783)  
**Your AutoSolve Settings Tab under reCaptchaV3 should look like this.![V3Accounts_config.PNG](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/12313649871767)**

**Alternative Setup (Empty Solvers + Static Unique ISP Proxies)**

If you do not have trusted Google Accounts then you can still get good results on V3 drops with Empty Solvers and good proxies. Without accounts though, it is best to use a lower limit on the number of solves per account like 30-40, and have other solvers on different proxies ready to stand in and take their place when that limit is hit. Unique ISP Proxies will give you the best results, while you can use unique static residential proxies you will have some difficulties with them.  
**Your Services Tab should have Local Solvers as Primary.**   
![LocalSolverOnlyPrimary.PNG](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/12313648565783)  
**Your AutoSolve Settings Tab under reCaptchaV3 should look like this.**  
![v3Solversconfig.PNG](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/12313651283479)

#### GeeTest

While there are a few different types of GeeTests challenges, the most commonly found one is slide Captcha which involves sliding a puzzle piece into place on the challenge. From interacting with the challenge GeeTest analyzes and attempts to determine whether you are a human or bot. Since GeeTest is not affiliated with Google, it will not benefit greatly from the use of your Trusted Google Accounts. 

**Recommended Setup (Empty Solvers + AutoSolve AI)**

Since GeeTest is not affiliated with Google, it does not benefit from trusted accounts. The best way to solve GeeTest Challenges is with Empty Solvers and AutoSolve AI. This will offer the fastest solve times, especially during high traffic drops.

**Alternative Setup (Third Party Services)**

While 2Captcha, AntiCaptcha, and CapMonster do support GeeTest challenges, during high traffic drops the solve times can be slow, so just be mindful of that.

# AutoSolve - OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Start AutoSolve – AYCD

AutoSolve - OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Start AutoSolve

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16221641626135-AutoSolve-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Start-AutoSolve/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

July 22, 2023 22:12

Introduction
------------

You can use the Start AutoSolve feature to start your Accounts/Solvers so that they are ready to receive incoming captcha requests.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To Start AutoSolve, you need to create Account/Solver tasks. Learn more about Accounts [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173)
    . Learn more about Solvers [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16217942144279)
    .
*   You should use Recommended mode for the best AutoSolve setup. You can enable Recommended mode in Settings > AutoSolve | General
*   If you are using Custom Mode, you can configure additional settings that change AutoSolve's behavior. You can learn more about configuring these settings [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13323057998231)
    .

Start AutoSolve (Recommended Mode)
----------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts/Solvers section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a play button titled **Account/Solvers** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Accounts/Solvers you want to start AutoSolve on.

3\. Click the **Options** button at the top right of the view or right-click on the selected Accounts/Solvers and click **Start AutoSolve**. 

4\. Your Accounts/Solvers will launch in AutoSolve mode. The first status you will see is **Warming Up**, which increases reCaptcha trust. Then the status will show **Waiting To Solve**, which means your Accounts/Solvers are ready to receive captcha requests.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16222050021399)

Start AutoSolve (Custom Mode)
-----------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts/Solvers section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a play button titled **Account/Solvers** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Accounts/Solvers you want to start AutoSolve on.

3\. Click the **Options** button at the top right of the view or right-click on the selected Accounts/Solvers and click **Start AutoSolve**. 

4\. The **AutoSolve Setup** dialog will appear. You can specify the priority of the Accounts/Solvers when starting AutoSolve on them. 

*   The **Top to Bottom** option will start your Accounts/Solvers in order from top to bottom.
*   The **Lowest Solves** option will start your Accounts/Solvers based on how many solves they have.
*   The **Random** option will start your Accounts/Solvers randomly.

5\. If you are solving reCaptcha, click the **Warmup Browser With Some Activity** checkbox. You do not need to click this checkbox if you are solving any other captcha type.

6\. Once you have configured the dialog, click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16222263013271)

7\. Your Accounts/Solvers will launch in AutoSolve mode. If you clicked the **Warmup Browser with Some Activity** checkbox, the first status you will see is **Warming Up**, which increases reCaptcha trust. Then the status will show **Waiting To Solve**, which means your Accounts/Solvers are ready to receive captcha requests.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16222280290711)

# Bitwarden - Secure Password Manager – AYCD

Bitwarden - Secure Password Manager

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360042545554-Bitwarden-Secure-Password-Manager/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



======================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

February 05, 2020 23:22

![Bitwarden_Logo_2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360057794753/Bitwarden_Logo_2.png)

Account Security and Password theft is a serious problem in the online space. Trying to ensure your accounts stay secure, and also remain accessible to you is imperative to being able to stay safe online.

The best way to make sure that you can stay secure online, even in the face of things like Data Breaches, is to use a different and randomly generated password for each of the online accounts that you create. This helps to ensure that even if there is a data breach and hackers get access to your password that they would not be able to use it to access any of your other online accounts.

Bitwarden is our officially recommended password manager and is completely free and open source. Bitwarden allows you to store your website credentials in an encrypted vault securely and then access them using a secure master password you set. You can access their desktop application on both Windows and macOS devices, they have a mobile app available on both iOS and Android, and they also have built-in browser extensions for numerous browsers.

Bitwarden has two versions. A free version that will remain free forever, which gives you access to all of Bitwarden and its apps. Meaning it will not limit the places you can install it, nor the number of passwords you can store. Bitwarden also gives you access to their secure password generator, which allows you to generate random passwords and automatically save those passwords for your accounts. The free version of Bitwarden is excellent and gives you access to the core and all of the amazing features at absolutely no cost.

If you want some added benefits to that package, Bitwarden Premium costs $10/year and comes with some great features. It includes 1GB of Encrypted file storage and, most importantly, has a built-in Authenticator allowing you to store your 2FA or Time-Based Authentication codes within your Bitwarden accounts! When you log in using Bitwarden it automatically will copy to clipboard your 2FA code allowing you to simply paste it into the field. This helps save you the hassle of having to go into additional applications like Google Authenticator to get your 2FA codes.

Learn more: [Bitwarden.com](https://bitwarden.com/)

[Download Bitwarden](https://bitwarden.com/#download)

[Bitwarden Premium](https://bitwarden.com/pricing/)

# OneClick - Tasks - Schedule Configs – AYCD

OneClick - Tasks - Schedule Configs

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/24174965128855-OneClick-Tasks-Schedule-Configs/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



======================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

June 14, 2024 05:51

Introduction
------------

You can use Schedule Configs to automate the starting and stopping of AutoSolve on your Accounts/Solvers. You can create a one-off schedule for a specific drop or a repeating Schedule, allowing you to fully automate for a known drop.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To Start AutoSolve, you need to create Account/Solver tasks. Learn more about Accounts [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173)
    . Learn more about Solvers [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16217942144279)
    .
*   You should use Recommended mode for the best AutoSolve setup. You can enable Recommended mode in Settings > AutoSolve | General
*   If you are using Custom Mode, you can configure additional settings that change AutoSolve's behavior. You can learn more about configuring these settings [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13323057998231)
    .

Creating a Schedule Config
--------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Schedule Configs section of OneClick in Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a clock titled Schedule Configs when you hover over it.

2\. Click on the **Create** button at the top right of the view. This will bring up the Create Schedule Config dialog.

![Edit_Schedule.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24196338458903)

3\. By default, Schedules are created in the All Category. You can override this and specify a category to have your Schedule created into a unique category.

4\. Specify a name for your Schedule. If you have any notes or details, you can include them in the Notes field.

5\. Specify the Start Date/Time of your schedule. This is the date and time that you want the selected AutoSolve Accounts/Solvers to be ready to solve. 

6\. Specify a Setup Before time. This is the duration before the scheduled start time that everything should be setup. 

7\. Specify the End Date/Time of your schedule. This is the date and time that your selected Accounts/Solvers will return to their previous state. 

8\. The Repeat After field allows you to set your Schedule Config to repeat after a certain amount of time has passed. This is useful to automatically Start Autosolve for regularly scheduled drops,  releases, or other automation that you have setup. 

![Date_Time.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24196338465047)

9\. Select the Categories of Accounts/Solvers that you want to start at the scheduled Date/Time in the event they are not already running AutoSolve.

![Tasks_Categories.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24196362672919)

10\. If you want to receive a webhook notification for when the Schedule is activated, click the **Send Setup Started Notification** checkbox. It will use the default webhook you have in Settings unless you override it with a specific one in Settings > Notifications. 

![Notifications.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/24196338474775)

11\. After configuring the dialog, click the Save button. Your Schedule Config will be created and shown in the table.

12\. Based on the duration of your Setup Before the Start Date/Time, the accounts/solvers in the selected categories will pause what they were doing and Start AutoSolve. At the scheduled End Date/Time, they will pick back up where they left off, whether they were farming or stopped. 

### Articles in this section

*   [OneClick - Tasks - Schedule Configs](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/24174965128855-OneClick-Tasks-Schedule-Configs)

# What Computer specs do I need to run AYCD Toolbox? – AYCD

What Computer specs do I need to run AYCD Toolbox?

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038184394-What-Computer-specs-do-I-need-to-run-AYCD-Toolbox/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=====================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

November 10, 2019 06:57

The system requirements for AYCD are as follows: 

Windows 8/8.1 | Windows 10 | Windows Server 2008 R2 | Windows Server 2012/R2 | Windows Server 2016

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Minimum** | **Recommended** |
| **CPU**: Dual Core CPU | **CPU**: Quad-Core CPU with at least 2.0Ghz |
| **RAM**: 8GB | **RAM**: 16GB |
| **Hard Disk Space**: 10GB | **Hard Disk Space**: 20GB |

While we do not officially support M1 Mac devices, we do release a version for you. You can learn about how to download the toolbox [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038115714)
.

# I can’t download AYCD (Oneclick, Spoof, Billing Converter, ServerGen, Toolbox) – AYCD

I can’t download AYCD (Oneclick, Spoof, Billing Converter, ServerGen, Toolbox)

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038705213-I-can-t-download-AYCD-Oneclick-Spoof-Billing-Converter-ServerGen-Toolbox/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

November 10, 2019 06:58

If you are having trouble downloading the Toolbox, there are a couple of possible reasons for this.

*   Ensure you are on the latest version of the Toolbox. If you are constantly being asked to update the Toolbox, or cannot launch some applications you may need to manually update. You can learn more about that [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043831854)
    .
*   I am trying to download the Toolbox from either **Russia** or **China** what can I do?
    *   A: To resolve this, you will need to access our website and try to download the application while using a VPN. Internet Service Providers(ISPs) from those regions do not allow access to the server AYCD is hosted on. The only workaround is to use a VPN.
*   I am not from Russia or China, and I am still having trouble downloading the Toolbox or the applications within it, what can I do?
    *   A: There are several possible reasons that you may be unable to download the program. The most common reason for this could be that you are on a **network that limits your access** or ability to download applications. This is common on **School networks** or some **public networks** and **w****ork networks**. The best solution for this is to connect on a home network.
    *   A: Another possible reason could be that you do not have a stable internet connection, to resolve this, please try to download the toolbox from a more stable location, or with your computer directly connected with an ethernet cable. 

If you are still having trouble downloading any of the applications, please open a ticket so that we can assist you further with your issue.

# OneClick - Status Messages – AYCD

OneClick - Status Messages

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039319873-OneClick-Status-Messages/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

November 21, 2019 05:49

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Status Message** | **Description** |
| **Testing One Click** | This means the application is in the process of testing to see if it gets a one click or a puzzle. |
| **Verifying Account** | This appears when verifying that your account is logged in from a previous session and does not need to relog. |
| **Scheduled** | This means that the profile has been scheduled to run, but has no run yet. You are limited to only 2 local account without using proxies by default. |
| **Taking a Break** | This means that the profile has already run and is taking a break waiting to begin again. |
| **BrowserError! Retrying..** | There was an error with the browser. If it does not resolve itself or goes into taking a break right away please restart the application to resolve this error. |
| **One Click Failed** | This means that the last time the application tested for one click it received a captcha puzzle instead of a one click. |
| **One Click Error** | This means a browser error occurred while trying to test for oneclick. Please check your proxies or restart the application. |
| **Two Factor Detected** | This means that you will need to fill out your accounts 2 factor authentication. |
| **Proxy Error** | Please check your proxies. This is caused by the proxy dropping connection to the proxy server. |
| **Connection Error** | This error will occur if the proxy can connect but can’t deliver data. We suggest restarting the application or getting in contact with your proxy provider. |
| **Solve ReCaptcha** | This message will appear if you have tested for one clicks and have received a captcha puzzle that you need to solve. |
| **Youtube Account Banned** | If you get this message please verify that your account is banned manually in the browser. If it is, it could take longer time to get oneclicks and is likely silently flagged. |
| **No Script Available** | This error will occur if you have toggled all the Google Scripts off in either settings or under the accounts advanced settings. |
| **Controller Error** | An error occured with this task, it will automatically fix itself after taking a short break. |

# Buying or Selling AYCD  – AYCD

Buying or Selling AYCD

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041408953-Buying-or-Selling-AYCD/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

January 01, 2020 11:05

Bot Mart is a safe and convenient place to buy, sell, rent and trade bots, group invites and more. One of the bots that Bot Mart supports is the AYCD Toolbox, and if your subscription or lifetime copy is something you are looking to sell, we highly recommend doing it through Bot Mart.

When you sell AYCD through Bot Mart, we can ensure that:

*   The buyer is receiving a completely fresh account that is not tied to your email address or account.
*   This means that as the seller, you keep your account and just have the product removed from your account. Since the buyer is getting a fresh account, it also means that they will receive a fresh discord token, so you don't have to worry about making your discord part of the transaction.
*   None of your billing or past payments are tied to the account you are selling when sold in Bot Mart.
*   Transactions for AYCD subscriptions or lifetimes handled by an AYCD Owner or Admin, making it safe and secure.

[Learn more about Buying and Sell in Bot Mart](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360042196574)

# AYCD is too large and zoomed in. It is taking up my screen. – AYCD

AYCD is too large and zoomed in. It is taking up my screen.

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040897814-AYCD-is-too-large-and-zoomed-in-It-is-taking-up-my-screen/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



======================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

December 31, 2019 03:46

Sometimes when you have a smaller monitor or a lower resolution display, some of the applications in the Toolbox can appear larger than your screen. This means that buttons to close or minimize that program are off the screen and cannot be clicked. While this can be frustrating, there is something that you can do to resolve this.

Pressing and holding alt and then space will create a dropdown, which will allow you to maximize AYCD and cause it to fill the screen, giving you access to the close, minimize and full-screen buttons again at the top.

After closing the application it will ask you if you want to save the new application size, which will ensure that the application opens back up again at the same size.![26.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360102340433/26.png)  
  

*   Before alt + space where the buttons are off-screen  
    ![25.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360102340393/25.png)
*   Pressing alt + space  
    ![mceclip0.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360054985113/mceclip0.png)
*   After maximized  
    ![27.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500001255922/27.png)

# No Mountable File System – AYCD

No Mountable File System

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040959154-No-Mountable-File-System/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

January 03, 2020 04:18

AYCD's Toolbox is a great tool that helps assist you with botting, and as you come to use, it will prove itself invaluable to you when it comes to drops. 

One of the more common issues that occur on older versions of macOS is the inability to run the installer. The error that displays is that the "Disk Image cannot be opened" along with the message that "No Mountable File Systems". 

There are a couple of things you can do to try and resolve this, but there is no guarantee you would be able to run it from your Mac on macOS.

1.  Ensure you have downloaded the file correctly and that it has been completely downloaded before you try to run it. 
2.  Ensure that you are not blocking the running of the Toolbox. Try changing the setting for **Allow apps downloaded from** to **App Store and identified developers**. This can be found if you go into **System Preferences** \> **Security & Privacy.** Now retry the AYCD Toolbox installer, if it still isn't opening you may have to override the application block in **Security & Privacy** as well.
3.  Ensure that the toolbox file is on your desktop. Sometimes there can be permission issues with where you have downloaded the dmg.
4.  Reboot your Mac if you haven't already. Sometimes there are issues after opening too many DMG files that is resolved by restarting.
5.  You can try to mount the DMG in Terminal. To do this:
    1.  Open Terminal
    2.  Type **hdiutil attach** in the terminal window but do not press enter yet. 
    3.  Drag the dmg file from your Finder window into the terminal window. This will fill in the file location of your DMG.
    4.  Press Enter and this should mount the DMG.
6.  If the above steps above do not work, you can open a ticket for us to take a look.

Alternatively, you can look at solutions that allow you to run Windows on your computers, such as [Parallels](https://www.parallels.com/ca/)
 and [Bootcamp](https://support.apple.com/en-ca/HT201468)
.
