# OneClick - Testing ReCaptcha  – AYCD

OneClick - Testing ReCaptcha

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038828114-OneClick-Testing-ReCaptcha/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



========================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

November 21, 2019 05:50

Introduction
------------

One of the most critical farming activities is testing your accounts, which is often the last step you must perform to achieve one-clicks. OneClick offers the ability to manually or automatically test your accounts.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You should use one unique ISP/DC proxy for each account. Learn more about adding and assigning proxies to Accounts [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039319653)
    .
*   We recommend the practices found in the Best Practices guide, which you can read about [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360048109593)
    .
*   We have found it best to test the accounts every 3-7 days. The Captcha Test Setup dialog has two available tests; the meaning of each is below.
    *   ReCaptcha V2 Checkbox is used by Shopify sites and is the more widely used version of ReCaptcha.
    *   ReCaptcha V3 Score is used in splash releases on websites such as Yeezy Supply and Adidas.
*   It is important to note that ReCaptcha V3 Scores tend to fluctuate frequently, so they are not a good indication of one-clicks. If you want to test and see if you have one-clicks, we recommend using the ReCaptcha V2 Checkbox test.

Manually Testing
----------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts/Solvers section in OneClick under Tasks. When you hover over it, the section icon looks like a play button titled Account/Solvers.

2\. Select the accounts you wish to test. Then click on Options, or right-click on an account from either drop-down menu and select Test ReCaptcha.  
![2024-05-17 14_01_37-OneClick v3.4.2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23561415441815)

3\. Read the ReCaptcha Test Warning dialog, and if you'd like to proceed, click Yes.

4\. In the following dialog, select the Captcha Version you'd like to test. We recommend selecting the ReCaptcha v2 Checkbox option and then clicking Start.

5\. The browser will load and test your account. If you do not one-click the challenge, we strongly recommend solving it. The action log will update you on whether you one-clicked the challenge or whether it failed.

Automatically test ReCaptcha
----------------------------

To Automatically test ReCaptcha while farming your accounts, you need to have an AutoSolve AI subscription. OneClick will automatically test your accounts for one-clicks using the ReCaptcha V2 Checkbox test and ReCaptcha V3 scores using the recaptcha-demo.appspot.com tester.

1\. Navigate to the Settings section in OneClick and click **AutoSolve | AI** on the left side.  
![2024-05-17 14_11_22-OneClick v3.4.2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23561431928599)

2\. Enable the checkbox next to Automatically test ReCaptcha while farming and click Save.

3\. OneClick will then automatically test your accounts when required.

4\. You can check when your accounts were tested by clicking on the Solve Speed result under Tasks > Accounts/Solvers.  
![2024-05-17 14_14_04-OneClick v3.4.2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23561415456151)

Testing Proxy V3 Score
----------------------

Testing your proxies without an account assigned allows you to see the proxy's quality. To begin, make sure that you have proxies added to OneClick. To learn about adding proxies to OneClick, click [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039319653)
.

1\. Navigate to the Proxies section in OneClick.

2\. Select the proxies you want to test, then click Options > Test Proxies.  
![2024-05-17 14_18_33-OneClick v3.4.2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23561415467543)

3\. From the following dialog, select ReCaptcha v3 Score and click Run.

4\. In the background, OneClick will start a browser and test the proxy. The score will be shown in the Page Load (ms) column.  
![2024-05-17 14_21_25-OneClick v3.4.2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23561431963031)

# OneClick - Accounts - Running Accounts, Scheduling & Taking a Break – AYCD

OneClick - Accounts - Running Accounts, Scheduling & Taking a Break

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039319713-OneClick-Accounts-Running-Accounts-Scheduling-Taking-a-Break/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/1500080569202/crw1.png)

**Crewie**

November 21, 2019 05:47

Introduction
------------

OneClick is the most powerful tool available when it comes to creating Trusted Google Accounts. It will automatically schedule and run your accounts to ensure every account gets the perfect amount of time running and resting.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   It is recommended to use proxies if you are farming more than two accounts.
*   We recommend against altering the run and sleep times, these have been determined to be optimal after testing across thousands of accounts.
*   OneClick will determine how many accounts would be the most stable amount to run based on your computer's available memory and CPU cores.
*   Some accounts will begin to log in right away while other accounts may go to a Scheduled status. This status means that they will begin when the currently running accounts go to "Taking a Break". OneClick will automatically manage this schedule for all accounts without any interference from you so you do not need to worry about manually starting accounts, or restarting accounts. 

### Running Your Accounts

1\. Navigate over to the Accounts section of OneClick. 

2\. Select the accounts which you would like to start farming.

3\. Click **Options**, then click **Start Farming**, or, right-click on an account and click **Start** **Farming**. This will then start the login process.

![3.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6555874700183/3.gif)

### Scheduled Accounts

"Scheduled" is a perfectly normal status. The number of scheduled accounts depends on how many accounts you have set to run, or how many accounts OneClick has determined to be safe. 

If you find you only have 2 accounts running at once, then it could be due to two reasons.

1.  You do not have proxies assigned to your account. OneClick will only run 2 accounts without Proxies to prevent the program from running too many accounts on the same IP. It is recommended that you run OneClick with DC proxies. You can find out more about proxies [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041355933)
    .
2.  If you already have proxies assigned but it is still only running 2 accounts at the same time then this could be because your computer has low ram or CPU. If this is the case then the system determined it would reduce the stability of the application if more accounts are running concurrently.

The important thing to remember is that all of your accounts will run regardless, they will begin once the currently running accounts go to take a break. If you really do want more accounts to be running concurrently then we would recommend looking into running on a server or upgrading your computer.

![1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6555918000535/1.png)

### Stopping Your Tasks / Taking a Break

1\. Navigate over to the Accounts section of OneClick. 

2\. Select the accounts which you would like to stop farming.

3\. Click **Options**, then click **Stop**, this will then start the login process. Or, right-click on an account and click **Stop**.

Having your accounts take a break while running is an important part of creating a Trusted Account. If your accounts are running 24/7 it will have extremely negative effects. This is why the scheduling system is so important, OneClick will have your accounts running for between 20 minutes to 60 minutes then it will take a break for between 3 and 8 hours. This helps to create a realistic amount of activity, and after rigorous testing, this is what we have found produces the best balance, and is best for one-click generation. 

It is common for the 'Action Log' to state 'Taking a break' however it is highly likely that the accounts will have run. If you are concerned about account activity you can check the account.  To do this you can right-click on an account and view the action log of the account to see its past activities.   
![4.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6555927761943/4.gif)

# Buying and Selling in Bot Mart – AYCD

Buying and Selling in Bot Mart

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360042196574-Buying-and-Selling-in-Bot-Mart/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

January 29, 2020 01:22

![botmart_logo.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360056206214/botmart_logo.png)

Bot Mart is a safe and convenient place to buy, sell, rent and trade bots, group invites and more. 

**Buying in Bot Mart**

*   Buying is open al all server members and is completely FREE. Simply browse WTS (Want to sell) channels and look for products you want. Bot Mart staff are friendly and there to help guide your purchases as well if you have any questions.

**Selling or Trading**

*   At Bot Mart customers safety is a top priority. Anyone looking to sell products must be properly vetted before being able to do so. This helps to protect both the buyer and seller. Some products may require you to join Bot Marts membership program which costs $1 a month while others will require you to be fully verified. To become a MEMBER, simply visit [https://www.botmart.io/](https://www.botmart.io/ "https://www.botmart.io/")
     and link your discord and checkout on their site.
*   To become a VERIFIED seller, visit #join-bot-mart and follow the steps there to be successfully VERIFIED and ready to buy/sell/trade without restrictions!

For more information, you can contact Bot Mart using the links below.

*   [@botmart](https://twitter.com/botmrt)
    
*   help@botmart.io
*   [discord.gg/botmart](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/discord.gg/botmart "http://discord.gg/botmart")

# Changing Update Channels – AYCD

Changing Update Channels

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041319414-Changing-Update-Channels/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

January 14, 2020 01:09

The AYCD Toolbox is a powerful way that we choose to deliver updates. It allows you to install, update, and uninstall applications and acts as the single application that you have to download from a website. 

Occasionally while new versions are being worked on, we may put them on a Preview or Dev channel. To access these versions and test some of the upcoming features and changes, you can switch your update channel by following the steps below.

1.  Launch, or open the AYCDToolbox.
2.  Go to Settings.  
    ![19.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500001255662/19.png)
3.  Change the Update Channel to Preview.  
    ![20.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360102340093/20.png)
4.  Save your settings and check for updates.  
    ![22.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500001255702/22.png)

# Can't reopen the Toolbox or any of the other applications.  – AYCD

Can't reopen the Toolbox or any of the other applications.

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360042375394-Can-t-reopen-the-Toolbox-or-any-of-the-other-applications/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=====================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

February 01, 2020 20:34

The AYCD Toolbox is the application that we use to help you manage and keep updated all of the different programs that we offer. If you are new to using AYCD and to the Toolbox, sometimes it can appear as though it has closed and cannot reopen when in all actuality, it is running in the background. The Toolbox will minimize to your taskbar when you are not using it always instead of always sitting on your screen. This can cause you to think the application has not shutdown. To get it to show all you need to do is go down to your hidden icon section and click on the AYCD Logo to have your Toolbox appear again.     

![Toolbox_Hidden_Icon.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360057422633/Toolbox_Hidden_Icon.png)

If the Toolbox won't open or you cannot find this application after looking here, please open up a ticket so someone from our support team will assist you further.

# Generator - Types of Proxies – AYCD

Generator - Types of Proxies

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043204733-Generator-Types-of-Proxies/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



========================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

February 10, 2020 20:06

Generator is a tool provided by AYCD that allows you to generate Servers as well as Datacenter Proxies from providers such as, Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud Platform (GCP), or Digital Ocean and other providers. While we do offer the generation of DC proxies through those various providers we also sell Residential proxies through our AYCD Residential Proxy Network. 

#### Type 1 - Datacenter User:Pass and IP Authenticated Proxies

User:Pass proxies are the most common type of DC proxy. They can be used by any person on any computer or network. As long as you have the Username and Password to the proxy you are able to authenticate yourself and use it. IP Authentication proxies are a less common form of DC proxy. These can only be used on the IP you have used to authenticate them. That means that only if you reside on the network using the IP you put in when generating the proxy will be able to access it. These proxies do not use a username and Password. 

#### Type 2 - Residential Proxies

Residential Proxies are proxies that are not from Data Centers and instead from residential IP blocks. These proxies are primarily used for tasks on bots, or manual tools such as Spoof as they are usually unbanned on a lot of sites. To learn more about the AYCD Residential Proxy Network, [you can check out this section of Zendesk](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/categories/360002571574-AYCD-Proxies)
.

# OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Tools - Setup Email Forwarding / Remove Email Forwarding / Check Current Email Forwarding / Export Last Checked Forwarding – AYCD

OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Tools - Setup Email Forwarding / Remove Email Forwarding / Check Current Email Forwarding / Export Last Checked Forwarding

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041171674-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Tools-Setup-Email-Forwarding-Remove-Email-Forwarding-Check-Current-Email-Forwarding-Export-Last-Checked-Forwarding/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")





![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

January 09, 2020 11:31

Introduction
------------

*   The Email Forwarding tool allows you to automate the email forwarding setup on your accounts in OneClick, forwarding mail received to a primary email address.
*   The Remove Email Forwarding tool will enable you to automate the removal of email forwarding on your accounts in OneClick.
*   The Check Current Email Forwarding and Export Last Checked Email Forwarding tools allow you to view and export a list of all the emails your accounts may be forwarded to.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Before forwarding your accounts, you must [add accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173#h_01EPF75YH4B6PB4WVSN5P7NJ10)
     to OneClick.
*   We recommend farming your accounts for at least 1-3 weeks before using any Account Tools.
*   We recommend using accounts that are at least a year old. If you use accounts made within six months and forward them, they are likely to be suspended. If you plan to use new accounts, please proceed with caution.
*   We recommend forwarding a maximum of 25 accounts to one account.

Setup Email Forwarding
----------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Accounts/Solvers section of OneClick in Tasks.

2\. Select the accounts you want to forward in the table.

3\. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view, or right-click on the table and click on **Tools**. This will bring up the **Tools** dialog for accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/17972491964055)

4\. Select **Setup Email Forwarding** in the Tools dialog.

5\. Click the **Start** button. After reading the warning dialogs that appear, click Yes if you want to continue.

6\. There are various ways you can forward your Gmail accounts. The first method is to use your existing **A****ccounts** in the Tasks > Accounts/Solvers section.

*   In the **Setup Email Forward** dialog, you will be asked to select an account to which you want the chosen account's emails forwarded to.
*   If your account has a recovery email set, you can enable the **Forward using recovery email if available** checkbox to set up email forwarding to the assigned recovery email.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092303576727)

7\. The second method is by using **Inbox Mail**. You can select Mail Credentials to use for forwarding. OneClick will use your mail credentials when Google asks you to verify a code to the email you are forwarding to. If you configure this dialog, OneClick will automate this process.

*   You must create a Mail Task Template in Inbox and add your Inbox API key to OneClick > Settings > Inbox Service. You can find your Inbox API key in Settings > Tasks. Follow the guide below to create a Mail Task Template.
*   You must add your mail credentials that receive your recovery email codes to Inbox, and then to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to add your mail credentials to Inbox](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    .
*   [To use Inbox Mail, follow this guide to configure a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   You can configure the **Mail Timeout in Minutes Override** field to configure the default timeout for waiting for OTP codes.
*   Input the emails you want your accounts forwarded to in the **Forward To Emails** field. One email account will be used for each OneClick account task.
*   If your account has a recovery email set, you can enable the **Forward using recovery email if available** checkbox to set up email forwarding to the assigned recovery email.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092319456919)

10\. After you have made your selections, click Proceed. The accounts will be forwarded one by one.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/17973733322263)

11\. You can determine whether an account was successfully forwarded by looking at the Action Log column. If successfully forwarded, it will state **Forwarded To: accountname** in green, with the Status **All Mail Filter Success**. If you have an AYCD/Discord Webhook in Settings, you will receive notifications confirming whether the setup was successful.

Remove Email Forwarding
-----------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Accounts section of OneClick. 

2\. Select the accounts you want to remove forwarding on from the table.

3\. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view, or right-click on the table and click on **Tools**. This will bring up the **Tools** dialog for accounts.

4\. Click the dropdown and choose the **Remove Email Forwarding** option.

![2023-04-06_19_47_57-Tools.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092362075415)

5\. Click the **Start** button.

6\. Please ensure you read the following warning dialogs. If you wish to continue after reading the warning dialog, click **Yes**.

7\. OneClick will start the job by warming up and logging into the accounts. 

8\. You can determine whether an account's forwarding was removed by looking at the Action Log column. If it was successfully removed, the status will say "**Removed Forwarding Success"** in green. If you have an AYCD/Discord Webhook in Settings, you will receive notifications confirming whether the setup was successful or not.

![2023-04-06_19_51_05-OneClick_x_Crewie_v3.0.3.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092362075671)

Check Current Email Forwarding
------------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Accounts section of OneClick. 

2\. Select the accounts whose forwarding you want to check in the table.

3\. Click on the Tools button at the top right of the view or right-click on the table and click on Tools. This will bring up the Tools dialog for accounts.

4\. Click the dropdown and choose the **Check Current Email Forwarding** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092400103447)

5\. Click the **Start** button.

6\. OneClick will then start the browsers and begin to check whether they are forwarded or not. The result will be shown in the Action Log.  

![Check-2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092400104983)

Export Last Checked Email Forwarding
------------------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Accounts section of OneClick. 

2\. Select the accounts whose forwarding you want to export in the table.

3\. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view, or right-click on the table and click on **Tools**. This will bring up the **Tools** dialog for accounts.

4\. Click the dropdown and choose the **Export Last Checked Email Forwarding** option.

5\. Click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092400106519)

6\. Click Yes on the dialog to open the file location, then open the CSV export. This will contain the Account Email in one column and the Forwards To email in the other column. 

![Export-2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092416091799)

# OneClick - Freeing Up Storage Space – AYCD

OneClick - Freeing Up Storage Space

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043215133-OneClick-Freeing-Up-Storage-Space/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



======================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/1500080569202/crw1.png)

**Crewie**

February 10, 2020 23:40

Introduction
------------

Running OneClick means that you are running an individual browser for each account that you have in OneClick. You may find that OneClick uses up a large amount of space on your hard drive, especially if OneClick has been running for a while. This occurs because each running browser caches various resources, which, in turn, helps with page load times. Occasionally, if you are working on a server or in an environment with a low amount of hard drive space, you will need to clear it.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   It is essential to differentiate between browser cache and browser data. The browser cache includes browsing history and cookies but not your Google login session. Browser data encompasses everything and includes your Google login session. 
*   Browser cache is essential for fast ReCaptcha solve times; as such, it is recommended only to clear your browser cache when necessary.  
*   It is recommended to have your Automatic Cache Cleanup set to Partial or Disabled. 

Automatic Cache Cleanup
-----------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Accounts/Solvers** section in OneClick under **Settings**. 

2\. In the top-right of this section, you will find the **Automatic Cache Cleanup** option. Click on the drop-down arrow and select one of the options, which will be explained below.  
**Disabled** - This option does not cleanup any of the browser cache; this optimises website load times but will use a large amount of storage space.  
**Partial** - This option cleans the majority of the cache that you accumulate when running OneClick. However, some cache will be kept, especially if it drastically improves load times.   
**Complete** \- This clears all the cache every time your accounts run. As a result, you will find websites take longer to load.  
![2023-12-04 22_49_48-Clipboard.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19562232486295)

3\. We recommend setting Automatic Cache Cleanup to either **Partial** or **Disabled**.

4\. Once you have selected an option, click **Save**.   

Cleanup Old Files
-----------------

1\. Navigate to the **General** section in OneClick under **Settings**.

2\. On the right-hand side of this section, you will find the **Cleanup Old Files** option.   
![Screenshot 2023-12-04 230346.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19562232493463) 

3\. Click on the button and read the following warning. If you are happy, you can click **Proceed** to continue.  
![Screenshot 2023-12-04 230457.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19562232496535)

4\. Once you click proceed, OneClick will attempt to clear the old files. Once finished, a small dialog will appear stating how many files were successfully deleted.   
![Screenshot 2023-12-04 230651.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19562215514647) 

Reset > Browser Data
--------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Accounts/Solvers** section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon resembling a play button titled Accounts/Solvers.

2\. Select the accounts for which you wish to reset the browser data.

3\. Right-click on the accounts, and a drop-down menu will appear.

4\. Click Reset> Browser Data from that dropdown menu, and OneClick will automatically clear the browser data for the selected task(s).  
![Screenshot 2023-12-04 231406.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19562232504471)

# TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Create One Task / Many Tasks / Import / Import from Profile Builder / Export – AYCD

TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Create One Task / Many Tasks / Import / Import from Profile Builder / Export

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18178508966679-TabSentry-Tasks-Browsers-Create-One-Task-Many-Tasks-Import-Import-from-Profile-Builder-Export/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")





![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

October 10, 2023 21:59

Introduction
------------

You can create tasks in TabSentry to mass enter queue and raffle releases, manage multiple accounts with unique browser sessions, and many other use cases. You can create individual or bulk tasks. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   The data in all Toolbox programs is secured and encrypted automatically. Given the sensitive nature of Billing and Credit Card information, you can further secure data in TabSentry with a [Password Lock](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7446003478039)
    .
*   You can navigate to the Tasks > Browsers section of TabSentry and click File - Export to download an example CSV file with the TabSentry Tasks import format.
*   Use categories to simplify organization.
*   If you already have existing Browser Tasks in Profile Builder, you can import them to TabSentry. 
*   If you want to have autofill access to address and payment information, you should add them to TabSentry before creating your tasks. [Click here to learn how to create / import Profiles to TabSentry.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/17572661864599)
    
*   If you want to use proxies with your tasks, you should add them to TabSentry before creating your tasks. [Click here to learn how to add proxies to TabSentry.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18203862924311)
    
*   If you want to use Chrome Extensions with your tasks, you should add them to TabSentry before creating your tasks. [Click here to learn how to add Extensions to TabSentry.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18257661017879)
    

Create One Task / Many Tasks
----------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Tasks > Browsers** section in TabSentry. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled Browsers.

2\. Click on **File** and then **Create** **One Task** to create one Task. If you want to create multiple tasks at once, click the **Create Many Tasks** button. This will open the task creation dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18178760964119)

3\. By default, Tasks are created in the **All Category**. You can override this and specify a category to have your Task created into a unique category.

4\. Specify a **Nickname** for your Task.

5\. Specify the **Website URLs** to use for your Task. The Username and Password provided above will only auto-fill on the Website URLs you have specified. These Website URLs will all be opened when the browser first launches.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23298784128023)

6\. If you are creating many tasks, specify the number of tasks to create in the **Quantity** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23298762822551)

7\. Specify which **Browser Type** you want to use for the Task. We support the following browsers:

\-

**Camo Chrome**

*   If your objective is to keep your accounts alive and in good standing with Google, you should use Camo Chrome.
*   Camo Chrome is essential for using the [Traffic & SEO](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/21313462266647)
     modules.
*   Camo Chrome is our in-house anti-detect browser. It gives your tasks a unique fingerprint, even from the same device, reducing the risk of account bans and detections.
*   Camo Chrome can solve cf / reCaptcha challenges with AutoSolve and farming accounts/solvers.
*   Camo Chrome does not support the majority of account tools in OneClick.
*   Camo Chrome supports visibly seeing what your tasks are doing by clicking the eye on the task to show the browser.

**AYCD Browser**

*   AYCD Browser can solve most captcha challenges with AutoSolve and farming accounts/solvers. 
*   AYCD Browser is very lightweight compared to the other browsers. You can run, on average, 3-4 AYCD Browsers for every 1 Chrome/Camo Chrome browser.
*   AYCD Browser supports all account tools, such as forwarding, enabling 2FA, changing passwords/recovery emails, and more.
*   AYCD Browser supports visibly seeing what your tasks are doing by clicking the eye on the task to show the browser.

**Chrome**

*   Chrome can solve cf / reCaptcha challenges with AutoSolve and farming accounts/solvers.
*   Chrome is identical to using Chrome manually on your computer. We do not alter the fingerprint for each Chrome task. 
*   Chrome does not support the majority of account tools in OneClick.
*   Chrome supports visually seeing what your tasks are doing by clicking the eye on the task to show the browser.

**Brave**

*   Brave is similar to Camo Chrome. We recommend trying Camo Chrome, and if you are having anti-bot issues, swap to Brave to see if you have more success.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23298762830359)

8\. If you want to use Extensions, specify which Extensions to use on your Tasks. Camo Chrome, Google Chrome, and Brave support Extensions_. AYCD Browser does not support Extensions._

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23298762838679)

9\. You can configure **Autofill** for your tasks. This will enable Autofill on specified Website URLs.

10\. Specify a **Username/Email** and **Password**. If your account has **2FA (TOTP)** configured, you can provide the Secret Key to view the 2FA code.

11\. Specify a **Profile** to use for your Task. If you still need to create a Profile, you can create one with the **Create New Profile** button. This Profile will be available for Autofill on any website. Click here to learn more about Profile creation.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19042125724055)

12\. If you are creating many tasks, you can input multiple accounts at once in the **Accounts** field. Your accounts will be distributed between the created tasks. Each new account should be separated with a new line in either username:password:proxy or username:::password:::proxy. 

13\. Specify which **Profiles** to use for your Tasks. Your Profiles will be available for Autofill on any website. [Click here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/17572661864599)
 to learn more about Profile creation.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19042104696087)

14\. You can assign a **Proxy** to your Task. If you have not added any proxies to TabSentry, add one with the **Create New Proxy** button. [Click here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18203862924311)
 to learn more about adding Proxies to TabSentry.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18181430633879)

15\. If you are creating many tasks, you can assign multiple Proxies to your tasks. [Click here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18203862924311)
 to learn more about adding Proxies to TabSentry.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18181399737111)

16\. You can assign **Proxy Exclusions** to your tasks. This will prevent your proxies from being used when you access certain website URLs. You should not use this feature unless you have a specific use case.

17\. You can assign **Custom Data** to your tasks. This will be accessible from Task Assistant when running tasks for easy copy-paste access. This can be anything from queue entry codes to data related to your Task and more. 

18\. Click the **Create** button to create a Custom Data entry.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32323825259799)

19\. Specify a **Key** for your Custom Data entry. Then specify a **Value** for your Custom Data entry. The Value field will be accessible for easy copy-paste access.

20\. Specify the **Value Format** for the custom data entry. You can choose **Text** or **TOTP Secret Key**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32323825260951)

21\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. You can easily copy information from Custom Data and delete Custom Data with the trash can icon.

*   You can distribute unique Custom Data to your tasks with the Distribute Custom Data tool. [Click here to learn how to use the distribute custom data tool.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19348105137943)
    

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32323836968983)

22\. You can specify a **Group Name** for Multi-Browser Control (MBC). Multi-Browser Control (MBC) allows you to control multiple child browsers from one parent browser. MBC can mimic Mouse Movements, Keyboard Clicks, and Page Navigation. MBC enables you to automate tedious tasks such as auto-filling account details, entering queues, entering raffles, and more. This group determines which child browsers are controlled by the parent browser. [Click here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18200568034967)
 to learn more about Multi-Browser Control (MBC).

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18179808790039)

23\. You can assign **Features** to your tasks. Features range from IMAP Mail OTP code detection, automated Activity Generation, Auto Refresh, and more. [Click here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/search?utf8=%E2%9C%93&query=TabSentry+-+Tasks+-+Features)
 to learn more about features.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18181506838807)

24\. You can optionally configure various **Browser Options** for your tasks. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32323836969623)

25\. Using the Create Many Tasks option, you can import your **OneClick Browser Data** or your **Profile Builder Browser Data**. This will automatically import the browser data from your OneClick tasks or Profile Builder Account Tasks to your TabSentry Tasks.

*   Importing OneClick Browser Data is useful for Google Forms raffles and sites that use reCaptcha. 
*   Importing Profile Builder Browser Data is useful for doing additional work on your account tasks that Profile Builder does not support.

26\. Specify which type of User Data you want to use with your task with the **User Data Type** dropdown.

\- Select **Local** if you want your OneClick browser data copied. This will create a copy of your data. Sometimes, sessions are more frequently logged out when using this option.

\- Select **Remote** if you want to use the same browser data directly. This option will keep your sessions logged in more consistently but can cause issues with your OneClick browser data since it uses the same user directory. Anything you do in TabSentry with a Remote task will also be reflected in your OneClick browser data.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32323836971031)

27\. You can configure which browser storage type to use with the **Browser Data Storage Type** dropdown.

*   **Lightweight** uses the least data and requires much less storage space. We have not noticed any downsides to using the lightweight option.
*   **Standard** mimics the browser data directory of a standard browser. It uses more data and requires more available storage.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32323836975127)

28\. After configuring your Task(s), click the **Save** button. 

Import
------

1. Navigate over to the Browsers section of TabSentry in Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled **Browsers** when you hover over it.

2\. Click on **File** and then **Import**. This will open the Import Tasks dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18182937627415)

3\. By default, Tasks are imported to the **All Category**. You can override this and specify a category to have your Tasks imported into a unique Category.

4\. If you want to import with a CSV File, click the blue spreadsheet icon and select your Tasks CSV. You can access our CSV template by clicking **File** \> **Export,** which will export an empty CSV with the correct column headers.

5\. If you want to import with Copy/Paste format, paste your task info in the **Copy/Paste** field. Each account will create one Task. Each new account should be separated with a new line in either username:password:proxy or username:::password:::proxy. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18182987003159)![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18182989147799)

6\. After configuring the Import Tasks dialog, click the **Import** button.

Import from Profile Builder
---------------------------

1. Navigate over to the Browsers section of TabSentry in Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled **Browsers** when you hover over it.

2\. Click on **Options,** then click **Import from Profile Builder**. This will open the Import from Profile Builder dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18183080067863)

3\. Select the **Browser Tasks** you want to import to TabSentry. This will automatically create Tasks and Profiles in TabSentry based on what was in Profile Builder. The task setup, browser data, Extensions, Proxy, and other associated info will be imported to TabSentry. 

4\. After configuring the Import from Profile Builder dialog, click the **Import** button. Your Tasks and Profiles will be automatically created.

Export
------

1. Navigate over to the Browsers section of TabSentry in Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled **Browsers** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Tasks you want to Export. Click on **File** and then **Export**. This will open the Export Tasks dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18183154175639)

3\. The Export dialog will appear, confirming you want to Export the selected Tasks. Click **Yes** to continue. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18183170866327)

4\. The Completion dialog will appear, confirming your Tasks CSV was successfully exported. Click **Yes** to open the exported file(s) location.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18183171714839)

# AYCD Toolbox and macOS – AYCD

AYCD Toolbox and macOS

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040956434-AYCD-Toolbox-and-macOS/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

January 03, 2020 02:55

AYCD's Toolbox is a great tool that helps assist you with botting, and as you come to use, it will prove itself invaluable to you when it comes to drops. 

**AYCD is compatible and can work with macOS**. While we release a version for macOS it isn't officially fully supported by AYCD. On newer versions of macOS, the Toolbox applications run very well, but on some and especially older Macs and versions of macOS you can run into a lot of issues. So while we do not officially support it macOS, I wanted to detail several workarounds or solutions to bugs that some older versions of macOS can experience.

#### Cannot open the Installer

This issue is usually due to your computer Security & Privacy settings. This error is usually resolved by allowing downloads from Identified Developers. This can be done by going to System Preferences, clicking on Security & Privacy and adjusting the allow apps downloaded setting. Sometimes you will also have to allow the installer to be run from here.  
![clipboard-image__1_.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360054317414/clipboard-image__1_.png)![mceclip2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360054317634/mceclip2.png)

If you do not find the option there you can also try to right-click and open the application using open from the right-click menu.

![mceclip0.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360061370974/mceclip0.png)

#### Cannot start OneClick

This error could be occurring for a number of reasons. Below there are a number of things you can check to try to resolve this issue.

*   Do you have the Java Runtime downloaded? This is a dependency in order to use the Toolbox applications.  
    ![4.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360086627093/4.png)
*   Have you let the applications install completely? Sometimes with slower internet connections, the application can take much longer to download. Sometimes the entire update file is not downloaded, which prevents it from being able to install properly. Ensuring that you wait for the file to download fully will help ensure that the file can run properly.
*   If you did not let one of the applications install correctly you can uninstall it from the toolbox by right-clicking on the application and selecting Uninstall.  
    ![5.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360086627113/5.png)

####  No Mountable File System

This is one of the more common bugs experienced by older versions of macOS to learn more about troubleshooting that error [click here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040959154)
.

If you are still having issues, we recommend looking at solutions that allow you to run Windows on your computers, such as [Parallels](https://www.parallels.com/ca/)
 and [Bootcamp](https://support.apple.com/en-ca/HT201468)
.

# What is AutoSolve? – AYCD

What is AutoSolve?

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043730513-What-is-AutoSolve/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=====================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

February 22, 2020 00:02

**AutoSolve** is the latest tool by AYCD. It allows you to connect **OneClick** with any **Bots** that support AutoSolve, turning OneClick into your **Captcha Solving Hub**. By sending your requests to OneClick, you can direct them to your Accounts, Empty Solvers, or optionally to a supported 3rd Party Solver like 2Captcha, AntiCaptcha, or CapMonster.

With the power of AutoSolve and OneClick, you will drastically reduce your setup time for drops and allow you to focus all your solving attention in one place instead of trying to manage solvers across multiple bots. In addition, by connecting with AutoSolve AI, you can have it attempt to solve the captcha for you, nearly automating the entire process.

# Can't Connect - Verizon Internet  – AYCD

Can't Connect - Verizon Internet

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360044786893-Can-t-Connect-Verizon-Internet/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

March 10, 2020 19:58

Some users on Verizon Internet have issues connecting with AYCD. These errors can show in a few different ways. 

*   The Update Server Cannot Be Reached! - This can display in red at the top of the Toolbox.  
    ![](https://media.discordapp.net/attachments/540742865710481428/685980711634731036/Screen_Shot_2020-03-07_at_5.41.41_PM.png)
*   The Site Cannot Be Reached or ERR\_CERT\_COMMON\_NAME\_INVALID - This can display when trying to start browsers or access the website.   
    ![](https://media.discordapp.net/attachments/540742865710481428/685981015185162261/Screen_Shot_2020-03-07_at_5.43.15_PM.png)

Those are just some of the common errors that you can see when trying to log in, authenticate or update applications.

### The Solution

We have been able to determine that if this is happening to you and you are on Verizon Fios Internet it is a result of McAfee Home Protection which is installed on the Router. This will need to be disabled through the Fios app installed on your phone and is the only place that this can be resolved. 

1.  Launch the Verizon Fios app on your phone.
2.  Go to Account and Settings
3.  Click on Security Settings 
4.  Click on Home Network Protection and disable it. 
5.  If you are still having the issue you may need to go into Default Web Restrictions and disable that as well. 

![](https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/686028042333585459/686029099013373982/image0.png)

# AutoSolve - Third-Party Solver Setup – AYCD

AutoSolve - Third-Party Solver Setup

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043745313-AutoSolve-Third-Party-Solver-Setup/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



========================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

February 23, 2020 19:21

**AutoSolve** is the latest tool by AYCD, allowing you to connect **OneClick** with supported **bots** to make OneClick a **default Captcha Solver**. By connecting bots to OneClick, it becomes your Captcha solving hub - allowing for captcha challenges to be sent to OneClick and optionally to supported 3rd party solvers like 2Captcha, AntiCaptcha and CapMonster.

As mentioned in addition to using OneClick as your solver, AutoSolve has added support to integrate third-party solver services. Once these are setup, you will be able to activate, deactivate and prioritize which services you would like to use to solve captchas. Below are sections walking you through the setup for each of the supported Providers.

*   [2Captcha](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043745313-AutoSolve-Third-Party-Solver-Setup#h_f6c2b769-3e66-4101-977c-bcfc2ad427e1)
    
*   [AntiCaptcha](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043745313-AutoSolve-Third-Party-Solver-Setup#h_d22d9ba9-06c3-46cb-b36b-17a80fedf4ea)
    
*   [CapMonster](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043745313-AutoSolve-Third-Party-Solver-Setup#h_4dcc334e-facd-44fe-bb2b-aed48f19d540)
    
*   Captchas.io
*   Captchaai.com
*   [Best Captcha Solver](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043745313-AutoSolve-Third-Party-Solver-Setup#h_e38fed30-ac36-4d70-8026-4998be049dac)
    

Before you begin to set these up, I would highly recommend reviewing our [AutoSolve - Getting Started Guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
. It will walk you through some of the basics of AutoSolve, as well as help you setup AutoSolve with your bots. 

### 2Captcha AutoSolve Setup:

2captcha is a service made to automate the captcha recognition process. You can learn more about them on their website [2Captcha.com](https://2captcha.com/)
. If you'd like to learn how to create an account and utilize their service, you can use the following steps to do so:

1.  To begin, you will first need to create a 2Captcha account. You can register for an account if you do not already have one [here](https://2captcha.com/auth/register)
    . 
2.  Once you register your account, you will need to confirm your account with an email they will send you.  
    ![Email.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360059379833/Email.png)
3.  After you successfully create an account, you will be prompted to select your user type. In this case, you will select "I'm A Customer".   
    ![2Captcha_Customer.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360058466434/2Captcha_Customer.png)
4.  After confirming your account and selecting the user type of customer, you will be on the 2Captcha Dashboard. To use the 2Captcha service, you will need to add funds to your account. You can do this by clicking on the Add Funds button.   
    ![Add_Funds.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360059379873/Add_Funds.png)
5.  Either before or after you deposit funds into your account, you can copy your 2Captcha API Key to your clipboard, as you will need to put it in the AutoSolve Dashboard.  
    ![API_KEY.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360059380153/API_KEY.png)
6.  Next, you will need to open the AYCD Toolbox and Launch OneClick. In OneClick, go to the AutoSolve tab and click Go To Dashboard.
7.  From the dashboard under **Third Party Solver API Keys**, click on Add API Key.  
    ![Add_3rd_API_Key.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360101393854/Add_3rd_API_Key.png)
8.  Select 2Captcha from the Service dropdown set an easy to identify nickname for your API Key, for example, "2Captcha - Stealthy". Then paste your API Key from the 2Captcha website into the API Key field and click Save.  
    ![2Cap.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360103557193/2Cap.png)
9.  After you have added the API Key, you can switch back to OneClick and use the Update API Keys button, which will automatically sync your 2Captcha API Key from the AutoSolve dashboard to OneClick.  
    ![OneClick_Integration.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360059381213/OneClick_Integration.png)

With these steps complete and funds in your 2Captcha account, you can begin to use their service to solve Captchas through AutoSolve. You can use the links below to visit their website or learn more about 2Captcha.

[2Captcha Website](https://2captcha.com/)
 | [2Captcha For Customer](https://2captcha.com/for-customer)
 

### AntiCaptcha AutoSolve Setup:

AntiCaptcha is a service that will solve the Captcha's you receive for you. You can learn more about them on their website [Anti-Captcha.com](https://anti-captcha.com/mainpage)
. If you'd like to learn how to create an account and utilize their service, you can use the following steps to do so:

1.  To begin, you will first need an AntiCaptcha account. If you do not already have one, you can register for one [here](https://anti-captcha.com/clients/entrance/register)
    .  
    ![mceclip0.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360059381853/mceclip0.png)
2.  Go to your email inbox, after opening the email you will receive your login, as well as a temporary password that will allow you to log in to your account. Alongside that, you will receive your API Key/Secret Key. Keep in mind that you will only be able to use it once you add funds to your AntiCaptcha account.  
    ![email_copy.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360058478214/email_copy.png)
3.  To activate your Anti-Captcha API Key, you will need to add funds to your account. Using the Username and Password that you were given login to your account. After you are logged into your account, you can use the add funds button in order to add funds to your account and activate your API Key.   
    ![Add_Funds.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360059382053/Add_Funds.png)
4.  Next, you will need to open the AYCD Toolbox and Launch OneClick. In OneClick, go to the AutoSolve tab and click Go To Dashboard.
5.  From the dashboard under **Third Party Solver API Keys**, click on Add API Key.  
    ![Add_3rd_API_Key.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360103557233/Add_3rd_API_Key.png)
6.  Select AntiCaptcha from the Service dropdown set an easy to identify nickname for your API Key, for example, "AntiCaptcha - Stealthy". Then paste your API Key from the AntiCaptcha Email into the API Key field and click Save.  
    ![AC.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360101394234/AC.png)
7.  After you have added the API Key, you can switch back to OneClick and use the Update API Keys button, which will automatically sync your AntiCaptcha API Key from the AutoSolve dashboard to OneClick.  
    ![OneClick_Integration.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360058475874/OneClick_Integration.png)

With these steps completed and funds in your AntiCaptcha account, which is required in order to activate your API Key, you can begin using their service. You can use the links below to visit their website or to learn more about AntiCaptcha. 

[AntiCaptcha Website](https://anti-captcha.com/mainpage)
 | [Customer Login](https://anti-captcha.com/clients/entrance/login)

### CapMonster AutoSolve Setup:

CapMonster is an automatic captcha recognition cloud service. Unlike some other Solvering providers, CapMonster solves captcha using a system. To learn more about their service, you can visit their website [CapMonster.cloud](https://capmonster.cloud/)
. If you'd like to learn how to create an account with them and use their service can use the following steps to do so:

1.  To begin, you will first need a CapMonster account. If you do not already have one, you can register for one [here](https://capmonster.cloud/Account/Register)
    .
2.  Once you enter your email address to register, they will send you an email with your username and password. In that email, you will be able to confirm your login credentials on the Zennolab website, where you can manage your profile information such as your password. In that email, you will also see a link to login to CapMonster.  
    ![Email.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360059383233/Email.png)
3.  Once you login to CapMonster, you will see a place to add funds to your account, again this is something you will need to do in order to utilize their service.   
    ![add_funds.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360058478194/add_funds.png)
4.  Once you add funds, you can copy the API Key from their dashboard as well so that you can add it to AutoSolve and begin using it.  
    ![copy_key.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360059383273/copy_key.png)
5.  Next, you will need to open the AYCD Toolbox and Launch OneClick. In OneClick, go to the AutoSolve tab and click Go To Dashboard.
6.  From the dashboard under **Third Party Solver API Keys**, click on Add API Key.  
    ![Add_3rd_API_Key.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360101394294/Add_3rd_API_Key.png)
7.  Select CapMonster from the Service dropdown set an easy to identify nickname for your API Key, for example, "CapMonster - Stealthy". Then paste your API Key from the CapMonster Email into the API Key field and click Save.  
    ![CM.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500002507582/CM.png)
8.  After you have added the API Key, you can switch back to OneClick and use the Update API Keys button, which will automatically sync your CapMonster API Key from the AutoSolve dashboard to OneClick.  
    ![OneClick_Integration.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360058478294/OneClick_Integration.png)

With these steps completed and funds added to your CapMonster account, you can begin using their service. You can use the links below to visit their website or to learn more about CapMonster.

[CapMonster Website](https://capmonster.cloud/)
 | [CapMonster Dashboard](https://capmonster.cloud/Dashboard)

### Best Captcha Solver AutoSolve Setup:

Best Captcha Solver is a Captcha Solver solves the captchas for you. It works on all types of captchas including ReCaptcha (V2, V3 and Invisible), GeeTest, Captcha and Image-based Captchas. To learn more about their service, you can visit their website [BestCaptchaSolver.com](https://bestcaptchasolver.com/)

1.  To begin, you will first need to create a BestCaptchaSolver account. You can register for an account if you do not already have one [here](https://bestcaptchasolver.com/register)
    .
2.  Once you register your account, you will need to confirm your account with an email they will send you.![reg.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360068385954/reg.png)
3.  After confirming your account, you will be on the Best Captcha Solver Dashboard. To use the Best Captcha Solver service, you will need to add funds to your account. You can do this by clicking on the + button. ![add.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360068385974/add.png)
4.  Either before or after you deposit funds into your account, you can copy your Best Captcha Solver Access Token to your clipboard, as you will need to put it in the AutoSolve Dashboard.![access.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360068386074/access.png)
5.  Next, you will need to open the AYCD Toolbox and Launch OneClick. In OneClick, go to the AutoSolve tab and click Go To Dashboard.
6.  From the dashboard under **Third Party Solver API Keys**, click on Add API Key.  
    ![Add_3rd_API_Key.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360101394374/Add_3rd_API_Key.png)
7.  Select Best Captcha Solver from the Service dropdown set an easy to identify nickname for your API Key, for example, "Best Captcha Solver - Crewie". Then paste your API Key from the Best Captcha Solver website into the API Key field and click Save.  
    ![BCS.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360101394454/BCS.png)
8.  After you have added the API Key, you can switch back to OneClick and use the Update API Keys button, which will automatically sync your Best Captcha Solver Access Token from the AutoSolve dashboard to OneClick.![oneclic.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360068386154/oneclic.png)

With these steps complete and funds in your Best Captcha Solver account, you can begin to use their service to solve Captchas through AutoSolve. You can use the links below to visit their website or learn more about Best Captcha Solver.

[Best Captcha Website](https://bestcaptchasolver.com/)

# Supported Bots - Polaris – AYCD

Supported Bots - Polaris

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360045141414-Supported-Bots-Polaris/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

March 26, 2020 03:52

**AutoSolve** is the latest tool by AYCD, allowing you to connect **OneClick** with supported **bots** to make OneClick a **default Captcha Solver**. By connecting bots to OneClick, it becomes your Captcha solving hub - allowing for captcha challenges to be sent to OneClick and optionally to supported 3rd party solvers like 2Captcha, AntiCaptcha and CapMonster.

### Polaris AutoSolve Setup:

Before proceeding with the setup for Polaris and AutoSolve it is **required** that you have read the [**AutoSolve - Getting Started Guide**](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
. Once you have read through the Getting Started Guide and you have AutoSolve is enabled you can proceed with the setup using the steps below.

1.  Launch OneClick and go to the AutoSolve menu option. From here you can use the Go To Dashboard button, or you can go to the website [dashboard](https://aycd.io/account)
     and access your AutoSolve Dashboard.  
    ![Go_To_Dashboard.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360063462533/Go_To_Dashboard.png)
2.  Next from the dashboard, you will need to create an **AutoSolve Bot API Key** for Polaris, remember that you will need a different one for each bot. When selecting a name it is best to use the name of the bot so that you can easily identify it.![CreateAPIKeyPolaris.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360063462713/CreateAPIKeyPolaris.png)
3.  Once you have created your API Key for Polaris, use the copy button and it will copy your Bot API Key to your clipboard.
4.  If you don't already have Polaris open, open it and go to settings.  
    ![PolarisSettings.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360062575874/PolarisSettings.png)
5.  In Settings, there are two AYCD AutoSolve boxes. Here you will be able to find the **AYCD AutoSolve Key** which corresponds to the API Key you just created for Polaris, all you need to do is paste the code you copied into there. ![APIKeyPolaris.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360063476573/APIKeyPolaris.png)
6.  To get your **AYCD AutoSolve Access Token**, go back to the dashboard, and press copy under the AutoSolve Access Token, then you can paste that token into Polaris under the **AYCD AutoSolve Access Token** field.![CopyAccessToken.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360062588914/CopyAccessToken.png)![PolarisAccessToken.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360063477293/PolarisAccessToken.png)
7.  After you have added both your Access token and Bot API Key click on Save.![PolarisSaveSettings.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360062589174/PolarisSaveSettings.png)

At this point, the setup portion for Polaris and AutoSolve has been completed.

### Running Polaris with AutoSolve:

Create your Polaris tasks, once you have completed the above steps in settings there is nothing specific you need to do in order to use AutoSolve.

In OneClick there is some setup required dependent on if you wish to use your OneClick Accounts:

1.  Launch OneClick and go to the Accounts menu option. Right-click an account and Start AutoSolve, or click on Options > Start AutoSolve > All, to start up all your accounts as Solvers within the category.![Start_AutoSolve.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360062593574/Start_AutoSolve.png)
2.  Upon trying to start an account, you will be asked whether to skip Login Verification and if you wish to warm up the browser before becoming a Solver. The Automatic Google Account Login Verification verifies whether the account is logged into the browser. The Warm Up Browser With Some Activity warms up the browser by performing some activities in the browser before becoming the Solver, which includes watching YouTube and Google Searches. Click the Start button when you are ready to start your Solvers!![2021-06-08_23_47_09-Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/4402361759639/2021-06-08_23_47_09-Window.png)
3.  Then when you run your Polaris tasks when there is a task that requires a captcha it will request it from your accounts in OneClick.

# Supported Bots - EveAIOX – AYCD

Supported Bots - EveAIOX

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360045744313-Supported-Bots-EveAIOX/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

March 27, 2020 00:19

**AutoSolve** is the latest tool by AYCD, allowing you to connect **OneClick** with supported **bots** to make OneClick a **default Captcha Solver**. By connecting bots to OneClick, it becomes your Captcha solving hub - allowing for captcha challenges to be sent to OneClick and optionally to supported 3rd party solvers like 2Captcha, AntiCaptcha and CapMonster.

### EveAIOX AutoSolve Setup:

Before proceeding with the setup for EveAIOX and AutoSolve it is **required** that you have read the [**AutoSolve - Getting Started Guide**](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
. Once you have read through the Getting Started Guide and you have AutoSolve is enabled you can proceed with the setup using the steps below.

1.  Launch OneClick and go to the AutoSolve menu option. From here you can use the Go To Dashboard button, or you can go to the website [dashboard](https://www.aycd.io/account)
     and access your AutoSolve Dashboard.  
    ![Go_To_Dashboard.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360063675153/Go_To_Dashboard.png)
2.  Next from the dashboard, you will need to create an **AutoSolve Bot API Key** for EveAIOX, remember that you will need a different one for each bot. When selecting a name it is best to use the name of the bot so that you can easily identify it.  
    ![CreateAPIKeyEVE.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360063675233/CreateAPIKeyEVE.png)
3.  Once you have created your API Key for EveAIOX, use the copy button and it will copy your Bot API Key to your clipboard.
4.  If you don't already have EveAIOX open, open it and go to Captcha.  
    ![EveCaptchaPortal.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360063675913/EveCaptchaPortal.png)
5.  In Captcha, there is an AutoSolve section. Here is where you will be able to add the API key you just created for EveAIOX, all you need to do is paste the code you copied previously.  
    ![BotAIOKeyEVEAIOX.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360063676613/BotAIOKeyEVEAIOX.png)
6.  To get your AYCD Access Token, go back to the dashboard, and press copy under the AutoSolve Access Token, then you can paste that token into EveAIOX under the AYCD Access Token field.  
    ![CopyAccessToken.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360062789074/CopyAccessToken.png)![AccessTokenEVE.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360063677333/AccessTokenEVE.png)

After you have added both your Access token and Bot API Key EveAIOX will autosave the values and at this point, the setup portion for EveAIOX and AutoSolve has been completed.

### Running EveAIOX with AutoSolve:

In order to use AutoSolve, you will need to ensure that when creating your tasks you configure the Advanced Setting for the task. 

**TASK Specific Configuration:**

1.  Configure your task as you normally would for EveAIOX but before creating or saving the tasks ensure you go over to the  **Advanced** option.   
    ![advanced_none.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360062792914/advanced_none.png)
2.  Under the **Advanced Captcha Settings,** there are some things you will need to configure. You will first need to click on the Advanced Captcha Settings, then put a **1** inside the AutoSolve Box.  
    ![advanced_filled.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360062792874/advanced_filled.png)
3.  After you have configured the **Advanced Captcha Settings** and the rest of your task you can save and create them.

Before starting your tasks you will want to Configure OneClick and AutoSolve. In OneClick there is some setup required dependent on if you wish to use your OneClick Accounts:

1.  Launch OneClick and go to the Accounts menu option. Right-click an account and Start AutoSolve, or click on Options > Start AutoSolve > All, to start up all your accounts as Solvers within the category.  
    ![Start_AutoSolve.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360062793374/Start_AutoSolve.png)
2.  Upon trying to start an account, you will be asked whether to skip Login Verification and if you wish to warm up the browser before becoming a Solver. The Automatic Google Account Login Verification verifies whether the account is logged into the browser. The Warm Up Browser With Some Activity warms up the browser by performing some activities in the browser before becoming the Solver, which includes watching YouTube and Google Searches. Click the Start button when you are ready to start your Solvers!![2021-06-08_23_47_09-Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/4402361411863/2021-06-08_23_47_09-Window.png)
3.  Then when you run your Eve tasks when there is a task that requires a captcha it will request it from your accounts in OneClick.

# AYCDToolbox Always Asks me to Update, I Can't Update the AYCDToolbox – AYCD

AYCDToolbox Always Asks me to Update, I Can't Update the AYCDToolbox

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043831854-AYCDToolbox-Always-Asks-me-to-Update-I-Can-t-Update-the-AYCDToolbox/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

March 01, 2020 07:35

If you are having issues updating the Toolbox, you may see symptoms such as:

*   Not being able to update applications, such as Spoof, the Billing Converter, ServerGen or being unable to start them.
*   Always being asked to update the Toolbox when you launch it.

If you are experiencing these symptoms it is likely due to being on an older version of the Toolbox that had some auto-updating issues. An example of this may be if your Toolbox is on the old design instead of the new one as illustrated in the image below:

![Outdated_Toolbox.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360059974773/Outdated_Toolbox.png)

### Download AYCDToolbox Again

Since the auto-update function does not work on the previous version you will need to download the latest version of the Toolbox from the account [dashboard](https://aycd.io/account)
. To learn more about how to download the Toolbox you can visit [this guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360053313853)
. Once you download the latest update to your Toolbox it will look similar to the screenshot below.

![24.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360102340553/24.png)

# AutoSolve - FAQ – AYCD

AutoSolve - FAQ

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360046527613-AutoSolve-FAQ/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

April 09, 2020 00:42

Below we have added some Frequently Asked Questions for AutoSolve. Since it is a new service there will be more and more added to it overtime.

**Q: What is AutoSolve?**

*   AutoSolve is a service provided by AYCD that allows you to configure [supported bots](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043237394)
     to send their Captcha Token requests to OneClick. In OneClick you can route them to your accounts or to third party services. AutoSolve is not an Automatic Solving Service, but a Captcha Hub that allows you to direct requests.

**Q: What are the benefits of AutoSolve?**

*   AutoSolve has several benefits, depending on your use case. First, it allows you to request Captcha tokens in the same browser you have been farming your Google Accounts. This means that your trust does not diminish as much as you are not transferring your Google Account to a new Browser. Another benefit of AutoSolve is being able to have Multiple bots request captchas from the same location. This means that if you have multiple bots, you do not need to have different solvers open, you can concentrate on one place. It also allows you to use the 3rd Party Solver services we have added.

**Q: Can I run multiple Bots at the same time with AutoSolve?** 

*   Yes you can connect multiple bots by providing each with a different Bot API Key. It is important that each Bot you have setup with AutoSolve uses a different API Key.

 **Q: Can I have multiple instances of OneClick open with AutoSolve Enabled?** 

*   Yes, it will evenly split the requests between all open and running instances of OneClick through a technique called Round Robin. What this means is it will split the requests one at a time evenly between all open and running copies of OneClick with Solvers.

**Q: How many tasks can I run in my bot per Gmail?**

*    There is no set amount of tasks you are limited to run. It is good to stay in the range of 10-20 per Gmail but you can run as many as you'd like as once you run out of one-clicks the solvers will show up one at a time with the Captchas for you to solve and send back to your bots.

**Q: My AutoSolve is Grayed out. What do I do?**

*   If you have enabled AutoSolve in settings, and its status still shows as grayed out, that means you do not have an active subscription to AutoSolve. 

**Q: Does AutoSolve Solve Captcha Puzzles for Me?**

*   No, AutoSolve cannot solve captcha puzzles for you. It forwards the requests for your bot to your browsers in OneClick. Hopefully, it receives a one-click, but in the event, you have untrusted accounts, or in the event of Shopify Checkpoint, it will have you solve the Captcha puzzle to solve.

**Q: Does AutoSolve work with Checkpoint Captchas?**

*   No, currently the Checkpoint Solver is disabled, it is best to use the Checkpoint Solver in your bot at this time.

**Q: Do I need to have a Toolbox Subscription in order to use AutoSolve?**

*   Yes. Since AutoSolve requests go through OneClick without a Toolbox subscription you will be unable to use AutoSolve.

**Q: Does Using AutoSolve Guarantee me a one-click?**

*   No it does not. AutoSolve forwards requests from your bot to your accounts in OneClick and will request the token there. 

**Q: Do I need to have Solvers open in my bot and OneClick to use AutoSolve?**

*   No, you should not solve captcha requests in your bot and close any solvers that it opens as it can mess with the tokens being sent back from OneClick. You should be using the Solvers in OneClick.

**Q: Can I run my Bot on one computer, and run AYCD in another?**

*   Yes, AutoSolve does not need to be installed on the same machine as your bot and can be used separately. There will be a slight bit of latency between the devices, but it should be very minimal.

**Q: I can't show my browsers for AutoSolve, are they all show up along the bottom?**

*   You do not need to show your browsers. As long as AutoSolve is running the Solvers will only show themselves one at a time if there is a manual solve required. The only Solver that will show up automatically is a Shopify Checkpoint Solver. But keep in mind you should not be trying to show the Normal Solvers, they will show on their own when required. 

**Q: How soon before a drop should I start AutoSolve?**

*   You can start up AutoSolve 5-10 minutes before the drop. You just need to ensure you have enough time for all the Solvers to startup which will not be too long depending on the number of solvers you are starting.
