# Supported Bots - ESnkrs – AYCD

Supported Bots - ESnkrs

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360046477534-Supported-Bots-ESnkrs/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/1500080569202/crw1.png)

**Crewie**

April 20, 2020 11:23

**AutoSolve** is the latest tool by AYCD, allowing you to connect **OneClick** with supported **bots** to make OneClick a **default Captcha Solver**. By connecting bots to OneClick, it becomes your Captcha solving hub - allowing for captcha challenges to be sent to OneClick and optionally to supported 3rd party solvers like 2Captcha, AntiCaptcha and CapMonster.

### ESnkrs AutoSolve Setup:

Before proceeding with the setup for ESnkrs and AutoSolve it is **required** that you have read the [**AutoSolve - Getting Started Guide**](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
. Once you have read through the Getting Started Guide and you have AutoSolve is enabled you can proceed with the setup using the steps below.

1.  Launch OneClick and go to the AutoSolve menu option. From here you can use the Go To Dashboard button, or you can go to the website [dashboard](https://www.aycd.io/account)
     and access your AutoSolve Dashboard.![Go_To_Dashboard.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360067633613/Go_To_Dashboard.png)
2.  From your dashboard get your AYCD Access Token by pressing copy under the AutoSolve Access Token, then you can paste that token into ESnkrs in the AutoSolve field.  
    ![CopyAccessToken.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360067633633/CopyAccessToken.png)![access.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360066587114/access.png)
3.  After you have added in your Access Token put a colon after your access token  **:![token_2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360067633913/token_2.png)**
4.  Next from the dashboard, you will need to create an **AutoSolve Bot API Key** for ESnkrs, remember that you will need a different one for each bot. When selecting a name it is best to use the name of the bot so that you can easily identify it. ![api.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360067634053/api.png)
5.  Once you have created your API Key for ESnkrs, use the copy button and it will copy your Bot API Key to your clipboard.
6.  Next to your Access Key and colon, you will need to paste your API Key. So it should look like below with both Keys Entered.![2020-04-20_12_13_45-Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360067634093/2020-04-20_12_13_45-Window.png)
7.  After pasting the API Token, select AYCD from the captcha methods dropdown and click Check AYCD.![step6.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360067634453/step6.png)
8.  If you have entered the information correctly you should see AutoSolve authentication successful!  
    ![success.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360066592074/success.png)

### Running ESnkrs with AutoSolve:

Create your ESnkrs tasks, once you have completed the above steps in settings there is nothing specific you need to do in order to use AutoSolve.

1.  Launch OneClick and go to the Accounts menu option. Right-click an account and Start AutoSolve, or click on Options > Start AutoSolve > All, to start up all your accounts as Solvers within the category.![Start_AutoSolve.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360066593234/Start_AutoSolve.png)
2.  Upon trying to start an account, you will be asked whether to skip Login Verification and if you wish to warm up the browser before becoming a Solver. The Automatic Google Account Login Verification verifies whether the account is logged into the browser. The Warm Up Browser With Some Activity warms up the browser by performing some activities in the browser before becoming the Solver, which includes watching YouTube and Google Searches. Click the Start button when you are ready to start your Solvers!![2021-06-08_23_47_09-Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/4402361397655/2021-06-08_23_47_09-Window.png)
3.  Then when you run your ESnkrs tasks when there is a task that requires a captcha it will request it from your accounts in OneClick.

# Supported Bots - Wrath – AYCD

Supported Bots - Wrath

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360046649954-Supported-Bots-Wrath/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



============================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/1500080569202/crw1.png)

**Crewie**

April 22, 2020 22:02

**AutoSolve** is the latest tool by AYCD, allowing you to connect **OneClick** with supported **bots** to make OneClick a **default Captcha Solver**. By connecting bots to OneClick, it becomes your Captcha solving hub - allowing for captcha challenges to be sent to OneClick and optionally to supported 3rd party solvers like 2Captcha, AntiCaptcha and CapMonster.

### Wrath AutoSolve Setup:

Before proceeding with the setup for Wrath and AutoSolve it is **required** that you have read the [**AutoSolve - Getting Started Guide**](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
. Once you have read through the Getting Started Guide and you have AutoSolve is enabled you can proceed with the setup using the steps below.

1.  Launch OneClick and go to the AutoSolve menu option. From here you can use the Go To Dashboard button, or you can go to the website [dashboard](https://www.aycd.io/account)
     and access your AutoSolve Dashboard.![Start_AutoSolve.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360067025734/Start_AutoSolve.png)
2.  From your dashboard get your AYCD Access Token by pressing copy under the AutoSolve Access Token, then you can paste that token into Wrath in the AutoSolve field.  
    ![CopyAccessToken.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360068101833/CopyAccessToken.png)![wrath_acc.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360068274213/wrath_acc.png)
3.  Next from the dashboard, you will need to create an **AutoSolve Bot API Key** for Wrath, remember that you will need a different one for each bot. When selecting a name it is best to use the name of the bot so that you can easily identify it.![api_wrath.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360067025894/api_wrath.png)
4.  Once you have created your API Key for Wrath, use the copy button and it will copy your Bot API Key to your clipboard. Then paste the API key into the API Key field. ![wrath_api.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360068274373/wrath_api.png)
5.  Finally, click Save to save the AutoSolve Settings.![wrath_api.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360068274453/wrath_api.png)

### Running Wrath with AutoSolve:

Create your Wrath tasks, once you have completed the above steps in settings there is nothing specific you need to do in order to use AutoSolve.

1.  Launch OneClick and go to the Accounts menu option. Right-click an account and Start AutoSolve, or click on Options > Start AutoSolve > All, to start up all your accounts as Solvers within the category.![Start_AutoSolve.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360067025994/Start_AutoSolve.png)
2.  Upon trying to start an account, you will be asked whether to skip Login Verification and if you wish to warm up the browser before becoming a Solver. The Automatic Google Account Login Verification verifies whether the account is logged into the browser. The Warm Up Browser With Some Activity warms up the browser by performing some activities in the browser before becoming the Solver, which includes watching YouTube and Google Searches. Click the Start button when you are ready to start your Solvers!![2021-06-08_23_47_09-Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/4402368175767/2021-06-08_23_47_09-Window.png)
3.  Then when you run your Wrath tasks when there is a task that requires a captcha it will request it from your accounts in OneClick.

# OneClick - Getting Started Guide – AYCD

OneClick - Getting Started Guide

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173-OneClick-Getting-Started-Guide/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

November 06, 2020 02:29

Introduction
------------

OneClick is the most powerful tool that allows you to generate activity and farm on your Google accounts while giving you access to the most powerful suite of tools to ensure managing your accounts is effortless. Use tools like Setting up Email Forwarding, Change Passwords, SMS Verify your Accounts, and more. You can import your accounts into OneClick, Assign Proxies, and Start Farming, then let OneClick's automated scheduling system run and sleep your accounts, creating a truly effortless experience.

[Tasks](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173-OneClick-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01EPF9GDH09WYE0W0JK8DVK4C9)
 | [Account Tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173-OneClick-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01H1MWREXR97AA0HR998NPZ0YK)
 | [AutoSolve](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173-OneClick-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01EPFDX16PWGRM9PFA1TWBTAEH)

Tasks
-----

Whether you are managing your accounts with our Account Tools, creating solvers, farming them to build trust, utilizing our local SMS and inbox features, or using AutoSolve to automate solving captchas in other programs, this is where that all happens.

[Adding Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173-OneClick-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01EPF75YH4B6PB4WVSN5P7NJ10)
 | [Adding Solvers](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173-OneClick-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01EPF75NHPB9J6QPDYHVFM1AYT)
 | [Assign Proxies](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173-OneClick-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01EPF75NHPB9J6QPDYHVFM1AYT)
 | [Farming Accounts/Solvers](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173-OneClick-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01EPF98C6ZHC5FQV9B4M6NGFM7)

#### Adding Accounts to OneClick

You can add accounts either individually or in bulk. If you have multiple accounts to add, we recommend using the Import feature.

1\. Navigate over to the Accounts/Solvers section of Profile Builder in Tasks. This section has the play button icon titled **Accounts/Solvers** when you hover over it.

2\. Click on the File button at the top left of the view, then click Add Account.

3\. Begin adding your account information, starting with a Category. By default, Accounts are created in the All Category. You can override this and specify a category name to add your accounts to a unique category.

4\. If you want to add any notes to be stored with the account, you can include them here.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/21365759036823)

5\. Enter the Email and Password for your account.

6\. If your account has either a Security Answer or a Recovery Email, enter that so that OneClick can automate the login process for the account. If your account has neither measure implemented, leave the fields blank.

7\. Ifyour account hase 2-Factor Authentication(2FA) setut, enter the 2FA secret here. If you don't have 2FA but want to learn how to add it to your account using Account Tools after you have added an account to OneClick, you can learn more about that [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13361546549527)
.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/21365759040663)

8\. Specify the Browser Type to use in the **Browser Type** dropdown. You can choose **AYCD Browser** or **Chrome Browser**. You can see the supported captcha and modes in the **Supported Features** section.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/21365775253527)

9\. You can view your Account Action History in the **History** section. This is where important events that happen during farming or when using tools are logged to your accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/21365759042839)

10\. Optionally, you can adjust specific settings in the **Advanced** section.

*   You can enable **Data Saver** mode to save data when using metered proxies.
*   You can enable **Custom Schedule** if you want to use a custom farming schedule. (Not recommended) 
*   You can enable **Advanced Settings** to customize which farming scripts are run on your tasks.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/21365759045271)

#### 11\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Create** button.

#### Adding Accounts to OneClick (Bulk)

You can make a copy of the [OneClick CSV Import](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1kmdBjsBFDY2zHZvxMBnGI1-V47SMdRZwJVMMNigB2w8/copy)
 format for accounts in OneClick.

1\. Navigate over to the Accounts/Solvers section of Profile Builder in Tasks. This section has the play button icon titled **Accounts/Solvers** when you hover over it.

2\. Click on the File button at the top left of the view, then click Import. This will open the Import Accounts dialog.

3\. Click on the icon to the right of the dialog that looks like a piece of paper. Use your system's file explorer to select the file location for the CSV import format.

3\. Click on Import, and OneClick will add the accounts using the CSV you provided.

![TasksImportAccounts.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16797871767063)

Optionally you can import them using the Copy and Paste format. The only required parts to adding the account are the Email and Password; the other parts are optional.

Email:::password  
Email:::password:::Proxy  
Email:::password:::SecurityAnswerOrRecoveryEmail  
Email:::password:::SecurityAnswerOrRecoveryEmail:::Proxy

![TasksImportCopyPasteAccounts.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16797856328599)

#### Adding Solvers

Solvers are browsers without an account attached. They are primarily used for AutoSolve, but can also be used to farm and create trusted browser sessions. They can be great while used in tandem with accounts.

1\. Navigate over to the Accounts/Solvers section of Profile Builder in Tasks. This section has the play button icon titled Accounts/Solvers when you hover over it.

2\. Click on the File button at the top left of the view, then click Add Solver.

3\. Begin adding information, starting with a Category. By default, Accounts are created in the All Category. You can override this and specify a category name to add your accounts to a unique category.

4\. Enter a name. Each solver will use this incremented with an index. 

5\. Enter a quantity of solvers to create. 

6\. Specify which **Proxy Categories** you want to use for the solvers.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/21365870150039)

7\. Specify the Browser Type to use in the **Browser Type** dropdown. You can choose **AYCD Browser** or **Chrome Browser**. You can see the supported captcha and modes in the **Supported Features** section.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/21365870150807)

8\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Create** button.

#### Assigning Proxies

Having a proxy assigned is crucial for running your accounts in OneClick and building up trust in your accounts. To assign proxies to your account, you need to add them to OneClick first. You can add proxies under the Proxies view, File > New. [Learn more about Adding Proxies to OneClick](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039319653)
.

1\. From the Accounts view, select the accounts you want to assign a proxy to from the Table.

2\. After you have selected accounts, click the Options button, then the Assign Proxies button.

3\. Use the dropdown to select the category of proxies you want to use. You can use the checkbox below to prevent OneClick from using proxies assigned to accounts you have not selected.

4\. Click Assign when you are ready.![1.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6555758582679)

5\. If you want to assign a specific proxy to an account, this can be done individually. You can use the right-click menu under Proxy > Set Proxy. You can also do this by double-clicking on the proxy column.  
![2.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6555750434839)

#### Farming Accounts/Solvers

After assigning proxies to your accounts, you can start running them and building trust. OneClick has a lot of automation and intelligence built into it. OneClick automatically determines how many accounts should run simultaneously based on your computer specifications. Additionally, OneClick determines when the accounts should run, when to take a break, and then automatically start them again after the break time has finished. This intelligence also allows OneClick to schedule accounts when running more accounts than your computer can safely run at once.

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of OneClick.

2\. Select the accounts you want to start farming on from the Accounts Table.

3\. Click on Options in the top right, then click Start Farming. Optionally you can start farming on accounts individually by clicking the Start button that looks like a play button icon from the actions column.  
  
![3.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6555753405463)

When you are running your accounts, there are some possible statuses that you could see.

**Scheduled:** This status will show on an account scheduled to run. When an available running slot has opened up for an account, one of the scheduled accounts will begin running. OneClick will schedule an account if you are trying to run more accounts than your computer can run simultaneously or if you are trying to run more than 15 at the same time.

**Taking a Break:** This status will show after an account has run for a set time. It is normal to see this status after an account is running, and you do not need to stop and start the account as it will start again after a preset amount of time. Accounts will take breaks for 3-8hrs before trying to run again.

![mceclip1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360096632174)

[Click here to read more in-depth on Running Accounts, Scheduling & Taking a Break](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039319713)

Account Tools
-------------

OneClick has several Account Tools to help you manage your account once they're in OneClick. We have tools that automate actions like [Enabling 2FA](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13361546549527)
, [Setting up Mail Forwarding](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041171674)
, [Changing the Recovery Email](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13255947807383)
, [Changing Account Passwords](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13255371831191)
, and [more](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/sections/360006095753)
.

AutoSolve
---------

AutoSolve is used within OneClick but requires a separate subscription to enable and use it. Using it allows you to connect OneClick with supported bots in order to make OneClick your default Captcha Solver. Harnassing the power of your trusted accounts right from OneClick makes OneClick and AutoSolve a crucial combination. You can read more about the AutoSolve service by [clicking here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
.

# Supported Bots - SwiftSole – AYCD

Supported Bots - SwiftSole

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360046435074-Supported-Bots-SwiftSole/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/1500080569202/crw1.png)

**Crewie**

April 19, 2020 11:52

**AutoSolve** is the latest tool by AYCD, allowing you to connect **OneClick** with supported **bots** to make OneClick a **default Captcha Solver**. By connecting bots to OneClick, it becomes your Captcha solving hub - allowing for captcha challenges to be sent to OneClick and optionally to supported 3rd party solvers like 2Captcha, AntiCaptcha and CapMonster.

### SwiftSole AutoSolve Setup:

Before proceeding with the setup for SwiftSole and AutoSolve it is **required** that you have read the [**AutoSolve - Getting Started Guide**](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
. Once you have read through the Getting Started Guide and you have AutoSolve is enabled you can proceed with the setup using the steps below.

1.  Launch OneClick and go to the AutoSolve menu option. From here you can use the Go To Dashboard button, or you can go to the website [dashboard](https://www.aycd.io/account)
     and access your AutoSolve Dashboard.![Go_To_Dashboard.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360066492914/Go_To_Dashboard.png)
2.  From your dashboard get your AYCD Access Token by pressing copy under the AutoSolve Access Token.![CopyAccessToken.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360066492934/CopyAccessToken.png)
3.  When you have copied the Access Token open SwiftSole and navigate to the in-app Settings, scroll down and paste the Access Token into the Access Token field. You may have to hold your finger down for a couple of seconds to make the Paste prompt appear.  
    ![Access_Token.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360095827494/Access_Token.png)
4.  Next from the dashboard, you will need to create an **AutoSolve Bot API Key** for SwiftSole, remember that you will need a different one for each bot. When selecting a name it is best to use the name of the bot so that you can easily identify it.  
    ![Save_API.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360098117013/Save_API.png)
5.  Once you have created your API Key for SwiftSole, use the copy button and it will copy your Bot API Key to your clipboard then paste the API Key into the API Key field.   
    ![API_Key.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360098122033/API_Key.png)
6.  Now that you have added your AutoSolve credentials tap Enable AYCD to connect AutoSolve and SwiftSole. You will receive a Success popup if AutoSolve has been set up correctly.  
    ![Enabled_AutoSolve.PNG](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360095823734/Enabled_AutoSolve.PNG)

After you have added both your Access token and Bot API Key you have successfully set up AYCD AutoSolve. Should you wish to change the credentials you must disable AutoSolve before you are able to alter the credentials.

### Running SwiftSole with AutoSolve:

In order to use AutoSolve, you will need to ensure that when creating your tasks you configure the Captcha Mode for the task. 

**TASK Specific Configuration:**

1.  Configure your task as you normally would for SwiftSole but before creating or saving the tasks ensure you go over to the Captcha Mode option.  
    ![Captcha_Mode_1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360095826294/Captcha_Mode_1.png)
2.  Tap on the Captcha Harvester to expand the options, then select AYCD.  
    ![Captcha_Mode_2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360098120873/Captcha_Mode_2.png)
3.  After you have configured the **Captcha Mode** and the rest of your task you can save and create them.

Before starting your tasks you will want to Configure OneClick and AutoSolve. In OneClick there is some setup required dependent on if you wish to use your OneClick Accounts:

1.  Launch OneClick and go to the Accounts menu option. Right-click an account and Start AutoSolve, or click on Options > Start AutoSolve > All, to start up all your accounts as Solvers within the category.![Start_AutoSolve.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360066492974/Start_AutoSolve.png)
2.  Upon trying to start an account, you will be asked whether to skip Login Verification and if you wish to warm up the browser before becoming a Solver. The Automatic Google Account Login Verification verifies whether the account is logged into the browser. The Warm Up Browser With Some Activity warms up the browser by performing some activities in the browser before becoming the Solver, which includes watching YouTube and Google Searches. Click the Start button when you are ready to start your Solvers!![2021-06-08_23_47_09-Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/4402362042007/2021-06-08_23_47_09-Window.png)
3.  Then when you run your SwiftSole tasks when there is a task that requires a captcha it will request it from your accounts in OneClick.

# Toolbox Applications - Settings - Backup / Restore / Reset – AYCD

Toolbox Applications - Settings - Backup / Restore / Reset

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500005980861-Toolbox-Applications-Settings-Backup-Restore-Reset/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



===============================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

April 05, 2021 19:11

Introduction
------------

The Toolbox applications all feature Backup and Restore options within the Settings. This allows you to create encrypted backups and quickly restore from an earlier point. Please note that all backups are encrypted and can be only accessed by the account that made them. All Toolbox applications include an auto backup feature.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can configure auto backups, which automatically back up the program based on time intervals you can configure.

Save Auto Backup
----------------

1\. Navigate to Settings > General in your Toolbox application. Click the **Save Auto Backup** button.

2\. The Toolbox Application will create the auto-backup. An auto backup backs up everything in the application. A dialog will appear confirming the auto backup was saved successfully.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25765443951767)

Save Backup
-----------

1\. Navigate to Settings > General in your Toolbox application. Click the **Save Backup** button.

2\. The **Backup Options** dialog will appear. You can select which sections of the application you want to back up.

3\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25765443953175)

4\. A dialog will appear confirming you successfully backed up the application. Your file explorer will automatically open the file location with the saved backup.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25765647673751)

Minimum Automatic Backup Interval
---------------------------------

1. Navigate to Settings > General in your Toolbox application. Click the **Minimum Automatic Backup Interval** dropdown menu.

2\. You can adjust how often the application's automatic backups occur. We recommend leaving this on default and only changing this setting if you are running low on storage space. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25765768232215)

Restore
-------

1\. Navigate to Settings > General in your Toolbox application. Click the **Restore** button.

2\. The **Restore** dialog will appear. You can select an auto-backup in the dialog or manually select a backup through File Explorer by clicking the blue spreadsheet icon at the bottom right of the dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25765443956375)

3\. If you choose an Auto-Backup, the Auto-Backup dialog will appear. You can select one of the timestamps and then click the **Restore** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25765443958039) 4. The **Restore** dialog will appear. You can select which sections of the program you want to restore. 

5\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Next** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25765632826519)

6\. A dialog will appear asking if you want to clear your existing data with the restore. 

*   If you want to clear your existing data, click the **Yes** button.
*   If you do not want to clear the data, click the **No** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25765632827927)

7\. A dialog will appear confirming the backup was imported successfully.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25765632828439)

Reset
-----

1\. Navigate to Settings > General in your Toolbox application. Click the **Reset** button.

2\. The **Reset** dialog will appear. You can select which sections of the program you want to reset. 

3\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Reset** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25765808350743)

4\. A confirmation dialog will appear. If you are sure you want to reset the selected sections of the program, click the **Yes** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25765776152343)

5\. A confirmation dialog will appear confirming the selected features have been reset to factory defaults.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25765784551191)

# Supported Bots - Whatbot – AYCD

Supported Bots - Whatbot

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500008556222-Supported-Bots-Whatbot/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/1500080569202/crw1.png)

**Crewie**

April 27, 2021 22:24

**AutoSolve** allows you to connect **OneClick** with supported **bots** to make OneClick a **default Captcha Solver**. By connecting bots to OneClick, it becomes your Captcha solving hub - allowing for captcha challenges to be sent to OneClick and optionally to supported 3rd party solvers like 2Captcha, AntiCaptcha, and CapMonster.

### Whatbot AutoSolve Setup:

Before proceeding with the setup for Whatbot and AutoSolve it is **required** that you have read the [**AutoSolve - Getting Started Guide**](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
. Once you have read through the Getting Started Guide and you have AutoSolve is enabled you can proceed with the setup using the steps below.

1.  Launch OneClick and go to the AutoSolve menu option. From here you can use the Go To Dashboard button, or you can go to the website [dashboard](https://www.aycd.io/account)
     and access your AutoSolve Dashboard.  
    ![Go_To_Dashboard.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500013098882/Go_To_Dashboard.png)
2.  From your dashboard get your AYCD Access Token by pressing copy under the AutoSolve Access Token.![Copy_Access_Token.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500010458061/Copy_Access_Token.png)
3.  In Whatbot, you will navigate under the Settings gear to the AYCD AutoSolve section to paste your Access Token.![mceclip0.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500013078341/mceclip0.png)![mceclip1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500012711922/mceclip1.png)![mceclip2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500013078381/mceclip2.png)
4.  Next from the dashboard, you will need to create an **AutoSolve Bot API Key** for Whatbot, remember that you will need a different one for each bot. When selecting a name it is best to use the name of the bot so that you can easily identify it.  
    ![mceclip3.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500012711962/mceclip3.png)
5.  Once you have created your API Key for Whatbot, use the copy button and it will copy your Bot API Key to your clipboard.  
    ![mceclip4.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500012711982/mceclip4.png)  
      
    
6.  After adding your Access Token and API Key, you can click Save in order to connect Whatbot to AutoSolve. We highly encourage that you run a test task afterwards to ensure everything is properly setup. ![mceclip0.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500013420341/mceclip0.png)![mceclip6.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500012712002/mceclip6.png)

### Running Whatbot with AutoSolve:

Create your Whatbot tasks, once you have completed the above steps in settings there is nothing specific you need to do in order to use AutoSolve.

1.  Launch OneClick and go to the Accounts menu option. Right-click an account and Start AutoSolve, or click on Options > Start AutoSolve > All, to start up all your accounts as Solvers within the category.  
    ![Start_AutoSolve.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500013098862/Start_AutoSolve.png)
2.  Upon trying to start an account, you will be asked whether to skip Login Verification and if you wish to warm up the browser before becoming a Solver. The Automatic Google Account Login Verification verifies whether the account is logged into the browser. The Warm Up Browser With Some Activity warms up the browser by performing some activities in the browser before becoming the Solver, which includes watching YouTube and Google Searches. Click the Start button when you are ready to start your Solvers!![2021-06-08_23_47_09-Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/4402375040023/2021-06-08_23_47_09-Window.png)
3.  Then when you run your Whatbot tasks when there is a task that requires a captcha it will request it from your accounts in OneClick.

*   [AutoSolve - Getting Started Guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide)

# My Toolbox is Showing White, I can't Login! – AYCD

My Toolbox is Showing White, I can't Login!

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500001440742-My-Toolbox-is-Showing-White-I-can-t-Login/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=======================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

January 09, 2021 08:16

During login to the AYCD Toolbox on older versions of it, such as 2.0.4, you will be shown a white screen and be unable to login. This is caused by changes made to the login process in the most recent updates. To resolve this, you will need to go to the [Website Dashboard](https://aycd.io/account)
 and download the latest version of the Toolbox, as you will be unable to download it automatically. You can check what version of the Toolbox you are on in the bottom left-hand corner when trying to login. To see an example of what this issue looks like, please see the screenshot below.

![mceclip0.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360100263214/mceclip0.png)

# Generator - Getting Started Guide – AYCD

Generator - Getting Started Guide

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060614453-Generator-Getting-Started-Guide/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

December 11, 2020 08:57

### What is Generator?

Generator is a tool provided by AYCD that allows you to generate Servers and Datacenter Proxies.

[Servers](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060614453-Generator-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01ESCHAF64MZWS44E0P4SN4KG5)
 | [Proxies](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060614453-Generator-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01ESCHANZAB3YEQWWSSXXG9XPT)
 | [Tips and Tricks](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060614453-Generator-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01ESDDGNXAY9GRJM58EA24R6CS)

Servers
-------

When starting Generator, the first tab is the Servers option. Under Servers, you will be able to generate servers, create Images of servers that you can create more servers or restore from, and lastly add and manage your provider credentials in order to create servers. _When using Generator for Server and Proxy generation, it is important that you regularly check the Account and Billing dashboard of the providers you use. This will help ensure that you do not leave instances running and are aware of all charges to your account._

[Adding Provider Credentials](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060614453-Generator-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01ESB34JMN4SGP1BG8F32VXVT0)
 | [Creating a Server](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060614453-Generator-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01ESB34Y2F5R3E7GDD6XD0XQGQ)
 | [Connecting to Servers](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060614453-Generator-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01ESB35D24BK94X8SZWJ2GFMJ3)
 | [Stopping and Destroying Servers](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060614453-Generator-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01ESB35Q9YVY513EENX9B2ET4Z)
 | [Creating and Managing Images](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060614453-Generator-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01ESB362C8KWB0397VJJVGNQ0E)

#### Adding Provider Credentials

While in the Credentials option, you can add providers for generating both servers and proxies, here it is important to note that the only providers that allow for generating servers currently are Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Google Cloud Platform (GCP). If you do not already have the information required to add a Provider, such as AWS or GCP, you can [click here to learn how to set up an account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553)
.

1.  To add a Provider, press the Create button.
2.  Select a Provider from the dropdown.
3.  Name the Credential so that you can easily differentiate and identify it.
4.  Add the required information for the Credential as each Provider will have slightly different information. 

![AddProviderCredential.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360099242834/AddProviderCredential.gif)

#### Creating a Server

Once you have added Credentials for either AWS or GCP, you can begin to use that to create instances. As we are in the server section, we will look at creating a server. When creating a server, we have options to create multiple servers at once or creating within multiple regions. We also have the ability to create servers using Images, set the Time Zone of the server, and preinstall things like Google Chrome or Discord. 

1.  Press File > Create this will bring up a dialog where you will be able to specify a name for the server, choose the provider and credentials, and other information to create the server. 
2.  Once you have finished configuring the settings for the server you can press the create button in order to start the creation process.  
3.  This will start the server's Initialization process where it will set up and begin to run.

![CreateServer.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000375601/CreateServer.gif)  
![GenerateServerStatic.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000371182/GenerateServerStatic.png)

#### Connecting to Servers

Once you have created and your server is setup you will see the Running status. This means that your server has finished initializing and is ready to be connected to. 

1.  In order to connect to a generated server, you need to have a server running.  
    ![ServerRunning.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360099242854/ServerRunning.png)
2.  From there you will click on the Blue Connection icon which will bring up the server credentials. After that, you can press the connect button to connect to the server using the Remote Desktop Connection software you have on your computer.  
    ![2020-12-12_02-09-56__1_.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000371102/2020-12-12_02-09-56__1_.gif)

You can also export the RDP files for servers in order to import them into Server Managers or move them to other devices. To do this go to File > Export.

#### Stopping and Destroying Servers

Once you have a server created you have options to stop a server on the Provider, allowing you to stop and eventually resume the server. When stopping Servers, it is important to remember that there are still costs associated with them. You can read about those costs here: [AWS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/Stop_Start.html)
 | [GCP](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/instances/stop-start-instance)
   
![2020-12-12_02-49-39__1_.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000375581/2020-12-12_02-49-39__1_.gif)![2020-12-12_02-51-56__1_.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000371122/2020-12-12_02-51-56__1_.gif)

In order to destroy a server, you will press the X icon or right-click an instance and select destroy.  
![2020-12-12_02-59-28__1_.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000371142/2020-12-12_02-59-28__1_.gif)

#### Creating and Managing Images

A new feature to Generator is the ability to create Server Images and to be able to create servers from those Images. Server Images create a copy of the server state, both the programs installed and files on the computer, allowing you to resume use of the server at a later date or create dedicated servers with your setup for drops. Keep in mind there is a small cost associated with storing Instance Images. You can view the Pricing for [GCP](https://cloud.google.com/compute/disks-image-pricing#imagestorage)
, and [AWS](https://aws.amazon.com/ebs/pricing/)
. In order to create an image of a server, you need to have a server running.

1.  Right Click on a Server and Press Create Instance Image.
2.  Name your Instance and then save the Instance. It will take some time to create, but the status will change to Available once it is done.  
    ![2020-12-12_03-30-51__1_.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360099242794/2020-12-12_03-30-51__1_.gif)

Once you have an Instance Image Available you can do things like creating copies for different regions, syncing the status, or deleting the images. ![2020-12-12_03-37-12__2_.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000371162/2020-12-12_03-37-12__2_.gif)

![2020-12-12_03-49-50__1_.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/360099242814/2020-12-12_03-49-50__1_.gif)

Proxies
-------

Proxies is the second tab within Generator. Under Proxies, you will be able to generate proxies, manage your provider credentials in order to create proxies, and lastly, add proxy lists into Generator for use when generating Cookies and Accounts. _When using Generator for Server and Proxy generation it is important that you regularly check the Account and Billing dashboard of the providers you use. This will help ensure that you do not leave instances running and are aware of all charges to your account._

[Adding Provider Credentials](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060614453-Generator-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01ESB34JMN4SGP1BG8F32VXVT0)
 | [Creating Proxies](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060614453-Generator-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01ESBRFYX1K6T9AFMVGYMSGEVN)
 | [Destroying Proxies](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060614453-Generator-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01ESBRSJ5TNJT1V83CY3A19BGW)
 | [Adding Proxies](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360060614453-Generator-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01ESBS1VTY3BN8A2XK4NJNJH5Y)

#### Adding Provider Credentials

While in the Credentials option you can add providers for generating both servers and proxies. If you have not added credentials before you will need to add them before being able to generate proxies. If you do not already have the information required to add a Provider, such as AWS, GCP, or Digital Ocean, you can [click here to learn how to set up an account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039783553)
.

1.  To add a Provider press the Create button.
2.  Select a Provider from the dropdown.
3.  Name the Credential so that you can easily differentiate and identify it.
4.  Add the required information for the Credential as each Provider will have slightly different information. 

![2020-12-12_14-50-19__1_.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000375541/2020-12-12_14-50-19__1_.gif)

#### Creating Proxies

Once you have added the credentials for any of the supported providers you can begin to create proxies. If you are looking to create a larger number of proxies check out our [Limit Increase Guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039297874)
.

1.  Press Create this will bring up a dialog where you will be able to specify the number of proxies you wish to generate, choose the provider and credentials as well as other information in order to create the proxies.
2.  Once you have finished configuring the settings for the proxies you can press the create button in order to start the creation process.
3.  This will start the proxies' Initialization process where it will set up and begin to run.

#### Destroying Proxies

Proxies cannot be stopped and can only be destroyed, to destroy a proxy click the X icon or right-click the proxy instance and select destroy. 

#### Adding Proxies

Inside of Generator, we have a Testing section. This section will allow you to test and store ISP, DC, Residential, or other proxies that you may have. You can use the proxies you store here in order to do things like generate Accounts or Cookies. 

1.  Click on Create.
2.  Paste the Proxies you have and Set a Category name or select an existing one from the drop-down, then press Save.

![2020-12-12_15-04-18__1_.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000375561/2020-12-12_15-04-18__1_.gif)

When Testing Proxies, you have the option to select the Target Testing Website, as well as delete proxies that returned dead or slow from your latest test. These options are found on the right-hand side under the Options button.

![2020-12-12_15-13-16__1_.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000371082/2020-12-12_15-13-16__1_.gif)

Tips and Tricks
---------------

In Generator it is now possible to reorder Sidebar Icons. This order will save between restarts making it more customizable to the way you use Generator.  
![reorder.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/1500000372522/reorder.gif)

# Profile Builder - Proxies – AYCD

Profile Builder - Proxies

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13257962802455-Profile-Builder-Proxies/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

March 21, 2023 15:05

Introduction
------------

You can use proxies with many modules in Profile Builder. You can use them in Browsers, when generating accounts, using Tuner, and many other areas of Profile Builder!

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Different types of proxies work on various sites! If you need help deciding which proxies to use, consult with a cook group such as [AYCD Pro](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/aycdpro?referralCode=ZENDESK)
    . 

Adding Proxies
--------------

1\. Navigate to the **Proxies** tab within Profile Builder.

2\. Click the **Create** button. This will open a dialog for you to input your proxies.

3\. By default, Proxies are created to the All **Category.** You can override this and specify a category name to have your Proxies created in or use the dropdown to assign them to an existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13257477754903)

4\. Once you have finished inputting your proxies, you can click on **Save**, and you will see your proxies in the Proxies table.

Testing & Copying Proxies
-------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Proxies** tab within Profile Builder.

2\. Select the Proxies from the table that you want the test.

3\. Use the **Target** dropdown to specify which website you want to test your proxies on. If the website you want to test is not listed in the dropdown menu, select **Custom** to pick any website. 

![Proxies-Testing-1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13672328321303)

4\. Choose which test you want to use on your proxies. **Ping** is the most commonly used testing type.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15927883352599)  
  
5\. After testing your proxies, their speed will be displayed under the Page Load (ms) column.

6\. You can use the **Delete Dead Proxies** option to remove any proxies that have timed out or have connection issues.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13257720786711)

7\. You can use the **Delete Slow Proxies** option to remove any proxies that are not fast enough for your use case. After selecting the option, specify a maximum acceptable ping in milliseconds. Any proxies testing speeds above the ping you specify will be removed.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13257747534103)

8\. After you have tested your proxies and filtered them out, if you want to use them outside of Profile Builder, use the **Copy to Clipboard** button under **Options** or by selecting your proxies, right-clicking, and choosing the **Copy to Clipboard** option to copy your proxies to your clipboard.![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13257643755287)

9\. Use the **Test Localhost** button to test the speed of your local network.![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13257689762583)

Now you have added proxies to Profile Builder. You can test your proxy speeds and use them with many modules in Profile Builder.

# TabSentry - Tasks - Task Assistant – AYCD

TabSentry - Tasks - Task Assistant

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18228383559831-TabSentry-Tasks-Task-Assistant/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

October 12, 2023 18:21

Introduction
------------

Task Assistant lets you easily view Mail (IMAP) & SMS OTP codes, configure Multi Browser Control (MBC), access billing information, utilize Features, and access your tasks' Custom Data.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To use Task Assistant, you need to create tasks in TabSentry. [Click here to learn how to create tasks on TabSentry](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18178508966679)
    .
*   The selected task or browser in focus determines what information is shown on Task Assistant. To view data from another task, click the task within the TabSentry UI or click the browser to bring it into focus.
*   To get the most functionality out of Task Assistant, you should configure the task as specific as possible to tailor your needs. You can do this by entering information when creating the tasks, such as username and password in the Autofill section, assigning a billing profile, and assigning features to your task, such as Mail (IMAP) and SMS OTP features.
*   When coming across a site with autofill options, the browser will attempt to autofill as many fields as possible through Chrome's built-in autofill. In instances where it can't, you can **Click** the field you want to copy in Task Assistant to copy to your clipboard for easier access. Chrome autofill will only be available for the username and password on sites entered in the URL section under the Autofill section of a task.
*   You should use Custom Autofill instead of relying on Chrome Autofill for a much more consistent autofill solution that works on all websites and is compatible with [MBC Recordings](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19342776979479)
    . [Click here to learn how to use Custom Autofill](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18707075013271)
    .

Using Task Assistant
--------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Tasks > Browsers** section in TabSentry. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled Browsers.

2\. Click the **Start** button to start the task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28520888261655)

3\. After starting a task, click the Task Assistant button.   
4\. The first section in Task Assistant is the **Account** section. You can find your task **Username**, **Password**, and **TOTP Code**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28524188897047)

5\. The second section of Task Assistant is **Payment Details**. You can find the payment information from the profile assigned to your task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28525369042327)  

6\. The third section of Task Assistant is **Address Info**. You can find the address information from the profile assigned to your task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28525369042839)

7\. The fourth section of Task Assistant is **Custom Data**. You can assign Custom Data to your tasks and access the data within Task Assistant. [You can generate Custom Data and assign it to multiple tasks by following this guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19348105137943)
. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28525382831255)  
8\. The fifth section of Task Assistant is the Mail view.  When using a Mail (IMAP) OTP feature, Task Assistant will automatically scan for incoming emails that contain OTP codes.

*   If Task Assistant does not display the correct code automatically, you can click the downward arrow on the OTP field to show other potential OTP codes.
*   Click the Trash can icon to remove a code from Task Assistant.
*   Click here to learn how to create a [Mail (IMAP) OTP Feature](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18207357073431)
    .

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28525539787031)

9\. The sixth section of Task Assistant is the SMS view, where you can view OTP codes and request phone numbers. [Click here to learn how to configure an SMS OTP feature](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/20573107430551)
.

*   You can request SMS phone numbers from Task Assistant when using an SMS OTP feature.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28525539787287)

*   After requesting a number, TabSentry waits for the SMS provider to send an OTP code. Once it receives one, it will be shown in Task Assistant. You can copy the code from Task Assistant.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28525526229527)

10\. The seventh section of Task Assistant is MBC (Multi-Browser Control). You can start MBC from this section, which lets you control multiple browsers from one parent browser. [Click here to learn how to use MBC](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18200568034967)
.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28525526229783)

11\. The final section of Task Assistant is Features. The features section allows you to trigger the features assigned to your tasks.

*   You can trigger Custom Autofill with the **Wand** button. [Click here to learn how to create a Custom Autofill feature](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18707075013271)
    .
*   You can enable/disable AutoSolve AI from the Features section. [Click here to learn how to create an AutoSolve AI feature.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18501944197143)
    
*   You can enable/disable features such as [Monitor URL Change](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18258747628823)
     and [Auto Refresh](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18200084745623)
     from the features section.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28525539788183)

*   [TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - How to Scrape Data from Websites](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28640622181399-TabSentry-Tasks-Browsers-How-to-Scrape-Data-from-Websites)
    
*   [TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Tools - Create / Replay MBC Recording](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19342776979479-TabSentry-Tasks-Browsers-Tools-Create-Replay-MBC-Recording)
    

[See more](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/sections/18178135157783-TabSentry)

# OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Tools - Enable IMAP – AYCD

OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Tools - Enable IMAP

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13259481783959-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Tools-Enable-IMAP/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/1500080569202/crw1.png)

**Crewie**

March 21, 2023 15:54

Introduction
------------

The Enable IMAP feature will allow you to enable IMAP on the selected accounts quickly. This can save you lots of time when forwarding accounts. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Before enabling IMAP, you must [add accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173#h_01EPF75YH4B6PB4WVSN5P7NJ10)
     to OneClick. After adding the accounts, you can use Account Tools.
*   We recommend farming your accounts for at least a week and for the accounts to be at least one year old before setting up forwarding or IMAP.

Enabling IMAP
-------------

![2023-03-21_15_51_31-OneClick_x_Crewie_v2.8.1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13259380064151)

1\. Navigate over to the Accounts section of OneClick. 

2\. Select the accounts in which you want to enable IMAP in the table.

3\. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view or right-click on the table and click on **Tools**. This will bring up the **Tools** dialog for accounts.

4\. Click the dropdown and choose Enable IMAP.

5\. Click Start.

6\. The operation will start, and the Action Log will update as it performs the job. 

![2023-03-21_15_53_52-OneClick_x_Crewie_v2.8.1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13259443010071)

Once the job is completed, the Action Log will reflect whether it was successful or not. In this case, 3/3 of accounts had IMAP enabled. If any accounts run into errors, the Action Log will reflect the error, and the Status will provide some information on the issue.

*   [OneClick - Account Ratings](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5751258079895-OneClick-Account-Ratings)

*   [OneClick - Testing ReCaptcha](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038828114-OneClick-Testing-ReCaptcha)

# Supported Bots - Mek AIO – AYCD

Supported Bots - Mek AIO

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500009078161-Supported-Bots-Mek-AIO/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 02, 2021 12:46

Introduction
------------

You can use AutoSolve with various bots and applications. AutoSolve, paired with [AutoSolve AI](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/captchapass?referralCode=ZENDESK)
, is the ultimate captcha-solving solution.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can use your **AutoSolve API Key** to send **reCaptcha** challenges to **OneClick through AutoSolve**. Refer to this guide to learn more about setting up [OneClick Accounts/Solvers](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
    .
*   If you have [AutoSolve AI](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/captchapass?referralCode=ZENDESK)
    , reCaptcha challenges that appear in your Accounts/Solvers will automatically solve.

AutoSolve API Key
-----------------

1\. Navigate to the **AutoSolve** section in OneClick.

2\. Create a new AutoSolve API Key or select one of your existing AutoSolve API Keys. Then right-click the selected API Key and click the **Copy API Key** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16754980000535)

3\. Navigate to the **Tools** section in Mek AIO. Then go to the **Cap Gen tab.**

4\. Click the **+** button to create a Cap Gen solver.

5\. Select **AYCD** under the dropdown.

6\. Specify a name for your AYCD solver. You can input anything in this field.

7\. Input your **AutoSolve API Key** to the AYCD Api Token field. 

8\. When you have finished configuring the Cap Gen solver, click the **Save** button.

9\. Your AYCD Cap Gen solver should look similar to the screenshot below.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14777456397719)

10\. Navigate to your Task group in Mek AIO.

11\. Under the Captcha Service dropdown, select the option that you named your AYCD Cap Gen solver.

12\. Click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14777578122519)

13\. You can test your setup by running tasks on the **Checkpoint Test** site preset. Navigate to the Task section of Mek.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19612160272279)

14\. Start tasks. If the captchas are sent to OneClick and solved on your OneClick Accounts/Solvers, your setup is working.

AutoSolve AI Key
----------------

1\. Navigate to the **AutoSolve** section in OneClick.

2\. Click the **View AutoSolve AI Key** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16754980003095)

3\. Click the **Copy AutoSolve AI Key** button in the dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16754967184407)

4\. Navigate to the **Setting** section in Mek AIO. 

5\. Input your **AutoSolve AI Key** in the AYCD AI Token field. 

6\. Click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16754964303383)

7\. If you want reCaptcha challenges to be automatically solved when they appear in the MEK local harvesters, navigate to the **Task** section of Mek. Create tasks for the **Checkpoint Test** preset site.

8\. Enable the **LocalSolve** option on your tasks.

9\. Do not select a provider within the task group for the **Captcha Service.**

**![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19612234360599)**

10\. Open a Mek harvester set to Shopify-Checkpoint.

11\. Start tasks. If the captchas are automatically solved when they appear in the Mek harvester, your setup is working.
