# AutoSolve - Getting Started Guide – AYCD

AutoSolve - Getting Started Guide

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

February 22, 2020 21:40

### What is AutoSolve?

AutoSolve is the latest tool by AYCD. It allows you to connect OneClick with any bots that support AutoSolve, turning OneClick into your Captcha Solving Hub. By sending your requests to OneClick, you can direct them to your Accounts, Empty Solvers, or optionally to a supported Third-Party Solver such as 2Captcha, AntiCaptcha, or CapMonster. _While AutoSolve on its own does not enable the Automatic solving of captcha challenges, since its inception, we have integrated backup solving solutions such as AutoSolve AI or Speech to Text solving that allow for a more automated experience_.

* * *

The best way to find out if your bot supports AutoSolve is to look for their AutoSolve section or reach out to their team. _We have [a list of some supported bots](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043237394)
, but this list does not include every program that supports AutoSolve_.

[OneClick Setup](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8H1N7RP3SB7YNAADTGDRT)
 | [AutoSolve Dashboard/Connecting Bots](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8GQ04A2VGJQGP8XQNE3VG)
 | [Using AutoSolve](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8G6PFQE778TK32TVTP202)
 | [Third-Party Solver Setup](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8FNRK599630B998BMFZZP)
 | [AutoSolve Settings](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8F6ZX8A4MVASG05VTCG2W)
 | [Additional Resources](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8ETH9FCKY7RXEBNPDY2CA)

### OneClick Setup

AutoSolve enables you to turn OneClick into your Captcha Solving Hub. But before you can start to forward your requests to OneClick, some setup is required.

[Requirements](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8DKA0794QRY1TRJ1YX8S8)
 | [Troubleshooting](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8E7D8779TH3D50TA8KDK3)
 

#### Requirements

Before using AutoSolve to connect your bots with OneClick, you must first confirm you have subscriptions for both the **AYCD Toolbox** and **AutoSolve** or just one subscription to the Captcha Pass. You can confirm this under the [Billing Dashboard](https://aycd.io/account#billing)
. You should also ensure you are running the latest version of OneClick in the Toolbox.

#### Troubleshooting

A problem that you may run into after enabling AutoSolve is that the tab remains grayed out. A grayed out AutoSolve tab can occur for two reasons:

1.  You do not have an AutoSolve or an AYCD Pro subscription. You can confirm your active subscriptions from the [Billing tab of the Account Dashboard](https://aycd.io/account#billing)
    .   
    ![ActiveSubDashboard.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436109335319)
2.  There was an issue with your subscription syncing to the Toolbox. To resolve this, you can follow the steps below.  
    1.  Go to the [AutoSolve Dashboard](https://aycd.io/account#autosolve)
        .
    2.  From there, click on Reset Access Token.  
        ![ResetAccessToken.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436094791703)
    3.  Go back to the Toolbox, click on Exit, and restart the Toolbox.  
        ![ExitToolBox.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436066197399)
    4.  After the Toolbox starts back up, you should be able to start OneClick, and the AutoSolve tab should no longer be grayed out.  
        ![EnabledAutoSolveOneClick.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436100800535)

### AutoSolve Dashboard/Connecting Bots

The AutoSolve Dashboard is where you will be able to get your AutoSolve Access Token, create and delete Bot API Keys, create AutoSolve Companion App Keys, and also add Third-Party Solvers. If you have not already done the basic setup for OneClick above, I would recommend you complete that first.

1.  Go to the [AutoSolve Dashboard](https://aycd.io/account#autosolve)
     using the direct link, the Go To Dashboard button in OneClick, or by navigating there on your own. If you are setting up AutoSolve for a bot you are using, you should have that open now.
2.  Use the Copy Access Token button from the Dashboard or in OneClick to copy it to your clipboard. Paste it into the bots Access Token field.  
    ![CopyAccessToken.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436129031319)
3.  Next, you will need to create an AutoSolve Bot API Key. Click on the Create button below the AutoSolve Bot API Keys section. Select a name that is easy to identify, usually the bot's name. _IMPORTANT: The API Key must be unique for each bot and instance of a bot you are running. Using the same API Key in multiple places will result in your solves not being delivered correctly.  
    _
4.  Select your API Key from the dropdown menu and click on the copy button then paste it into the API Key field in your bot. If your bot has a save button make sure you do that in order to save your AutoSolve configuration there.  
    ![CreateBotAPIKey.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436123110935)

Once you have completed the above steps to configure AutoSolve with your bot, you will need to Start AutoSolve. Once started, you should be able to send requests to OneClick to be solved. Some bots may require additional configuration. Make sure to check with their support team if you have any additional questions about using AutoSolve with their bot.

### Using AutoSolve

AutoSolve can be configured to work in several different ways. I will cover some of the setups below. If you are looking for our recommendations and best practices based on the type of captcha you will be solving, you can read about that [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847)
.

[Basic Setup](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8C3W240Q1EZTNVZNSV2HW)
 | [Empty Solvers](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G0B4G2H2X3R33HEK4PRV77NA)
 | [Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8CPGXVNH18QKN05JDPFDX)
 | [Third Party Solvers](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8CZCR0WJ3KE3W1VJER363)

#### Basic Setup

Before starting your bot tasks for a drop, no matter how AutoSolve is configured, you will want to ensure that you have OneClick open and Connected to AutoSolve. Without that, your requests will have nowhere to go.

1.  First, make sure you open OneClick. Without it open, your requests will have nowhere to go.
2.  Connect to AutoSolve within OneClick. There are a few ways you can do that. You can use the right-click dropdown option Start AutoSolve or Options > Start AutoSolve > Selected/All.
3.  A dialog will appear and give you some options. The order you want to start the solvers in, if you want to skip the login verification(_I would only do this if you are certain the Accounts are logged in_), and if you want to warm the browsers up with a search before going into AutoSolve mode.  
    ![AutoSolveSetupDialog.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436159518487)
4.  After you start the solvers along the bottom, you should see the AutoSolve Status show Connected in green and an AutoSolve Enabled on the accounts you started with AutoSolve.
5.  Once you are sure you are connected you can begin to start your bot tasks to send requests to AutoSolve.  
    ![StartAutoSolveOneClick.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436161394455)

#### Empty Solvers

If you are using AutoSolve for captchas that do not require a high level of trust in the same way that ReCaptcha V2/V3 challenges do, you have the option of creating Accountless Solvers. They are only able to be Started with AutoSolve. To create them:

1.  Go to the Accounts Section of OneClick and press File > Add Solver.  
    ![FileAddSolvers.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436187093271)
2.  A dialog will appear and give you some options to create the empty solvers.  
    *   **Category:** The Category for the Solvers. It is best to add these to a category separate from accounts so you don't mix them up.
    *   **Name:** The name that will come before the number from 1 - the quantity of solvers you are creating. 
    *   **Quantity:** The number of solvers that you want to create.
    *   **Proxy Category:** The category of proxies that you want to be assigned to the solvers that you are creating.  
        ![AddSolverOptions.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436187905559)
3.  Once you press Save it will create the number of solvers you set in the Category you specified.   
    ![CreatedEmptySolvers.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436188842135)
4.  To use the Solvers you created just Start AutoSolve on them and they can start receiving captcha requests.

These Solvers can only be used for AutoSolve, they cannot be started for farming only for AutoSolve. Empty solvers are very useful in cases where trust is not a big factor for the type of captcha you are solving. Their true strength shows paired with AutoSolve AI to nearly automate the entire solving process. 

#### Accounts

When you are using AutoSolve for captchas that require a high level of trust like ReCaptcha V2 and V3 challenges you will want to use farmed and trusted accounts in order to increase your chances of getting a one-click captcha, a very easy captcha challenge, or in the case of V3 to return a high trust score. To use Accounts, you will want to have them added to OneClick and farmed well for the best success. _[This guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173#h_01EPF75YH4B6PB4WVSN5P7NJ10)
 talks about Adding your Accounts to OneClick in-depth and provides more information on Farming._

1.  Once you have your accounts added to AutoSolve, the process for starting them with AutoSolve is similar to the Empty Solvers. You can either right-click an account and Start AutoSolve on it, or you can multi-select the accounts and go to Options > Start AutoSolve > Selected.
2.  After you start the accounts with AutoSolve along the bottom, you should see the AutoSolve Status show Connected in Green and an AutoSolve Enabled on the accounts you started.
3.  Once you are sure you are connected you can begin to start your bot tasks to send requests to AutoSolve.  
    ![StartAutoSolveOneClick.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436229018263)

#### Third-Party Solvers

If you are primarily using Third-Party Solvers for solving incoming captcha challenges it works a bit different than using local solvers. Before you begin, you will want to make sure that you've added the service you are using through the AutoSolve Dashboard. You can learn more about that in the guide [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043745313)
. 

1.  Go to the AutoSolve Section and check the middle column for your Third Party Provider API Key that you added through the AutoSolve Dashboard. If you do not see it there, press Sync Dashboard in order to update the services table.  
    ![AutoSolveSyncDashboard.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436182001687)
2.  Click on the checkbox in order to enable the Third Party Provider keys that you want to use for AutoSolve. _The service you want a majority of the requests to go to should be shown with the Status Primary_. _You can drag the services around in the first table to reorder_ them.
3.  Press the Connect button in the top left to manually connect to AutoSolve, then you can begin to send requests to AutoSolve which will forward them to the service you enabled.  
    ![EnableAndConnectThirdParty.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436207362327)

### Third-Party Solver Setup

In order to use Third Party Solvers like 2Captcha, AntiCaptcha, and CapMonster you will need to first set those up. You can learn about creating accounts for those services and setting them up [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043745313)
. 

### AutoSolve Settings

The AutoSolve view has several different tabs that hold additional information and settings that you can view and configure. Each section is explained below. Click [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847)
 to learn more about our AutoSolve Settings recommendations.

[Services](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C89QKCAX8SA9KV819NKPMA)
 | [Tasks](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8A1F9JNNY75F111BREW35)
 | [Connections](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8AGKB5KT0S5Y0CX2V5MT5)
 | [Errors](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C8AVE7DCX39F6YME5R65J4)
 | [Routes](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G35CV5N3XP86DFPGC9G616C1)
 | [Settings](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G3W7D608P83EJK2XKYNKAQZS)

#### Services

The Services tab is the first tab of the AutoSolve section within OneClick. It is split into 3 separate tables.

**Active Services**

The first table in this section will show you any services that you have enabled for AutoSolve and the priority of those services for your incoming solve requests. Here you can see whether a service is a backup solving method such as AI Solve, or a Primary service like your OneClick Solvers. You can move services around to change the priority. _A backup service (AutoSolve AI, Google Speech, and Wit.ai) requires local solvers to be started with AutoSolve to be usable_.  
![AutoSolveActiveService.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436244472471)

**Available Services**

The table in the middle shows available Solving Services for AutoSolve and will allow you to enable and disable them. To add Third-Party Solvers you can follow the guide we have [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043745313)
. This table will also allow you to right-click on a service and copy the API Key for it or sync your account's balance. For 2Captcha and AntiCaptcha, you can also set an API Redirect allowing you to send requests to a different service so long as it uses the exact same API as either the respective services.  
![AutoSolveAvailableServices.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436245410199)

**API Keys**

The last table shows any Bot API Keys you have created. Here you can quickly Copy the Bot API Key of a created key or export all of them to a CSV. If you have created or deleted a Bot API Key with OneClick open, please ensure you click Sync Dashboard to update this table.  
![AutoSolveAPIKeys.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436246217751)

#### Tasks

The Tasks tab is used to see your incoming Captcha Requests sent to AutoSolve. Here you can see various information like the API Key sending the request, the URL of the request, the version of the captcha, which service got used to solve each request, and more. Under File, you can Create Test Tasks to test your routes. Under options, you can cancel any incoming Captcha Requests.

![AutoSolveTasksView.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436241986839)

#### Connections

The Connections tab allows you to see which API Keys are connected to AutoSolve. It will show the Name of the API Key, the API Key itself, and the time it was connected to OneClick. Bots can show they have connected during various states depending on the bot. It will show when you open the bot or when the first request gets sent to AutoSolve. Under Options, you can refresh this list and Reset a connection.  
![AutoSolveConnectionsView.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436275382551)

#### Errors

The Errors tab is primarily for diagnosing any errors you encounter with tasks that enter OneClick. The table will show the TaskId, the URL of the request, the Version, the Service, and then the error code. You can find the explanations of the respective errors below.

#### Routes

The Routes tab allows you to specify account categories for AutoSolve requests based on several factors. You can read more about routes in the dedicated guide we have [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479)
.

#### Settings

The last tab in this view deals with settings specific to AutoSolve and gives you a great deal of control in how your accounts will behave when using AutoSolve.

**General**

_Local Solver Limit_: This setting determines the maximum number of solvers you can run simultaneously for AutoSolve. When this setting is at 0, it will allow OneClick to determine the maximum number of solvers you can run automatically. You can override this setting by setting a specific maximum.

_Maximum Manual Solves At Once_: This setting determines how many solvers can have a status of Manual Solve Required (MSR) before AutoSolve starts to send additional challenges to third party solvers you have enabled. Then, as you manually solve the challenges locally, requests will begin to send to local solvers until you reach this maximum again. A default limit of 0 means that no MSR challenges will send to other solving services.

_Automatically stop a solver when multiple errors occur_: Enabling this setting will stop a solver when multiple errors occur and allow for a scheduled solver to take its place.

_Cancel the AutoSolve task when you close the local browser solving it_: Enabling this setting will cause a task to be canceled if you click on the X to close the solver when it is displaying a challenge. 

_Use Companion solving as a backup when Manual Solve Required(MSR)_: Enabling this setting will send requests that require a manual solve to your AYCD companion mobile app. 

![AutoSolveGeneralSettings.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436333094807)

**Captcha Specific**

Local Solver Overflow Limit (reCaptcha V2, GeeTest): This setting determines the number of times a challenge will be redirected to another solver if it does not get a one-click. Setting it to 0 will prevent requests from being redirected. We suggest leaving this setting at 0.

_Max Solvers Per Local Solver (All)_: This setting determines the maximum number of solves a solver will complete before stopping itself. When a solver is stopped, other scheduled solvers will begin to start up to your local solver limit.

_Max Requests Per Challenge For AI Solve (reCaptcha V2, GeeTest)_: This setting determines the maximum number of requests AI Solve will use for each challenge it has to solve. This gives you control over how many requests will be used to try to solve a challenge. The task will say Manual Solve Required (MSR) if the limit is reached.

_Auto-Warmup Solve Count_ _(reCaptcha V2, reCaptcha V3)_: This setting determines how many solves a solver can perform before it performs an automatic warmup. To preserve solving speeds, a maximum of 20% of solvers can be automatically warming up simultaneously. Leaving this setting at 0 means the feature is disabled.

_Send webhook when manual solve is required (reCaptcha V2, GeeTest)_: Enabling this setting will send a webhook to a Discord channel you have specified in the Main Settings view by providing a webhook url when a solver receives a Manual Solver Required (MSR) status.

_Automatically stop a solver when Manual Solver Required (MSR) (reCaptcha V2, GeeTest)_: Enabling this setting will stop a solver when it does not get a one-click or is unable to be solved by AI Solve within the maximum requests per challenge.

_Automatically restart a solver when the Max Solvers Per Local Solver limit is reached (All)_: Enabling this setting will restart a solver and queue it to start again once the solver has reached the Maximum Solves Per Local Solver limit. The solver will be added to the end of the queue if you have scheduled solvers waiting to start.![AutoSolveCaptchaSpecific.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436310054295)

###   
Additional Resources

AutoSolve has several resources that can help provide you with valuable information when managing your accounts and setting up. A few of them, including some tips, are listed below.

[Multiple Instances](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C88ATZ8SHS0AJASGQ9HZXT)
 | [Metrics View](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C88X9E42J8PE4J06QSCKAC)
 | [Useful Guides](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide#h_01G4C89471HXH7DSV0G2KBRCAV)
 

#### Multiple Instances

If you have addon session and plan on using AutoSolve with more than 1 instance of OneClick, it is very important to understand how AutoSolve manages requests received from your bots. 

Requests sent from your bots to AutoSolve first reach a queue that directs them to OneClick using a round-robin technique. This technique evenly splits the requests in order amongst all your open copies of OneClick. What this means is that you get 5 solve requests from a bot and have 2 copies of OneClick running that OneClick A will get request 1, OneClick B will get request 2, OneClick A will get request 3, OneClick B will get request 4, and OneClick A will get request 5.

#### Metrics View

Using the Metrics view allows you to see long-lasting statistics for each Account and Solver on the requests solved and the average time it took to solve the request split into each request type.

Metrics for your accounts are shown in the Accounts View by selecting Metrics from the view type dropdown.  
![MetricsView.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6436349699479)

[AutoSolve - Recommendations and Best Practices](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847)

[AutoSolve AI - Getting Started Guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4418658160791)

# OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Tools - Show & Load URL – AYCD

OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Tools - Show & Load URL

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13320585314583-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Tools-Show-Load-URL/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

March 23, 2023 19:03

Introduction
------------

The Show & Load URL tool allows you to show the browser and load a specified URL for an account. This feature can perform bulk actions on an account, such as checking account activity, reviewing security alerts, changing account settings, and more.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You must have account or solver tasks created in the Accounts section of OneClick to use the Show & Load URL Tool.

Show & Load URL
---------------

1\. Navigate over to the **Accounts** section in OneClick.   
2\. Select the accounts you want to Show & Load URL’s on. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view or right-click on the selected accounts and click on Tools. This will bring up the Tools dialog for accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13648379237143)  
3\. Click the dropdown and choose **Show & Load URL**.  
4\. Click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13648476000663)

5\. If you want to load the URL after the browser is shown, click the **Load URL after Browser Shown** checkbox.

6\. If you want to skip checking if your account is logged into Google when starting tasks click the **Skip Automatic Google Account Login Verification**

7\. Click the **Start** button. 

Now you can easily use the Show & Load URL Tool in OneClick.

# AutoSolve - Routes – AYCD

AutoSolve - Routes

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=====================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

April 08, 2022 04:50

### What are AutoSolve Routes?

AutoSolve Routes introduces a way to route AutoSolve requests based on different values. This feature replaces API Key category bindings and offers new and improved functionality. Before we go over creating a new AutoSolve Route and exploring all the different options that are available, we will first review how OneClick receives requests and at what point routes affect the solver requests.

[How Requests are Directed](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G5BBSN1NE7KECB7612BV53G6)
 | [Creating an AutoSolve Route](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G05J271ACG17BQ35PY1A682D)
 | [Route Configurations](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G05NJ28NEY478MVC14SQ19HG)
 | [Managing Routes](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G05J2D66AQ1G4BSZER76W2A9)
 | [Testing AutoSolve Routes](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G5D772AS6VP8WQPB5Y6SPK1B)

### How Requests are Directed

AutoSolve will direct the requests it receives differently depending on how many instances of OneClick you have running at a given time.

[Single Instance](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G5BD82W2DHAMKRWZRT324W1D)
 | [Multiple Instances](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G5BD8CRB2HBY4CHZE03B68WN)

#### Single Instance

If you do not have an addon session or only plan to have one instance of OneClick open using AutoSolve, then all the requests sent from your AutoSolve Bot API Keys will be received by this instance. After the request reaches OneClick, if you have any enabled routes, the request will be assigned to a solver that matches the route criteria you have created. 

#### Multiple Instances

If you have an addon session and plan on using AutoSolve with more than 1 instance of OneClick, requests sent from your bots to AutoSolve first reach a queue that directs them to OneClick using a round-robin technique. This technique evenly splits the requests in order amongst all your open copies of OneClick. What this means is that you get 5 solve requests from a bot and have 2 copies of OneClick running that OneClick A will get request 1, OneClick B will get request 2, OneClick A will get request 3, OneClick B will get request 4, and OneClick A will get request 5. That means that even if you have a route set on OneClick A, that requests from an API Key you have created a route for will still be sent to OneClick B since it is only after the request reaches OneClick that the enabled routes will decide which solver to assign the request to.

### Creating an AutoSolve Route

When creating an AutoSolve Route you have the option to direct the requests either to a category of accounts in OneClick or to a supported Third Party Provider. 

[Routes to Account Categories](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G5BE4CVNVR5XBGQVSSPHX38W)
 | [Routes to Third Party Services](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G5BE4VTYYDMKTVG9MS9RM1XK)

#### Routes to Account Categories

Routes created for Account Categories will allow you to send requests that meet your route criteria to the category of accounts you specify.

1.  To create a Route for an Account Category, go to the Routes tab in the AutoSolve View.
2.  Click the create button and select Account Category as the Target Type before clicking Next.
3.  This will open a dialog that you can use to configure your route. You can read about the available configurations and how they work [below](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G05NJ28NEY478MVC14SQ19HG)
    .

![CreatingAccountRoutes.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6712502123031)

#### Routes to Third Party Services

Routes created for a Third Party Service will allow you to send requests that meet your route criteria to a Third Party Provider you choose.

1.  To create a Route for a Third Party Service, go to the Routes tab in the AutoSolve View.
2.  Click the create button and select Third Party Service as the Target Type before clicking Next.
3.  This will open a dialog that you can use to configure your route. You can read about the available configurations and how they work [below](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G05NJ28NEY478MVC14SQ19HG)
    .

![CreatingThirdPartyServiceRoutes.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6712560473367)

### Route Configurations

The main purpose of Routes is to allow further customization to the AutoSolve service. You can now separate requests coming from the same API Key by using Routes which would be ideal if you were running different sites at the same time. Below you can find an explanation for each of the options offered in the dialog.

[Target Category](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G05NP337A537VKBEVK6P3G90)
 | [Nickname](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G05NPQFX8JPZ9R4YTCDSJTEC)
 | [Priority](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G05NQ79ED6CFAS5F3G9Z188T)
 | [Route Type](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G05NQWHYQCH4W54A9Y3AFRSK)

#### Target Category

The target category to route matching requests to. Once a category is bound to an enabled route, the solvers in that category will only receive requests that match the highest priority enabled route. This means that requests that match this route will only go to this category. When requests don't match any of the routes you have, they will go to any category that does not have a route. If there are no active accounts/solvers in the category, the request will be stuck waiting to solve.

#### Nickname

A nickname for this route to help identify it easier. This is not required. Utilizing nicknames can make it easier to organize your routes and manage them. As mentioned, you do not need to provide a nickname and can leave this field blank. 

#### Priority

The priority for this route, where the lower the number, the higher the priority. A priority of 0 is the highest. _The lower numbered route will always be used before a higher numbered route._ Routes with the same priority will be used in the order they were added. Changing the priority can be useful when you have some routes that you would prefer to be used before others. It is recommended to leave this field at the default value unless you have a specific use case. 

#### Route Type

The type of routing to filter incoming requests by. The route type will determine what field is used to route your AutoSolve requests to the target category you have selected. 

#### API Key 

When setting up AutoSolve, each program requires a unique API Key. You can route the requests from that unique API Key to the selected target category using this route type. For example, if you want to use different accounts/solvers for different programs, you can route each individual API Key to different target categories. 

When multiple API Keys are selected, as seen below, multiple routes will be created.

![mceclip1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/5328730128279)

#### Task ID

Each request is received with a Task ID provided by the program sending the request as a way to identify it. This route type should only be used when the developers of the program you are using instruct you to.  
A use case for Task ID's would be if a bot sends out captcha requests with the Task ID containing "checkout" for requests that pertain to the checkout process, while also sending out requests with the task ID containing "AddToCart" for requests that pertain to the add to cart process. 

This is an example of how you would configure a TaskID route if your bot included the term "AddToCart" as a part of the task ID.

![mceclip5.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/5329286835735)

#### Site URL

Each request is received with a Site URL provided by the program sending the request in order to load the captcha challenge. For example, a Site URL may look like this:

https://recaptcha-demo.appspot.com/recaptcha-v2-checkbox.php

In the case you are receiving requests from the same program with a different Site URL, you can route the different requests based on the Site URL to different target categories. This is useful when the captcha type is different between Site URLs or the Site URLs require different setups. For example, if one site requires a faster solve time, you can use the target category of accounts/solvers that you know will solve faster. If another site does not require a fast solve time, you can use the target category of accounts/solvers that may solve slower. When you are receiving requests with different Site URLs from different programs, it is preferable to use the API Key route type instead. 

In this image, you can see an example of what a route would look like if you are designating a specific category for a YeezySupply drop.![mceclip3.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/5329126315159)

#### Captcha Version

Each request is received with a Captcha Version provided by the program sending the request in order to load the captcha challenge. For example, if you are receiving some requests that are reCaptcha2 Checkbox and some requests that are GeeTest. Using the Captcha Version route type, you can route the reCaptcha2 Checkbox requests to a target category with one-click accounts and route the GeeTest requests to a target category with empty solvers using AutoSolve AI. When you are receiving requests with different Captcha Versions from different programs, it is preferable to use the API Key route type instead. 

  
![](https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/776251664826236949/962123584833224804/unknown.png)

Above you can see a route set up for all v3 requests to be assigned to a particular category of solvers named OneClicks. 

### Filter Type

The type of filtering to use when comparing the value to the incoming request's value. Some route types only support certain filter types. For example, the API Key route type only supports the Equals filter type.

#### Equals

The Equals filter type compares the value from the request exactly to the value of the route. For example, the API Key route type only supports Equals since the API Key is a constant value. However, the Site URL route type supports all the filter types since the Site URL can vary. It is not recommended to use the Equals filter type for the Site URL route type. 

#### Includes

The Includes filter type compares the value from the request to include/contain the value of the route. For example, an AutoSolve request may have a Site URL as follows:

https://recaptcha-demo.appspot.com/recaptcha-v2-checkbox.php 

In the case where you don't know exactly what the Site URL will be, but you know a part of it such as the domain you can use the Includes filter type to find a partial match. You can set the value as follows: 

recaptcha-demo.appspot.com

Using the above value and the Includes filter type will match the entire Site URL:

https://recaptcha-demo.appspot.com/recaptcha-v2-checkbox.php 

#### Excludes

The Excludes filter type compares the value from the request to exclude/ignore the value of the route. For example, an AutoSolve request may have a Site URL as follows:

https://recaptcha-demo.appspot.com/recaptcha-v2-checkbox.php 

In the case where you don't know exactly what the Site URL will be, but you know a part of it such as the domain you can use the Excludes filter type to ignore a partial match. You can set the value as follows: 

recaptcha-demo.appspot.com

Using the above value and the Excludes filter type will match the entire Site URL:

https://recaptcha-demo.appspot.com/recaptcha-v2-checkbox.php 

It is not recommended to use this filter type and to use the Includes filter type instead. 

#### Starts With

The Starts With filter type compares the value from the request to determine if it starts with the value of the route. For example, an AutoSolve request may have a Site URL as follows:

https://recaptcha-demo.appspot.com/recaptcha-v2-checkbox.php 

In the case where you don't know exactly what the Site URL will be, but you know what it starts with you can use the Starts With filter type to find a partial match at the beginning of the Site URL. You can set the value as follows: 

https://recaptcha-demo.appspot.com

Using the above value and the Starts With filter type will match the entire Site URL:

https://recaptcha-demo.appspot.com/recaptcha-v2-checkbox.php 

#### Ends With

The Ends With filter type compares the value from the request to determine if it ends with the value of the route. For example, an AutoSolve request may have a Site URL as follows:

https://recaptcha-demo.appspot.com/recaptcha-v2-checkbox.php 

In the case where you don't know exactly what the Site URL will be, but you know what it ends with you can use the Ends With filter type to find a partial match at the ending of the Site URL. You can set the value as follows: 

recaptcha-v2-checkbox.php

Using the above value and the Ends With filter type will match the entire Site URL:

https://recaptcha-demo.appspot.com/recaptcha-v2-checkbox.php 

### Value

The value to filter incoming requests by. The value will depend on the route type and the filter type. 

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Route Type** | **Value Description** |
| API Key | The value is a drop-down of all the API Keys you have. If you do not see some of your API Keys, you can click Sync Dashboard in the AutoSolve tab. |
| Task ID | The value is a text field for you to provide a partial Task ID depending on the selected filter type. |
| Site URL | The value is a text field for you to provide a partial or complete Site URL depending on the selected filter type. |
| Captcha Version | The value is a drop-down of the different captcha types available. |

### Managing Routes

You can manage the routes you have already created from the same section. You have the ability to Edit or Delete an existing Route or to Enable and Disable them.  
[Deleting Routes](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G05P0J11PQY3EZE43Y632KY2)
 | [Enabling and Disabling Routes](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G05P13TNC736TRC2ZCNW9VKT)
 | [Editing Routes](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G05P1R0M360AZQG02Y2PBG14)
  

#### Deleting Routes

The delete option is the same as in all other applications. Select the routes you wish to delete and then click Edit > Delete > Select to delete the routes.   
![java_xh8HXQjM7m.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/5330352562967) 

#### Enabling and Disabling Routes

Rather than continuously creating and deleting routes you are able to enable and disable routes from the Options > Enable/Disable route option. When a route is disabled it will not have any effect on any incoming requests. ![java_uMvvBfy1UA.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/5330375932695) 

#### Editing Routes

After a route has been created you are able to make some modifications by editing it. To edit a route you can either right-click on it and click Edit or select the route and click the Edit button on the top left.

The modifications you can make from the editing menu are limited to the Target Category, Nickname, and Priority. _Any edits made to routes will not affect requests that have already been received by OneClick it will only affect new requests._

![EditingTasks.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/5331139288983)

### Testing AutoSolve Routes

Once you have created an AutoSolve Route, you can create Test Requests which will mimic OneClick receiving requests and assigning them to solvers. When creating requests, you can have them automatically cancel or for them to be solved by your solvers. _I would not recommend sending requests to be solved as this will burn your one-clicks. Additionally, this will not test your connection to any bot. These requests originate from inside OneClick._

[Creating Test Tasks](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G5D77VZD1T1PEZY222Q4FFJA)
 | [Canceling Requests](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/5313012707479-AutoSolve-Routes#h_01G5D783AXE11VXC9MVEVK9JSZ)
 

#### Creating Test Tasks

1.  Test Requests are created from the Tasks tab in the AutoSolve view in OneClick. 
2.  Click on File > Create Test Tasks and configure them using the Dialog.  
    **Captcha Version:** This is a dropdown containing request types you can select.  
    **API Key:** The AutoSolve Bot API Key that will be used as the origin of the requests. _Again, this will not send requests from the Bot attached to the API Key but is used as the originating API Key in order to test your routes.  
    _**Quantity:** The number of tasks to be created.  
    **Do not solve:** Unchecking this box will send the requests to be solved by your accounts and will use up one-clicks for reCaptcha requests. 
3.  The information on the right side is filled automatically depending on the request type you select. Once you are satisfied with your selections, hit Create.

#### Canceling Requests

If you create live requests that you want to cancel you can do so easily by following the steps below.

1.  From the Tasks tab in the AutoSolve view in OneClick, click Options > Cancel Pending Requests > All. If you only want to cancel specific ones, you can select them and use Options > Cancel Pending Requests > Selected.

![CancelingRequests.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/6723696330391)

### Articles in this section

*   [OneClick - Tasks - Schedule Configs](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/24174965128855-OneClick-Tasks-Schedule-Configs)
    
*   [AutoSolve - Getting Started Guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide)
    
*   [AutoSolve - OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Start AutoSolve](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16221641626135-AutoSolve-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Start-AutoSolve)
    
*   [AutoSolve - Recommendations and Best Practices](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4452713165847-AutoSolve-Recommendations-and-Best-Practices)

# Profile Builder - Cards - Cards - Create New Profiles/Add to Existing Profiles – AYCD

Profile Builder - Cards - Cards - Create New Profiles/Add to Existing Profiles

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151-Profile-Builder-Cards-Cards-Create-New-Profiles-Add-to-Existing-Profiles/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

March 23, 2023 21:58

Introduction
------------

Creating New Profiles from Cards and adding Cards to existing Profiles is the quickest way to swap cards between drops and build a complete billing profile.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Before Creating New Profiles or adding to Existing Profiles, you must generate or Import Cards to Profile Builder.

Creating New Profiles
---------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Cards section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the credit card icon labeled Cards when you hover over it.

2\. Select the cards you want to use to create new profiles from in the table.

3\. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view or right-click on the table and click on **Tools**.

4\. Click the dropdown and choose **Create New Profiles using Cards**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39332048156695)

5\. Click the Start button.

6\. This will open the Create New Profiles dialog. Here, you can specify the Profile Information that you want to use with the selected cards. 

*   You can utilize expressions when creating new profiles from cards. You can use expressions to mass jig your profiles. [You can learn more about expressions by clicking here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058115713)
    .

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38915910947223)

7\. If you have an existing Profile you want to model this after, you can use the Use Existing Profile button toward the bottom of the dialog.

8\. You can select a Profile, and it will import that data into the dialog. If you have an Expression Template created, you can also use Expression Templates. [Click here to learn how to use Expression Templates](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058115713)
.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38915908772375)

9\. If you are creating your profiles manually and have selected multiple cards, you should use [**Expressions**](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058115713)
 so that you can jig the profile data more efficiently. 

10\. By default, the Profiles Amount will be 1 for Creating New Profiles, meaning that 1 Profile will be created per selected card. If you want to create multiple profiles per card, you can change the **Profiles Amount** option.

11\. After you have finished configuring the profile, you can use the **Save** button in the bottom right of the dialog to Create New Profiles using the selected Cards.

12\. After successfully creating your profiles, you can find your new profiles under the Profiles section. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39332061947671)

13\. In the Cards table, you can also see which Cards are already assigned to Profiles using the Profiles Column.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39332048157719)

Add to Existing Profiles
------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Cards section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the credit card icon labeled Cards when you hover over it.

2\. Select the cards you want to use to create new profiles from in the table.

3\. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view or right-click on the table and click on **Tools**. This will bring up the **Tools** dialog for cards.

4\. Click the dropdown and choose **Add Cards to existing Profiles**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39332061948823)

5\. The Add to Existing Profiles dialog will appear. Your profiles are shown in this dialog and organized by the category they are saved in. Select the profiles you want to add your cards to.

*   If you select fewer profiles than cards, then Profile Builder will not use all of your cards.
*   If you select fewer cards than profiles, Profile Builder will reuse the same cards.

6\. If you want to use the name on the card that is already saved to your profile, enable the **Use name on card from Profile Payment Details** checkbox.

7\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

 ![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38915910955543)

7\. A dialog will appear confirming that your cards were added to existing profiles.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38915908787991)

8\. In the Cards table, you can also see which Cards are assigned to Profiles using the Profiles Column.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39332048157719)

# Supported Bots - NikeShoeBot – AYCD

Supported Bots - NikeShoeBot

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500008692361-Supported-Bots-NikeShoeBot/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=========================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

April 28, 2021 18:55

Introduction
------------

You can use AutoSolve with various bots and applications. AutoSolve, paired with [AutoSolve AI](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/captchapass?referralCode=ZENDESK)
, is the ultimate captcha-solving solution.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can use your **AutoSolve API Key** to send **reCaptcha** challenges to **OneClick through AutoSolve**. [To learn how to Start AutoSolve tasks, refer to this guide.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16221641626135)
    
*   If you have [AutoSolve AI](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/captchapass?referralCode=ZENDESK)
    , reCaptcha challenges that appear in your Accounts/Solvers will automatically solve.
*   You can use your **AutoSolve AI Key** to solve captcha challenges in the **NSB Solver**.

AutoSolve API Key
-----------------

1\. Navigate to the **AutoSolve** section in OneClick.

2\. Create a new AutoSolve API Key or select one of your existing AutoSolve API Keys. Then right-click the selected API Key and click the **Copy API Key** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23827816531479)

3\. Navigate to the **Captcha** section in NSB. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23827782082199)

4\. Click the AYCD button and input your **AutoSolve API Key** in the API Key field.

5\. Click the **Connect** button. A success message will appear confirming you have connected to AutoSolve.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23827816543511)

AutoSolve AI Key
----------------

1\. Navigate to the **AutoSolve** section in OneClick.

2\. Click the **View AutoSolve AI Key** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23827816548503)

3\. Click the **Copy AutoSolve AI Key** button in the dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23827816554391)

4\. Navigate to the **Profile** section in NSB. 

5\. Click the **Settings** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23827782116503)

6\. Click the **APIs** button. Input your **AutoSolve AI Key** in the AI API Key field. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23827816578327)

7\. Click the **Save** button. 

8\. Navigate to the **Captcha** section in NSB. Create a captcha solver and enable the AI toggle.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23827816584855)9\. Open the solver with the **Play** button. You have successfully configured AYCD with NSB.

# OneClick - AutoSolve - Account Metrics View for Solve Info – AYCD

OneClick - AutoSolve - Account Metrics View for Solve Info

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13320908391959-OneClick-AutoSolve-Account-Metrics-View-for-Solve-Info/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

March 23, 2023 19:13

Introduction
------------

In order to keep track of individual task performance during drops, you can switch to the Metrics view. This view displays information such as total solves and average solve time for each individual task. This is particularly useful in identifying which tasks may be struggling more than others, allowing you to adjust your setup accordingly.

Viewing Metrics![SPljLXVDrJ.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13320866653591)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Ov9rAXS9US.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13320870419735)

1\. Navigate over to the Accounts page in OneClick.  
2\. Click on the **View Type** dropdown on the top right and select **Metrics.**

Tips & tricks
-------------

![EditColumn.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13625960946711)

1\. By going to the Options menu on the top right, and clicking **Edit Columns** you can enable more captcha types to view metrics for.

# OneClick - AutoSolve - Recommended Mode – AYCD

OneClick - AutoSolve - Recommended Mode

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13746239754519-OneClick-AutoSolve-Recommended-Mode/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

April 10, 2023 23:41

Introduction
------------

The Recommended Mode helps you simplify your AutoSolve setup by automatically using the recommended AutoSolve settings that we have found give the best chance for success. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Recommended Mode will configure a majority of AutoSolve Settings to be what we have found the most successful for solving captchas.
*   Recommended Mode automatically rotates which solver is in use. You should have more Accounts / Solvers running than what you specify in the **Local Solver Limit** setting. This will enable your solvers to rotate automatically after they solve many captchas, leading to overall faster solving times.
*   You can read more on AutoSolve in the [AutoSolve Getting Started Guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094)
    .

Recommended Mode
----------------

1\. Navigate to the **AutoSolve | General** section in OneClick under Settings. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15259815290135)

2\. Click on the **AutoSolve Mode** dropdown, and select **Recommended**. This will automatically configure your AutoSolve Settings setup using the settings we have found to give the best success.

![AutoSolve-Automatic-Mode-1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13819862823703)

3\. Click on Save. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14414458777879)

Additional Settings
-------------------

Local Solver Limit: You can specify how many solvers run simultaneously with the **Local Solver Limit** setting. If this setting is set to 0, OneClick will automatically detect a safe number of solvers to run simultaneously.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13748208643479)

Max Manual Solves At Once: You can control when challenges are distributed to your third-party solvers with the **Max Manual Solves At Once** setting. This limit determines how many solvers showing _manual solve required_ before sending challenges to any of your enabled third-party solvers.  ![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13748213129623)

Max Running Accounts: If you changed the developer setting for **Max Running Accounts** in the Advanced tab under Settings, it is recommended to reduce that number to avoid overloading your system, as Solvers may farm and solve simultaneously.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13766723884311)

Cancel the AutoSolve task when you close the local browser solving it: If you want the AutoSolve task to cancel when you close the local browser solving it, you can click the **Cancel the AutoSolve task when you close the local browser solving it** checkbox. You should only select this option if you want better control over canceling difficult-to-solve tasks.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13748666711447)

Once you finish customizing your Settings, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14414468390167)

Starting AutoSolve Tasks
------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Accounts** section in OneClick. 

2\. Select the Solvers that you want to start with AutoSolve from the Accounts table. 

3\. Click on the **Options** button at the top right of the view or right-click on the table and click on **Start AutoSolve**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13770424079511)

4\. If you have the **Automatically Farm Solvers while using AutoSolve** setting enabled, your  Solvers will begin waiting for captchas with the farming cycles starting 5-10 minutes after that in the background.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15259825914519)

# Inbox - Inventory - Create/Import/Export – AYCD

Inbox - Inventory - Create/Import/Export

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14762796842519-Inbox-Inventory-Create-Import-Export/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 22, 2023 16:24

Introduction
------------

You can manually manage your inventory in Inbox by adding individual items or importing them in mass using the CSV format. You can also export items created to a CSV for use elsewhere.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You should use the Category system to your advantage to keep your inventory items organized.
*   You can import items you scraped from Scraped Data right into your Inventory.   
    

Create
------

1\. Navigate to the Inventory section of Inbox. 

2\. Click on **File** and then **Create**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14762486614167)

3\. By default, Inventory Items are created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Inventory Items created into a unique or existing category.

4\. Specify a **Name** for the Inventory Item. This is the only mandatory selection.

5\. Specify an **Order ID** for the Inventory Item. 

6\. Specify **Notes** for the Inventory Item.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/22204461063831)

7\. Specify the **Item Cost** of the Inventory Item.

8\. Specify the **Sale Price** of the Inventory Item.

9\. Specify an **Item URL** for the Inventory Item. This can be a direct product link, StockX link, Event link, or any other URL you want. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/22204461064727)

10\. Specify the **Tracking Number** of the Inventory Item. Inbox supports automatically updating Tracking statuses for numerous companies individually or in bulk. 

11\. Specify the **Delivery Company** of the Inventory Item. A list of supported Delivery Companies is below.

*   [AfterShip](https://www.aftership.com/)
    
*   [DHL](https://www.dhl.com/us-en/home/tracking.html)
    
*   [FedEx](https://www.fedex.com/en-us/tracking.html)
    
*   [LaserShip](https://lasershipp.com/)
    
*   [OnTrac](https://www.ontrac.com/)
    
*   [Sagawa](https://www.sagawa-exp.co.jp/english/)
    
*   [Shopify URL](https://www.shopify.com/)
     (Order Confirmation URLs)
*   [UPS](https://www.ups.com/us/en/Home.page)
    
*   [USPS](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input)
    
*   [Yamato](https://track.kuronekoyamato.co.jp/english/tracking)
    

12\. Specify the **Item Weight** of the Inventory Item.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/22204433332247)

13\. You can create **Custom Data** for your Inventory Item. This can be any data you want to use, such as email addresses, purchase dates, stores purchased from, and more.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/22204433335959)

14\. After configuring your Item, click the **Save** button to add the Item to your Inventory.

Import
------

1\. Navigate to the Inventory section of Inbox. 

2\. Click on **File** and then **Import**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14762957536279)

3\. By default, Inventory Items are imported in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to import your Inventory Items into a unique or existing category.

4\. Click the blue spreadsheet icon. This will open the file explorer, and you can select the CSV file you want to import. You can create a copy of the example format by clicking [here](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1QtfB0804pU_LyoqvLJalXb17KC1ZSDJLK_c9iojm1r8/copy)
.

5\. After configuring the Import Items dialog, click the **Import** button to add the items to your Inventory.

Export
------

1\. Navigate to the Inventory section of Inbox. 

2\. Select the Inventory items you want to export in the table.

3\. Click on **File** and then **Export**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14763156900759)

4\. Click the **Yes** button to export the Inventory Items you selected.

5\. View the exported Inventory Items file location by clicking the **Yes** button in the Completed - Export Items dialog. This will open the file explorer with your exported CSV file.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14763305803031)

# Inbox - Proxies – AYCD

Inbox - Proxies

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14714207498391-Inbox-Proxies/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 19, 2023 18:05

Introduction
------------

You can use proxies with modules in Inbox. You can use them for tracking shipments and when using the Unsubscribe Tool.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You should use proxies when updating the shipping status of Inventory items or when you use the Automatically Update Tracking Info option.

Adding Proxies
--------------

1\. Navigate to the **Proxies** tab within Inbox.

2\. Click the **Create** button. This will open a dialog for you to input your proxies.

3. By default, Proxies are created to the All **Category.** You can override this and specify a category name to have your Proxies created in or use the dropdown to assign them to an existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14745614970519)

4\. Once you have finished inputting your proxies, you can click on **Save**, and you will see your proxies in the Proxies table.

Testing & Copying Proxies
-------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Proxies** tab within Inbox.

2\. Select the Proxies from the table that you want the test.

3\. Use the **Target** dropdown to specify which website you want to test your proxies on. If the website you want to test is not listed in the dropdown menu, select **Custom** to pick any website. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14745619577623)

4\. After testing your proxies, their speed will be displayed under the Page Load (ms) column.

5\. You can use the **Delete Dead Proxies** option to remove any proxies that have timed out or have connection issues.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14745620236951)

6\. You can use the **Delete Slow Proxies** option to remove any proxies that are not fast enough for your use case. After selecting the option, specify a maximum acceptable ping in milliseconds. Any proxies testing speeds above the ping you specify will be removed.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14745650526103)

7\. You can use the **Delete Duplicate Proxies** option to remove any duplicate proxies.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14745644979479)

8\. After you have tested your proxies and filtered them out, if you want to use them outside of Inbox, use the **Copy to Clipboard** button under **Options** or by selecting your proxies, right-clicking, and choosing the **Copy to Clipboard** option to copy your proxies to your clipboard.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14745675553943)

9\. Use the **Test Localhost** button to test the speed of your local network.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14745670667543)

Now you have added proxies to Inbox. You can test your proxy speeds and use them with many modules in Inbox.

# Inbox - Mail - Emails - Tools - Delete Emails from Mail Server – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Emails - Tools - Delete Emails from Mail Server

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14767519235735-Inbox-Mail-Emails-Tools-Delete-Emails-from-Mail-Server/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 22, 2023 19:02

Introduction
------------

The Delete Emails from Mail Server Tool allows you to delete selected emails from the mail server and remove them from Inbox.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Before using the Delete Emails from Mail Server Tool, you must have synced mail to Inbox.
*   Depending on your email account settings, this may only archive the mail.
*   This action cannot be undone.

Delete Emails from Mail Server
------------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Emails section of Inbox in Mail. This section has an envelope icon titled **Emails** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the emails you want to delete from the mail server in the table.

3\. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view or right-click on the table and click on **Tools**. This will bring up the **Tools** dialog for Inbox.

4\. Click the dropdown and choose **Delete Emails from Mail Server**.

5\. Click **Start**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14767818309655)

6\. A dialog will open to confirm you want to delete the selected emails from the mail server. To proceed, click the **Yes** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14767820025495)

7\. You can cancel the Delete Emails from Mail Server task by clicking the **Cancel** button.

8\. You can hide the task status dialog by clicking the **Run in Background** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14767820472343)

9\. You can view the status of all your Inbox tasks by clicking the **Running Background Tasks** button on the top right of Inbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14767949942551)

10\. Once it has been successfully deleted, a dialog will appear showing it was completed successfully.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14767946564503)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Configure Priority Monitoring – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Configure Priority Monitoring

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14775521510167-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Configure-Priority-Monitoring/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



============================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 23, 2023 01:25

Introduction
------------

You can configure Prioity Monitoring to monitor credentials at a faster rate than what's specified in Settings. This is useful when you have time-sensitive Emails that you need to view, such as raffle wins, OTP codes, and more.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To use Priority Monitoring, you need to add Mail Credentials to Inbox.
*   You should only use Priority Monitoring with credentials that contain time-sensitive Emails. 

Configure Priority Monitoring
-----------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Credentials section of Inbox in Mail. This section has an icon that looks like a book with a person on it titled **Credentials** when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **Options** button at the top right of the view, then the **Configure Priority Monitoring** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14776006731671)

3\. You can select which mail credentials you want to monitor activity on at a faster refresh interval in the **Configure Mail Monitoring** dialog with the checkboxes.

4\. You can specify the **Refresh Interval** in seconds your mail credentials are checked for new emails. This value must be at least 15 seconds.

5\. After configuring the Mail Monitoring dialog, click the **Save** button.

6\. A dialog confirming the amount of Mail Credentials to be actively monitored and the refresh interval you set will appear.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14776209572375)

# Inbox - Mail - Scraper Data - Tools - Send to Inventory – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Scraper Data - Tools - Send to Inventory

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14776534402071-Inbox-Mail-Scraper-Data-Tools-Send-to-Inventory/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==========================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 23, 2023 03:14

Introduction
------------

You can use the Send to Inventory Tool to populate the Inventory section of Inbox with your Scraper Data. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You must have information populated in the Scraper Data section of Inbox. You can learn more about how to add information to Scraper Data here.
*   You can select multiple scraper templates to aggregate your data. One way you can use this to your advantage is to aggregate Order and Shipping confirmations. This ensures that your order number, tracking number, product information, and other relevant information can be included.

Send to Inventory
-----------------

1\. Navigate over to the Scraper Data section of Inbox in Mail. This section has an icon with three lines and dots titled **Scraper Data** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Scraper Data items that you want to create Inventory items for from the table.

3\. Click on the Tools button at the top right of the view or right-click on the table and click on Tools. This will bring up the Tools dialog for scraped data.

4\. Click on the dropdown and choose **Send to Inventory**.

5\. Click **Start**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14776332181399)

6\. By specifying data keys, You can configure the Send to Inventory dialog to match your data to the various Inventory fields. 

7\. By default, Inventory Items are created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Inventory Items created into a unique or existing category. 

8\. To prevent creating duplicate Inventory items, you can select a **Unique Inventory Data Key**. For this example, I use my Order ID since each of my Inventory Items has a unique Order ID. If a duplicate Inventory Item is found, the Inventory item will be updated. If no duplicate items are found, a new Inventory item will be created.

9\. Specify which data key to use for the **Name** of the Inventory Item. For example, for Nike, one of the elements I use in my Scraper Template is the Product. Therefore, I will use that as my data key for the Name field. 

10\. Specify which data key to use for the **Item URL** of the Inventory Item. This can be a direct product link, StockX link, Event link, or any other URL you want. 

11\. Specify which data key to use for the **Notes** of the Inventory Item. This can be any useful information you want.

12\. Specify which data key to use for the **Item Cost** of the Inventory Item. 

13\. Specify which data key to use for the **Tracking Number** of the Inventory Item.

14\. Optionally, specify **Custom Data Keys**. This data will appear in the Inventory table view as a column. You can keep track of any additional data we do not support by default. For this example, I specify Size and Address for my Custom Data Keys.

15\. When you have finished configuring the Send to Inventory dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15424438403223)

16\. Your data will populate in the Inventory tab based on the configuration you provided in the previous dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15424771384727)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Update Monitored Folders – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Update Monitored Folders

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14800082928663-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Update-Monitored-Folders/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 23, 2023 23:34

Introduction
------------

You can use the Update Monitored Folders option to adjust which folders Inbox monitors through IMAP. This is useful if you have sorting rules setup on your mail accounts and only want to monitor for emails on specific labels.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Before updating monitored folders, you must Mail Credentials added to your Inbox. Learn how to add Mail Credentials to Inbox here. 
*   Updating monitored folders can ensure that you never miss an email. If some of your emails that you want to view in Inbox go to a particular folder, ensure you select it when updating monitored folders. 

Updated Monitored Folders
-------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Credentials section of Inbox in Mail. This section has an icon that looks like a book with a person on it titled **Credentials** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Email Credentials that you want to modify the Monitored Folders for from the table.

3\. Click the **Tools** button at the top right of the view, then select the **Update Monitored Folders** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31040883416087)

4\. You can select which folders you want Inbox to monitor. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31040938566807)

5\. After configuring the Update Monitored Folders dialog, click the **Update** button.

# Inbox - Mail - Emails - Tools - Download Email Body – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Emails - Tools - Download Email Body

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14790362547223-Inbox-Mail-Emails-Tools-Download-Email-Body/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 23, 2023 17:16

Introduction
------------

You can download selected emails to your computer using the Download Email Body Tool. This will allow you to search for specific text from the summary of the email body.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Before using the Download Email Body Tool, you must have synced mail to Inbox.
*   Using the Download Email Body Tool will take up additional space on your computer as the data is stored there.

Download Email Body
-------------------

1. Navigate over to the Emails section of Inbox in Mail. This section has an envelope icon titled **Emails** when you hover over it.

2. Select the emails you want to download the email body from in the table.

3\. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view or right-click on the table and click on **Tools**. This will bring up the **Tools** dialog for Inbox.

4\. Click the dropdown and choose **Download Email Body**.

5\. Click **Start**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14790763547799)

6\. You can view the status of all your Inbox tasks by clicking the **Running Background Tasks** button on the top right of Inbox. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14792300156183)

7\. A Completed - Download Email Body dialog will appear once the operation is finished, confirming the operation was successful.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14790995627287)
