# Inbox - Mail - Scraper Data - View/Import/Export – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Scraper Data - View/Import/Export

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14789019466007-Inbox-Mail-Scraper-Data-View-Import-Export/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 23, 2023 16:33

Introduction
------------

You can import/export your Scraper Data via CSV format. You can easily import existing data and use the Send to Inventory Tool to populate your Inventory. Learn more about the Send to Inventory Tool here.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To Import, the first row in your CSV must contain headers. If you need an example of the CSV, please use the file -> Export option with no items selected to export an example file.

View Scraper Data
-----------------

1\. Navigate to the Scraper Data section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, you will see an icon with three lines and dots titled **Scraper Data**.

2\. Click the Scraper Templates dropdown button. The Choose Scraper Templates dialog will appear.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27396808998679)

3\. Select the scraper template(s) you want to view. Then, click the **Save** button.

4\. Your scraper data will appear in the tableview. 

5\. You can choose multiple Scraper Templates to aggregate your data in one table view. The Combine Data Key dropdown can group your scraper templates by a common element. For example, in the image below, I am grouping my Supreme Order and Shipping confirmations by the Order Number. The Order Number is the same across both the Order and Shipping confirmations, so the order confirmation will be grouped with the matching shipping confirmation.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27396762308631)

Import Scraper Data
-------------------

1\. Navigate to the Scraper Data section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, you will see an icon with three lines and dots titled **Scraper Data**.

2\. Click the **File** button, then click **Import**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14789155778711)

3\. Click the blue spreadsheet icon. This will open the file explorer, and you can select the CSV file you want to import.

4\. After selecting your CSV file, click the **Import** button.

5\. The completed dialog confirms your data was successfully imported.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14789209740695)

Export Scraper Data
-------------------

1\. Navigate to the Scraper Data section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, you will see an icon with three lines and dots titled **Scraper Data**.

2\. Select the Scraper Data you want to export in the table.

3\. Click the **File** button, then click **Export**.

4\. Click the **Yes** button in the Export Items dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14789307706519)

4\. Click the Yes button in the Completed—Export Items dialog to view the exported Scraper Data file location. This will open the file explorer with your exported CSV file.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14789315735959)

# OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Tools - Change Password – AYCD

OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Tools - Change Password

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13255371831191-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Tools-Change-Password/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



======================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/1500080569202/crw1.png)

**Crewie**

March 21, 2023 13:39

Introduction
------------

The Change Password feature will allow you to update your Accounts' passwords quickly. This is a valuable security measure to ensure that only you can access your accounts.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Before changing your accounts' passwords, you must [add accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058782173#h_01EPF75YH4B6PB4WVSN5P7NJ10)
     to OneClick. After adding the accounts, you can use Account Tools.
*   We recommend farming your accounts for at least a week before changing any account information.

Change Password
---------------

1\. Navigate over to the **Tasks > Accounts / Solvers** page in OneClick

2\. Select the accounts whose passwords you want to change in the table.

3\. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view, or right-click on the table and click on **Tools**. This will bring up the **Tools** dialog for accounts.

4\. Click the dropdown and choose **Change Password**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092221146647)

5\. Click the **Start** button.

6\. The **Account Verification Options** dialog will appear. The general tab displays some information related to the dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092237291159)

7\. You can select Mail Credentials to use for account recovery. OneClick will use your mail credentials if Google asks you to verify a code to your recovery email. If you configure this dialog, OneClick will automate this process.

*   You must create a Mail Task Template in Inbox and add your Inbox API key to OneClick > Settings > Inbox Service. You can find your Inbox API key in Settings > Tasks. Follow the guide below to create a Mail Task Template.
*   You must add your mail credentials that receive your recovery email codes to Inbox, and then to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to add your mail credentials to Inbox](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    .
*   [To use Inbox Mail, follow this guide to configure a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    

8\. You can configure the **Mail Timeout in Minutes Override** field to configure the default timeout for waiting for OTP codes.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092237291543)

9\. You can select SMS credentials to use for account recovery. OneClick will use your SMS credentials if Google asks for a one-time SMS code when changing your recovery email.

*   [If you want to use Local SMS, follow this guide to add your SMS credentials.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19514938436119)
    
*   [If you want to use Inbox SMS, follow this guide to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    

10\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Next** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092221147799)

6\. The **Change Account Password** dialog will appear. You can leave the Custom Password field blank for a random password or input a specific password; once you have decided, click the **Run** button.

![2023-03-21_13_46_07-Change_Account_Password.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/13255586371479)

7\. The operation will start, and the Action Log will update as it performs the job. If the task is successful, the status will show **Password Changed**.

8\. To access the new passwords, you can double-click on the Account; the new password will be shown in the Password field.

# Inbox - Mail - Task Templates - Create Mail Task Template – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Task Templates - Create Mail Task Template

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855-Inbox-Mail-Task-Templates-Create-Mail-Task-Template/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/1500080569202/crw1.png)

**Crewie**

May 24, 2023 20:10

Introduction
------------

The Inbox API uses task templates. Your templates can contain one or more Mail Credentials, with a limit of 20 in a singular template. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To create a Mail Task Template, you must first add Mail Credentials. You can read how to add credentials [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    .
*   Task Templates are useful when you are using catchall emails (email credentials that receive emails from various email addresses) and want to use them to confirm your OTP login codes on sites such as Outlook/Hotmail and Nike.

Enabling the Inbox API
----------------------

1\. Navigate to the Settings > Tasks section of Inbox. 

2\. Click the **Enable Tasks** checkbox.

3\. Click the **Save** button. Inbox will show a dialog that says the application needs to be restarted. Click the **Yes** button on that dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25870239470359)

4\. Navigate to the Settings > Tasks section of Inbox. Your Inbox API Key will appear. You can copy the API key and input it into applications that support the Inbox API. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25870194798103)

Creating a Task Template
------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Task Templates section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with two lines on it appears titled **Task Templates**.

2\. Click the **Create** button at the top left of the view.  
![2023-05-24_21_03_45-Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14820928607383)  

3\. This opens the Create Mail Task Template dialog. Input the Name of the Category you'd like to add, then input the Name of the Template in the field below Name.

4\. On the right-hand side, select the Mail Credentials. You can expand the category by clicking the arrow icon(>). Click on the box to select the credentials you want in this template.  
![2023-05-24_21_07_35-Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14821056171543)  

5\. Your template will then be created; if needed, you can edit it by clicking on it and then clicking Edit.  
![2023-05-24_21_09_19-Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14821113719831)

# OneClick - Accounts - Account Tools - Add to Mail – AYCD

OneClick - Accounts - Account Tools - Add to Mail

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13320050297239-OneClick-Accounts-Account-Tools-Add-to-Mail/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

March 23, 2023 18:47

Introduction
------------

The Add to Mail Tool enables you to send your Gmail accounts to the Mail section of OneClick. This allows you to transfer your IMAP Mail Credentials between Toolbox applications easily.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To use the Add to Mail Tool, you must enable 2FA on your account. If you don’t already, you can use the [Enable 2FA Tool](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13361546549527)
    .
*   The Add to Mail Tool will automatically create an App Password for you, which is required to access IMAP when using Gmail accounts.

Add to Mail
-----------

1\. Navigate to the **Accounts/Solvers** section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a play button titled Accounts/Solvers.

2\. Select the accounts you want to add to Mail, then click the Tools button. Select the Add to Mail Tool and click the **Start** button.

3\. A dialog will appear asking if you wish to proceed and mentioning that an app password will be generated. Click **Yes** if you want to proceed.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19530086530455)

6\. The action log will display the status, **Added to Mail** in green, once the task is finished successfully.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19530086535575)

7\. [Now, you can easily transfer your Mail Credentials across Toolbox applications such as Inbox, TabSentry, and Profile Builder! Click here to learn more.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19515035829271)

# Inbox - Inventory - Expenses - Create/Import/Export – AYCD

Inbox - Inventory - Expenses - Create/Import/Export

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/15213382771607-Inbox-Inventory-Expenses-Create-Import-Export/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

June 10, 2023 18:29

Introduction
------------

You can create and manage Expenses with Inbox.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can use the Send to Expenses Tool in Scraper Data to populate information such as receipts for Proxies, Servers, Cook Groups, Bots, and more.

Create
------

1\. Navigate to the Expenses section in Inventory. This section has an icon that looks like a sheet with a $ on it titled **Expenses**.

2\. Click on the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Create** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15213944340631)

3\. By default, Expenses are created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Expense Item created into a unique or existing category.

4\. Specify a **Name** for the Expense.

5\. Specify the **Date** for the Expense.

6\. Input **Notes** related to the Expense.

7\. Click the **New Property** button in the Items section. Input the name of the Expense and the price of the Expense. You can create multiple Expense Items within one Expense. 

8\. Once you have finished configuring your Expense, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15214089859607)

Import
------

1\. Navigate to the Expenses section in Inventory. This section has an icon that looks like a sheet with a $ on it titled **Expenses**.

2\. Click on the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Import** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15214113811223)

3\. By default, Expenses are imported in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Expense Items imported into a unique or existing category.

4\. Click the blue spreadsheet icon. This will open the file explorer, and you can select the CSV file you want to import.

5\. After configuring the Import Items dialog, click the **Import** button to add the items to your Expenses.

Export
------

1\. Navigate to the Expenses section in Inventory. This section has an icon that looks like a sheet with a $ on it titled **Expenses**.

2\. Select the Expense items you want to export in the table.

3\. Click on the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Export** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15214361989783)

4\. Click the **Yes** button to export the Expense Items you selected.

5\. View the exported Expense Items file location by clicking the **Yes** button in the Completed - Export Items dialog. This will open the file explorer with your exported CSV file.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15214368273815)

# OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Account Tools - Enable 2FA / Disable 2FA / Remove Security Keys – AYCD

OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Account Tools - Enable 2FA / Disable 2FA / Remove Security Keys

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13361546549527-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Account-Tools-Enable-2FA-Disable-2FA-Remove-Security-Keys/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")





![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

March 25, 2023 14:29

Introduction
------------

OneClick includes many Tools that allow you to manage 2FA on your selected accounts quickly and without a phone number. This will save you lots of time when managing your accounts.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Before enabling 2FA, you must [add accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16222455171479)
     to OneClick. After adding the accounts, you can use Account Tools.
*   We recommend farming your accounts for at least a week and for them to be at least a year old before using Account Tools on them.
*   The **Enable 2FA Tool** will add 2FA to your account via Authenticator/TOTP. The Enable 2FA Tool does not require any phone numbers.
*   The **Disable 2FA Tool** will remove every 2FA Authenticator/TOTP from your account. It will not remove other forms of 2FA, such as security keys, SMS, or email.
*   The **Remove Security Keys Tool** will remove all security keys from your account. You should only use this if the Enable 2FA Tool fails and a security key is still on your account.

Enable 2FA
----------

1\. Navigate to the **Accounts/Solvers** section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a play button titled Accounts/Solvers.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to use the Enable 2FA Tool, then click the Tools button. Select the Enable 2FA Tool and click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19532564518679)

3\. The **Account Verification Options** dialog will appear. The general tab displays some information related to the dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092185110295)

4\. You can select Mail Credentials to use for account recovery. OneClick will use your mail credentials if Google asks you to verify a code to your recovery email. If you configure this dialog, OneClick will automate this process.

*   You must create a Mail Task Template in Inbox and add your Inbox API key to OneClick > Settings > Inbox Service. You can find your Inbox API key in Settings > Tasks. Follow the guide below to create a Mail Task Template.
*   You must add your mail credentials that receive your recovery email codes to Inbox, and then to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to add your mail credentials to Inbox](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    .
*   [To use Inbox Mail, follow this guide to configure a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    

5\. You can configure the **Mail Timeout in Minutes Override** field to configure the default timeout for waiting for OTP codes.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092185110551)

6\. You can select SMS credentials to use for account recovery. OneClick will use your SMS credentials if Google asks for a one-time SMS code when changing your recovery email.

*   [If you want to use Local SMS, follow this guide to add your SMS credentials.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19514938436119)
    
*   [If you want to use Inbox SMS, follow this guide to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    

7\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Next** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/22943891986583)

8\. Once you have finished configuring the dialog, click the **Next** button. The Enable 2FA dialog will appear. If you want to proceed, click the **Yes** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092185110935)

9\. Once the job is completed, the Action Log will reflect whether it was successful. If any accounts run into errors, the Action Log will reflect the error, and the Status will provide some information on the issue. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19532564521111)

Disable 2FA
-----------

1\. Navigate to the **Accounts/Solvers** section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a play button titled Accounts/Solvers.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to use the Disable 2FA Tool, then click the Tools button. Select the Disable 2FA Tool and click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19533091165591)

3\. The **Account Verification Options** dialog will appear. The general tab displays some information related to the dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092185110295)

4\. You can select Mail Credentials to use for account recovery. OneClick will use your mail credentials if Google asks you to verify a code to your recovery email. If you configure this dialog, OneClick will automate this process.

*   You must create a Mail Task Template in Inbox and add your Inbox API key to OneClick > Settings > Inbox Service. You can find your Inbox API key in Settings > Tasks. Follow the guide below to create a Mail Task Template.
*   You must add your mail credentials that receive your recovery email codes to Inbox, and then to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to add your mail credentials to Inbox](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    .
*   [To use Inbox Mail, follow this guide to configure a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    

5\. You can configure the **Mail Timeout in Minutes Override** field to configure the default timeout for waiting for OTP codes.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092185110551)

6\. You can select SMS credentials to use for account recovery. OneClick will use your SMS credentials if Google asks for a one-time SMS code when changing your recovery email.

*   [If you want to use Local SMS, follow this guide to add your SMS credentials.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19514938436119)
    
*   [If you want to use Inbox SMS, follow this guide to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    

7\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Next** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/22943891986583)

8\. The Disable 2FA dialog will appear. If you want to proceed, click the **Yes** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19533144269847)

9\. Once the job is completed, the Action Log will reflect whether it was successful. If any accounts run into errors, the Action Log will reflect the error, and the Status will provide some information on the issue.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19533091167255)

Remove Security Keys
--------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Accounts/Solvers** section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a play button titled Accounts/Solvers.

2\. Select the accounts from which you want to remove security keys, then click the Tools button. Select the Remove Security Key Tool and click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19533144271127)

3\. The **Account Verification Options** dialog will appear. The general tab displays some information related to the dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092185110295)

4\. You can select Mail Credentials to use for account recovery. OneClick will use your mail credentials if Google asks you to verify a code to your recovery email. If you configure this dialog, OneClick will automate this process.

*   You must create a Mail Task Template in Inbox and add your Inbox API key to OneClick > Settings > Inbox Service. You can find your Inbox API key in Settings > Tasks. Follow the guide below to create a Mail Task Template.
*   You must add your mail credentials that receive your recovery email codes to Inbox, and then to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to add your mail credentials to Inbox](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    .
*   [To use Inbox Mail, follow this guide to configure a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    

5\. You can configure the **Mail Timeout in Minutes Override** field to configure the default timeout for waiting for OTP codes.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092185110551)

6\. You can select SMS credentials to use for account recovery. OneClick will use your SMS credentials if Google asks for a one-time SMS code when changing your recovery email.

*   [If you want to use Local SMS, follow this guide to add your SMS credentials.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19514938436119)
    
*   [If you want to use Inbox SMS, follow this guide to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    

7\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Next** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/22943891986583)

8\. The Remove Security Keys dialog will appear. If you want to proceed, click the **Yes** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19533091171607)

9\. Once the job is completed, the Action Log will reflect whether it was successful. If any accounts run into errors, the Action Log will reflect the error, and the Status will provide some information on the issue.

# Profile Builder - Accounts - Tasks - Copy to Clipboard Settings – AYCD

Profile Builder - Accounts - Tasks - Copy to Clipboard Settings

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13909056274199-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-Copy-to-Clipboard-Settings/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



============================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

April 17, 2023 00:41

Introduction
------------

You can export your Account tasks in many different copy-paste formats by configuring your Copy to Clipboard Settings. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can configure your Copy to Clipboard settings to match your bot's account import format. This lets you easily export your account information from Profile Builder into various bots and programs, regardless of the format.
*   You can easily copy the email from your accounts to add to profiles with the [Mass Distribute Tool](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/21341444931607)
     or to create accounts in Profile Builder. 

Copy to Clipboard Settings
--------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Click on **Options** and then **Copy to Clipboard Settings**. This will open the Copy to Clipboard Settings dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28380592282775)

3\. Click the **Add** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28380592284311)

4\. Specify a **Configuration Name**.

5\. Specify which **Values** to include when copying to the clipboard. You can select Values such as Username, Password, Proxy, CC information, 2FA Secret Key and more.

6\. Specify which **Separator** to use when copying to the clipboard. This separator varies based on the bot you are using. For example, the separator AYCD uses is :

7\. You can add additional Values and Separators with the **Add** button. You can remove Values and Separators with the **Remove** button.

8\. You can view an example of your configuration in the **Example** box.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28380561958167)

**Examples:**

\- If your bots account import format is username:password:proxy, you can configure your Copy to Clipboard settings based on the screenshot below.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16207592119063)

\- If you want to extract your account username to generate accounts via a list of emails or use the Distribute Email feature in Profiles, you can configure your Copy to Clipboard settings based on the screenshot below.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16207592120087)

\- If you want to extract your account username, password, CVV, and proxy to import accounts to Quickcop, you can configure your Copy to Clipboard settings based on the screenshots below.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16207575115543)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16207553642903)

9\. After configuring your settings, click the **Save** button. Then click the **Save** button in the Select Configuration dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28380561959191)

10\. Select the account(s) you wish to export. Right-click and then click the **Copy to Clipboard** button. Your accounts will be copied in the format you configured in the Copy to Clipboard Settings.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28380592288407)

# TabSentry - Proxies – AYCD

TabSentry - Proxies

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18203862924311-TabSentry-Proxies/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



========================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/1500080569202/crw1.png)

**Crewie**

October 11, 2023 20:11

Introduction
------------

You can use proxies within the Tasks module in TabSentry. When you add proxies you can assign them to your Tasks.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Different types of proxies work on various sites! If you need help deciding which proxies to use, consult with a cook group such as [AYCD Pro](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/aycdpro?referralCode=ZENDESK)
    . 

Adding Proxies
--------------

1\. Navigate to the **Proxies** tab within TabSentry.

2\. Click the **Create** button. This will open a dialog for you to input your proxies.

3\. By default, Proxies are created to the All **Category.** You can override this and specify a category name to have your Proxies created in or use the dropdown to assign them to an existing category.

![Screenshot 2023-10-11 205901.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18203902986135)

4\. Once you have finished inputting your proxies, you can click on **Save**, and you will see your proxies in the Proxies table.

Testing & Copying Proxies
-------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Proxies** tab within TabSentry.

2\. Select the Proxies from the table that you want the test.

3\. Use the **Target** dropdown to specify which website you want to test your proxies on. If the website you want to test is not listed in the dropdown menu, select **Custom** and input a URL to test them on any website. ![2023-10-11 21_05_55-Clipboard.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18203847594135)

![2023-10-11 21_07_04-Clipboard.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18203888637335)

4\. Choose which test you want to use on your proxies. **Ping** is the most commonly used testing type.

![Screenshot 2023-10-11 211109.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18204189645719)  
5\. After testing your proxies, their speed will be displayed under the Page Load (ms) column.

6\. You can use the **Delete Dead Proxies** option to remove any proxies that have timed out or have connection issues.

![Screenshot 2023-10-11 211245.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18204189652887)

7\. You can use the **Delete Slow Proxies** option to remove any proxies that are not fast enough for your use case. After selecting the option, specify a maximum acceptable ping in milliseconds. Any proxies testing speeds above the ping you specify will be removed.

![Screenshot 2023-10-11 211437.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18204189657239)

8\. After you have tested your proxies and filtered them out, if you want to use them outside of TabSentry, use the **Copy to Clipboard** button under **Options** or by selecting your proxies, right-clicking, and choosing the **Copy to Clipboard** option to copy your proxies to your clipboard.  
![2023-10-11 21_16_18-Clipboard.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18204189662487)

9\. Use the **Test Localhost** button to test the speed of your local network.  
![2023-10-11 21_18_01-Clipboard.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18204189668759)

Now you have added proxies to TabSentry. You can test your proxy speeds and use them with many modules in TabSentry.

# TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Multi Browser Control (MBC) – AYCD

TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Multi Browser Control (MBC)

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18200568034967-TabSentry-Tasks-Browsers-Multi-Browser-Control-MBC/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

October 11, 2023 18:27

Introduction
------------

You can setup Multi Browser Control (MBC) to mimic keystrokes and mouse movements across multiple browsers simultaneously. This feature minimizes the time required for tedious clicking across multiple browsers.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Multi Browser Control can be configured on existing tasks by editing them or configuring newly generated tasks.

Creating New Tasks With Multi Browser Control
---------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Tab Sentry. 

2\. Click on **File** and then click either **Create One Task** or **Create Many Tasks**.

![1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18200578715287)

3\. Configure the task generation dialogue and enter an MBC Group Name

![2.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18201274365463)

4\. Click **Save** to finish creating the tasks.

5\. Start your tasks

6\. Select **MBC** in the task assistant menu and click **Start MBC** 

![3.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18201261943447)

7\. After doing so the keystrokes and mouse actions will be replicated on the other tasks within that MBC Group.

![MBC.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18201274378775)

Configuring Existing Tasks To Enable Multi Browser Control
----------------------------------------------------------

1\. Right click the tasks in the Tasks menu.

2\. Click **Edit** 

![4.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18201308065687)

3\. Go to the MBC section and enter a Group Name

![5.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18201261971607)

4\. Click **Save**.

Tips and Tricks
---------------

The Task Assistant will indicate in the MBC Status section whether the browser in focus is the Child or Parent; the parent browser is the one  
whose interactions will be mirrored by the child.

![6.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18201444412567)

# TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Browser Data - Clear Browser Data / Import Cookies / Export Cookies / Clear Cookies – AYCD

TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Browser Data - Clear Browser Data / Import Cookies / Export Cookies / Clear Cookies

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18233308688279-TabSentry-Tasks-Browsers-Browser-Data-Clear-Browser-Data-Import-Cookies-Export-Cookies-Clear-Cookies/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")





![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/1500080569202/crw1.png)

**Crewie**

October 12, 2023 21:16

Introduction
------------

The browser data options available on the right-click menu can be especially helpful when setting up your tasks. They can be used to avoid creating new tasks by clearing browser data or cookies, importing cookies, or exporting cookies.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You must have already created tasks for these options.

Clear Browser Data
------------------

1. Navigate over to the Browsers section of TabSentry in Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled **Browsers** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Tasks from the table you want to Clear Browser Data for. You can use CTRL + Click, Shift + Click, or click the box on the left-hand side.

3\. Right-click, select Browser Data, then **Clear Browser Data**.  
![Screenshot 2023-10-17 174408.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18337466752919)

Import Cookies
--------------

1. Navigate over to the Browsers section of TabSentry in Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled **Browsers** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Tasks from the table you want to Import Cookies for. You can use CTRL + Click, Shift + Click, or click the box on the left-hand side to select multiple items.

3\. Start or ensure your tasks are currently running. You cannot Import Cookies to tasks that are not running.

3\. Right-click, select Browser Data, then **Import Cookies**.

![Screenshot 2023-10-17 174722.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18337434456599)

4\. Paste the cookies in a JSON format into the Import Cookies dialog.  
5\. Click Import.  
6\. The following dialog will state whether the cookies were successfully imported.

Export Cookies
--------------

1. Navigate over to the Browsers section of TabSentry in Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled **Browsers** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the task from the table you want to Export Cookies for. You can only Export Cookies from running tasks one browser at a time.

3\. Right-click, select Browser Data, then **Export Cookies** from the menu.![Screenshot 2023-10-17 213259.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18345106921623)

4\. A dialog will then appear with the cookies if the export is successful.

Clear Cookies
-------------

1. Navigate over to the Browsers section of TabSentry in Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled **Browsers** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Tasks from the table you want to Clear the Cookies for.

3\. Right-click, select Browser Data, then **Clear Cookies** from the menu.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18345082870423)

4\. This will then clear the cookies from your browser.

# Inbox - Inventory - Tools - Update Tracking Info – AYCD

Inbox - Inventory - Tools - Update Tracking Info

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14763577067927-Inbox-Inventory-Tools-Update-Tracking-Info/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 22, 2023 16:51

Introduction
------------

You can update the tracking status on your Inventory Items with the Update Tracking Info button. Inbox supports automatically updating Tracking statuses for numerous companies individually or in bulk. A list of supported Delivery Companies is below.

*   [AfterShip](https://www.aftership.com/)
    
*   [DHL](https://www.dhl.com/us-en/home/tracking.html)
    
*   [FedEx](https://www.fedex.com/en-us/tracking.html)
    
*   [LaserShip](https://lasershipp.com/)
    
*   [OnTrac](https://www.ontrac.com/)
    
*   [Sagawa](https://www.sagawa-exp.co.jp/english/)
    
*   [Shopify URL](https://www.shopify.com/)
     (Order Confirmation URLs)
*   [UPS](https://www.ups.com/us/en/Home.page)
    
*   [USPS](https://tools.usps.com/go/TrackConfirmAction_input)
    
*   [Yamato](https://track.kuronekoyamato.co.jp/english/tracking)
    
*   [YunExpress](https://www.yuntrack.com/)
    

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You should use proxies to avoid being rate-limited by the carrier website if you are tracking multiple shipments.
*   You can automatically update tracking info by enabling this option in [Settings](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)
    . 
*   You can add an AYCD/Discord Webhook to Settings to receive a notification of the status of your tracking number.
*   [To update tracking info, you need to add Inventory Items to Inbox. Click here to learn how to do this.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14762796842519)
    

Update Tracking Info
--------------------

1\. Navigate to the Inventory section of Inbox.

2\. Select the Inventory Items you want to update the tracking status on.

3\. Click on the Tools button at the top right of the view or right-click on the table and click on Tools. This will bring up the Tools dialog for Inventory.

4\. Click on the dropdown and choose **Update Tracking Info**. If you want to use proxies when updating tracking information, ensure you set a category in Settings > Inventory.

5\. Click **Start**.

![Tools_Update_Info.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15027712206871)

6\. Inbox will update the tracking info status in the table view. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14884340705687)

# Inbox - SMS - Credentials - Adding SMS Credentials  – AYCD

Inbox - SMS - Credentials - Adding SMS Credentials

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14818934003735-Inbox-SMS-Credentials-Adding-SMS-Credentials/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/1500080569202/crw1.png)

**Crewie**

May 24, 2023 19:04

Introduction
------------

SMS Credentials are used in the SMS section of the Inbox to be used either to get numbers locally or by using the API. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   The setup for all the providers is different, so we have created separate sections of the guide for each provider. You can access the guide [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4405583780631)
    .
*   I highly recommend reviewing the guides found in the Inbox section of Zendesk. These guides contain all the information you need to use Inbox. 

Adding SMS Credentials
----------------------

![2023-05-24_20_01_49-Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14818831048983)

1\. Navigate over to the Credentials section within SMS in Inbox. This section has an icon that looks like a book with a person on it titled **Credentials** when you hover over it.

2\. Click on the **Create** button at the top left of the view.

3\. This opens the Create Provider Credentials dialog. You can select the Provider from the drop-down and include the necessary information for your specific provider. The [SMS Credentials Guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4405583780631)
 covers how to find this information for each Provider. 

4\. Once you have added the information, click **Save**.  
![2023-05-24_19_55_12-Window.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14818628584343)

5\. Inbox will try to check the Balance to verify whether the credentials are valid, and it'll display a Status Message along with the balance found.

With SMS Provider Credentials added to Inbox, you can create Task Templates.

# TabSentry - Features - Configs - Auto Refresh – AYCD

TabSentry - Features - Configs - Auto Refresh

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18200084745623-TabSentry-Features-Configs-Auto-Refresh/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



========================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

October 11, 2023 18:11

Introduction
------------

You can create Feature configurations in TabSentry to amplify your Tasks capabilities. With the Auto Refresh Feature, you can automatically refresh your tasks based on a time range.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   The data in all Toolbox programs is secured and encrypted automatically. Given the sensitive nature of Billing and Credit Card information, you can further secure data in TabSentry with a [Password Lock](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7446003478039)
    .
*   Use categories to simplify organization.
*   Use a large Auto Refresh Interval to have a better chance of going undetected by anti-bot.

Auto Refresh
------------

1\. Navigate over to the Configs section of TabSentry in Features. This section has an icon that looks like a bullseye with an arrow in it titled **Configs** when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **Create** button. This will open the Choose Task Feature dialog. Select **Auto Refresh** in the dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20723767551127)

3\. Click Next.

4\. By default, Features are created to the **All Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Features created in or use the dropdown to assign them to an existing category.

5\. Specify a **Name** for the Feature. 

6\. Optionally, you can specify URLs that the Feature should apply to in the **Matching URLs** field. If you leave the Matching URLs field blank, your Auto Refresh interval will apply to all URLs.

7\. Optionally, you can use **wildcards** by inputting a **\*** in your URLs. A wildcard will apply that Feature to any URL that matches after the \*. For example, \*.aycd.io/test/\* will match aycd.io/test/1 and auth.aycd.io/test/2 but not aycd.io/other or google.com/test. 

8\. Specify the range at which the page will be refreshed in seconds in the **Refresh Interval** fields. A random value between the min and max will be chosen for every page refresh.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18200272183319)

9\. After configuring the Task Feature dialog, click the **Save** button. 

Using the Auto Refresh Feature on your Tasks
--------------------------------------------

1. Navigate over to the Browsers section of TabSentry in Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled **Browsers** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Tasks from the table to which you want to assign your Auto Refresh Feature and **right-click > Edit > Edit**. This will open the Edit Task dialog.

3\. Navigate to the **Features** section of the Edit Task dialog. 

4\. Select the Auto Refresh Feature you want to use with your Tasks. 

5\. After configuring the Edit Task dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18200275895319)

6\. Select the browser tasks that you want to run from the table and **right-click > Start** or start your Tasks with the Play button.

8\. Your Tasks will Start and automatically refresh based on how you configured the Feature.

# Inbox - SMS - Task Templates - Creating Task Templates – AYCD

Inbox - SMS - Task Templates - Creating Task Templates

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231-Inbox-SMS-Task-Templates-Creating-Task-Templates/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==========================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 23, 2023 20:07

Introduction
------------

Creating SMS Task Templates allows you to create unique Provider and Country configurations to request numbers from specified websites and automatically handle Outlook SMS OTP codes.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Before creating a Task Template, you must add SMS Provider credentials. [Click here to learn how to add SMS Credentials to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14818934003735)
    

Creating Task Templates
-----------------------

1\. Navigate to the Task Templates section in Inbox under SMS. This section has an icon that looks like a book titled **Task Templates** when you hover over it.

2\. Click on Create. This will open the Create SMS Task Template dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27088670753047)

3\. By default, templates are created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your template created into a unique category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27088625729047)

4\. Click **Create** under the Provider Configs section and configure the Create SMS Provider Config dialogue.

5\. Select which SMS Provider you want to use. Then select which SMS Credentials you wish to use.

6\. Specify which Number Type you want to use. You can choose between Rental (long-term) and Standard numbers. 

7\. If you selected Standard, use the App button to specify which app or website you want to request a phone number for. The App dialog will display a list of all available apps or websites for which the provider offers phone numbers.

8\. If you selected Standard, use the Country button to specify which countries you want to request a phone number for. The Country dialog will display a list of all available countries for which the provider offers phone numbers. You can select multiple countries if you wish.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27088625735063)

9\. If you selected Rental, specify which Rental Type you want to use. The dialog will display a list of all your available rental types.

10\. If you selected Rental, specify which Rental Number you want to use. The dialog will display a list of all your available rental numbers.

11\. To have the SMS Task Template automatically retry when phone numbers are out of stock, click the Retry Request When Out of Numbers checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27088632054039)

12\. Click Save after configuring your provider.

13\. Multiple Provider Configurations can be added to an SMS Task template; however, it's not required. To reorganize the priority, simply drag the configurations around.

![java_3105VAzr6o.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27088632056343)

14\. Click Save to finalize the SMS Task Template.

# Inbox - Inventory - Changing Inventory View Type – AYCD

Inbox - Inventory - Changing Inventory View Type

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14854068940183-Inbox-Inventory-Changing-Inventory-View-Type/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 25, 2023 23:27

Introduction
------------

You can toggle the inventory view type to switch from Sales Data, Tracking Data, or a Custom Data view. These views vary from showing the profit and losses for items to the shipment tracking view for your Inventory. With the Custom Data view, you can choose which columns appear in your Inventory tableview.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   If you want to enhance your knowledge about utilizing the Update Tracking Info feature within the Inventory section, please refer to [this guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14763577067927)
    . 

Changing Inventory View Type
----------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Inventory tab within Inbox.

2\. Select the View Type dropdown in the top right corner. Clicking on this will allow you to select between the different view types.

*   The Sales Data View shows profits and losses on your inventory items.

**![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15092425670551)**

*   The Tracking Data view shows the tracking information of your Inventory items.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15092458818711)

*   The Custom Data view enables you to create a custom view type with customizable columns by clicking the **Options** button on the top right, then the **Edit Columns** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15092463298199)

### Articles in this section

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Create/Import/Export / Import From OneClick/Profile Builder](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Create-Import-Export-Import-From-OneClick-Profile-Builder)

# TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Tools - Distribute Custom Data – AYCD

TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Tools - Distribute Custom Data

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19348105137943-TabSentry-Tasks-Browsers-Tools-Distribute-Custom-Data/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



======================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

November 27, 2023 10:19

Introduction
------------

You can use the Distribute Custom Data Tool to mass assign Custom Data to your tasks. You can do this by providing a list of data, generating Custom Data with AI, or by using our preset randomized data.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To use the Distribute Custom Data Tool, you need to create Tasks. [Click here to learn how to create Tasks.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18178508966679)
    

Distribute Custom Data
----------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Browsers section of TabSentry in Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled **Browsers** when you hover over it.

2\. Select one of your tasks in the tableview, click the **Tools** button, select the **Distribute Custom Data** option, and click **Start**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19348185669655)

3\. You can provide a **List** of values. Each value needs to be separated with a new line.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19348185672215)

4\. You can generate values with **AI**. This utilizes chatGPT to generate values based on the description you provide. The more detailed your description, the higher quality data chatGPT will generate.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19348289138071)

5\. A confirmation dialog will appear once you click the **Save** button. This will show the estimated cost of the AI job. AYCD includes 100,000 free tokens per month with your subscription.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19348272315159)

6\. You can use our preset randomized data sets in the **Random** section. This includes Dates, Names, Address info, and other miscellaneous values.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19348272315287)

7\. After configuring the Distribute Custom Data dialog, click the **Save** button. A dialog will appear confirming your Custom Data has been distributed. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19348289144343)

8\. You can verify your data has been distributed by editing one of your tasks, and navigating to the Custom Data section. For example, on this task I distributed a random Adult Age value.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19348289145111)

# Inbox - Mail - Emails - Tools - Unsubscribe – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Emails - Tools - Unsubscribe

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14766722253207-Inbox-Mail-Emails-Tools-Unsubscribe/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 22, 2023 18:33

Introduction
------------

The Unsubscribe Tool in Inbox allows you to automate the process of unsubscribing from all future mail from the sender of the selected emails.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Before unsubscribing you need to add your email credentials to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to add your Outlook accounts to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   Before unsubscribing you must sync emails to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to sync emails to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/23546038453271)
    

Unsubscribe
-----------

1\. Navigate to the Emails section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, you will see an envelope icon titled **Emails**.

2\. Select the email(s) you want to unsubscribe from in the table. 

3\. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view or right-click on the table and click on **Tools**. This will bring up the **Tools** dialog for Inbox.

4\. Click the dropdown and choose **Unsubscribe**.

5\. Click **Start**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27559951347095)

6\. You can specify a **Proxy Category** to use for unsubscribing. This is optional and only needed to avoid being rate-limited on certain sites where you are unsubscribing from emails. 

7\. If you have proxies assigned to your mail accounts, you can click the **Use proxies from mail accounts** checkbox to use the proxies from your mail credentials for unsubscribing. 

8\. You can click the **Delete successfully unsubscribed emails from mail server** checkbox to delete successfully unsubscribed emails from the mail server automatically. 

9\. You can enable the **Allow AI Unsubscribe as fallback** checkbox to use AI through chat GPT as a fallback if the standard unsubscribe fails.

\- You receive a free 1 million AYCD Chat AI tokens per month, which you can use on [AI Scraper](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14768392098071)
 and the Unsubscribe tool. If you run out of tokens, you can add your own OpenAI API Key to [Settings > Chat GPT Service.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)

\- When AI is used as a fallback, a confirmation dialog will appear that you need to manually confirm you want to use your AYCD Chat AI tokens. You can disable this dialog and allow the AYCD Chat AI to automatically use your tokens with the **Allow Background AI** checkbox in [Settings > ChatGPT Service](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)
.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27559951348759)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27559951350807)

10\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button.

11\. You can cancel the unsubscribe task by clicking the **Cancel** button.

12\. You can hide the task status dialog by clicking the **Run in Background** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14766836779159)

13\. You can view the status of all your Inbox tasks by clicking the **Running Background Tasks** button on the top right of Inbox. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27559951351959)

14\. Once it has successfully unsubscribed, it will show a dialog showing the number of times it was completed successfully.

![Unsub-tools-3.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27559951352983)

15\. If Inbox fails to Unsubscribe from a selected email, it will show a dialog with each email it could not unsubscribe from. You can double-click on these to load the email and manually unsubscribe.

![Unsub-tools-failed-1.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27559951361047)

16\. If you selected the **Delete successfully unsubscribed emails from the mail server**, a dialog will appear showing the progress of the delete operation.

![Unsub-tools-6.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27559951361943)

![Unsub-tools-8.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27559951363095)

# OneClick - Tasks - SMS Credentials - Create / Import / Export – AYCD

OneClick - Tasks - SMS Credentials - Create / Import / Export

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19514938436119-OneClick-Tasks-SMS-Credentials-Create-Import-Export/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

December 02, 2023 20:33

Introduction
------------

SMS Credentials are used when your accounts encounter an SMS challenge during login. You can add third-party credentials to automate this process.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   The setup for each third-party SMS provider differs, so we have created separate sections of the guide for most providers. [Click here to learn more about setting up SMS providers.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4405583780631)
    
*   You can use the Transfer From App button to automatically import your credentials if you have SMS credentials saved in another Toolbox application, such as Profile Builder or Inbox.

Create SMS Credentials
----------------------

1\. Navigate to the **SMS** section of OneClick in Tasks. This section has a message bubble with a checkmark icon titled SMS when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **File** button, then click the **Create** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19514973820055)

3\. The Create Provider Credentials dialog will appear.

4\. Specify which provider you want to use in the **Provider** dropdown.

5\. Specify a name for the SMS credential.

6\. Specify the API key for the credential. This will vary depending on which SMS Provider you are using. [Click here to learn more about setting up SMS providers.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4405583780631)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19514968377367)

7\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

Import SMS Credentials
----------------------

1\. Navigate to the **SMS** section of OneClick in Tasks. This section has a message bubble with a checkmark icon titled SMS when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **File** button, then click the **Import** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19515000314647)

3\. Click the blue file icon to select the CSV file you want to import. If you need an example of our CSV format, click the File > Export option with no items selected.

4\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

Export SMS Credentials
----------------------

1\. Navigate to the **SMS** section of OneClick in Tasks. This section has a message bubble with a checkmark icon titled SMS when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Credentials you want to export, click the **File** button, then click the **Export** button.

3\. A dialog will appear confirming if you want to proceed with the export. Click the **Yes** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19515506249495)

4\. A dialog will appear asking if you want to open the exported file's location. Click the **Yes** button. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19514952799255)

# Inbox - Mail - Scraper Templates - Configure Webhook Format – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Scraper Templates - Configure Webhook Format

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/15199401465367-Inbox-Mail-Scraper-Templates-Configure-Webhook-Format/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

June 09, 2023 21:42

Introduction
------------

You can configure custom AYCD/Discord Webhook Embed formats. These Webhook notifications are sent whenever you receive new Scraper Data. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Before you configure your webhooks, you need to create a Scraper Template. You can learn how to Create a Scraper Template [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14768392098071)
    .
*   You must have enabled New Scraper Data notifications in Settings > Notifications to receive webhooks.
*   You can customize webhooks for each Scraper Template you have.

Configure Webhook Format
------------------------

1. Navigate over to the Scraper Template section of Inbox in Mail. This section has an icon with three lines within a box titled **Scraper Template** when you hover over it.

2\. Select a Scraper Template. Click the **Options** button, then click **Configure Webhook Format**. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15199623481623)

3\. The Mail Scraper Notification Config dialog will appear. Click the **Create** button. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15200045264791)

4\. Specify which data key to use for this notification field. You can create multiple Notification Fields if you want to use multiple Data Keys.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15200054619415)

5\. If you want the data in the Webhook to be spoiler/hidden, click the **Use Spoiler/Hidden Field?** checkbox. This is useful for data such as Order Numbers and Tracking Numbers.

6\. After selecting a Data Key, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15200259476631)

7\. After creating your Data Key(s), click the **Save** button.

![Screenshot](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15202018023191)

Your webhook notifications will now be in the format you created.

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Microsoft (Outlook/Hotmail) Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27311400040727-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Microsoft-Outlook-Hotmail-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28227969764375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Schedule-Tools-Review-Scheduled-Tools)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27040329117847-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Unlock-Account)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook - Change / Verify/Update Recovery Email](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27032547222551-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Change-Verify-Update-Recovery-Email)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Enable Email Forwarding / Disable Email Forwarding / Check Email Forwarding](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/26963363437719-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Enable-Email-Forwarding-Disable-Email-Forwarding-Check-Email-Forwarding)
