# OneClick - Settings – AYCD

OneClick - Settings

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13323057998231-OneClick-Settings/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



========================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

March 23, 2023 20:24

Introduction
------------

The Settings View gives you more control over OneClick and AutoSolve. It allows you to configure a Discord Webhook to enable notifications, enable Password Lock, modify how the Accounts are run, configure max open browser limits, and more.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To avoid unintended consequences, read the description and understand what each setting does before modifying it. You can use Reset > App Config to reset the application-specific settings.

OneClick > Settings > General
-----------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Settings > General** section in OneClick.

2\. From the General Tab, you can configure settings like the AYCD/Discord Webhook to send notifications to your Discord Channel

3\. To view your logs or share them with us when troubleshooting, click the **Package Logs** button.

4\. You can view Release Notes by clicking the **Release Notes** button.

5\. You can save your data with the **Save All Data** button. You can configure a Keyboard Shortcut for this setting. You can learn more about Keyboard Shortcuts below.

6\. The **Generate System Report** button allows you to create a system report, which can help diagnose issues with system resources impacting the program.

7\. You can easily create backups, restore backups, and reset your application in the **Backup/Restore/Reset** section, [which you can read about more here.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500005980861-Using-the-Backup-and-Restore-functions)

8\. If OneClick takes up too much system space, the **Cleanup Old Files** button will delete old files that are no longer necessary for the program to run without signing out of your sessions.

9\. You can use the **Move Resource Directory** feature to transfer OneClicks files to a different location, even if that location is another drive.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092026148631)

10\. After configuring the General section, click the **Save** button.

OneClick > Settings > Password Lock
-----------------------------------

1\. The password lock feature enables you to add an extra layer of protection by creating a unique password that will be required anytime the program is idle for a specified time or when initially opened. You can learn more about the password lock feature [here.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7446003478039)

OneClick > Settings > ChatGPT Service
-------------------------------------

1\. You can view your Balance for the AYCD Text AI in the **Settings > ChatGPT Service** section. AYCD provides 1,000,000 free monthly tokens, which can be used to create text content on OneClick or to use AI tools in Profile Builder and Inbox.

2\. If you enable the **Allow Background AI** checkbox, OneClick will automatically use AI without requiring user interaction.

3\. If you enable the **Automatically use OpenAI when out of AYCD Tokens** checkbox, OneClick will automatically switch to your OpenAI API key when you run out of AYCD Tokens.

4\. Click the **View AI Request History** button to view the AI requests made by OneClick.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092041931927)

5\. If you run out of AYCD Tokens or want to use your OpenAI Key, add it to the **API Key** section.

6\. You can specify the maximum number of tokens per request in the **Max Tokens** field.

7\. You can specify which chat model to use in the **Chat Model Type** dropdown.

8\. After configuring the ChatGPT Service section, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092041932311)

OneClick > Settings > Inbox Service
-----------------------------------

1\. Input your Inbox API Key into the **API Key** field. You can enable the Inbox API and find your API Key in **Inbox | Settings > Tasks**. [Click here and read the Tasks section of the Inbox | Settings > Tasks guide to learn more about the Inbox API.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)
 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092026149527)

2\. After configuring the Inbox Service section, click the **Save** button.

OneClick > Settings > Keyboard Shortcuts
----------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Settings > Keyboard Shortcuts** section of OneClick. You can configure hotkeys for various actions in the Keyboard Shortcuts section. 

2\. Click the **Enable Keyboard Shortcuts** button. A dialog will appear asking if you want to enable keyboard shortcuts. Please read the dialog and click the **Yes** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19530799675415)

3\. Select an Action to which you want to assign a hotkey and click the **Edit** button or **right-click> edit**.

4\. The Edit Keyboard Shortcut Dialog will appear. You can configure a key combination to trigger the selected Action in this dialog. 

5\. You can specify whether or not the main program window must be focused for the keyboard shortcut to work with the **Require App Focus** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19530815262231)

6\. After configuring the Keyboard Shortcuts section, click the **Save** button.

OneClick > Settings > Notifications
-----------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Settings > Notifications** section of OneClick. You can specify specific webhook URLs for various notification types in OneClick.

2\. Click the **checkbox** next to the Notification to enable specific notifications. You are not required to fill in a webhook if you have one saved in Settings > General.

3\. You can specify a different webhook in the Notification Tile if you want the Notification sent to a different webhook than what's saved in Settings > General. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092041933719)

4\. When you have finished configuring the Notifications section, click the **Save** button.

OneClick > Settings > Features
------------------------------

1\. In the **Settings > Features** section, you can easily activate Discord Rich Presence and display that you're playing OneClick by AYCD on your Discord status.

2\. Click **Allow Chrome Extensions from the Chrome Web Store** to allow the installation of Chrome extensions from the Chrome Web Store. Some extensions can interfere with our browser controller and cause issues.![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19530846413207)

3\. After configuring the **Features** section, click the **Save** button.

OneClick > Settings > Accounts/Solvers
--------------------------------------

1\. In the **Settings > Accounts/Solvers** section, you can configure farming tasks to sleep overnight in a specific timezone with the **Sleep Accounts/Solvers at Night** dropdown. You should choose a timezone that matches your proxy region.

2\. You can use the **Automatic Cache Cleanup** setting to help free up low disk space. This setting will automatically run when the browser is closed after farming or using specific account tools. We recommend setting this value to Partial or Disabled.

3\. You can specify which website to use when testing reCaptcha v2 in the **reCaptcha v2 Test Site** dropdown.

4\. You can specify which website to use when testing reCaptcha v3 in the **reCaptcha v3 Test Site** dropdown.

5\. The **Configure Scripts** button will allow you to customize which Google activities you want your tasks to generate activity with.

6\. You can use the **Clear All Cache Files** button to clear the cache files of all browsers. This setting does not remove your login or session data cookies. Clearing All Cache Files will clear the cache of images, JavaScript, videos, and more. This setting is also useful if the OneClick system has low storage space.

7\. If you want to receive notifications when errors occur, enable the **Send Errors to Discord** checkbox.

8\. Enable the **Stop Login on Manual Action Required** checkbox if you want your tasks to stop attempting to log in and close the browser when a manual action is required.

9\. If you want to disable the warning shown when using an account tool for an account with insufficient time spent farming, enable the **Disable Farm Time Warning for Account Tools** checkbox. We only recommend enabling this setting if you know what you are doing. 

10\. Enable the **Disable Pre-Warmup for Accounts** checkbox to disable the warmup procedure after an account is started. We recommend enabling this setting only if you know what you are doing. 

11\. Enable the **Ignore Connection Errors** checkbox if you want to ignore connection errors while farming and allow tasks that encounter this error to continue farming in future cycles.

12\. If your password is changed randomly during the farming/login flow, enable the **Include New Password in Password Changed Webhook** checkbox if you want your password to be included in webhook notifications.

13\. If you want important statuses, such as SMS Verification Required, saved to your account history, enable the **Save Important Statuses to Action Log** checkbox.

14\. Enable the **Use Basic Search** checkbox to revert from Advanced Search in OneClick > Tasks > Accounts/Solvers to Basic Search.

15\. Enable the **Disable Automatic Check 2FA During Farming** checkbox to disable OneClick automatically verifying your accounts' 2FA status when farming.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092041934487)
-------------------------------------------------------------------

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092026150423)

13\. After configuring the **Accounts/Solvers** section, click the **Save** button.

OneClick > Settings > Task Grid
-------------------------------

1\. Navigate to **Settings > Task Grid**. You can configure where browsers open on your screen(s) with Task Grid. By default, browsers will open randomly towards the left side of your screen. Task grid enables you to organize the layout via a grid system (Rows X Columns) and pick which monitor they open on.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092041934999)

OneClick > Settings > Advanced
------------------------------

1\. Navigate to **Settings > Advanced**. In this section, you can configure how many browser tasks run simultaneously.

2\. You can specify how many tasks run at once while farming with a proxy in the **Max Running Accounts** field in the Farming (Proxy) section. By default, this value is set to 0. When set to 0, OneClick will automatically detect a safe amount of tasks to run simultaneously.

3\. You can specify how many tasks run at once while farming without a proxy in the **Max Running Accounts** field in the Farming (Localhost) section. You should run a maximum of 2 accounts without a proxy.

4\. In the **AutoSolve Browser Limit** field, you can specify how many tasks run simultaneously when using AutoSolve. By default, this value is set to 0. When set to 0, OneClick will automatically detect a safe number of tasks to run simultaneously.

5\. In the **Concurrent Login/Tool Limit** field, you can specify how many tasks run simultaneously when using Account Tools, logging in, or warming up.

5\. AYCD, Chrome, Chrome (Headless), Camo Chrome, and Camo Chrome (Headless) have independent limits due to their variation in intensity. You can click between each tab to configure each browser type.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092041935895)

6\. After configuring the **Advanced** section, click the **Save** button.

OneClick > Settings > AutoSolve | General
-----------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to **Settings > AutoSolve | General**. You can configure various settings related to AutoSolve here.

2\. The **Max Manual Solves At Once** setting determines how many solvers can have a status of Manual Solve Required (MSR) before AutoSolve sends additional challenges to third-party solvers you have enabled. Then, as you manually solve the challenges locally, AutoSolve requests will be sent to local solvers until you reach this maximum again. A default limit of 0 means no MSR challenges will be sent to other solving services. You should leave this value at 0.

3\. The **Third Party Overflow Percentage** setting determines how many requests will be sent to your available third-party services when the requests are in queue for local solvers/accounts. For example, if you have 10 local solvers/accounts and receive 10 requests, all of your solvers/ accounts will be busy solving. Nothing will be sent to third-party services. However, if you receive 10 more requests while those are still being solved, and your overflow percentage is set to 20%, the last 2 of those 10 requests will be sent to third-party services. If you want to use multiple third-party providers, we recommend using this setting.

4\. The **AutoSolve Mode** setting should be set to Recommended mode. Recommended mode will automatically configure AutoSolve with our recommended settings and prevent changing them. Custom will allow more control over how AutoSolve works, but you will need to know what you are doing. 

5\. **The Automatically Farm Solvers while using AutoSolve setting** determines if you would like Accounts and/or Solvers to farm when they are scheduled for AutoSolve automatically. If you enable this setting, when you start AutoSolve, your **scheduled** Solvers will automatically begin farming after a short time. To get scheduled Solvers, you need to have more Solvers running than what you specify in the AutoSolve Browser Limit field in Settings > Advanced. For example, if AutoSolve Browser Limit is set to 25, you should have at least 50 Solvers. 25 of your Solvers will be ready to solve captchas in AutoSolve mode, while the other 25 automatically farm. 

6\. Enable the **Cancel the AutoSolve task when you close the local browser, solving it** setting if you want the AutoSolve task to be cancelled when you close the browser.

7\. Enable the **Automatically enable AutoSolve AI when Local Accounts/Solvers are activated** setting if you want AutoSolve AI to be automatically enabled when you start AutoSolve.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19531184403607)

8\. After configuring the **AutoSolve | General** section, click the **Save** button.

OneClick > Settings > AutoSolve AI
----------------------------------

1\. Navigate to **Settings > AutoSolve | AI** in OneClick. You can configure settings related to AutoSolve AI when farming in this section.

2\. If you have AutoSolve AI, you should enable the **Automatically test reCaptcha while farming** checkbox. Testing and solving captchas is another way to prove to Google that you are a human.  
3\. You can specify which captcha types are automatically tested in the **Automatic reCaptcha Test Mode** dropdown. 

4\. You can specify the frequency of automatic tests for reCaptcha v2 in the **Automatic reCaptcha v2 Test Frequency** field.

5\. You can specify the frequency of automatic tests for reCaptcha v3 in the **Automatic reCaptcha v3 Test Frequency** field.

6\. If you have AutoSolve AI, enable the **Enable AutoSolve AI for manual reCaptcha testing** checkbox. If this setting is enabled, when manually testing for reCaptcha v2, AutoSolve AI will automatically solve the challenge.

7\. You can enable the **Stop all solvers when AutoSolve AI balance is zero** checkbox if you want all accounts/solvers to be stopped when your AutoSolve AI balance is zero.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19531137776791)

8\. After configuring the **AutoSolve | AI** section, click the **Save** button.

OneClick > Settings > AutoSolve AI Desktop
------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to **Settings > AutoSolve | AI Desktop** in OneClick. You can configure settings related to AutoSolve AI Desktop in this section.

2\. If you use a home PC, you should set the **Desktop Mode** setting to Home. If you are using a server, you should set the **Desktop Mode** setting to Server. If you want to use a custom delay, you should set the **Desktop Mode** setting to **Custom**.

3\. If you set **Desktop Mode** to **Custom,** you can set a custom **Action Delay (ms).**

4\. If you want AI Desktop to solve reCaptcha challenges automatically, enable the **Enable support for reCaptcha challenges** checkbox. 

5\. If you want to receive a notification when AutoSolve AI Desktop is enabled/disabled, enable the **Send AYCD/Discord Webhook when AutoSolve AI Desktop is toggled on/off** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19531137778071)

7\. After configuring the **AutoSolve | AI Desktop** section, click the **Save** button.

OneClick > Settings > AutoSolve | reCaptcha V2, reCaptcha v3, GeeTest, DataDome, and Cloudflare Turnstile 
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1\. In the **Settings > reCaptcha V2** section**,** you can customize the challenge's solving speed in the **Solve Speed** dropdown according to your preferences. We recommend keeping this set to **Normal** to ensure the account/solver is not rate-limited or flagged.

2\. If you want AutoSolve AI to use complex mouse movements when solving, enable the **Use Complex Mouse Movements** checkbox. We do not recommend enabling this setting.

3\. If you want to use the proxy assigned to your Account/Solver and ignore the proxy sent with an AutoSolve captcha request, enable the **Always Use Account/Solver Proxy (Ignore Solve Request Proxy)** checkbox. We only recommend enabling this setting if you know what you are doing.

4\. If you want to receive a webhook notification when an account/solver needs a manual solve, enable the **Send Webhook When Manual Solve Required** checkbox. 

5\. You can specify the maximum number of solves that can be performed simultaneously for each captcha type with the **Max Concurrent Solves** setting. This setting is useful when solving multiple captcha types since some captchas are more intensive on your machine than others.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/22080096144791)

6\. After configuring your settings, click the **Save** button.

OneClick > Settings > Browsers 
-------------------------------

1\. Navigate to **Settings > Browsers**. If you have startup or controller error issues when starting your tasks, try using the **Reset AYCD Browser or Reset Camo Chrome** buttons. These buttons can help fix your errors. If you continue to run into these errors, make a ticket in the AYCD support discord.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092026151831)

### Articles in this section

# TabSentry - Features - Configs - Custom Autofill – AYCD

TabSentry - Features - Configs - Custom Autofill

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18707075013271-TabSentry-Features-Configs-Custom-Autofill/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

October 31, 2023 19:28

Introduction
------------

You can create Feature Configurations in TabSentry to amplify the capabilities of your Tasks. Creating Custom Autofill Configurations allow you to correlate data from your tasks, such as Profiles, Accounts, Custom Data, IMAP, and Mail OTP codes, to any supported field on any website.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You should use Categories to simplify the organization of your Features.
*   Make sure that you assign the data that you plan to use in the Autofill Template to the task. For example, if you try to use the Data Key for the Address without a Profile Assigned, nothing will happen.
*   You can use Custom Autofill to automatically click checkboxes or other CSS/XPath Selectors on websites.
*   You can use Custom Autofill to Scrape and save text or links for a selection and save it to the Custom Data on your task. 
*   You can use Custom Autofill combined with Multi-Browser Control and autofill across all your browser tasks with one click. [Click here to learn about Multi-Browser Control](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18200568034967)
    .

Creating Custom Autofill Feature Configurations (Tasks View)
------------------------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the **Browsers** section of TabSentry in **Tasks**. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled Browsers when you hover over it.

2\. Start a Task and navigate to the website/fields you want to create a Custom Autofill Config for. You can test this feature on a website such as RoboForm.

3\. Manually input any data that you want to be mapped for your Custom Autofill Configuration. This could include things like Billing and Shipping Details, Payment Details, Sex, Age, Birth Place, TOTP Codes, IMAP OTP Codes, and more.

![2025-02-17_19-27-16.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126193851287)

4\. After filling out the fields you would like mapped manually, select the task from the Tasks > Browsers table view. Click **Tools** and select **Create Custom Autofill Config**, then click **Start**.

![01.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126193857687)

5\. By default, Features are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name for your feature by typing a new one or selecting an existing one through the dropdown.

6\. Specify a Name for the Custom Autofill Task Feature.

7\. Specify which Match URLs that the feature should be used on. You can leave this field empty to use your Custom Autofill Feature with all websites.  
![2025-02-17_19-32-24.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126193858711)

8\. Under Custom Autofill Items there is a list of the selectors that were found automatically based on where you entered information on the webpage. You can edit the Custom Autofill Items by clicking on one from the table and using the Edit button or double-clicking on them. 

![2025-02-17_19-40-29.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126193860631)

9\. If our auto-detection fails to find the selector, you want to add another selector, or you want to find the selector for a button, either in the Edit Custom Autofill Item dialog or by using the Create button you can use the **Enable Click to Find Selector**. _(This feature will only work when selecting a task that has a browser started and creating a Custom Autofill Feature)_

*   After clicking the Enable Click to Find Selector button, go to the open browser that you are creating a Custom Autofill Task Feature for and click on the selector. This will populate the selector section with the selector for the field or button you are trying to find.

![2025-02-17_19-34-27 (1).gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126193862039)

10\. Select the Data Key section from the sidebar in the dialog. In this section you will be able to assign a Data Key to be used on your selector. Your selection here determines how your Custom Autofill Template will interact with the selector and the task. You can assign information from:

*   Task Information (Username, Password) (Autofill)
*   Profiles (Billing/Shipping Address information) (Autofill)
*   Custom Data assigned to the Task (Autofill)
*   IMAP / Mail OTP Codes (Autofilling of IMAP / Mail OTP Codes)
*   SMS OTP Codes (Requesting Phone Numbers from SMS Task Features & Autofilling SMS OTP Codes)
*   Clicking and True/False (Automated Clicking of Selectors)
*   Scraping Data to save Text or Links to a Custom Data value (Text and Links)

![2025-02-17_19-40-12.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126165562391)

11\. Select the Options section from the sidebar in the dialog. In this section, you can modify how the item interacts with the autofill system.

*   Nickname: You can set a nickname for the selector. This can help you identify it when its selector value is confusing.
*   Input Method: The **Default** method works well in most cases, but if the data is not being filled or the website is giving you issues, you can change the Input Method to **Typing (Slow)** or experiment with other options.
*   Delay After Autofilling: You can configure a delay after autofilling the specified target field. This is useful if something on the page changes after you autofill the field.
*   Repeat Amount: You can configure the **Repeat Amount** field to repeat an autofill action. This is useful if you want to click a button multiple times or autofill data multiple times.
*   Repeat delay: You can configure this if you are using the Repeat Amount field and want to add a delay between each repetition of the autofill action.

![2025-02-17_19-40-56.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126165565207)

12\. After configuring your Autofill Item, click the **Save** button.

13\. Repeat the above actions to configure and assign Data Keys to your Custom Autofill Items. Remember that you must have the data assigned to the task in some way if you want to use it. For example, if you want to use the **TOTP Code** Data Key, you should have the **TOTP Secret Key** filled out for the Task.

14\. Once your Custom Autofill Items are configured, click the **Save** button. 

15\. A dialog will appear asking if you want to update any of your browser tasks with the new custom autofill config that you just made. Click the Yes button.

![2025-02-17_19-45-03.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126165566871)

16\. Select the tasks you want to assign your new Custom Autofill Feature Configuration to. It must be assigned to your Tasks to be used.

17\. After selecting the Browser Tasks that you want to assign your Custom Autofill Feature to, click **Save**.

![2025-02-17_19-46-33.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126193876759)

Creating Custom Autofill Feature Configurations (Features View)
---------------------------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the **Configs** section of TabSentry in **Features**. This section has an icon that looks like a bullseye with an arrow in it titled Configs when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **Create** button. This will open the Choose Task Feature dialog. In the dropdown, select **Custom Autofill**.

3\. Click **Next**.

![2025-02-20_15-04-19.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126165572247)

4\. By default, Features are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name for your feature by typing a new one or selecting an existing one through the dropdown.

5\. Specify a Name for the Custom Autofill Task Feature.

6\. Specify which Match URLs that the feature should be used on. You can leave this field empty to use your Custom Autofill Feature with all websites.

![2025-02-20_14-59-56.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126165576599)

7\. Click on the Create Button in order to start creating Custom Autofill Items. Since there is no active browser when creating a Custom Autofill Feature this way, it is not possible to detect any fields automatically or use the Click to Find Selector options. You will need to enter the selectors manually. 

![2025-02-20_15-00-41.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126193883159)

8\. Select the Data Key section from the sidebar in the dialog. In this section, you can assign a Data Key to your selector. Your selection here determines how your Custom Autofill Template will interact with the selector and the task. You can assign information from:

*   Task Information (Username, Password) (Autofill)
*   Profiles (Billing/Shipping Address information) (Autofill)
*   Custom Data assigned to the Task (Autofill)
*   IMAP / Mail OTP Codes (Autofilling of IMAP / Mail OTP Codes)
*   SMS OTP Codes (Requesting Phone Numbers from SMS Task Features & Autofilling SMS OTP Codes)
*   Clicking and True/False (Automated Clicking of Selectors)
*   Scraping Data to save Text or Links to a Custom Data value (Text and Links)

![2025-02-20_15-01-20.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126165587095)

9\. Select the Options section from the sidebar in the dialog. In this section, you can modify how the item interacts with the autofill system.

*   Nickname: You can set a nickname for the selector. This can help you identify it when its selector value is confusing.
*   Input Method: The **Default** method works well in most cases, but if the data is not being filled or the website is giving you issues, you can change the Input Method to **Typing (Slow)** or experiment with other options.
*   Delay After Autofilling: You can configure a delay after autofilling the specified target field. This is useful if something on the page changes after you autofill the field.
*   Repeat Amount: You can configure the **Repeat Amount** field to repeat an autofill action. This is useful if you want to click a button multiple times or autofill data multiple times.
*   Repeat delay: You can configure this if you are using the Repeat Amount field and want to add a delay between each repetition of the autofill action.

![2025-02-20_15-01-54.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126193887383)

10\. After configuring your Autofill Item, click the **Save** button. Remember that you must have the data assigned to the task in some way if you want to use it. For example, if you want to use the **TOTP Code** Data Key, you should have the **TOTP Secret Key** filled out on the Task.

11\. Once you have added all of your Custom Autofill Items, click the **Save** button. 

![2025-02-20_15-02-25.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126193888663)

12\. A dialog will appear asking if you want to update any of your browser tasks with the new custom autofill config that you just made. Click the Yes button.

![2025-02-17_19-45-03.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126165566871)

13\. Select the tasks you want to assign your new Custom Autofill Feature Configuration to. It must be assigned to your Tasks to be used.

14\. After selecting the Browser Tasks you want to assign your Custom Autofill Feature to, click **Save**.

![2025-02-17_19-46-33.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30126193876759)

Running Custom Autofill Feature Configurations 
-----------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the **Browsers** section of TabSentry in **Tasks**. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled Browsers when you hover over it.

2\. Start a Task and navigate to the website that has the fields for your Custom Autofill Configuration. You can test this feature on a website such as RoboForm.

3\. Open Task Assistant and navigate to the Features section. This section has an icon that looks like a bullseye with an arrow in it titled **Features** when you hover over it.

4\. Click the Autofill button in Task Assistant on your Custom Autofill Feature Configuration. This will trigger the Autofill and fill in any fields that correlate with your Custom Autofill Feature Configuration.

![custom autofill demo.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18710036838167)

5\. If you have Multi-Browser Control (MBC) enabled, when you click the Autofill button, it will trigger the Custom Autofill Feature Configuration across all of your controlled browsers. This is excellent to use on Google Form Raffles, entering information into your accounts, and more!

![mbc custom autofill demo.gif](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/18712502183703)

# Profile Builder- Settings - AutoSolve Service / Captcha Setup – AYCD

Profile Builder- Settings - AutoSolve Service / Captcha Setup

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27813183619479-Profile-Builder-Settings-AutoSolve-Service-Captcha-Setup/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



=========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

November 14, 2024 23:29

Introduction
------------

You can configure AutoSolve with Profile Builder to automate captcha solving through third-party providers and OneClick solvers.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To use AutoSolve with Profile Builder, you need a plan that includes AutoSolve. Below is a list of plans with links, including AutoSolve.
    *   [Captcha Pass](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/captchapass?referralCode=ZENDESK)
        
    *   [Traffic Pass](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/trafficpass?referralCode=ZENDESK)
        
    *   [Ultimate Pass](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/ultimatepass?referralCode=ZENDESK)
        

Creating an AutoSolve API Key
-----------------------------

1\. Open the OneClick application in Toolbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27813183611799)

2\. Navigate to the Services section of OneClick in AutoSolve. When you hover over this section, an icon looks like a cube titled **Services**.

3\. Click the **Create Bot API Key** button. Specify a **Name** for your API key, then click the **Create** button. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27813210867095)

4\. Select your newly created API key, right-click, and **Copy API Key**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27813210867863)

5\. Open Profile Builder and navigate to **Settings > AutoSolve Service**. 

6\. Paste your AutoSolve API Key in the **AutoSolve API Key** field, then click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27813183614487)

7\. You have now successfully connected OneClick to Profile Builder. You can now select the **AutoSolve** option when creating/editing account tasks.

# Create TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Tools - Distribute Website URLs – AYCD

Create TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Tools - Distribute Website URLs

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/25952391910423-Create-TabSentry-Tasks-Browsers-Tools-Distribute-Website-URLs/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



======================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

August 27, 2024 21:08

Introduction
------------

You can use the Distribute Website URLs Tool to assign Website URLs to your tasks in bulk. Each task will receive one unique URL. This tool is useful when you need to verify accounts in bulk. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To use the Distribute Website URLs Tool, you need to create Tasks. [Click here to learn how to create Tasks.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18178508966679)
    

Distribute Website URLs
-----------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Browsers section of TabSentry in Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled **Browsers** when you hover over it.

2\. Select one of your tasks in the tableview, click the **Tools** button, select the **Distribute Website URLs** option, and click **Start**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25952415406487)

3\. The **Distribute URLs** dialog will appear. You can input the URLs you want to distribute to your tasks. Each task will receive one unique URL.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25952391905047)

4\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. The Website URLs will be distributed to your tasks.

# TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Tools - Create / Replay MBC Recording – AYCD

TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Tools - Create / Replay MBC Recording

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19342776979479-TabSentry-Tasks-Browsers-Tools-Create-Replay-MBC-Recording/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

November 27, 2023 01:07

Introduction
------------

You can use the Create MBC Recording Tool to create a recording of yourself using the browser. The MBC Recording Tool will record your keyboard, mouse movements, custom autofill, and navigation. You can then run your MBC Recordings on your tasks to automate tedious jobs such as raffle entries, account details autofill, and more.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To use the Create MBC Recording Tool, you need to create Tasks. [Click here to learn how to create Tasks.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18178508966679)
    
*   You can use Features such as [Custom Autofill](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18707075013271)
     and [AutoSolve AI](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18501944197143)
     with MBC Recordings. Custom Autofill will use the unique data within your tasks when auto-filling on a webpage.
*   MBC Recordings automatically handles variations in captcha-solving speed, IMAP Mail OTP Codes, and other timing variables.
*   You should not speed through a MBC Recording. The best way to have consistency across the tasks you want to run your MBC Recording is to take your time when moving the mouse, clicking on the page, and navigating throughout the browser.
*   MBC Recordings cannot be used across different browser types If you switch browser types, you need to recreate your MBC Recordings.
*   Take advantage of the **Max Concurrently Running Browsers** field in Settings | Tasks. You can set this value to however many browsers your device can handle running simultaneously. If you start more tasks than the value in the Max Concurrently Running Browsers field, the extra tasks will automatically be Scheduled. When one of your tasks finishes replaying a recording, your Scheduled tasks will automatically start.

Create MBC Recording
--------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Browsers section of TabSentry in Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled **Browsers** when you hover over it.

2\. Select one of your tasks in the table view, click the **Tools** button, select the **Create MBC Recording** option, and click **Start**. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19345080097047)

3\. By default, MBC Recordings are created to the **All Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your MBC Recordings created in or use the dropdown to assign them to an existing category.

4\. Specify a **Name** for the recording.

5\. If you would like the browser to be in view when you are replaying an MBC Recording, click the **Show Browser on Start** checkbox. 

6\. If you would like the task to stay open after replaying an MBC Recording, click the **Keep Browser Open When Replay Completed** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19348381570455)

7\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. Your task will start, and MBC Recording will be activated. You can record your keyboard, mouse movements, custom autofill, and navigation. Below is a video example of an MBC Recording that resets the password on an account with IMAP Mail OTP code handling.

8\. After you have finished recording, stop your task. Your MBC Recording will be saved to the MBC Recordings section. This section has a computer mouse icon titled MBC Recordings when you hover over it.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19345640688151)

9\. You can view helpful information related to your MBC Recordings, such as the recording duration, which Browser Type you used, and the Starting URLs on your recording. 

10\. You can export your recording by clicking the **File** button and then clicking the **Export** button. This lets you quickly transfer your recordings to a different device or share it with friends. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19345663528343)

11\. You can import your recording by clicking the **File** button and then the **Import** button.

Replay MBC Recording
--------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Browsers section of TabSentry in Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled **Browsers** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the tasks you wish to run your MBC Recording on from the table, click the **Tools** button, select the **Replay MBC Recording** option, and click **Start**. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19345774177559)

3\. The Replay MBC Recording dialog will appear. Choose the MBC Recording you want to replay, then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19345778558487)

4\. Your tasks will begin replaying the MBC Recording. The task Status will show **Replaying**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19345778559639)

5\. When your task finishes replaying the MBC Recording, the task status will say **MBC Replay Complete**. If you did not select **Keep Browser Open When Replay Completed**, then the browser will close when it reaches this status.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19345778559895)

6\. Below is a video example of an MBC Recording that resets the password on an account with IMAP Mail OTP code handling.

# Inbox - Mail - Emails - Tools - Download Attachments – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Emails - Tools - Download Attachments

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/15721260007703-Inbox-Mail-Emails-Tools-Download-Attachments/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

July 01, 2023 21:13

Introduction
------------

With Inbox, you can easily download attachments from your emails to your computer.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   In order to view Emails in Inbox, you will need to add your mail provider credentials, we have created a separate guide for that [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703-Inbox-Mail-Credentials-Create-Import-Export)
    .

Download Attachments
--------------------

1\. Navigate over to the **Emails** section of Inbox in Mail. This section has an icon that looks like an envelope, titled **Emails,** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Emails that you would like to download attachments from in the table.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15721319357079)

3\. Click on the **Tools** button in the top right of the view, or right-click on the table and click on Tools. This will bring up the Tools dialog for emails.

4\. Click on the dropdown and choose **Download Attachments**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15721359076247)

5\. Click the **Start** button.

6\. Once the download is finished, you will see a new window asking if you want to open the folder where the attachments are stored on your computer.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15721340161431)

### Articles in this section

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - OAuth2 Automatic / Manual Login (Gmail/Outlook/Hotmail/Yahoo/AOL)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/25709284534295-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-OAuth2-Automatic-Manual-Login-Gmail-Outlook-Hotmail-Yahoo-AOL)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate AOL Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29071604718615-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-AOL-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - iCloud - Create / Generate / Import / Delete / Export](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Yahoo Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29062200244503-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Yahoo-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Microsoft (Outlook/Hotmail) Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27311400040727-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Microsoft-Outlook-Hotmail-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28227969764375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Schedule-Tools-Review-Scheduled-Tools)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27040329117847-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Unlock-Account)

# OneClick - Tasks - Text Content - Generate With AI / Create / Import / Export – AYCD

OneClick - Tasks - Text Content - Generate With AI / Create / Import / Export

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16035312242583-OneClick-Tasks-Text-Content-Generate-With-AI-Create-Import-Export/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

July 14, 2023 19:17

Introduction
------------

You can create text content for use with the Appeal Disabled Account tool. You can do this manually or generate them using AI.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can use text content with the Appeal Disabled Account Tool. Learn more about the Appeal Disabled Account Tool [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16033826199063)
    .
*   You can use ChatGPT directly within OneClick to generate unique text content. AYCD includes 1,000,000 tokens per month for free for AI usage.

Generate Text Content With AI
-----------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Text Content section in OneClick under Tasks. When you hover over it, the icon looks like a piece of paper titled **Text Content**.

2\. Click on **File**, then **Generate With AI**. This will open the Generate With AI dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16041514164503)

3\. By default, Text Content is created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Text Content created into a unique category.

4\. Specify the number of text items to generate in the **Quantity** field.

5\. You can optionally provide context relevant to your Google accounts. For example, if you want your text to be about reading news, you can say, "I read news on my Google account." You can find some example context below that we use when our accounts need to be appealed.

*   Craft an appeal message to Google, asking them to restore a disabled Gmail account. The letter must be at least 75 words long. Be sure to include at least one of these points: how the account contains sensitive data, that it is used to follow important news updates on both Google News and other similar sites, or that it helps keep track of online transactions. Additionally, reference a recent email notification stating the account had been reinstated, only to find upon attempting to log in again today that the account was disabled. Ensure your response is at least 75 words.  
    

5\. Once you have finished configuring the dialog, click **Next**. This will open the Generate Text Content - Confirmation dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32091780359063)

8\. The Generate Text Content - Confirmation dialog will show you the estimated cost of the operation. AYCD includes 1,000,000 tokens per month for free for AI usage. If you use all of your balance, you can add your own OpenAI API key in Settings | ChatGPT Service.

9\. Once you have confirmed the dialog is correct, click the **Proceed** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16035173613335)

10\. The completed dialog confirms your text was successfully generated.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16035222553367)

11\. You can use your Text Content to submit an appeal for your disabled Google accounts. You can learn more about appealing disabled Google accounts [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16033826199063)
.

Create Text Content
-------------------

1\. Navigate to the Text Content section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a piece of paper button titled **Text Content** when you hover over it.

2\. Click on **File**, then **Create**. This will open the Create Text Content dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16041522204439)

3\. By default, Text Content is created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Text Content created into a unique category.

4\. Specify which type of text content to generate in the **Type** dropdown.

5\. Input the text content to save in the **Text Content** field. You can use Data Keys to inject information such as the Date, Time, and Day of the Week into your text content. 

6\. The **Example Output** **Text** field will show some example data injected. 

7\. Once you have finished configuring the dialog, click **Save**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16035422670743)

Import
------

1\. Navigate to the Text Content section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a piece of paper button titled **Text Content** when you hover over it.

2\. Click on **File,** then Import. This will open the Import Items dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16041540125591)

3\. By default, Text Content is imported to the All **Category** if a category is not provided in the CSV file. If a category is not provided in the CSV file, you can override this and specify a category name to have your Text Content imported into a unique category.

4\. Click the blue page icon on the right side of the dialog and select the CSV file you want to import.

5\. Once you have finished configuring the dialog, click the **Import** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16035555524503)

6\. The completed dialog confirms your CSV file was successfully imported. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16035598006935)

Export
------

1\. Navigate to the Text Content section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a piece of paper button titled **Text Content** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Text Content items you want to Export in the table.

3\. Click on **File**, then **Export**. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16041516164247)

4\. The Export Items dialog will display how many Items you want to export. If the dialog looks correct, click the **Yes** button.

5\. The completed dialog confirms your Items were successfully exported. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/16035680450583)

# Inbox - Mail - Emails - Tools - Create AI Scraper / Mail Scraper Template – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Emails - Tools - Create AI Scraper / Mail Scraper Template

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14768392098071-Inbox-Mail-Emails-Tools-Create-AI-Scraper-Mail-Scraper-Template/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



============================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 22, 2023 19:30

Introduction
------------

Scraper templates allow you to extract data from your emails to a CSV or view it in the Scraper Data tab. You can scrape various data from emails, such as order confirmations, product names, order totals, raffle wins, purchase links, verification links, and more. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Before using the Create Mail Scraper Template Tool, you must sync emails to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to sync emails to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/23546038453271)
    
*   You can customize your Mail Scraper Template to extract any data you wish.
*   After you create a Mail Scraper Template, you can run the Scraper Template on your emails. [Click here to learn how to run a Mail Scraper Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14791462218007)
    

Create AI Mail Scraper Template
-------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Emails section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, you will see an envelope icon titled **Emails**.

2\. Select the email from which you want to create an AI Mail Scraper in the tableview. 

3\. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view or right-click the email from the table and click on **Tools**. This will bring up the **Tools** dialog for Inbox.

4\. Select the **Create Mail Scraper Template** option. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27395613004311)

5\. Select the **Scraper AI** Template Type.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27395627820695)

6\. Click the **Next** button. The Create Mail Scraper template will appear.

7\. In the **General** category, you can specify a category. By default, mail scraper templates are created in the All Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your mail scraper templates created into a unique category.

8\. Specify a **Name** for the Scraper Template.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33774830178583)

9\. In the **Filters** category, you can add filters to the template to include/exclude emails by subject, sender, or specific body elements.

*   This will help ensure you are only exporting certain emails.
*   You can confirm that your filter is valid by clicking the **Add Filter** button.
*   In the screenshot example below, I have three filters configured:
    *   The first filter ensures only emails with **shipped** in the subject are included.
    *   The second filter ensures only emails from **walmart.com** are included.
    *   The third filter ensures only emails that do not include **canceled** in the subject are included.
    *   These filters ensure that I only scrape relevant emails to my scraper template.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33774823422487)

10\. In the **Elements** category, you can specify which element(s) you want to scrape.

*   You should write them similarly to how they are listed in the email. For example, if you want to scrape the Order Number from Walmart shipping emails, you should type Order number, since that is what they have written in the email.
*   You can click the subject of the email to scrape data from the subject.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33774830180247)

11\. Repeat this process for each element you want to scrape. 

12\. You can include extra fields such as **To Email** and **R****eceived At** to be exported as separate element columns in the Scraper Data view. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33774830181143)

13\. In the **Webhook** category, you can configure a Webhook Format to receive webhook notifications when your emails are scraped, which can include elements in your scraper template. [Click here to learn how to configure webhook format.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/15199401465367)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33774830185239)

14\. In the **Inventory** category, you can setup scraper templates to send information directly to the [**Inventory**](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14762796842519)
 [section](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14762796842519)
 of Inbox after you run your mail scraper template.

*   You can use the inventory section of Inbox to manage inventory items and track packages directly from shipping providers such as FedEx, UPS, USPS, and many other providers. [Click here to learn how to use the shipment tracking feature](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14763577067927)
    .

15\. If you want to automatically send your scraped data to the Inventory section based on your send to inventory format, enable the **Enable Automatic Inventory Scraping** checkbox.

*   By default, Inventory Items are created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Inventory Items created into a unique or existing category. 
*   To prevent creating duplicate Inventory items, you can select a **Unique Inventory Data Key**. I use my Order ID for this example since each of my Inventory Items has a unique Order ID. If a duplicate Inventory Item is found, the Inventory Item will be updated. A new Inventory item will be created if there are no duplicate items.
*   Optionally, specify **Other Data Keys**. This data will appear in the Inventory table view as a column. For this example, I used the **Received At** extra field to add a column that displays when the email was received.
*   Specify which data key to use for the **Name** of the Inventory Item. 
*   Specify which data key to use for the **Item URL** of the Inventory Item. This can be a direct product link, StockX link, Event link, or any other URL you want. 
*   Specify which data key to use for the **Notes** of the Inventory Item. This can be any useful information you want.
*   Specify which data key to use for the **Item Cost** of the Inventory Item. 
*   Specify which data key to use for the **Tracking Number** of the Inventory Item.
*   When you have finished configuring the Send to Inventory Format dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33774830187927)

16\. In the **Options** category, you can configure whether you want emails to be automatically scraped with the **Automatically Scrape Mail** checkbox.

17\. You can configure whether you want the AI scraper to look for links based on your element queries with the **Include Links For AI Scraper** checkbox.

18\. You can configure whether you want to scrape all links in the email body and export them as separate columns in the Scraper Data view with the **Scrape All Links** checkbox.

19\. After configuring the AI mail scraper template, click the **Save** button.

20\. This will add the Template to Scraper Templates. You can use the **Run Email Scraper Tool** on all your emails or just a selection of emails to extract the selected data to the Scraper Data view. [Click here to learn how to run your newly created scraper template](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14791462218007)
.

21\. After scraping your emails, you can view your scraped data in the Scraper Data section of Inbox. [Click here to learn how to manage your Scraper Data](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14789019466007)
.

Create Standard Mail Scraper Template
-------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Emails section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, you will see an envelope icon titled **Emails**.

2\. Select the email from which you want to create an AI Mail Scraper in the tableview. 

3\. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view or right-click the email from the table and click on **Tools**. This will bring up the **Tools** dialog for Inbox.

4\. Select the **Create Mail Scraper Template** option. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27395613004311)

5\. Select the **Standard** template type. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27395627830167)

6\. Click the **Next** button. The Create Mail Scraper template will appear.

7\. In the **General** category, you can specify a category. By default, mail scraper templates are created in the All Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your mail scraper templates created into a unique category.

8\. Specify a **Name** for the Scraper Template.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775123166615)

9\. In the **Filters** category, you can add filters to the template to include/exclude emails by subject, sender, or specific body elements.

*   This will help ensure you are only exporting certain emails.
*   You can confirm that your filter is valid by clicking the **Add Filter** button.
*   In the screenshot example below, I have three filters configured:
    *   The first filter ensures only emails from **POP MART** are included.  
        
    *   The second filter ensures only emails with **confirmed** in the subject are included.
    *   The third filter ensures only emails that do not include shipped in the subject are included.
    *   These filters ensure that I only scrape relevant emails to my scraper template.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775129796631)

10\. In the **Elements** category, you can specify which element(s) you want to scrape.

*   To select an element to scrape, mouse over the email and click the element that contains the data you want to extract.
*   You can click the subject of the email to scrape data from the subject.

11\. After clicking an element on the email, the **Add Template Element** dialog will appear. In this dialog, you can select a **Name** for the element to be displayed as the column header in the Scraper Data view or exported CSV.

12\. The **Content Type** is how the data should be parsed. Usually, you will want to use **text**. You can also use **Link** to extract links or **HTML** to extract data that is unavailable as text.

13\. Text **Format Type** is how the data should be formatted. Depending on the data, you can choose various formats to suit your needs.

14\. The **Regex AI** section allows you to use ChatGPT to find more complicated or dynamic text that Content Type cannot easily parse or is inconsistent.

*   For example, you can use Regex AI if you are looking for the color in a big block of text, but cannot use any of the available format types to narrow it down, describe what you want to find, and click Generate. This will use your configured ChatGPT provider, AYCD or OpenAI, to generate a Regex expression to find what you want.
*   You should attempt to use an AI Mail Scraper Template (covered in the section above) before using Regex AI.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775515894167)

15\. You can narrow the output using the **Format Type** to get more refined data. In this case, I want to extract the second word after size. You can also use the format type **Numbers**, but this will not include any standard sizing like Small, Medium, etc.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775489534999)

16\. After configuring the **Add Template Element** dialog, click the **Add** button.

17\. You can repeat the Add Template Element process until you have completely configured the Inbox Scraper Template. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775489536151)

19\. You can include extra fields such as **To Email** and **R****eceived At** to be exported as separate element columns in the Scraper Data view. 

20\. In the **Webhook** category, you can configure a Webhook Format to receive webhook notifications when your emails are scraped, which can include elements in your scraper template. [Click here to learn how to configure webhook format.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/15199401465367)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775515900439)

21\. In the **Inventory** category, you can setup scraper templates to send information directly to the [**Inventory**](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14762796842519)
 [section](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14762796842519)
 of Inbox after you run your mail scraper template.

*   You can use the inventory section of Inbox to manage inventory items and track packages directly from shipping providers such as FedEx, UPS, USPS, and many other providers. [Click here to learn how to use the shipment tracking feature](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14763577067927)
    .

22\. If you want to automatically send your scraped data to the Inventory section based on your send to inventory format, enable the **Enable Automatic Inventory Scraping** checkbox.

*   By default, Inventory Items are created in the All **Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Inventory Items created into a unique or existing category. 
*   To prevent creating duplicate Inventory items, you can select a **Unique Inventory Data Key**. I use my Order ID for this example since each of my Inventory Items has a unique Order ID. If a duplicate Inventory Item is found, the Inventory Item will be updated. A new Inventory item will be created if there are no duplicate items.
*   Optionally, specify **Other Data Keys**. This data will appear in the Inventory table view as a column. For this example, I used the **Quantity** element I scraped to add a column that displays the quantity of my order.
*   Specify which data key to use for the **Name** of the Inventory Item. 
*   Specify which data key to use for the **Item URL** of the Inventory Item. This can be a direct product link, StockX link, Event link, or any other URL you want. 
*   Specify which data key to use for the **Notes** of the Inventory Item. This can be any useful information you want.
*   Specify which data key to use for the **Item Cost** of the Inventory Item. 
*   Specify which data key to use for the **Tracking Number** of the Inventory Item.
*   When you have finished configuring the Send to Inventory Format dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775489537431)

23\. In the **Options** category, you can configure whether you want emails to be automatically scraped with the **Automatically Scrape Mail** checkbox.

24\. You can configure whether you want to scrape all links in the email body and export them as separate columns in the Scraper Data view with the **Scrape All Links** checkbox.

25\. After configuring the Standard mail scraper template, click the **Save** button.

26\. This will add the Template to Scraper Templates. You can use the **Run Email Scraper Tool** on all your emails or just a selection of emails to extract the selected data to the Scraper Data view. [Click here to learn how to run your newly created scraper template](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14791462218007)
.

27\. After scraping your emails, you can view your scraped data in the Scraper Data section of Inbox. [Click here to learn how to manage your Scraper Data](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14789019466007)
.

# TabSentry - Features - Configs - SMS OTP – AYCD

TabSentry - Features - Configs - SMS OTP

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/20573107430551-TabSentry-Features-Configs-SMS-OTP/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==============================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

January 14, 2024 23:05

Introduction
------------

You can create feature configurations in TabSentry to amplify your tasks' capabilities. You can configure an SMS OTP Feature to request phone numbers from third-party providers automatically, scan for OTP codes, and display them in Task Assistant for easy copy-paste access.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   The data in all Toolbox programs is secured and encrypted automatically. Given the sensitive nature of Billing and Credit Card information, you can further secure data in TabSentry with a [Password Lock](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7446003478039)
    .
*   Use categories to simplify organization.
*   You can click here to learn how to import your SMS Credentials to TabSentry.
*   You can use SMS OTP combined with [Custom Autofill](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18707075013271)
     and [MBC Recordings](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19342776979479)
     to handle SMS requirements for account creation, verifying automatically, and more.

Create SMS OTP Feature Config (Local SMS Credentials)
-----------------------------------------------------

1. Navigate over to the Configurations section of TabSentry in Features. This section has an icon that looks like a bullseye with an arrow in it titled **Configs** when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **File** button, then the **Create** button. This will open the Choose Task Feature dialog. Select **Mail OTP** in the dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20574352517399)

3\. Select **SMS OTP** in the dropdown menu, then click the **Next** button.

4\. By default, Features are created to the **All Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Features created or use the dropdown to assign them to an existing category.

5\. Specify a **Name** for the Feature.

6\. Select which **SMS Provider** you want to use. Then select which **SMS Credentials** you wish to use.

7\. Specify which **Number Type** you want to use. You have the option of using **Rental** (long-term) and **Standard** numbers. 

8\. If you selected **Standard**, specify which app/website you want to request a phone number with the **App** button. A list of all available apps/websites for which the provider offers phone numbers will be displayed in the App dialog.

9\. If you selected **Standard**, specify which countries you want to request a phone number with the **Country** button. A list of all available countries for which the provider offers phone numbers will be displayed in the Country dialog. You can select multiple countries if you wish.

10\. If you selected **Rental**, specify which **Rental Type** you want to use. A list of all your available rental types will be displayed in the dialog.

11\. If you selected **Rental**, specify which **Rental Number** you want to use. A list of all your available rental numbers will be displayed in the dialog.

12\. If you want the SMS OTP feature to automatically retry when phone numbers are out of stock, click the **Retry Request When Out of Numbers** checkbox.

13\. If you want to randomize the **Country** that is used for each new SMS request, enable the **Randomize Target Ordering** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32324696345239)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23386164634903)

13\. After configuring the SMS OTP Feature dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20576619823895)

14\. A dialog will appear asking if you want to assign the Feature to your task(s); click the **Yes** button, then select the tasks to which you wish to assign the Feature.

Create SMS OTP Feature Config (Inbox SMS Credentials)
-----------------------------------------------------

1. Navigate over to the Configurations section of TabSentry in Features. This section has an icon that looks like a bullseye with an arrow in it titled **Configs** when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **File** button, then the **Create** button. This will open the Choose Task Feature dialog. Select **Mail OTP** in the dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20574352517399)

3\. Select **SMS OTP** in the dropdown menu, then click the **Next** button.

4\. By default, Features are created to the **All Category**. You can override this and specify a category name to have your Features created or use the dropdown to assign them to an existing category.

5\. Specify a **Name** for the Feature.

6\. Specify the **Inbox Task Template Name** you want to use. [Click here to learn how to create an Inbox Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20729565985943) 7. After configuring the SMS OTP Feature dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20576619823895)

8\. A dialog will appear asking if you want to assign the Feature to your task(s); click the **Yes** button, then select the tasks to which you wish to assign the Feature.

Using Your SMS OTP Feature Config
---------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks > Browsers section in TabSentry. This section has an icon that looks like a globe titled browsers.

2\. If you still need to assign your SMS OTP feature to your tasks, select the tasks you want to use, right-click > edit > edit, and navigate to the Features section. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20577185506327)

3\. Select the SMS OTP config you want to use and any other configs you want assigned to your tasks, and then click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20577185506967)

4\. Start your task(s) and navigate to the website you wish to use SMS. Open **Task Assistant** and navigate to the SMS section.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20578085430295)

5\. Click the **Request Phone Number** button. When the Phone Number is sent, you can copy it and paste it into the website.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20578111978519)

6\. Once you request an SMS code from the number, the code will appear in Task Assistant. You can click the code to copy it to your clipboard. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20578111980055)

7\. When you are finished with the code, click the trash can icon to clear the code from Task Assistant.

Using Your SMS OTP Feature Config with Custom Autofill and MBC Recordings
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

1\. Follow steps 1-3 in the previous section to set up your SMS OTP feature config with your tasks.

2\. Start your task(s) and navigate to the website you wish to use SMS. Open **Task Assistant** and navigate to the SMS section.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20578085430295)

3\. Click the **Request Phone Number** button. When the Phone Number is sent, you can copy it and paste it into the website. **Wait to click** the submit/request OTP button on the website.

4\. Navigate to the **Tools** button in TabSentry and select the **Create Custom Autofill Config** tool. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20578965578519)

5\. Select and edit the phone number Custom Autofill item. For this example, I will verify my Nike SNKRS accounts with SMS. 

6\. Navigate to the Data Key section, select **SMS Phone Number** **(Excluding Dial Code)**, and click the **Save** button.

7\. After assigning the SMS Phone Number to the Custom Autofill Item, click the **Save** button in the Custom Autofill Task Feature dialog.

8\. [For an in-depth view of how to use Custom Autofill, click here.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18707075013271)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20578965582871)

9\. Click the submit/request code button on the website. Once you receive the SMS OTP code, enter it on the website. **Wait to submit** the OTP code.

10\. Navigate to the **Tools** button in TabSentry and select the **Create Custom Autofill Config** tool. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/20579124075159)

11\. Select and edit the OTP code Custom Autofill item. For this example, I will verify my Nike SNKRS accounts with SMS. 

12\. Navigate to the Data Key section, select **SMS OTP Code**, and click the **Save** button.

13\. After assigning the SMS OTP Code to the Custom Autofill Item, click the **Save** button in the Custom Autofill Task Feature dialog.

14\. [For an in-depth view of how to use Custom Autofill, click here.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18707075013271)

15\. You can now use your Custom Autofill configs with MBC Recordings to automate the SMS verification process on Nike. You can apply this process to any similar website/flow. I've included a video example below of the entire process, from the initial MBC Recording to running the recording on other tasks. 

### Articles in this section

*   [TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Edit - Edit / Move / Copy / Delete](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18253934486423-TabSentry-Tasks-Browsers-Edit-Edit-Move-Copy-Delete)
    
*   [TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Browser Data - Clear Browser Data / Import Cookies / Export Cookies / Clear Cookies](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18233308688279-TabSentry-Tasks-Browsers-Browser-Data-Clear-Browser-Data-Import-Cookies-Export-Cookies-Clear-Cookies)
    
*   [TabSentry - Tasks - Browsers - Multi Browser Control (MBC)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18200568034967-TabSentry-Tasks-Browsers-Multi-Browser-Control-MBC)
