# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Reset Password – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Reset Password

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27031102196375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Reset-Password/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



============================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

October 13, 2024 01:37

Introduction
------------

You can use the Outlook Reset Password tool in Inbox to change the password on your Outlook account. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To reset the password on your Outlook accounts, you need to add your accounts to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to add your account credentials to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   You can configure the number of tasks that run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools.

Reset Password
--------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to reset the password on, then click the **Tools** button and select Outlook Reset Password.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461466125207)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461466126487)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461497305111)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461466128279)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Outlook forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461497308055)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. [Click here and navigate to the Captcha Services section of the Inbox Settings guide to learn how to configure automatic captcha solving](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461497309591)

8\. You can automatically move accounts Inbox successfully logged in to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461497310615)

9\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will start resetting the passwords on your accounts. If the password is successfully reset, the status will display **Password Changed**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461497311255)

# Supported Bots - Stellar – AYCD

Supported Bots - Stellar

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27453630591639-Supported-Bots-Stellar/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

October 31, 2024 01:32

Introduction
------------

You can use AutoSolve with various bots and applications. AutoSolve, paired with [AutoSolve AI](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/captchapass?referralCode=ZENDESK)
, is the ultimate captcha-solving solution.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*    You need to create tasks in OneClick in order for captchas to solve. [To learn more about setting up account tasks, click here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16222455171479)
    . [To learn more about setting up solver tasks, click here.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16217942144279)
    
*   You need to start AutoSolve mode on your OneClick tasks to solve captchas. [Click here to learn how to Start AutoSolve.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16221641626135)
     

AutoSolve API Key
-----------------

1\. Navigate to the **AutoSolve** section in OneClick.

2\. Click the **Create Bot API Key** button to create a new AutoSolve API Key or select one of your existing AutoSolve API Keys.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27453653918999)

3\. After creating and selecting your AutoSolve API key, right-click the selected API Key and click the **Copy API Key** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27453630590999)

4\. Navigate to the Settings section in Stellar, then go to the AYCD tab. Input/paste your AutoSolve API Key into the **AYCD API Key** field. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27455157070615)

5\. Click the **Reconnect AYCD** button. If the connection is successful, the AYCD status will show **On**. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27455157070871)

### Articles in this section

*   [OneClick - Tasks - Schedule Configs](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/24174965128855-OneClick-Tasks-Schedule-Configs)
    
*   [AutoSolve - Getting Started Guide](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043228094-AutoSolve-Getting-Started-Guide)
    
*   [AutoSolve - OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Start AutoSolve](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16221641626135-AutoSolve-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Start-AutoSolve)

# TabSentry - Utilities – AYCD

TabSentry - Utilities

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/33772572579479-TabSentry-Utilities/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



============================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

July 28, 2025 18:23

Introduction
------------

The Utilities section includes useful tools such as a mass . trick Gmail and catchall generator. These tools are helpful for mass-creating emails to use with your TabSentry tasks.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can add the emails you generate to your TabSentry tasks by editing them or creating new ones. [Click here to learn how to create TabSentry tasks.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/18178508966679)
    

Jig Gmail Address
-----------------

1\. Navigate to the **Utilities** section in **TabSentry**.

2\. Click the **Run Jig Gmail Address** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33772566276631)

3\. Paste your list of **Gmail** accounts into the **Original Gmail Addresses** field.

*   The Jig Gmail Address tool only works with Gmail accounts.
*   Jigged Gmail Addresses work on some websites to create accounts, but they are more easily detected by websites' anti-bots.

4\. If you want to add periods to your list of Gmails, enable the **Use Period (.) Jig** checkbox.

5\. If you want to add the + character with random numbers following it to your list of **Gmails**, enable the **Use Plus (+) Jig** checkbox.

6\. Specify the number of variations to generate for each Gmail provided with the **Quantity** field.

7\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Generate** button. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33772572567575)

8\. A dialog will appear with your list of generated emails. You can click the dialog to copy the list of emails to your clipboard.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33772572568343)

Generate Catchall Emails
------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Utilities** section in Profile Builder.

2\. Click the **Run Generate Catchall Emails** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33772572569495)

3\. Enter your catchall domain into the **Catchall Domain** field.

4\. Enter the number of variations to generate for your catchall domain into the **Quantity** field.

5\.  After configuring the dialog, click the **Generate** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33772572570775)

6\. A dialog will appear with your list of generated emails. You can click the dialog to copy the list of emails to your clipboard.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33772572571799)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Options - Copy to Clipboard Settings – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Options - Copy to Clipboard Settings

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/33775994894487-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Options-Copy-to-Clipboard-Settings/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

July 28, 2025 20:08

Introduction
------------

You can export your Account tasks in many different copy-paste formats by configuring your Copy to Clipboard Settings. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can configure your Copy to Clipboard settings to match your bot's account import format. This lets you easily export your account information from Profile Builder into various bots and programs, regardless of the format.
*   You can easily copy the email from your accounts to add to profiles with the [Mass Distribute Tool](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/21341444931607)
     or to create accounts in Profile Builder. 

Copy to Clipboard Settings
--------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled Accounts.

2\. Click on **Options** and then **Copy to Clipboard Settings**. This will open the Copy to Clipboard Settings dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775994877847)

3\. Click the **Add** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775994878615)

4\. Specify a **Configuration Name**.

5\. Specify which **Values** to include when copying to the clipboard. You can select Values such as Username, Password, App Password, Proxy, IMAP information, and Recovery Email.

6\. Specify which **Separator** to use when copying to the clipboard. This separator varies based on the bot you are using. For example, the separator AYCD uses is :

7\. You can add additional Values and Separators with the **Add** button. You can remove Values and Separators with the **Remove** button.

8\. You can view an example of your configuration in the **Example** box.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775994880919)

**Examples:**

\- If your bots account import format is username:password:proxy, you can configure your Copy to Clipboard settings based on the screenshot below.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775994882327)

\- If you want to extract your account username to generate accounts via a list of emails or use the Distribute Data Tool in the Profiles section, you can configure your Copy to Clipboard settings based on the screenshot below.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775994883863)

9\. After configuring your settings, click the **Save** button. Then click the **Save** button in the Select Configuration dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775994885527)

10\. Select the account(s) you wish to export. Right-click and then click the **Copy to Clipboard** button. Your accounts will be copied to your clipboard in the format you configured in the Copy to Clipboard Settings dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33775994887447)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27040329117847-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Unlock-Account/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



============================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

October 13, 2024 20:48

Introduction
------------

You can use the **Outlook Unlock Account** tool to automatically unlock your accounts via SMS or IMAP OTP codes when Microsoft prompts you.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To unlock your Outlook/Hotmail accounts, you need to add your email credentials to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to do this.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   Microsoft prompts for various forms of verification during login. Inbox can automatically handle TOTP 2FA and recovery email codes. You can add your TOTP 2FA Secret Key and recovery email to your credentials.  
*   You can configure the number of browsers that run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools. 

Outlook Account Unlock
----------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to use the Outlook Account Unlock tool on, then click the Tools button and select **Outlook Unlock Account** tool.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37458248339991)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37458248341783)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37458248345623)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37458240538775)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Outlook forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37458380556183)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. [Click here and navigate to the Captcha Services section of the Inbox Settings guide to learn how to configure automatic captcha solving](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37458380557463)

8\. You can automatically move accounts Inbox successfully logged in to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37458380559639)

9\. Your accounts will attempt to unlock. When they successfully unlock, the **Browser Status** will show **Account Verified**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37458376088215)

### Articles in this section

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - OAuth2 Automatic / Manual Login (Gmail/Outlook/Hotmail/Yahoo/AOL)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/25709284534295-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-OAuth2-Automatic-Manual-Login-Gmail-Outlook-Hotmail-Yahoo-AOL)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate AOL Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29071604718615-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-AOL-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - iCloud - Create / Generate / Import / Delete / Export](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Yahoo Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29062200244503-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Yahoo-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Microsoft (Outlook/Hotmail) Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27311400040727-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Microsoft-Outlook-Hotmail-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28227969764375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Schedule-Tools-Review-Scheduled-Tools)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27040329117847-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Unlock-Account)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook - Change / Verify/Update Recovery Email](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27032547222551-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Change-Verify-Update-Recovery-Email)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Enable Email Forwarding / Disable Email Forwarding / Check Email Forwarding](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/26963363437719-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Enable-Email-Forwarding-Disable-Email-Forwarding-Check-Email-Forwarding)
    

[See more](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/sections/14713920388503-Inbox)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Generate Aliases / Import Email Aliases – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Generate Aliases / Import Email Aliases

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27295035971735-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Generate-Aliases-Import-Email-Aliases/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



============================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

October 24, 2024 09:23

Introduction
------------

You can use the **Outlook Generate Aliases** tool to create email aliases and save them to your Inbox Account credentials. You can use the **Outlook Import Email Aliases** tool to automatically import/scrape your existing alias emails on your Outlook accounts and save them to your Inbox Account credentials. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To log into your Outlook/Hotmail accounts, you need to add your email credentials to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to add your Outlook accounts to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   Microsoft prompts for various forms of verification during login. Inbox can automatically handle TOTP 2FA and recovery email codes. You can add your TOTP 2FA Secret Key and recovery email to your credentials.  
*   You can configure the number of browsers that run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools. 
*   Once your alias emails are saved to your account, you can utilize the Inbox API with your alias emails.

Outlook Generate Email Aliases
------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to use the Outlook Import Email Aliases tool, then click the Tools button and select **Outlook Generate Aliases**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462918805399)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462918806551)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462918808087)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462933731351)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Outlook forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462933732119)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. [Click here and navigate to the Captcha Services section of the Inbox Settings guide to learn how to configure automatic captcha solving](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462933732887)

8\. Specify how many aliases to generate. Due to Outlook's limitations, the maximum quantity you can generate at a time is 2. You can generate up to a total of 10 per account. You can run the tool over multiple sessions to generate more aliases.

9\. You can select a folder to automatically save new Aliases to a CSV with the **Export New Aliases to CSV** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462933734679)

10\. You can automatically move accounts Inbox successfully generated aliases on to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462918812439)

11\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your accounts will begin generating aliases, and save them to the Email Aliases section of your Account credentials.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462933735959)

Outlook Import Email Aliases
----------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to use the Outlook Import Email Aliases tool, then click the Tools button and select **Outlook Import Email Aliases**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462933737239)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462918806551)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462918808087)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462933731351)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Outlook forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462933732119)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. [Click here and navigate to the Captcha Services section of the Inbox Settings guide to learn how to configure automatic captcha solving](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462933732887)

8\. You can automatically move accounts Inbox successfully import aliases in to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462918812439)

9\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to import alias emails from your Outlook and save them to the Email Aliases section of your Account credentials.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462933738263)

10\. You can view your email aliases by selecting your account tasks, right-click > edit > edit, and then go to the Aliases section.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27295123084823)

11\. Alternatively, you can manually add your email aliases to your account by editing the account credentials, going to the **Email Aliases** section, and inputting your email aliases.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27295062082199)

12\. You can export your aliases with the **Export Aliases to CSV** tool.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462918818327)

# Inbox - Mail - Emails - Tools - Run Mail Scraper – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Emails - Tools - Run Mail Scraper

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14791462218007-Inbox-Mail-Emails-Tools-Run-Mail-Scraper/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



============================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

May 23, 2023 17:55

Introduction
------------

You can run the Mail Scraper on existing templates to extract data from your emails. You can view the extracted data in the Scraped Data tab after selecting the template or in a CSV form.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   Before using the Create Mail Scraper Template Tool, you must sync emails to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to sync emails to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/23546038453271)
    
*   You can customize your Mail Scraper Template to extract any data you wish.
*   After you create a Mail Scraper Template, you can run the Scraper Template on your emails. [Click here to learn how to run a Mail Scraper Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14791462218007)
    
*   You can view your scraped data in the scraper data section after running a mail scraper template. [Click here to learn more about viewing scraped data.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14789019466007)
    

Run Mail Scraper
----------------

1\. Navigate to the Emails section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, you will see an envelope icon titled **Emails**.

2\. Select the email from which you want to create an AI Mail Scraper in the tableview. 

3\. Click on the **Tools** button at the top right of the view or right-click the email from the table and click on **Tools**. This will bring up the **Tools** dialog for Inbox.

4\. Select the **Run Mail Scraper Template** option. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27396419416087)

6\. The Run Mail Scraper dialog will appear. From here, you can select the template(s) that will be used to export the selected items.

*   If you select an AI Scraper Template, the Run Scraper AI - Confirmation dialog will appear. This dialog will tell you how many tokens running the scraper will cost. You receive 1,000,000 tokens per 30 days from AYCD. If you need more tokens, you can add your own OpenAI API key in Settings > ChatGPT Service.![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27397089101335)![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27397093592599)

7\. We recommend enabling the **Show results dialogs after completed** checkbox. A dialog will appear after your data is scraped, telling you which emails were successfully scraped and which failed to scrape.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27396452409879)

8\. After selecting your mail scraper template(s), click the **Export** button.

9\. You can view the status of all your Inbox tasks by clicking the **Running Background Tasks** button on the top right of Inbox. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14792284553367)

10\. The results dialog shows your selected emails, and which ones were successful or failed.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27396424233495)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27396457117335)

11\. The completed dialog confirms your data was successfully exported.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14791897516055)

12\. After you have exported Mail using a Template, you can view the exported data either by clicking on the folder icon in the Scraper Templates tab or Navigating to the Scraper Data section and selecting your Mail Template in the **Scraper Template(s)** dropdown. Your exported data will populate in the Scraper Data section.

13\. You can choose multiple Scraper Templates to aggregate your data in one table view. You can export your Scraper Data via CSV by clicking **File,** then the **Export** button. [Click here to learn more about Scraper Data.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14789019466007)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/14884771546775)

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - OAuth2 Automatic / Manual Login (Gmail/Outlook/Hotmail/Yahoo/AOL)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/25709284534295-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-OAuth2-Automatic-Manual-Login-Gmail-Outlook-Hotmail-Yahoo-AOL)

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - iCloud - Create / Generate / Import / Delete / Export](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Yahoo Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29062200244503-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Yahoo-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Microsoft (Outlook/Hotmail) Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27311400040727-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Microsoft-Outlook-Hotmail-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28227969764375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Schedule-Tools-Review-Scheduled-Tools)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27040329117847-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Unlock-Account)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook - Change / Verify/Update Recovery Email](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27032547222551-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Change-Verify-Update-Recovery-Email)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Enable Email Forwarding / Disable Email Forwarding / Check Email Forwarding](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/26963363437719-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Enable-Email-Forwarding-Disable-Email-Forwarding-Check-Email-Forwarding)

# Profile Builder - Utilities – AYCD

Profile Builder - Utilities

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/33771467306903-Profile-Builder-Utilities/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



========================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

July 28, 2025 17:51

Introduction
------------

The Utilities section includes useful tools such as a mass . trick Gmail and catchall generator. These tools are helpful for mass-creating emails with profiles and accounts.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can add the emails you generate to your profiles with the Mass Distribute tool. [Click here to learn how to use the Mass Distribute tool](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/21341444931607)
    .
*   You can use the emails you generate when creating account tasks. When using the Create Many Tasks option, use the Username From List option and paste in your list of generated emails.

Jig Gmail Address
-----------------

1\. Navigate to the **Utilities** section in Profile Builder.

2\. Click the **Run Jig Gmail Address** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33772304881303)

3\. Paste your list of **Gmail** accounts into the **Original Gmail Addresses** field.

*   The Jig Gmail Address tool only works with Gmail accounts.
*   Jigged Gmail Addresses work on some websites to create accounts, but they are more easily detected by websites' anti-bots.

4\. If you want to add periods to your list of Gmails, enable the **Use Period (.) Jig** checkbox.

5\. If you want to add the + character with random numbers following it to your list of **Gmails**, enable the **Use Plus (+) Jig** checkbox.

6\. Specify the number of variations to generate for each Gmail provided with the **Quantity** field.

7\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Generate** button. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33772299088663)

8\. A dialog will appear with your list of generated emails. You can click the dialog to copy the list of emails to your clipboard.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33772304883607)
-------------------------------------------------------------------

Generate Catchall Emails
------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Utilities** section in Profile Builder.

2\. Click the **Run Generate Catchall Emails** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33772420608535)

3\. Enter your catchall domain into the **Catchall Domain** field.

4\. Enter the number of variations to generate for your catchall domain into the **Quantity** field.

5\.  After configuring the dialog, click the **Generate** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33772420609815)

6\. A dialog will appear with your list of generated emails. You can click the dialog to copy the list of emails to your clipboard.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33772397896087)

*   [Profile Builder - Profiles - Edit, Basic Jig, & Mass Edit](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13264022167703-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Edit-Basic-Jig-Mass-Edit)
    
*   [Profile Builder - Profiles - Expressions & Expression Templates](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058115713-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Expressions-Expression-Templates)

*   [Profile Builder - Profiles - Tools - AI Address Jig](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16039185026583-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Tools-AI-Address-Jig)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28227969764375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Schedule-Tools-Review-Scheduled-Tools/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



============================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

December 03, 2024 01:45

Introduction
------------

You can use the Schedule Tools feature in Inbox to schedule multiple tools at once to run at specific times. Schedule Tools allows you to run multiple tools without additional interaction and schedule them at different times when needed.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To manage your Outlook accounts, you need to add your accounts to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to add your account credentials to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   You can configure how many tasks run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools.

Schedule Tools
--------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to schedule tools on, then click the **Tools** button and select **Schedule Tools**. Then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28227954305047)

3\. Specify a **Nickname** for the schedule.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28227954307095)

4\. Click the **Create** button. 

5\. Specify the **Date/Time** the tool should be scheduled to run. Ensure you specify a date/time in the future.

6\. Select the tool you want to add to your schedule.

7\. Click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28227969762455)

8\. Repeat this process for each tool you want to add to your schedule. After configuring the Create Tool Schedule Group dialog, click the **Next** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229221660311)

9\. If you want emails to populate in the emails tab after your account is logged in, enable the **Sync mail after login** checkbox.

10\. If you want to force relogin into the credentials before running your tools, enable the **Force re-login credentials** checkbox. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229189015703)

11\. Select a **Mail Task Template** to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229189016215)

12\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used if your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow or other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, you need to select it with the **Edit** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31948604724375)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   You must also assign the phone number that correlates with each task to the task. You can do this by selecting your task, right-clicking> Edit > Edit, going to the Credentials section, and inputting the phone number.
*   After creating your Mail Task Template, you need to select it with the **Edit** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31948604725911)

13\. Optionally, you can specify a **Custom Password** to use if Outlook forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229221664919)14\. If you selected the generate aliases tool, specify how many aliases to generate. Due to Outlook's limitations, the maximum quantity you can generate at a time is 2. You can generate up to a total of 10 per account. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229189020311)

15\. If you selected the Change Recovery Email tool, select the emails you want to use as recovery emails on your accounts. You can select accounts already saved to the **Mail Accounts** section in Inbox or provide a list of emails in the **Emails** section of the dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229221679127)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229189022871)

16\. If you selected the Enable Forwarding Tool, select the emails you want your accounts to forward to. You can select accounts already saved to the **Mail Accounts** section in Inbox or provide a list of emails in the **Emails** section of the dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229189022999)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229189023511)

17\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will start based on the date/time you specified.

18\. You can add a Discord webhook to Settings > General or Settings > Notifications to receive notifications when your schedule starts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229306767255)  

19\. You can view your scheduled tools by navigating to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

20\. Select the accounts you want to schedule tools on, then click the **Tools** button and select **Schedule Tools**. Then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229306767639)

21\. Your tool schedule groups will appear. You can view the progress on if a tool schedule group has been successfully completed.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229306768151)

22\. You can double-click a tool schedule group or select the tool schedule group and click the edit button to view the specific tool schedule group and the progress of each tool you selected.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229306768535)

23\. If a scheduled tool has not started, you can edit which tool you want to run or remove it from the schedule by double-clicking it or selecting it and clicking the edit button. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28229306768919)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook - Change / Verify/Update Recovery Email – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook - Change / Verify/Update Recovery Email

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27032547222551-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Change-Verify-Update-Recovery-Email/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

October 13, 2024 05:36

Introduction
------------

To change the recovery email on your Outlook accounts, you can use the **Outlook Change Recovery Email** tool in Inbox. You can use the **Outlook** **Verify/Update Recovery Email** tool to check if the recovery email assigned to your account credentials matches what is currently on your Outlook account. If it does not match, Inbox automatically updates the account and removes any extra recovery emails.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To change the recovery email on your Outlook accounts, you need to add your accounts to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to add your account credentials to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   You can configure the number of tasks that run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools.
*   If you attempt to run a tool and encounter the error status **No Recovery Email**, you need to edit your account task by right-clicking > Edit > Edit, then go to the credentials section and paste in a recovery email. You can use a catchall email for your recovery email. 

Change Recovery Email
---------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to change the recovery email on, then click the **Tools** button and select **Outlook Change Recovery Email**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459753036439)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459743283735)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459743285271)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459753049111)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Outlook forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459743288727)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. [Click here and navigate to the Captcha Services section of the Inbox Settings guide to learn how to configure automatic captcha solving](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459743289879)

8\. Select the emails you want to use as recovery emails on your accounts. You can select accounts already saved to the Mail Accounts section in Inbox or provide a list of emails in the Emails section of the dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459753059863)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459753061655)

9\. You can automatically move accounts, Inbox successfully changed recovery email, into a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459753065879)

10\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to change recovery emails. If they are successfully changed, the status will display **Recovery Email Changed**. Your account credentials will automatically be updated with the new recovery email.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459753071511)

Verify/Update Recovery Email
----------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to Verify/Update the recovery email on, then click the **Tools** button and select **Outlook Verify/Update Recovery Email**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459753073559)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459743283735)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459743285271)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459753049111)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Outlook forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459743288727)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. [Click here and navigate to the Captcha Services section of the Inbox Settings guide to learn how to configure automatic captcha solving](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459743289879)

8\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to verify/update recovery emails. If they are successfully verified, the status will display **Recovery Email Verified**. Your account credentials will automatically be updated with the recovery email assigned to them if it does not match what is currently saved in your Microsoft account. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37459743303575)

### Articles in this section

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - iCloud - Create / Generate / Import / Delete / Export](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Yahoo Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29062200244503-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Yahoo-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Microsoft (Outlook/Hotmail) Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27311400040727-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Microsoft-Outlook-Hotmail-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28227969764375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Schedule-Tools-Review-Scheduled-Tools)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27040329117847-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Unlock-Account)

# Inbox - Settings – AYCD

Inbox - Settings

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791-Inbox-Settings/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

May 21, 2023 18:38

Introduction
------------

The Settings View gives you more control over the Inbox. It allows you to configure a Discord Webhook to enable notifications, enable the use of Mail and SMS tasks, modify the Mail and inventory systems, configure how many browsers run concurrently for OAuth2/Outlook, and more.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To avoid unintended consequences, read the description and understand what each setting does before modifying it. You can use Reset > App Config to reset the application-specific settings.

General
-------

1\. Navigate to the **Settings** section in Inbox.

2\. You can configure a webhook to receive notifications related to Inbox in the **AYCD/Discord Webhook** field.

3\. You can package logs to provide them to us when troubleshooting in tickets with the **Package Logs** button.

4\. The program's database can be saved with the **Save All Data** button. The database is saved automatically, but you can manually save it as a precaution.

5\. You can view release notes for Inbox with the **Release Notes** button.

6\. You can generate a system report that can be used to help diagnose issues with system resources impacting the program. 

7\. In the Backup/Restore/Reset section, you can manage, create, and restore backups and reset the application. [To learn more about managing backups, click here.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500005980861)

8\. You can remove unneeded files with the **Cleanup Old Files** button.

9\. If Inbox is taking up a lot of space on your local disk and you have other drives to which you want to move It, you can use the **Move Resource Directory** setting to move its directory. If you want to transfer data, ensure you use this button rather than attempting to move the installation directory manually.

10\. Once you finish configuring your settings, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37464090678551)

Password Lock
-------------

The Password Lock section enables you to add a Password required when opening Inbox or after a timeout. You can learn more about enabling Password Lock [here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/7446003478039)
.

ChatGPT Service
---------------

1\. The AYCD tab shows your token balance for AYCD Text AI. You will receive 1,000,000 monthly tokens for free for use across all AYCD Services, such as Regex AI, AI Scraper, and AI Unsubscribe.

2\. If you use all of your AYCD Text AI Balance, you can paste your OpenAI **API Key** in the OpenAI section.

3\. In the **Max Tokens** field, you can configure the maximum number of tokens to use per request. This limit ensures you stay within the model's token limit.

4\. You can specify which chat model to use in the **Chat Model Type** dropdown.

5\. When utilizing AYCD Chat AI, a confirmation dialog will appear that you need to manually confirm you want to use your AYCD Chat AI tokens. You can disable this dialog and allow the AYCD Chat AI to automatically use your tokens with the **Allow Background AI** checkbox

6\. You can **Automatically Confirm AI Prompts** instead of showing a confirmation dialog to allow AI to be used more seamlessly. You will need to ensure your usage is within your limits because you will not be prompted to confirm AI requests.

7\. You can automatically switch to your OpenAI API Key when you run out of AYCD Tokens by enabling the **Automatically use OpenAI when out of AYCD Tokens** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37464090679959)

7\. You can view your AI request history by clicking the **View AI Request History** button. You can export that data by selecting the data and clicking the **Export** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27561584089751)

8\. Once you finish configuring your settings, click the **Save** button.

Captcha Services
----------------

1\. You can add an AutoSolve API key to Inbox to handle captchas when encountered. Captchas will be sent to OneClick to solve and then sent back to Inbox.

2\. Alternatively, you can add your EZCaptcha API key to Inbox to handle captchas when encountered. Captchas will be sent directly to EZCaptcha to be solved, and then sent back to Inbox. We recommend using this option for ease of setup.

3\. After adding your AutoSolve API key or EZCaptcha API key, click the **Save** button. The status will show **Active** if it is successfully configured.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37464138131863)

Keyboard Shortcuts
------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Settings > Keyboard Shortcuts** section of Inbox. You can configure hotkeys to various actions in the Keyboard Shortcuts section. 

2\. Click the **Enable Keyboard Shortcuts** button. A dialog will appear asking if you want to enable keyboard shortcuts. Please read the dialog and click the **Yes** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25714367519895)

3\. Select an Action to which you want to assign a hotkey and click the **Edit** button or **right-click> edit**.

4\. The Edit Keyboard Shortcut Dialog will appear. You can configure a key combination to trigger the selected Action in this dialog. When you have finished configuring the Keyboard Shortcut, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25714367520279)

5\. After creating your Keyboard Shortcuts, click the **Save** button. 

User Interface
--------------

1\. You can configure the **Window Style** of the program with the dropdown menu.

2\. If you want a confirmation dialog to appear when you close Inbox, enable the **Ask Before Closing Program** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38867850183063)

Notifications
-------------

The Notifications Service view allows you to enable notifications for different operations throughout Inbox. 

1\. You can enable notifications for specific features in the program with the **checkbox** in the correlating feature box.

2\. Optionally, you can provide a Webhook URL to override your default one in Settings > General.

3\. Once you finish configuring your settings, click the **Save** button.

**![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/15201370475287)**

Inbox API
---------

The Inbox API view enables the Inbox API for the Mail and SMS views, allowing other programs to use Inbox remotely via your API key.

1\. To enable the Inbox API, click on the Inbox API tab in Settings, select the **Enable Inbox API** checkbox**,** and click Save. The program will prompt a restart to give you access to the Inbox API features.

2\. Once the program restarts, you will find your Inbox Tasks API Key, which you can use in Profile Builder or other programs that support the Inbox API.

3\. You can reset your Inbox API Key using the Reset API Key button. If you reset your API Key, you will need to update it where it is used, and any existing tasks may fail to return a result.

4\. You can configure the delay before automatically deleting old Mail Tasks to save storage space with the **Mail Task Auto Delete Delay** dropdown.

5\. You can configure the delay before automatically deleting old SMS Tasks to save storage space with the **SMS Task Auto Delete Delay** dropdown.

6\. Once you finish configuring your settings, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38867850184599)

Mail | Accounts
---------------

1\. You can specify how long Inbox waits for OTP codes with the **OTP Timeout in Minutes** field.

2\. You can use the **Reduce Network Usage By Retaining Cached Data** checkbox to reduce the frequency of re-downloading resources.

3\. You can use the **Stop On Manual Action Required** checkbox to stop tasks when a manual action is required. This can be useful when you do not want to interact with your tasks manually.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37464138137111)

Mail | Account Tools
--------------------

1\. You can configure how many browsers run simultaneously with the **Concurrent Browser Limit** field.

2\. You can configure how many browsers start simultaneously with the **Concurrent Startup Limit** field.

3\. You can automatically show the browser when possible with the **Show Browser When Possible** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38867821026583)

4\. Once you finish configuring your settings for each Account Tools section, click the **Save** button.

Mail | Emails
-------------

The Mail tab allows you to configure settings specific to Mail in Inbox.

1\. You can configure your **Automatic Sync Interval**. This value determines when Inbox looks for emails from all active mail credentials in minutes. A value of 0 indicates you do not want to sync mail automatically. 

2\. The **Sync Mail History** dropdown determines the number of days to sync older mail. You can adjust this value if you need mail older than the default value or if you want to sync less mail to improve stability. Syncing can take a long time if you use a high value and have a large inbox.

3\. You can specify the maximum number of days old an email can be to be shown in the Emails view with the **Emails Shown Restriction** dropdown. This does not affect email syncing and only affects what is shown in the emails view. This is useful if you want to limit the number of emails shown in the Emails view to reduce loading times and memory usage.

4\. You can specify how many accounts can sync simultaneously with the **Concurrent Sync Limit** field. You can configure this per provider. We recommend leaving this default unless you are experiencing lag or slow sync speed.

*   If you are experiencing program lag, you can try lowering this limit.
*   If your syncing is too slow, you can try increasing the limit. The higher the value, the more bandwidth is used.

5\. You can specify how many browsers are used simultaneously when using the [unsubscribe tool](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14766722253207)
 with the **Concurrent Unsubscribe Limit** field. 

6\. You can specify how many threads are used when [scraping mail](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14768392098071)
 with the **Concurrent Scraper Limit** field.

7\. You can pick which browser type to use when unsubscribing from emails with the **Unsubscribe Browser Type** dropdown.

8\. You can click the **Sync Mail On Startup** checkbox to automatically sync mail when you open the program and for all newly added credentials.

9\. You can click the **Disable Images** checkbox to disable images in mail to prevent tracking and improve email loading speed.

10\. When running the Unsubscribe Tool in Mail, you can click the **Show Unsubscribe Browser** checkbox to display the browser view.

11\. You can enable the Mail > Emails view to reload automatically with the **Enable Automatic Loading of New Mail** checkbox.

12\. You can disable using the mail account name as a category in the emails view when there is no custom category set with the **Disable Account Name Categories** checkbox.

13\. You can disable fuzzy searching when searching emails to prevent similar matches to your search term from being shown, and only show matches that exactly contain your search term with the **Disable Fuzzy (Similar) Search** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37464090685975)

14\. Once you finish configuring your settings, click the **Save** button.

Mail | Storage
--------------

1\. You can configure the number of days to keep unused emails in the local database with the **Automatically Delete Mail After Days** dropdown. This will delete emails that have not been used by any tools for the specified number of days. This only deletes the emails from Inbox and not from the IMAP provider. We recommend using this option if you have a lot of emails and want to keep the application running smoothly. You can keep specific emails by selecting them, right-clicking, and then selecting the **Mark as Important** option.

2\. You can delete old emails right away with the **Delete Old Emails Now** button. Similar to the above option, this will only delete emails that have not been used by tools and will only delete emails from Inbox, not from the IMAP provider. We recommend using this option if you have a lot of emails and want to keep the application running smoothly. If you need to retrieve emails you have removed, you can use the **Force Resync Mail** tool in Mail > Accounts.

3\. You can delete duplicate emails in the local database with the **Delete Duplicate Emails** button. Similar to the above option, this will only delete emails from Inbox and not from the IMAP provider. We recommend using this option if you are having trouble with lots of duplicate emails showing in the Emails view. If you need to retrieve emails you have removed, you can use the **Force Resync Mail** tool in Mail > Accounts.

4\. You can disable the email database encryption with the **Disable Emails Database File Encryption** checkbox. We do not recommend enabling this checkbox.

5\. You can enable saving summaries for emails with the **Enable Saving Summaries for Emails (Not Recommended)** checkbox. We do not recommend enabling this as it uses more database storage, which can slow down the application.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37464090691607)

Mail | Monitoring
-----------------

You can configure automatic syncing/monitoring for your emails in this section. 

1\. You can configure **Priority Mail Monitoring** on important mail accounts that you want to get emails and scraped data from right away.

*   Priority monitoring is not recommended when using the Inbox API, as it degrades performance and causes timeouts.

2\. The **Automatic Sync Interval** field is the number of minutes between syncing mail from all of your active mail accounts.

*   We do not recommend enabling Automatic Sync Interval to reduce the number of requests sent to the mail server.
*   You should manually sync or use priority monitoring above if automatic updates are necessary. 

3\. You can enable the **Sync Mail On Startup** checkbox if you want to automatically sync emails on startup for all mail accounts.

*   We do not recommend enabling Sync Mail On Startup if you have more than 100 mail accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38867821028759)

Mail | Task Grid
----------------

1\. You can configure where browsers open on your screen(s) with Task Grid. By default, browsers will open randomly towards the left side of your screen. Task grid enables you to organize their layout via a grid system (Rows X Columns).

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27062149025687)

2\. Once you finish configuring Task Grid, click the **Save** button.

Inventory | Sales
-----------------

The Inventory | Sales section covers currency and the master inventory CSV path for your inventory items.

1\. You can configure which currency to use for your inventory items with the **Currency** dropdown menu.

2\. The **Master Inventory CSV Path** section allows you to configure the path to your master Inventory. This file can be used as a master export, and your latest sales data will automatically populate matching column headers in your CSV.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25714422704919)

3\. Once you finish configuring your settings, click the **Save** button.

Inventory | Tracking
--------------------

The Inventory | Tracking section primarily deals with Tracking Information on Inventory items.

1\. Click the **Automatically Update Tracking** Info checkbox to enable Automatic Tracking Information Updates on your inventory items. Your Inbox must be kept open to update your tracking information automatically.

2\. You can specify a **Refresh Interval** in hours. This is the time between each automatic update of your tracking info.

3\. You can specify the **Proxy Categories** used to update an inventory item's tracking status. This is useful if you track many items and the carrier website rate-limits your tracking tasks.

4\. The **Preferred Weight Unit** dropdown menu lets you specify your p**referred weight unit** for your inventory. When running the Update Tracking Info Tool in Inventory, you can click the **Show Tracking Browser** checkbox to display the browser view.

6\. If you are having issues with FedEx tracking, you can enable the **Only Track One FedEx Package at a Time** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/25714422705303)

5\. Once you finish configuring your settings, click the **Save** button.

Browsers
--------

1\. If you are having issues with emails loading or viewing emails, you can click the **Reset AYCD Browser** button.

2\. If you are having issues with your tasks starting or are experiencing controller errors when using account tools, you can click the **Reset Camo Chrome** button. 

3\. If you have a custom Google Chrome Executable Path, you can specify it in the **Google Chrome Executable Path** field.

4\. If you have a custom Brave Executable Path, you can specify it in the **Brave Chrome Executable Path** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37464090692247)

### Articles in this section

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - OAuth2 Automatic / Manual Login (Gmail/Outlook/Hotmail/Yahoo/AOL)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/25709284534295-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-OAuth2-Automatic-Manual-Login-Gmail-Outlook-Hotmail-Yahoo-AOL)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate AOL Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29071604718615-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-AOL-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - iCloud - Create / Generate / Import / Delete / Export](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Yahoo Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29062200244503-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Yahoo-Accounts)
