*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28227969764375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Schedule-Tools-Review-Scheduled-Tools)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Add Safe Senders – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Add Safe Senders

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28182408394775-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Add-Safe-Senders/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

December 01, 2024 02:25

Introduction
------------

You can use the **Outlook Add Safe Senders** tool to add safe senders (such as specific email addresses or domains) to your Outlook account to avoid sending them to the Junk Folder. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To log into your Outlook/Hotmail accounts, you need to add your email credentials to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to add your Outlook accounts to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   Microsoft prompts for various forms of verification during login. Inbox can automatically handle TOTP 2FA and recovery email codes. You can add your TOTP 2FA Secret Key and recovery email to your credentials.  
*   You can configure how many OAuth2 browsers run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools. 

Outlook Add Safe Senders
------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to use the Outlook Add Safe Senders tool, then click the Tools button and select **Outlook Add Safe Senders**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37463115654551)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37463130849303)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37463115658263)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37463130856599)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Outlook forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37463115661975)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. [Click here and navigate to the Captcha Services section of the Inbox Settings guide to learn how to configure automatic captcha solving](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37463130860183)

8\. In the **Safe Senders** section, enter the email addresses and/or domains to add as safe senders. Separate each email address or domain with a new line.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37463115667095)

9\. You can automatically move accounts Inbox successfully logged in to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37463130862615)

10\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your accounts will begin adding safe senders to your Outlook accounts. If the task is successful, the status will display **Added X / X Safe Senders** (replace X with the number of email addresses/domains you specified).

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37463115671447)

### Articles in this section

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Create/Import/Export / Import From OneClick/Profile Builder](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Create-Import-Export-Import-From-OneClick-Profile-Builder)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - OAuth2 Automatic / Manual Login (Gmail/Outlook/Hotmail/Yahoo/AOL)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/25709284534295-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-OAuth2-Automatic-Manual-Login-Gmail-Outlook-Hotmail-Yahoo-AOL)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate AOL Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29071604718615-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-AOL-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - iCloud - Create / Generate / Import / Delete / Export](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Yahoo Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29062200244503-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Yahoo-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Microsoft (Outlook/Hotmail) Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27311400040727-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Microsoft-Outlook-Hotmail-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28227969764375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Schedule-Tools-Review-Scheduled-Tools)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27040329117847-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Unlock-Account)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook - Change / Verify/Update Recovery Email](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27032547222551-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Change-Verify-Update-Recovery-Email)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Enable Email Forwarding / Disable Email Forwarding / Check Email Forwarding](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/26963363437719-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Enable-Email-Forwarding-Disable-Email-Forwarding-Check-Email-Forwarding)
    

[See more](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/sections/14713920388503-Inbox)

# Profile Builder - Accounts - Tasks - Amazon - Create / Generate / Update – AYCD

Profile Builder - Accounts - Tasks - Amazon - Create / Generate / Update

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28250280592279-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-Amazon-Create-Generate-Update/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

December 03, 2024 20:01

Introduction
------------

You can generate and update (Enable 2FA, Add Address, Add Payment) your Amazon accounts using Profile Builder.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   If you want to use proxies for your account tasks, you can add them to Profile Builder. [Click here to learn how to add proxies to Profile Builder.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13257962802455)
    
*   If you want to use third-party SMS credentials, you can add them to Profile Builder. [Click here to learn how to add third-party SMS credentials to Profile Builder.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13311202059671)
    
*   If you want to confirm IMAP mail OTP codes automatically, you can use Inbox Mail Task Templates. [Click here to learn how to create an Inbox Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    

Tool Hyperlinks
---------------

You can click the text below to go directly to the section of this guide for that specific tool.

*   [Generating Amazon Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28250280592279-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-Amazon-Create-Generate-Update#01JD3Y9VMX68BHF262P6YTMJ1R)
    
*   [Updating Amazon Accounts (Enable 2FA, Add Address, Add Payment)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28250280592279-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-Amazon-Create-Generate-Update#01JE760MDPPZZ46VEYS93WV2CD)
    

Creating Tasks
--------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Click the **File** button, then either the **Create One Task** or **Create Many Tasks** button.

3\. Select the **Amazon** option in the Choose Account Task Type dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28250280576535)

4\. By default, tasks are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to create your task(s) into a unique category.

5\. You can select various browser types for your tasks. Learn more about each type below.

**Camo Chrome**

*   Camo Chrome is our in-house anti-detect browser. It gives your tasks a unique fingerprint, even from the same device, reducing the risk of account bans and anti-bot detections.

**Google Chrome**

*   Chrome is identical to using Chrome manually on your computer. We do not alter the fingerprint for each Chrome task. 

**Brave**

*   Brave is identical to using Brave manually on your computer. Brave has its own basic anti-fingerprinting solution and can help reduce the risk of anti-bot detections.

**Headless Browsers**

*   Headless browsers are more lightweight compared to their non-headless partners. You can run 2-3 headless browsers for every one non-headless browser.
*   You cannot visibly see what headless browsers are doing and cannot click the eye on your tasks to open the browser.
*   Headless browsers are more prone to anti-bot blocks.

6\. The **Account State** dropdown lets you specify whether the account has already been created. Once you generate an account, it automatically updates.

7\. Optionally, you can add notes to your account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28250280579607)

8\. If you used the Create One Task option, specify a **Username** for the account task.

9\. If you used the Create One Task option, specify a **Password** for the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251701687575)

10\. If you used the Create Many Tasks option, you have various ways to specify a Username:

*   You can use a catchall domain to create usernames with the **Username From Catchall** username assign type dropdown.
*   In the **Catchall** field, specify the domain. In the **Number of Account Tasks** field, specify the number of tasks to create.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251701691799)

*   If you selected profiles, you can use the email address assigned to the profile billing address with the **Username From Billing Address** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251701696023)

*   If you selected profiles, you can use the email address assigned to the profile shipping address with the **Username From Shipping Address** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251701697047)

*   You can specify a list of emails to use for your account usernames with the **Username From Provided List** dropdown. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251710411287)

*   The **Username From Account Tasks** dropdown menu allows you to select your existing account tasks and use the usernames from those tasks. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251710412055)

*   The **Username From Aliases** dropdown menu allows you to select your existing email aliases (from iCloud, for example) and them for the username on your account tasks.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251701703319)

11\. If you select the Create Many Tasks option, you can specify the password behavior with the **Password Assign Type** dropdown.

*   Select the **Same Password For All Tasks** dropdown if you want to use the same password for all your accounts.
*   You can specify the password you want to use in the **Password** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251710418199)

*   If you want to use a random password for all of your accounts, you can select the **Unique Password For Each Task** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251710419095)

12\. You can select a **Profile** to use with the account(s). Tasks use Profiles to auto-fill cards, addresses, and other information stored on the profile with your accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251701709975)

13\. You can select **Proxies** to use with your account(s).

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251710423703)

14\. You can choose how IMAP Mail OTP codes are handled with the **Mail Action Type** dropdown.

*   You can manually input mail OTP codes using the **M****anual** dropdown. When tasks encounter mail OTP codes, they will wait for you to input the code manually.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922498131479)

*   Profile Builder can automatically handle IMAP Mail OTP codes with the **Automatic** dropdown.
*   Before utilizing the Automatic option, you need to add your Inbox API key to Settings > Inbox Service.
*   If you are using catchall/forwarded email credentials (which receive your IMAP Mail OTP codes), you need to add them to a Mail Task Template. C[lick here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    .
*   Select your Mail Task Template in the **Inbox Task Template** section of the Create Task dialog. For your created Mail Task Templates to appear, you must have Inbox open/running.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922447836439)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922498131863)

*   You can configure your tasks to automatically stop when they encounter IMAP Mail OTP codes by selecting the **Stop On Mail Verification** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922474604311)

*   If a mail OTP code is not provided, you can choose how long a task will time out with the **Mail Timeout in Minutes Override** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922474604567)

15\. You can choose how SMS is handled with the **SMS Action Type** dropdown.

*   You can manually input mail OTP codes using the **M****anual** dropdown. When tasks encounter mail OTP codes, they will wait for you to input the code manually.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922498134039)

*   Profile Builder can automatically handle SMS OTP codes using the **Automatic** dropdown. You can use Local SMS or Inbox SMS.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922498143127)

*   If you are using Local SMS, you need to add your SMS Credentials to Profile Builder. [Click here to learn how to add your SMS Credentials to Profile Builder.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13311202059671)
    
*   Select an **SMS Provider**, and then select **SMS Credentials**.
*   Select the **App (Amazon)** and then select the **Country(s)** you want to use from the provider.
*   Specify how long the SMS Timeout should be in the **SMS Timeout in Minutes Override** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251710442391)

*   If you use Inbox SMS, you can use the Inbox API to handle SMS OTP codes. To use the Inbox API, you need to add your Inbox API key to Settings > Inbox Service.
*   You need to create an SMS Task Template in Inbox to use Inbox SMS in Profile Builder. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    .

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922447840919)

*   Select your SMS Task Template in the **Inbox Task Template** section of the Create Task dialog. For your created SMS Task Templates to appear, you must have Inbox open/running.
*   Specify how long the SMS Timeout should be in the **SMS Timeout in Minutes Override** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922498143511)

16\. After configuring the SMS Options dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922437788055)

*   You can configure your tasks to automatically stop when they encounter SMS OTP codes by selecting the **Stop On SMS Verification** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922498144023)

17\. Optionally, you can specify where an account is forwarded to in the **Forwards To** field.

18\. Optionally, you can specify an account's recovery email in the **Recovery Email** field.

19\. Optionally, if you are creating one task, you can specify the 2FA (TOTP) Secret Key in the **2FA Secret** Key field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251701733655)

20\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. Your tasks will appear in the tableview in the category you specified in the general tab. You can filter which store type is showing in the tableview by clicking the **Task Type** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28251760192023)

Generating Amazon Accounts
--------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Select the tasks you want to generate, click the **Tools** button, and select the **Amazon - Generate** option. Alternatively, right-click your tasks, click Tools, and select the **Amazon - Generate** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28252939253527)

3\. Enable the **Add address details** checkbox to have the address details from your profile automatically filled out for your Amazon account.

4\. Enable the **Add payment details** checkbox to have the card information from your profile automatically entered into your Amazon account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28252898648343)

5\. You can enable the **Replace Profile phone with verified phone** checkbox to automatically replace the phone number saved to your profile with the phone number used on the account task.

6\. You can enable the **Replace Profile email with Account Task email** checkbox to automatically change the email address saved to your profile with the profile used on the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28252898651031)

7\. You can enable the **Enable 2FA** checkbox to enable 2FA after the account is generated.

8\. You can enable the **Remove phone number after generation** checkbox to remove the phone number used during account generation after the account is successfully generated. This allows you to use the same phone number for multiple accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28252939262743)

9\. The **Backup Proxies** option allows you to configure backup proxies to retry a task when it fails.

10\. You can specify how often a new proxy is used with the **Backup Proxy Retries Amount** field.

11\. You can specify if you want to reuse backup proxies with the **Rotate backup proxies** checkbox.

12\. You can specify if you want the browser data cleared when using a new proxy with the **Reset browser data when using a new proxy** checkbox.![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31917989291415)13\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Run** button. Your tasks will begin to generate. Once they have successfully generated, the status will say **Generated**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28252939264151)

Updating Amazon Accounts (Enable 2FA, Add Address, Add Payment)
---------------------------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Select the tasks you want to update, click the **Tools** button, and select the **Amazon - Update** option. Alternatively, right-click your tasks, click Tools, and select the **Amazon - Update** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28255249547799)

3\. Enable the **Add address details** checkbox to have the address details from your profile automatically filled out for your Amazon account.

4\. Enable the **Add payment details** checkbox to have the card information from your profile automatically entered into your Amazon account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28255249551383)

5\. You can enable the **Replace Profile email with Account Task email** checkbox to automatically change the email address saved to your profile with the profile used on the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28255249553431)

6\. You can enable the **Enable 2FA** checkbox to enable 2FA after the account is generated.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28255203478039)

7\. The **Backup Proxies** option allows you to configure backup proxies to retry a task when it fails.

8\. You can specify how often a new proxy is used with the **Backup Proxy Retries Amount** field.

9\. You can specify if you want to reuse backup proxies with the **Rotate backup proxies** checkbox.

10\. You can specify if you want the browser data cleared when using a new proxy with the **Reset browser data when using a new proxy** checkbox.![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31917989293975)

11\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Run** button. Your tasks will begin to generate. Once they have successfully generated, the status will say **Updated**.

### Articles in this section

*   [Profile Builder - Profiles - Edit, Basic Jig, & Mass Edit](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13264022167703-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Edit-Basic-Jig-Mass-Edit)
    
*   [Profile Builder - Profiles - Expressions & Expression Templates](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058115713-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Expressions-Expression-Templates)
    
*   [Profile Builder - Profiles - Tools - Mass Distribute](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/21341444931607-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Tools-Mass-Distribute)
    
*   [Profile Builder - Profiles - Tools - AI Address Jig](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16039185026583-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Tools-AI-Address-Jig)
    
*   [Profile Builder - Profiles - ACO Slot Form / CSV Format](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13265031173143-Profile-Builder-Profiles-ACO-Slot-Form-CSV-Format)
    
*   [Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - AMEX (Business) (Generate, Import, Lock)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38616453675031-Profile-Builder-Cards-Tasks-AMEX-Business-Generate-Import-Lock)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Gmail Change Password – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Gmail Change Password

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/37491413264151-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Gmail-Change-Password/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==========================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

January 07, 2026 00:38

Introduction
------------

You can use the Gmail change password tool to change the password on your Gmail account.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To manage your Gmail accounts, you need to add your accounts to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to add your account credentials to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   You can configure the number of tasks that run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools.
*   We recommend using the Keep Alive tool on your accounts at least 2-5 times before using account tools.

Gmail Change Password
---------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to change the account password on, then click the **Tools** button and select **Gmail Change Password.**

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37491397236887)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37491413261975)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37491397238167)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37491397238423)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Google forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37491397238935)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. AutoSolve AI is required to solve Google Captchas automatically.

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37491397239063)

8\. You can automatically move accounts Inbox successfully changed password on to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37491397239447)

9\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will start resetting the passwords on your accounts. If the password is successfully reset, the status will display **Password Changed**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37491413263383)

### Articles in this section

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Create/Import/Export / Import From OneClick/Profile Builder](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Create-Import-Export-Import-From-OneClick-Profile-Builder)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - OAuth2 Automatic / Manual Login (Gmail/Outlook/Hotmail/Yahoo/AOL)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/25709284534295-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-OAuth2-Automatic-Manual-Login-Gmail-Outlook-Hotmail-Yahoo-AOL)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate AOL Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29071604718615-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-AOL-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - iCloud - Create / Generate / Import / Delete / Export](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Yahoo Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29062200244503-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Yahoo-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Microsoft (Outlook/Hotmail) Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27311400040727-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Microsoft-Outlook-Hotmail-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28227969764375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Schedule-Tools-Review-Scheduled-Tools)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27040329117847-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Unlock-Account)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook - Change / Verify/Update Recovery Email](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27032547222551-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Change-Verify-Update-Recovery-Email)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Enable Email Forwarding / Disable Email Forwarding / Check Email Forwarding](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/26963363437719-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Enable-Email-Forwarding-Disable-Email-Forwarding-Check-Email-Forwarding)
    

[See more](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/sections/14713920388503-Inbox)

# Profile Builder - Accounts - Tasks - POP MART - Create / Generate / Update / Reset – AYCD

Profile Builder - Accounts - Tasks - POP MART - Create / Generate / Update / Reset

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/32057587422743-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-POP-MART-Create-Generate-Update-Reset/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

May 12, 2025 23:03

Introduction
------------

You can generate, update, and reset your POP MART accounts using Profile Builder.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   If you want to use proxies for your account tasks, you can add them to Profile Builder. [Click here to learn how to add proxies to Profile Builder.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13257962802455)
    
*   If you want to use third-party SMS credentials, you can add them to Profile Builder. [Click here to learn how to add third-party SMS credentials to Profile Builder.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13311202059671)
    
*   If you want to confirm IMAP mail OTP codes automatically, you can use Inbox Mail Task Templates. [Click here to learn how to create an Inbox Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    

Tool Hyperlinks
---------------

*   You can click the text below to go directly to the section of this guide for that specific tool.
*   [Generating POP MART Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/32057587422743-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-POP-MART-Create-Generate-Update-Reset#01JCJ53TR683YQT6XZR95RW0E9)
    
*   [Updating POP MART Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/32057587422743-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-POP-MART-Create-Generate-Update-Reset#01JD10GBMEG4P5AV2BWEJ9J064)
    
*   [Resetting POP MART Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/32057587422743-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-POP-MART-Create-Generate-Update-Reset#01JJMY2V2V59RKJ38NGCB4Y340)
    
*   [POP MART Order Checker (Fetch Orders)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/32057587422743-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-POP-MART-Create-Generate-Update-Reset#01JR9BMQCYA9H2SX83MBY7R4J8)
    

Creating Tasks
--------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Click the **File** button, then either the **Create One Task** or **Create Many Tasks** button.

3\. Select the **Pop Mart** option in the Choose Account Task Type dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057587410199)

4\. By default, tasks are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to create your task(s) into a unique category.

5\. You can select various browser types for your tasks. Learn more about each type below.

**Camo Chrome**

*   Camo Chrome is our in-house anti-detect browser. It gives your tasks a unique fingerprint, even from the same device, reducing the risk of account bans and anti-bot detections.

**Google Chrome**

*   Chrome is identical to using Chrome manually on your computer. We do not alter the fingerprint for each Chrome task. 

**Brave**

*   Brave is identical to using Brave manually on your computer. Brave has its own basic anti-fingerprinting solution and can help reduce the risk of anti-bot detections.

**Headless Browsers**

*   Headless browsers are more lightweight compared to their non-headless partners. You can run 2-3 headless browsers for every one non-headless browser.
*   You cannot visibly see what headless browsers are doing and cannot click the eye on your tasks to open the browser.
*   Headless browsers are more prone to anti-bot blocks.

6\. The **Account State** dropdown lets you specify whether the account has already been created. Once you generate an account, it automatically updates.

7\. Optionally, you can add notes to your account.

8\. If you used the Create One Task option, specify a **Username** for the account task.

9\. If you used the Create One Task option, specify a **Password** for the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057587410711)

10\. If you used the Create Many Tasks option, you have various ways to specify a Username:

*   You can use a catchall domain to create usernames with the **Username From Catchall** username assign type dropdown.
*   In the **Catchall** field, specify the domain. In the **Number of Account Tasks** field, specify the number of tasks to create.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057587410967)

*   If you selected profiles, you can use the email address assigned to the profile billing address with the **Username From Billing Address** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057587411223)

*   If you selected profiles, you can use the email address assigned to the profile shipping address with the **Username From Shipping Address** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057587411479)

*   You can specify a list of emails to use for your account usernames with the **Username From Provided List** dropdown. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057575191703)

*   The **Username From Account Tasks** dropdown menu allows you to select your existing account tasks and use the usernames from those tasks. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057587411863)

*   The **Username From Aliases** dropdown menu allows you to select your existing email aliases (from iCloud, for example) and them for the username on your account tasks.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057575192215)

11\. If you select the Create Many Tasks option, you can specify the password behavior with the **Password Assign Type** dropdown.

*   Select the **Same Password For All Tasks** dropdown if you want to use the same password for all your accounts.
*   You can specify the password you want to use in the **Password** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057587412631)

*   If you want to use a random password for all of your accounts, you can select the **Unique Password For Each Task** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057587412887)

12\. You can select a **Profile** to use with the account(s). Tasks use Profiles to auto-fill cards, addresses, and other information stored on the profile with your accounts.

*   You can automatically match your profiles with your account tasks via email/username with the **Match Profiles by Email** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057587413015)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057587413143)

13\. You can select **Proxies** to use with your account(s).

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057587413271)

14\. You can choose how IMAP Mail OTP codes are handled with the **Mail Action Type** dropdown.

*   You can manually input mail OTP codes using the **M****anual** dropdown. When tasks encounter mail OTP codes, they will wait for you to input the code manually.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057587413783)

*   Profile Builder can automatically handle IMAP Mail OTP codes with the **Automatic** dropdown.
*   Before utilizing the Automatic option, you need to add your Inbox API key to Settings > Inbox Service.
*   If you are using catchall/forwarded email credentials (which receive your IMAP Mail OTP codes), you need to add them to a Mail Task Template. C[lick here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    .
*   Select your Mail Task Template in the **Inbox Task Template** section of the Create Task dialog. For your created Mail Task Templates to appear, you must have Inbox open/running.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057575193239)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057899621911)

*   You can configure your tasks to automatically stop when they encounter IMAP Mail OTP codes by selecting the **Stop On Mail Verification** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057899622551)

*   If a mail OTP code is not provided, you can choose how long a task will time out with the **Mail Timeout in Minutes Override** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057869848215)

15\. Optionally, you can specify where an account is forwarded to in the **Forwards To** field.

16\. Optionally, you can specify an account's recovery email in the **Recovery Email** field.

17\. Optionally, if you are creating one task, you can specify the 2FA (TOTP) Secret Key in the **2FA Secret** **Key** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057575197335)

18\. The **POPMART** dropdown lets you specify which region to generate in.

![PopMartRegions.png](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32965698448279)

19\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. Your tasks will appear in the tableview in the category you specified in the general tab. You can filter which store type is showing in the tableview by clicking the **Task Type** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057985761943)

Generating POP MART Accounts
----------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Select the tasks you want to generate, click the **Tools** button, and select the **POP MART Generate** option. Alternatively, right-click your tasks, click Tools, and select the **POP MART Generate** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057985762967)

3\. Enable the **Add address details** checkbox to have the address details from your profile automatically filled out for your POP MART account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057985763991)

4\. You can enable the **Replace Profile email with Account Task email** checkbox to automatically change the email address saved to your profile with the profile used on the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32058023689751)

5\. The **Backup Proxies** option allows you to configure backup proxies to retry a task when it fails.

6\. You can specify how often a new proxy is used with the **Backup Proxy Retries Amount** field.

7\. You can specify if you want to reuse backup proxies with the **Rotate backup proxies** checkbox.

8\. You can specify if you want the browser data cleared when using a new proxy with the **Reset browser data when using a new proxy** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057985766423)

9\. Click the **Run** button. Your tasks will begin to generate. Once they have successfully generated, the status will say **Generated**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32058023691287)

Updating POP MART Accounts
--------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Select the tasks you want to update, click the **Tools** button, and select the **POP MART Update** option. Alternatively, right-click your tasks, click Tools, and select the **POP MART Update** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057985767703)

3\. Enable the **Clear all existing address details** checkbox to have the address details from your profiles removed from your POP MART account.  
4\. Enable the **Add address details** checkbox to have the address details from your profile automatically filled out for your POP MART account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32058023693079)

5\. You can enable the **Replace Profile email with Account Task email** checkbox to automatically change the email address saved to your profile with the profile used on the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32058023689751)

6\. The **Backup Proxies** option allows you to configure backup proxies to retry a task when it fails.

7\. You can specify how often a new proxy is used with the **Backup Proxy Retries Amount** field.

8\. You can specify if you want to reuse backup proxies with the **Rotate backup proxies** checkbox.

9\. You can specify if you want the browser data cleared when using a new proxy with the **Reset browser data when using a new proxy** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32057985766423)

10\. Click the **Run** button. Your tasks will begin to update. Once they have successfully updated the accounts, the status will say **Updated**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32058241276567)

Resetting POP MART Account Passwords
------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Select the tasks you want to reset, click the **Tools** button, and select the **POP MART Reset** option. Alternatively, right-click your tasks, click Tools, and select the **POP MART Reset** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32058241276695)

3\. You can automatically reset your task's browser data with the **Reset browser data before resetting passwords** checkbox. You should use this if the account task was logged in prior to running the Reset Tool.

4\. You can configure how passwords should be assigned with the **Password Assign Type** dropdown.

*   Select the **Same Password For All Tasks** dropdown if you want to use the same password for all your accounts.
*   You can specify the password you want to use in the **Password** field.
*   If you want to use a random password for all of your accounts, you can select the **Unique Password For Each Task** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32058229457687)

5\. Enable the **Clear all existing address details** checkbox to have the address details from your profiles removed from your Best Buy account.  
6\. Enable the **Add address details** checkbox to have the address details from your profile automatically filled out for your Best Buy account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32058229458199)

7\. You can enable the **Replace Profile email with Account Task email** checkbox to automatically change the email address saved to your profile with the profile used on the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32058241278615)

8\. The **Backup Proxies** option allows you to configure backup proxies to retry a task when it fails.

9\. You can specify how often a new proxy is used with the **Backup Proxy Retries Amount** field.

10\. You can specify if you want to reuse backup proxies with the **Rotate backup proxies** checkbox.

11\. You can specify if you want the browser data cleared when using a new proxy with the **Reset browser data when using a new proxy** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32058229459095)

12\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Run** button. Your tasks will begin to reset. Once they have successfully reset the account password, the status will say **Password Reset**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32058464934679)

POP MART Order Checker (Fetch Orders)
-------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Select the tasks you want to fetch orders on (this tool logs in to your account and looks at the order history, then saves the details to the Orders section of Profile Builder), click the **Tools** button, and select the **POP MART Fetch Order** option. Alternatively, right-click your tasks, click Tools, and select the **POP MART Fetch Order** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33770977778071)

3\. If you want all of your orders to be fetched from the account, enable the **Fetch All Orders** checkbox.

4\. If you want only to check orders from a specific date range, you can specify the range in the **Date Range** fields.![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33770992373527)

5\. The **Backup Proxies** option allows you to configure backup proxies to retry a task when it fails.

6\. You can specify how often a new proxy is used with the **Backup Proxy Retries Amount** field.

7\. You can specify if you want to reuse backup proxies with the **Rotate backup proxies** checkbox.

8\. You can specify if you want the browser data cleared when using a new proxy with the **Reset browser data when using a new proxy** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33770992373911)

9\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Run** button. Your tasks will attempt to fetch orders on your account(s). If successful, the status will show **Orders Fetched**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33770992374551)

10\. You can find an overview of your fetched orders in the Accounts > Orders section of Profile Builder.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/33770992376727)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Gmail Enable Email Forwarding / Disable Email Forwarding / Check Email Forwarding / Add All Mail Filter – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Gmail Enable Email Forwarding / Disable Email Forwarding / Check Email Forwarding / Add All Mail Filter

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/37486427302807-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Gmail-Enable-Email-Forwarding-Disable-Email-Forwarding-Check-Email-Forwarding-Add-All-Mail-Filter/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")





![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

January 06, 2026 20:58

Introduction
------------

You can use the Gmail forwarding tools in Inbox to automatically forward incoming emails to the selected email address, disable forwarding, and check if forwarding is configured on your accounts.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To manage your Gmail accounts, you need to add your accounts to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to add your account credentials to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   You can configure the number of tasks that run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools.
*   You should forward a maximum of 25 Gmail accounts to 1 Gmail account to avoid account bans.
*   We recommend using the Keep Alive tool on your accounts at least 2-5 times before using forwarding tools.
*   We recommend using accounts that are at least a year old. If you use accounts made within six months and forward them, they are likely to be suspended. If you plan to use new accounts, please proceed with caution.

Gmail Enable Email Forwarding
-----------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to set up email forwarding. Then click the **Tools** button and select **Gmail Enable Email Forwarding.**

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486899036951)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915643287)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486899039383)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915646615)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Google forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915647639)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. AutoSolve AI is required to solve Google Captchas automatically.

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915653143)

8\. Select the emails you want your accounts to forward to. You can select accounts already saved to the **Mail Accounts** section in Inbox or provide a list of emails in the **Emails** section of the dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915654295)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486899045783)

9\.   You can enable the **Add All Mail Filter** checkbox to create a filter to forward all incoming mail, including spam, to the forwarding address. Without this filter, only mail in the primary inbox will be forwarded.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486899047191)

10\. You can automatically move accounts Inbox successfully forwarded to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37487647251479)

11\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to set up forwarding. If they are successful, the status will display **Email Forwarding Enabled**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37487663384343)

Gmail Disable Email Forwarding
------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to disable email forwarding for. Then click the **Tools** button and select **Gmail Disable Email Forwarding.**

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37487647253655)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915643287)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486899039383)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915646615)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Google forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915647639)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. AutoSolve AI is required to solve Google Captchas automatically.

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915653143)

8\. You can automatically move accounts' Inbox successfully disabled forwarding to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37487647251479)

9\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to remove forwarding. If they are successful, the status will display **Email Forwarding Disabled**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37487663386519)

Gmail Check Email Forwarding
----------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to check email forwarding on. Then click the **Tools** button and select **Gmail Disable Email Forwarding.**

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37487663387415)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915643287)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486899039383)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915646615)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Google forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915647639)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. AutoSolve AI is required to solve Google Captchas automatically.

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915653143)

8\. You can automatically move accounts' Inbox successfully checked the forwarding status to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37487647251479)

9\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to check the forwarding status. If they are successful, the status will display the email address to which the account is forwarded.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37487647261335)

Gmail Add All Mail Filter
-------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to add an all mail filter to. Then click the **Tools** button and select **Gmail Add All Mail Filter.**

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37488134729879)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915643287)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486899039383)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915646615)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Google forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915647639)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. AutoSolve AI is required to solve Google Captchas automatically.

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37486915653143)

8\. You can automatically move accounts' Inbox successfully added the all mail filter to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37487647251479)

9\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to add the all mail filter. If they are successful, the status will display **All Mail Filter Added**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37488151439895)

# Profile Builder - Cards - Cards - Create / Import (CSV & Copy/Paste) / Export (CSV & Custom CSV) Cards – AYCD

Profile Builder - Cards - Cards - Create / Import (CSV & Copy/Paste) / Export (CSV & Custom CSV) Cards

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38918145258647-Profile-Builder-Cards-Cards-Create-Import-CSV-Copy-Paste-Export-CSV-Custom-CSV-Cards/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")





![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

March 09, 2026 21:03

Introduction
------------

You can use the Cards > Cards section in Profile Builder to manage your hard and virtual cards. You can create/view card items in the UI and import/export them to CSV format.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can mass-import cards in CSV format. To obtain our CSV format, create a card manually in the Profile Builder UI and export it.
*   You can mass-import virtual cards using provider credentials/import tools. [You can learn how to import via provider credentials by following the provider-specific guides by clicking here](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/sections/360006268954-Profile-Builder)
    .

Creating Cards
--------------

1\. Navigate over to the Cards section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the credit card icon labeled Cards when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **File** button and then click the **Create** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39332036111639)

3\. By default, cards are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your cards created in a unique category.

4\. If you are manually adding virtual cards, select the **Parent Card Task** associated with this card. If you are adding hard cards, you can skip this step.

5\. You can configure the **State** of the card. The State of the card indicates whether it is locked or unlocked. For hard cards, this field is irrelevant, and you can set the State to any value.

6\. Optionally, you can save Notes to your card.

7\. You can input the information related to your card in the **Card Details** section. 

*   The website field is optional.

8\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38918136446743)

9\. Profile Builder will save your card to the **Cards** section in Profile Builder. 

*   You can double-click a card to view its details and add notes. 
*   You can copy information with the two blue spreadsheet icon. 
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .
*   You can export your cards to a CSV with File > Export.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39332013232663)

Importing Cards (CSV)
---------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Cards section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the credit card icon labeled Cards when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **File** button, then click the **Import** button, and then click the **CSV** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39332013233175)

3\. By default, cards are imported into the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your cards imported into a unique category.

4\. Select the **CSV File** you want to import by clicking the blue spreadsheet icon.

5\. Select the **Import Format** of the CSV file. 

*   In most cases, you should select AYCD for the import format.
*   If you are exporting from the [Slash](https://www.slash.com/)
     dashboard, you can select the Slash option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38919114941463)

9\. Profile Builder will import your card to the **Cards** section in Profile Builder. 

*   You can double-click a card to view its details and add notes.
*   In the Cards table, you can also see which Cards are assigned to Profiles using the Profiles Column.
*   You can copy information with the two blue spreadsheet icon. 
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .
*   You can export your cards to a CSV with File > Export.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39332013233815)

Importing Cards (Copy/Paste)
----------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Cards section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the credit card icon labeled Cards when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **File** button, then click the **Import** button, and then click the **Copy/Paste** option. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39332036113303)

3\. By default, cards are imported into the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your cards imported into a unique category.

4\. Input your card information into the **Copy/Paste** section.

*   The format you need to use is name:type:number:month:year:cvv

5\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Import** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38919114944151)

6\. Profile Builder will import your card to the **Cards** section in Profile Builder. 

*   You can double-click a card to view its details and add notes.
*   In the Cards table, you can also see which Cards are assigned to Profiles using the Profiles Column.
*   You can copy information with the two blue spreadsheet icon. 
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .
*   You can export your cards to a CSV with File > Export.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39332036114199)

Exporting Cards (CSV)
---------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Cards section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the credit card icon labeled Cards when you hover over it.

2\. Select the cards you want to export, and then click the **File** button, click the **Export** button, and then click the **CSV** option. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39332036114967)

3\. The Export Items dialog will appear, confirming if you want to proceed with the export. Click the **Yes** button to proceed with the export.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38920010024471)

4\. Click the **Yes** button in the Completed—Export Items dialog to view the exported accounts file location. The file explorer will open with your exported CSV file.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38920022267927)

Exporting Cards (Custom CSV)
----------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Cards section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the credit card icon labeled Cards when you hover over it.

2\. Select the cards you want to export, and then click the **File** button, click the **Export** button, and then click the **Custom** **CSV** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39332013235095)

3\. Select the **Fields To Export** that you want included in the exported CSV. 

*   You can select any fields you want to export.
*   You can create presets by selecting the fields you want to export and clicking the **Save Selected Fields As Preset** button.
*   You can clear your selected fields by clicking the **Clear Selection** button.
*   You can remove saved presets by selecting the preset and clicking the **Remove Selected Preset** button.

4\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Export** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38920022271255)

5\. The Export Items dialog will appear, confirming if you want to proceed with the export. Click the **Yes** button to proceed with the export.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38920010024471)

4\. Click the **Yes** button in the Completed—Export Items dialog to view the exported accounts file location. The file explorer will open with your exported CSV file.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38920022267927)

### Articles in this section

*   [Profile Builder - Profiles - Edit, Basic Jig, & Mass Edit](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13264022167703-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Edit-Basic-Jig-Mass-Edit)

*   [Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - AMEX (Business) (Generate, Import, Lock)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38616453675031-Profile-Builder-Cards-Tasks-AMEX-Business-Generate-Import-Lock)
    
*   [Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - Bill (Formerly Divvy) (Generate, Import, Lock, Unlock, Delete)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38618662610967-Profile-Builder-Cards-Tasks-Bill-Formerly-Divvy-Generate-Import-Lock-Unlock-Delete)
    
*   [Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - Capital One (Setup, Generate, Import, Lock, Unlock, Delete)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38489083866647-Profile-Builder-Cards-Tasks-Capital-One-Setup-Generate-Import-Lock-Unlock-Delete)
    
*   [Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - Citi (Generate, Import, Delete)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38702425613591-Profile-Builder-Cards-Tasks-Citi-Generate-Import-Delete)
