# Profile Builder - Accounts - Tasks - Best Buy - Create / Generate / Update / Reset – AYCD

Profile Builder - Accounts - Tasks - Best Buy - Create / Generate / Update / Reset

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27835309537431-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-Best-Buy-Create-Generate-Update-Reset/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

November 15, 2024 20:52

Introduction
------------

You can generate, update, and reset your Best Buy accounts using Profile Builder.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   If you want to use proxies for your account tasks, you can add them to Profile Builder. [Click here to learn how to add proxies to Profile Builder.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13257962802455)
    
*   If you want to use third-party SMS credentials, you can add them to Profile Builder. [Click here to learn how to add third-party SMS credentials to Profile Builder.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13311202059671)
    
*   If you want to confirm IMAP mail OTP codes automatically, you can use Inbox Mail Task Templates. [Click here to learn how to create an Inbox Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    

Tool Hyperlinks
---------------

*   You can click the text below to go directly to the section of this guide for that specific tool.
*   [Generating Best Buy Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27835309537431-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-Best-Buy-Create-Generate-Update-Reset#01JCJ53TR683YQT6XZR95RW0E9)
    
*   [Updating Best Buy Accounts (Clear and Update Address/Payment, Verify Email)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27835309537431-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-Best-Buy-Create-Generate-Update-Reset#01JD10GBMEG4P5AV2BWEJ9J064)
    
*   [Best Buy Update Store Pickup Person](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27835309537431-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-Best-Buy-Create-Generate-Update-Reset#01JD14FFE9EBDCERK3NVH2YRS6)
    
*   [Resetting Best Buy Account Passwords](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27835309537431-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-Best-Buy-Create-Generate-Update-Reset#01JD14FFE9EBDCERK3NVH2YRS6)
    
*   [Change Email on Best Buy Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27835309537431-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-Best-Buy-Create-Generate-Update-Reset#01JQ4M5XYZG2K9VJCPG6JV63E1)
    
*   [Best Buy Order Checker (Fetch Orders)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27835309537431-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-Best-Buy-Create-Generate-Update-Reset#01JR9BMQCYA9H2SX83MBY7R4J8)
    

Creating Tasks
--------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Click the **File** button, then either the **Create One Task** or **Create Many Tasks** button.

3\. Select the **Best Buy** option in the Choose Account Task Type dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27835836167959)

4\. By default, tasks are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to create your task(s) into a unique category.

5\. You can select various browser types for your tasks. Learn more about each type below.

**Camo Chrome**

*   Camo Chrome is our in-house anti-detect browser. It gives your tasks a unique fingerprint, even from the same device, reducing the risk of account bans and anti-bot detections.

**Google Chrome**

*   Chrome is identical to using Chrome manually on your computer. We do not alter the fingerprint for each Chrome task. 

**Brave**

*   Brave is identical to using Brave manually on your computer. Brave has its own basic anti-fingerprinting solution and can help reduce the risk of anti-bot detections.

**Headless Browsers**

*   Headless browsers are more lightweight compared to their non-headless partners. You can run 2-3 headless browsers for every one non-headless browser.
*   You cannot visibly see what headless browsers are doing and cannot click the eye on your tasks to open the browser.
*   Headless browsers are more prone to anti-bot blocks.

6\. The **Account State** dropdown lets you specify whether the account has already been created. Once you generate an account, it automatically updates.

7\. Optionally, you can add notes to your account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27835806588951)

8\. If you used the Create One Task option, specify a **Username** for the account task.

9\. If you used the Create One Task option, specify a **Password** for the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27835806594455)

10\. If you used the Create Many Tasks option, you have various ways to specify a Username:

*   You can use a catchall domain to create usernames with the **Username From Catchall** username assign type dropdown.
*   In the **Catchall** field, specify the domain. In the **Number of Account Tasks** field, specify the number of tasks to create.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27835806597399)

*   If you selected profiles, you can use the email address assigned to the profile billing address with the **Username From Billing Address** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27835836175895)

*   If you selected profiles, you can use the email address assigned to the profile shipping address with the **Username From Shipping Address** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27835836178839)

*   You can specify a list of emails to use for your account usernames with the **Username From Provided List** dropdown. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27835806604567)

*   The **Username From Account Tasks** dropdown menu allows you to select your existing account tasks and use the usernames from those tasks. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27835836181527)

*   The **Username From Aliases** dropdown menu allows you to select your existing email aliases (from iCloud, for example) and them for the username on your account tasks.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27835806608407)

11\. If you select the Create Many Tasks option, you can specify the password behavior with the **Password Assign Type** dropdown.

*   Select the **Same Password For All Tasks** dropdown if you want to use the same password for all your accounts.
*   You can specify the password you want to use in the **Password** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27835836187159)

*   If you want to use a random password for all of your accounts, you can select the **Unique Password For Each Task** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27835806612887)

12\. You can select a **Profile** to use with the account(s). Tasks use Profiles to auto-fill cards, addresses, and other information stored on the profile with your accounts.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27924900625303)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27924910063511)

13\. You can select **Proxies** to use with your account(s).

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27835806615063)

14\. You can choose how IMAP Mail OTP codes are handled with the **Mail Action Type** dropdown.

*   You can manually input mail OTP codes using the **M****anual** dropdown. When tasks encounter mail OTP codes, they will wait for you to input the code manually.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922502945943)

*   Profile Builder can automatically handle IMAP Mail OTP codes with the **Automatic** dropdown.
*   Before utilizing the Automatic option, you need to add your Inbox API key to Settings > Inbox Service.
*   If you are using catchall/forwarded email credentials (which receive your IMAP Mail OTP codes), you need to add them to a Mail Task Template. C[lick here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    .
*   Select your Mail Task Template in the **Inbox Task Template** section of the Create Task dialog. For your created Mail Task Templates to appear, you must have Inbox open/running.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922512758423)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922502947991)

*   You can configure your tasks to automatically stop when they encounter IMAP Mail OTP codes by selecting the **Stop On Mail Verification** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922512760087)

*   If a mail OTP code is not provided, you can choose how long a task will time out with the **Mail Timeout in Minutes Override** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31922502949271)

15\. Optionally, you can specify where an account is forwarded to in the **Forwards To** field.

16\. Optionally, you can specify an account's recovery email in the **Recovery Email** field.

17\. Optionally, if you are creating one task, you can specify the 2FA (TOTP) Secret Key in the **2FA Secret** **Key** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27835836205591)

18\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. Your tasks will appear in the tableview in the category you specified in the general tab. You can filter which store type is showing in the tableview by clicking the **Task Type** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899135077655)

Generating Best Buy Accounts
----------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Select the tasks you want to generate, click the **Tools** button, and select the **Best Buy - Generate** option. Alternatively, right-click your tasks, click Tools, and select the **Best Buy - Generate** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27841883414679)

3\. Enable the **Add address details** checkbox to have the address details from your profile automatically filled out for your Best Buy account.

4\. Enable the **Add payment details** checkbox to have the card information from your profile automatically entered into your Best Buy account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899135079575)

5\. Enable the **Verify mail** checkbox to automatically verify the account using the Mail Verification (IMAP) assigned to the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899091932695)

6\. You can enable the **Replace Profile email with Account Task email** checkbox to automatically change the email address saved to your profile with the profile used on the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899135082135)

7\. You can remove all store pickup people from your accounts with the **Clear All Existing Store Pickup People** checkbox.

8\. You can add a store pickup person to the account with the **Add Store Pickup Person** checkbox.

9\. You can select profiles to be used for adding new store pickup people with the **Edit** button. Alternatively, you can use the profiles assigned to the selected tasks with the **Use profiles assigned to tasks** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31918313926807)

10\. The **Backup Proxies** option allows you to configure backup proxies to retry a task when it fails.

11\. You can specify how often a new proxy is used with the **Backup Proxy Retries Amount** field.

12\. You can specify if you want to reuse backup proxies with the **Rotate backup proxies** checkbox.

13\. You can specify if you want the browser data cleared when using a new proxy with the **Reset browser data when using a new proxy** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31918376723607)

14\. Click the **Run** button. Your tasks will begin to generate. Once they have successfully generated, the status will say **Generated**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27841883415063)

Updating Best Buy Accounts (Clear and Update Address/Payment, Verify Email)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Select the tasks you want to update, click the **Tools** button, and select the **Best Buy - Update** option. Alternatively, right-click your tasks, click Tools, and select the **Best Buy - Update** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899091934743)

3\. Enable the **Clear all existing address details** checkbox to have the address details from your profiles removed from your Best Buy account.  
4\. Enable the **Add address details** checkbox to have the address details from your profile automatically filled out for your Best Buy account.

5\. Enable the **Clear all existing payment details** checkbox to clear all existing payment details from your Best Buy account.  
6\. Enable the **Add payment details** checkbox to have the card information from your profile automatically entered into your Best Buy account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899135083927)

7\. Enable the **Verify mail** checkbox to automatically verify the account using the Mail Verification (IMAP) assigned to the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899091935383)

8\. You can enable the **Replace Profile email with Account Task email** checkbox to automatically change the email address saved to your profile with the profile used on the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899091936535)

8\. You can remove all store pickup people from your accounts with the **Clear All Existing Store Pickup People** checkbox.

9\. You can add a store pickup person to the account with the **Add Store Pickup Person** checkbox.

10\. You can select profiles to be used for adding new store pickup people with the **Edit** button. Alternatively, you can use the profiles assigned to the selected tasks with the **Use profiles assigned to tasks** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31918313926807)

11\. The **Backup Proxies** option allows you to configure backup proxies to retry a task when it fails.

12\. You can specify how often a new proxy is used with the **Backup Proxy Retries Amount** field.

13\. You can specify if you want to reuse backup proxies with the **Rotate backup proxies** checkbox.

14\. You can specify if you want the browser data cleared when using a new proxy with the **Reset browser data when using a new proxy** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31918376723607)

15\. Click the **Run** button. Your tasks will begin to update. Once they have successfully updated the accounts, the status will say **Updated**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899091937047)

Best Buy Update Store Pickup Person
-----------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Select the tasks you want to reset, click the **Tools** button, and select the **Best Buy Update Store Pickup Person** option. Alternatively, right-click your tasks, click Tools, and select the **Best Buy Update Store Pickup Person** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29545191524759)

3\. You can remove all store pickup people from your accounts with the **Clear All Existing Store Pickup People** checkbox.

4\. You can add a store pickup person to the account with the **Add Store Pickup Person** checkbox.

5\. You can select profiles to be used for adding new store pickup people with the **Edit** button. Alternatively, you can use the profiles assigned to the selected tasks with the **Use profiles assigned to tasks** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31918313933591)

6\. The **Backup Proxies** option allows you to configure backup proxies to retry a task when it fails.

7\. You can specify how often a new proxy is used with the **Backup Proxy Retries Amount** field.

8\. You can specify if you want to reuse backup proxies with the **Rotate backup proxies** checkbox.

9\. You can specify if you want the browser data cleared when using a new proxy with the **Reset browser data when using a new proxy** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31918376732055)

10\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Run** button. Your tasks will begin to update the store pickup person. Once they are successful, the status will show **Updated Pickup Person**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/29545191529239)

Resetting Best Buy Account Passwords
------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Select the tasks you want to reset, click the **Tools** button, and select the **Best Buy- Reset** option. Alternatively, right-click your tasks, click Tools, and select the **Best Buy - Reset** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899135086487)

3\. You can automatically reset your task's browser data with the **Reset browser data before resetting passwords** checkbox. You should use this if the account task was logged in prior to running the Reset Tool.

4\. You can configure how passwords should be assigned with the **Password Assign Type** dropdown.

*   Select the **Same Password For All Tasks** dropdown if you want to use the same password for all your accounts.
*   You can specify the password you want to use in the **Password** field.
*   If you want to use a random password for all of your accounts, you can select the **Unique Password For Each Task** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899091939991)

5\. Enable the **Clear all existing address details** checkbox to have the address details from your profiles removed from your Best Buy account.  
6\. Enable the **Add address details** checkbox to have the address details from your profile automatically filled out for your Best Buy account.

7\. Enable the **Clear all existing payment details** checkbox to clear all existing payment details from your Best Buy account.  
8\. Enable the **Add payment details** checkbox to have the card information from your profile automatically entered into your Best Buy account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899135087255)

9\. Enable the **Verify mail** checkbox to automatically verify the account using the Mail Verification (IMAP) assigned to the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899313143959)

10\. You can enable the **Replace Profile email with Account Task email** checkbox to automatically change the email address saved to your profile with the profile used on the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899313145239)

11\. You can remove all store pickup people from your accounts with the **Clear All Existing Store Pickup People** checkbox.

12\. You can add a store pickup person to the account with the **Add Store Pickup Person** checkbox.

13\. You can select profiles to be used for adding new store pickup people with the **Edit** button. Alternatively, you can use the profiles assigned to the selected tasks with the **Use profiles assigned to tasks** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31918376732695)

14\. The **Backup Proxies** option allows you to configure backup proxies to retry a task when it fails.

15\. You can specify how often a new proxy is used with the **Backup Proxy Retries Amount** field.

16\. You can specify if you want to reuse backup proxies with the **Rotate backup proxies** checkbox.

17\. You can specify if you want the browser data cleared when using a new proxy with the **Reset browser data when using a new proxy** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31918313940375)

18\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Run** button. Your tasks will begin to reset. Once they have successfully reset the account password, the status will say **Password Reset**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27899434425239)

Change Email on Best Buy Accounts
---------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Select the tasks you want to change email on (this tool changes the account email on the website's end), click the **Tools** button, and select the **Best Buy Change Email** option. Alternatively, right-click your tasks, click Tools, and select the **Best Buy Change Email** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30885288806423)

3\. The configuration dialog will appear. In this dialog, you can specify the new email for your account.

*   You can use a catchall domain to use usernames with the **Username From Catchall** username assign type dropdown.
*   In the **Catchall** field, specify the domain.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30885260529175)

*   If you selected profiles, you can use the email address assigned to the profile billing address with the **Username From Billing Address** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30885260531095)

*   If you selected profiles, you can use the email address assigned to the profile shipping address with the **Username From Shipping Address** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30885288817047)

*   You can specify a list of emails to use for your account usernames with the **Username From Provided List** dropdown. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30885260533655)

*   The **Username From Account Tasks** dropdown menu allows you to select your existing account tasks and use the usernames from those tasks. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30885288819223)

*   The **Username From Aliases** dropdown menu allows you to select your existing email aliases (from iCloud, for example) and them for the username on your account tasks.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30885260537495)

4\. The **Backup Proxies** option allows you to configure backup proxies to retry a task when it fails.

5\. You can specify how often a new proxy is used with the **Backup Proxy Retries Amount** field.

6\. You can specify if you want to reuse backup proxies with the **Rotate backup proxies** checkbox.

7\. You can specify if you want the browser data cleared when using a new proxy with the **Reset browser data when using a new proxy** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31918313941015)

8\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Run** button. Your tasks will attempt to change the email on your account(s). If successful, the status will show "Changed Email."

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/30885288821783)

Best Buy Order Checker (Fetch Orders)
-------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Select the tasks you want to fetch orders on (this tool logs in to your account and looks at the order history, then saves the details to the Orders section of Profile Builder), click the **Tools** button, and select the **Best Buy Fetch Order** option. Alternatively, right-click your tasks, click Tools, and select the **Best Buy Fetch Order** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31245192171927)

3\. If you want all of your orders to be fetched from the account, enable the **Fetch All Orders** checkbox.

4\. If you want only to check orders from a specific date range, you can specify the range in the **Date Range** fields.![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31245192172183)

5\. The **Backup Proxies** option allows you to configure backup proxies to retry a task when it fails.

6\. You can specify how often a new proxy is used with the **Backup Proxy Retries Amount** field.

7\. You can specify if you want to reuse backup proxies with the **Rotate backup proxies** checkbox.

8\. You can specify if you want the browser data cleared when using a new proxy with the **Reset browser data when using a new proxy** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31918313942807)

9\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Run** button. Your tasks will attempt to fetch orders on your account(s). If successful, the status will show **Orders Fetched**. The **Orders** column in the Accounts > Tasks tableview will display the number of orders fetched. You can click the Orders value on each task to open a dialog with more details on your orders.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31246915329047)

10\. You can find an overview of your fetched orders in the Accounts > Orders section of Profile Builder.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/31245561235479)

# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Enable IMAP & OAuth2 Login – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Enable IMAP & OAuth2 Login

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27070985335447-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Enable-IMAP-OAuth2-Login/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

October 15, 2024 04:16

Introduction
------------

You can use the **Outlook Enable IMAP & OAuth2 Login** tool to enable IMAP on your account if necessary, and then complete OAuth2 Login for the selected Outlook accounts. 

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To change the recovery email on your Outlook accounts, you need to add your accounts to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to add your account credentials to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   You can configure how many tasks run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools.
*   To enable IMAP, your account must have a Recovery email assigned to it. [Click here to learn how to assign a Recovery email to your Microsoft account.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27032547222551)
    
*   In most cases, the Microsoft account must be at least 3-7 days old before the enable IMAP button will work.

Outlook Enable IMAP & OAuth2 Login
----------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts you want to enable IMAP on, then click the **Tools** button and select **Outlook Enable IMAP & OAuth2 Login**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462528753943)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462528755991)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462528757655)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462528759575)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Outlook forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462561019159)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. [Click here and navigate to the Captcha Services section of the Inbox Settings guide to learn how to configure automatic captcha solving](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462528764695)

8\. If you want to automatically sync mail after login, enable the **Sync mail after login** checkbox.

9\. If you want to force re-login into the credentials before running the OAuth2 Automatic Login tool, enable the **Force re-login credentials** checkbox.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462561021591)

10\. You can automatically move accounts Inbox successfully logged in to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462561023255)

11\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to enable IMAP. If they successfully enable IMAP, the browser status will display **Login Success**, and the IMAP status will display **Connected**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37462561024407)

### Articles in this section

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - OAuth2 Automatic / Manual Login (Gmail/Outlook/Hotmail/Yahoo/AOL)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/25709284534295-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-OAuth2-Automatic-Manual-Login-Gmail-Outlook-Hotmail-Yahoo-AOL)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate AOL Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29071604718615-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-AOL-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - iCloud - Create / Generate / Import / Delete / Export](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Yahoo Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29062200244503-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Yahoo-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Microsoft (Outlook/Hotmail) Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27311400040727-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Microsoft-Outlook-Hotmail-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28227969764375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Schedule-Tools-Review-Scheduled-Tools)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27040329117847-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Unlock-Account)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook - Change / Verify/Update Recovery Email](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27032547222551-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Change-Verify-Update-Recovery-Email)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Enable Email Forwarding / Disable Email Forwarding / Check Email Forwarding](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/26963363437719-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Enable-Email-Forwarding-Disable-Email-Forwarding-Check-Email-Forwarding)
    

[See more](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/sections/14713920388503-Inbox)

# Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - Stripe (Generate, Import, Lock, Unlock, Delete) – AYCD

Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - Stripe (Generate, Import, Lock, Unlock, Delete)

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38782166420247-Profile-Builder-Cards-Tasks-Stripe-Generate-Import-Lock-Unlock-Delete/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

March 03, 2026 21:15

Introduction
------------

You can use Profile Builder to generate, import, lock, unlock, delete, and manage Stripe virtual cards.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To manage your virtual cards in Profile Builder, you need an account and a credit/debit card with the selected provider.
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles to easily create or update your Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .

Adding Stripe Credentials
-------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Tasks section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the ship's wheel icon labeled **Tasks** when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **File** button at the top left of the view, then click **Create**.

3\. Select **Stripe** from the dialog and click the **Next** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331900678679)

4.  By default, virtual card credentials are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your virtual card credentials created in a unique category.

5\. Specify a **Task Name** for the Card Task. The Task Name is how you will identify this task in the tableview ui.

6\. Specify which **Browser Type** you want to use. In almost all cases, you should choose Camo Chrome or Google Chrome. If you are having issues with those browser types, you can try other available browser types.

7\. Optionally, you can save **Notes** to your virtual card credential.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38782580716439)

8\. Enter the **Authorization Secret Key** for the account.

9\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38782580717975)

13\. Your virtual card credential will be added to the tableview. You can now use the available Stripe tools to generate, import, and delete virtual cards.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331900679191)

Stripe Generate
---------------

1\. Navigate over to the Tasks section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the ship's wheel icon labeled **Tasks** when you hover over it.

2\. Select your Stripe virtual card credential. Click the **Tools** button, select the **Stripe Generate** option, and then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331900679575)

3\. By default, virtual cards are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your virtual cards created in a unique category.

4\. Specify the **Spend Limit** that will be applied to each generated virtual card. 

5\. Select the **Card Holder** you want to use for generating cards.

6\. Specify the **Spending Limit Type** to use.

7\. Specify the **Account Currency** that is selected on the account.

8\. Specify the number of virtual cards you want to generate with the **Quantity** field.

9\. Once you have configured the dialog, click the **Run** button. Your virtual cards will begin to generate.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38782610357015)

10\. You can click the status of the task to view the Action Log, which includes live details related to the tool you are using.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331911162647)

11\. Profile Builder will save your virtual cards to the **Cards** section in Profile Builder. 

*   You can double-click a virtual card to view its details and add notes. 
*   You can copy information with the two blue spreadsheet icon. 
*   You can utilize the Lock, Unlock, and Delete tools on your virtual cards.
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .
*   You can export your virtual cards to a CSV with File > Export.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331900693527)

Stripe Import
-------------

1\. Navigate over to the Tasks section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the ship's wheel icon labeled **Tasks** when you hover over it.

2\. Select your Stripe virtual card credential. Click the **Tools** button, select the **Stripe Import** option, and then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331911163415)

3\. The configuration dialog will appear. By default, virtual cards are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your virtual cards created in a unique category.

4\. Specify the **Import Quantity Limit,** which is the maximum number of cards to import. If you want to import all your cards, set the quantity to a high value, such as 100000.

5\. Click the **Run** button to start importing your virtual cards.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38782610364311)

6\. You can click the status of the task to view the Action Log, which includes live details related to the tool you are using.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331911162647)

7\. Profile Builder will save your virtual cards to the **Cards** section in Profile Builder. 

*   You can double-click a virtual card to view its details and add notes. 
*   You can copy information with the two blue spreadsheet icon. 
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .
*   You can export your virtual cards to a CSV with File > Export.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331900693527)

Stripe Lock/Unlock
------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Cards section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the credit card icon labeled **Cards** when you hover over it.

2\. Select your Stripe virtual cards that you want to lock/unlock. Click the **Tools** button, select the **Stripe Lock/Unlock** option, and then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331911163543)

3\. A dialog will appear explaining how the lock/unlock tool functions. When you run the Stripe Lock tool, the selected virtual cards will be locked and prevented from making charges. When you run the Stripe Unlock tool, Profile Builder will unlock the selected virtual cards. You only need to use the Stripe Unlock tool if your virtual cards are locked. Click the **Run** button to proceed.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38782580728215)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38782610367767)

4\. A dialog will appear that shows your task status and progress. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38782580730903)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38782580734999)

5\. If you locked your virtual cards, the icon in the **State** column will change to a red lock icon. If you unlocked your virtual cards, the icon in the **State** column will change to a blue checkmark.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331900693527)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331900694679)

Stripe Delete
-------------

1\. Navigate over to the Cards section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the credit card icon labeled **Cards** when you hover over it.

2\. Select the Stripe virtual cards that you want to delete. Click the **Tools** button, select the **Stripe Delete** option, and then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331911166103)

3\. When you run the delete tool, your virtual cards will be deleted from the Stripe Website. This action is not reversible. A dialog will appear explaining this. If you want to proceed, click the **Run** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38782610371863)

4\. A dialog will appear that shows your task status and progress.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38782610373527)

5\. Once your virtual cards have been deleted, they will be removed from the Profile Builder tableview UI.

# OneClick -  Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Tools - Account Recovery Tools – AYCD

OneClick - Tasks - Accounts/Solvers - Tools - Account Recovery Tools

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19501991882647-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Tools-Account-Recovery-Tools/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

December 02, 2023 01:46

Introduction
------------

You can use Account Recovery to solve login issues related to your account. This guide will cover the SMS Verify, Recover Locked Account, and Appeal Disabled Account Tools. Account Recovery handles IMAP Mail OTP, SMS OTP, password changes, and appeals.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You must add Accounts to OneClick before using an Account Recovery Tool. [Click here to learn how to add accounts to OneClick.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16222455171479)
    
*   You can add third-party SMS Credentials to OneClick to automate any SMS OTP code challenges encountered. [Click here to learn how to setup SMS Credentials in OneClick.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19514938436119)
    
*   You can add third-party Mail Credentials to OneClick to automate any Mail OTP code challenges encountered. [Click here to learn how to setup Mail Credentials in OneClick.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19515035829271)
    
*   There are many different challenges you can encounter during login. The Account Recovery dialog has handling for all the challenges you can encounter. You do not need to fill out each section of the Account Recovery dialog if your accounts are only experiencing a specific challenge. For example, if your account only requires an SMS challenge solved, you can exclusively fill in the SMS section. 
*   There is no harm in filling out every section of the Account Recovery dialog if you want to handle every situation automatically.

[Mail](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19501991882647-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Tools-Account-Recovery-Tools#01HGNYWN2EK2WFJY3G88V78SVK)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Accounts/Solvers** section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a play button titled Accounts/Solvers.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to use an Account Recovery Tool, then click the Tools button. Select the Account Recovery Tool you want to use and click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19514576529431)

3\. The Account Recovery Tool dialog will appear. Navigate to the Mail section.

4\. If your account requires an email code when logging in, and you have the Mail section filled out, the code portion will be automated.

5\. You can select Mail Credentials to use for account recovery. OneClick will use your mail credentials if Google asks you to verify a code to your recovery email. If you configure this dialog, OneClick will automate this process.

*   You must create a Mail Task Template in Inbox and add your Inbox API key to OneClick > Settings > Inbox Service. You can find your Inbox API key in Settings > Tasks. Follow the guide below to create a Mail Task Template.
*   You must add your mail credentials that receive your recovery email codes to Inbox, and then to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to add your mail credentials to Inbox](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    .
*   [To use Inbox Mail, follow this guide to configure a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    

6\. You can configure the **Mail Timeout in Minutes Override** field to configure the default timeout for waiting for OTP codes.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092143196951)

7\. If your account requires an email code when logging in, and you have the Mail section filled out, the code portion will be automated.

8\. Optionally, you can fill out other sections of the Account Recovery dialog if your accounts encounter additional challenges. When you have finished configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

[SMS](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19501991882647-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Tools-Account-Recovery-Tools#01HGNZW959FM5M7ZZXK8YE6JAT)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Accounts/Solvers** section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a play button titled Accounts/Solvers.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to use an Account Recovery Tool, then click the Tools button. Select the Account Recovery Tool you want to use and click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19514576531863)

3\. The Account Recovery Tool dialog will appear. Navigate to the SMS section.

4\. You can select the SMS Credentials you added to OneClick in the Local SMS section. [Click here to learn how to add SMS Credentials to OneClick.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19514938436119)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23570716661271)

5\. **Select** which SMS Provider you wish to use.

6\. **Select** which **SMS Credentials** you wish to use.

7\. Specify which **Number Type** you want to use. You can choose between **Rental** (long-term) and **Standard** numbers. 

8\. If you selected **Standard**, use the App button to specify which app or website you want to request a phone number for. The App dialog will display a list of all available apps or websites for which the provider offers phone numbers.

9\. If you selected **Standard**, use the Country button to specify which countries you want to request a phone number for. The Country dialog will display a list of all available countries for which the provider offers phone numbers. You can select multiple countries if you wish.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23570716666391)

10\. If you selected **Rental**, specify which **Rental Type** you want to use. The dialog will display a list of all your available rental types.

11\. If you selected **Rental**, specify which **Rental Number** you want to use. The dialog will display a list of all your available rental numbers.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/23570716668439)

12\. To have the task automatically retry when phone numbers are out of stock, click the **Retry Request When Out of Numbers** checkbox.

13\. You can select your SMS Task Template you setup in Inbox in the Inbox SMS section. To use Inbox Mail, you need to add your Inbox API key to Settings > Inbox Service. [Click here to learn how to find your Inbox API Key.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092143197463)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19514576533655)

14\. If your account requires an SMS code when logging in, and you have the SMS section filled out, the code portion will be automated.

15\. Optionally, you can fill out other sections of the Account Recovery dialog if your accounts encounter additional challenges. When you have finished configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

[Password](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19501991882647-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Tools-Account-Recovery-Tools#01HGP0EBXFXCA2BDS6XN9W9VKW)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Accounts/Solvers** section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a play button titled Accounts/Solvers.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to use an Account Recovery Tool, then click the Tools button. Select the Account Recovery Tool you want to use and click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19514576537239)

3\. The Account Recovery Tool dialog will appear. Navigate to the Password section.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19514576538647)

4\. If your accounts encounter a password change challenge, the Account Recovery Tool will automatically handle the challenge and change your password to a random 16-character password. Optionally, you can specify a specific password. 

5\. Optionally, you can fill out other sections of the Account Recovery dialog if your accounts encounter additional challenges. When you have finished configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

[Appeal](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/19501991882647-OneClick-Tasks-Accounts-Solvers-Tools-Account-Recovery-Tools#01HGP1BVH9E8NA14T6VXF4P5GZ)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1\. Navigate to the **Accounts/Solvers** section in OneClick under Tasks. This section has an icon that looks like a play button titled Accounts/Solvers.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to use an Account Recovery Tool, then click the Tools button. Select the Account Recovery Tool you want to use and click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/19514576539799)

3\. The Account Recovery Tool dialog will appear. Navigate to the Appeal section.

4\. Select the categories of appeal texts to use. [Click here to learn more about creating Appeal Text Content.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/16035312242583)

5\. Select **Google Accounts** if you want to use your existing Google Accounts loaded in OneClick as Contact Emails. We do not recommend using Google Accounts for contact emails for your appeals. 

6\. Select **Custom Emails** if you want to use other email services for your Contact Emails. We recommend using iCloud, Outlook, or Yahoo for contact emails. You should use one unique email per account you are appealing to make it more difficult for Google to identify a pattern with your appeals.

7\. Select **Catchall Emails** if you want to use a catchall email for your Contact Emails. We do not recommend using a catchall for contact emails for your appeals. 

8\. The **Use Recovery Email for Contact Email** checkbox will use your recovery email if a recovery email is saved to your task.

9\. The **Allow Reuse of Appeal Texts** checkbox will allow you to reuse your Appeal Text. This is not recommended. You should use one unique Appeal Text per account you are attempting to appeal.

10\. The **Allow Reuse of Contact Emails** checkbox will allow contact emails to be used multiple times when submitting appeals. This is not recommended. You should use one unique contact email per account you are attempting to appeal. 

11\. The **Enable 2FA if Required** checkbox will appear if enabling 2FA is required during the appeal process.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/32092127120023)

12\. If your accounts encounter an account disabled challenge and you can submit an appeal, the Account Recovery Tool will automatically handle the challenge and submit an appeal.

12\. Optionally, you can fill out other sections of the Account Recovery dialog if your accounts encounter additional challenges. When you have finished configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

# Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - AMEX (Business) (Generate, Import, Lock) – AYCD

Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - AMEX (Business) (Generate, Import, Lock)

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38616453675031-Profile-Builder-Cards-Tasks-AMEX-Business-Generate-Import-Lock/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



============================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

February 24, 2026 20:35

Introduction
------------

You can use Profile Builder to generate, import, lock, unlock, and manage AMEX (Business) virtual cards.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To manage your virtual cards in Profile Builder, you need an account and a credit/debit card with the selected provider.
*   As of 03/02/2026, only eligible [American Express® Business Cards (Business Platinum Card®, Business Gold Card, Plum Card®, Business Green Rewards Card, and Centurion) can create Virtual Cards](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/business/blueprint/help-center/business-card/virtual-card/faq.are-all-american-express-business-cards-eligible-to-create-virtual-cards.html)
    .
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles to easily create or update your Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .

Adding AMEX (Business) Credentials
----------------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Tasks section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the ship's wheel icon labeled **Tasks** when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **File** button at the top left of the view, then click **Create**.

3\. Select **AMEX (Business)** from the dialog and click the **Next** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331568375191)

4.  By default, virtual card credentials are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your virtual card credentials created in a unique category.

5\. Specify a **Task Name** for the Card Task. The Task Name is how you will identify this task in the tableview ui.

6\. Specify which **Browser Type** you want to use. In almost all cases, you should choose Camo Chrome or Google Chrome. If you are having issues with those browser types, you can try other available browser types.

7\. Optionally, you can save **Notes** to your virtual card credential.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38616453651479)

8\. Input the **Username** (email address) for your AMEX (Business) account.

9\. Input the **Password** for your AMEX (Business) account.

10\. Input the CSC of the primary card on the account (or whichever card Amex prompts you to confirm, it is the same card each time).

11\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331560507159)

12\. Your virtual card credential will be added to the tableview. You can now use the available AMEX (Business) tools to generate, import, lock/unlock, and delete virtual cards.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331560508951)

AMEX (Business) Generate
------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Tasks section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the ship's wheel icon labeled **Tasks** when you hover over it.

2\. Select your AMEX (Business) virtual card credential. Click the **Tools** button, select the **AMEX (Business) Generate** option, and then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331568376215)

3\. The configuration dialog will appear. By default, virtual cards are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your virtual cards created in a unique category.

4\. Optionally, you can specify a **Nickname** for your generated cards. You can use [Expressions](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058115713)
 in this field.

5\. Specify the **Spend Limit** that will be applied to each generated virtual card.

6\. Specify the **Reset Frequency** to apply to the card. This setting determines how often the spend limit is reset to its initial value. For example, in the screenshot below, the card has a $10,000 spend limit and resets every week.

7\. Specify the positions of your physical cards with the **Source Card Positions** dropdown menu. This is the position of your physical cards as it is shown on the website. If you only have one source card, select 1.

8\.  Specify the **Quantity Per Source Card,** which is the number of cards you want to generate for each source card.

9\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Run** button to start generating your virtual cards.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38616411433111)

10\. American Express will prompt you to confirm your identity via SMS or app notification for 2FA before Profile Builder can generate your virtual cards. You can manually handle this in the browser, and then Profile Builder will proceed with generating your virtual cards.   
11\. You can click the status of the task to view the Action Log, which includes live details related to the tool you are using.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331568376471)

12\. Profile Builder will save your virtual cards to the **Cards** section in Profile Builder. 

*   You can double-click a virtual card to view its details and add notes. 
*   You can copy information with the two blue spreadsheet icon. 
*   You can utilize the Lock, Unlock, and Delete tools on your virtual cards.
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .
*   You can export your virtual cards to a CSV with File > Export.

AMEX (Business) Import
----------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Tasks section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the ship's wheel icon labeled **Tasks** when you hover over it.

2\. Select your AMEX (Business) virtual card credential. Click the **Tools** button, select the **AMEX (Business) Import** option, and then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331560513431)

3\. The configuration dialog will appear. By default, virtual cards are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your virtual cards created in a unique category.

4\. Specify the number of virtual cards you want to import with the **Import Quantity Limit** field. You can set this to a high value, such as 1000000, to import all of your virtual cards.

5\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Run** button to start generating your virtual cards.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38616411441047)

6\. You can click the status of the task to view the Action Log, which includes live details related to the tool you are using.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331568376471)

7\. Profile Builder will save your virtual cards to the **Cards** section in Profile Builder. 

*   You can double-click a virtual card to view its details and add notes. 
*   You can copy information with the two blue spreadsheet icon. 
*   You can utilize the Lock, Unlock, and Delete tools on your virtual cards.
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .
*   You can export your virtual cards to a CSV with File > Export.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331568378135)

AMEX (Business) Lock
--------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Cards section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the credit card icon labeled **Cards** when you hover over it.

2\. Select your AMEX (Business) virtual cards that you want to lock/unlock. Click the **Tools** button, select the **AMEX (Business) Lock** option, and then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331560514455)

3\. A dialog will appear explaining how the lock/unlock tool functions. When you run the AMEX (Business) Lock tool, the selected virtual cards will be locked and prevented from making charges. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38616411442967)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38616453666583)

4\. A dialog will appear that shows your task status and progress. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38616411445015)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38616411448343)

5\. If you locked your virtual cards, the icon in the **State** column will change to a red lock icon. If you unlocked your virtual cards, the icon in the **State** column will change to a blue checkmark.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331568379031)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331560516375)

### Articles in this section

*   [Profile Builder - Profiles - Create / Generate / Import / Export (Format / Custom CSV) Profiles](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13258919736343-Profile-Builder-Profiles-Create-Generate-Import-Export-Format-Custom-CSV-Profiles)

# Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - Bill (Formerly Divvy) (Generate, Import, Lock, Unlock, Delete) – AYCD

Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - Bill (Formerly Divvy) (Generate, Import, Lock, Unlock, Delete)

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38618662610967-Profile-Builder-Cards-Tasks-Bill-Formerly-Divvy-Generate-Import-Lock-Unlock-Delete/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



======================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

February 24, 2026 21:52

Introduction
------------

You can use Profile Builder to generate, import, lock, unlock, and manage Bill (Formerly Divvy) virtual cards.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To manage your virtual cards in Profile Builder, you need an account and a credit/debit card with the selected provider.
*   You can sign up for Bill via their website. [Click here to access the Bill website](https://www.bill.com/)
    . 
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles to easily create or update your Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .

Adding Bill (Formerly Divvy) Credentials
----------------------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Tasks section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the ship's wheel icon labeled **Tasks** when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **File** button at the top left of the view, then click **Create**.

3\. Select **Bill (Formerly Divvy)** from the dialog and click the **Next** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331603373079)

4.  By default, virtual card credentials are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your virtual card credentials created in a unique category.

5\. Specify a **Task Name** for the Card Task. The Task Name is how you will identify this task in the tableview ui.

6\. Specify which **Browser Type** you want to use. In almost all cases, you should choose Camo Chrome or Google Chrome. If you are having issues with those browser types, you can try other available browser types.

7\. Optionally, you can save **Notes** to your virtual card credential.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38618662607639)

8\. Input the **Username** (email address) for your **Bill (Formerly Divvy)** account.

9\. Input the **Password** for your **Bill (Formerly Divvy)** account.

10\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38618654371351)

11\. Your virtual card credential will be added to the tableview. You can now use the available Bill tools to generate, import, lock/unlock, and delete virtual cards.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331628058135)

Bill (Formerly Divvy) Generate
------------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Tasks section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the ship's wheel icon labeled **Tasks** when you hover over it.

2\. Select your **Bill (Formerly Divvy**) virtual card credential. Click the **Tools** button, select the **Bill (Formerly Divvy Generate** option, and then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331603374103)

3\. The configuration dialog will appear. By default, virtual cards are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your virtual cards created in a unique category.

4\. Optionally, you can specify a **Nickname** for your generated cards. You can use [Expressions](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360058115713)
 in this field.

5\. Specify the **Spend Limit** that will be applied to each generated virtual card. To generate virtual cards without a limit, enable the **No Limit** checkbox.

6\. Optionally, you can create a budget for generated cards with the **Create a budget for generated cards** which enables the ability to mass delete and lock/unlock virtual cards.

7\. Click the **Configure Sources and Quantity** button. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38650455106583)

5\. Click the **Configure Sources and Quantity** button. The Select Quantity dialog will appear. Click the **Fetch** button to scrape the company and cardholder lists from your Bill (Formerly Divvy) Account. The browser will open and log in to your Bill (Formerly Divvy) account. This will let you choose which company and cardholder we use to generate virtual cards. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38650455107095)

6\. Once your source cards have been fetched, the Source Company and Card Holder section of the dialog will populate. 

*   You can select the Company you want to use when generating virtual cards.
*   You can select multiple cardholders.
*   Once you have selected all of your cardholders for the Source Card and specified the **Cards Quantity for Card Holder,** which is the number of cards you want to generate, click the **Add Source Configuration** button.
*   Repeat the bullet steps above for each Company/cardholder you want to configure. An example image of a completed dialog is below.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38650455107479)

7\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. The **Total Quantity** section of the dialog will populate with information based on how you configured the Select Quantity dialog. Click the **Run** button to start generating your virtual cards.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38650470390295)

8\. You can click the status of the task to view the Action Log, which includes live details related to the tool you are using.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331603374487)

9\. Profile Builder will save your virtual cards to the **Cards** section in Profile Builder. 

*   You can double-click a virtual card to view its details and add notes. 
*   You can copy information with the two blue spreadsheet icon. 
*   You can utilize the Lock, Unlock, and Delete tools on your virtual cards.
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .
*   You can export your virtual cards to a CSV with File > Export.

Bill (Formerly Divvy) Import
----------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Tasks section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the ship's wheel icon labeled **Tasks** when you hover over it.

2\. Select your **Bill (Formerly Divvy**) virtual card credential. Click the **Tools** button, select the **Bill (Formerly Divvy**) **Import** option, and then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331603375639)

3\. The configuration dialog will appear. By default, virtual cards are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your virtual cards created in a unique category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38651210256919)

4\. Click the **Configure Sources and Quantity** button. The Select Quantity dialog will appear. Click the **Fetch** button to scrape the company from your Bill (Formerly Divvy) Account. The browser will open and log in to your Bill (Formerly Divvy) account. This will let you choose which company we use to import virtual cards. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38651171745431)

5\. Select the **Company(s)** you want to use. The company checkboxes determine which company(s) to choose on the Bill Website when importing virtual cards. 

6\. Specify the **Import Quantity Limit for Source Card,** which is the maximum number of cards to import. If you want to import all your cards, enable the **Import all cards** checkbox. 

7\. Once you have selected your Source Card(s), click the **Add Source Configuration** checkbox. An example image of a completed dialog is below.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38651171745559)

8\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. The **Total Quantity** section of the dialog will populate with information based on how you configured the Select Quantity dialog. Click the **Run** button to start generating your virtual cards.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38651171746199)

9\. You can click the status of the task to view the Action Log, which includes live details related to the tool you are using.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331603375895)

10\. Profile Builder will save your virtual cards to the **Cards** section in Profile Builder. 

*   You can double-click a virtual card to view its details and add notes. 
*   You can copy information with the two blue spreadsheet icon. 
*   You can utilize the Lock, Unlock, and Delete tools on your virtual cards.
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .
*   You can export your virtual cards to a CSV with File > Export.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331603376535)

Bill (Formerly Divvy) Lock/Unlock
---------------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Cards section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the credit card icon labeled **Cards** when you hover over it.

2\. Select your **Bill (Formerly Divvy)** virtual cards that you want to lock/unlock. Click the **Tools** button, select the **Bill (Formerly Divvy) Lock/Unlock** option, and then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331604286487)

3\. A dialog will appear explaining how the lock/unlock tool functions. When you run the Bill (Formerly Divvy) Lock tool, the selected virtual cards will be locked and prevented from making charges. When you run the Bill (Formerly Divvy) tool, Profile Builder will unlock the selected virtual cards. You only need to use the Bill (Formerly Divvy) Unlock tool if your virtual cards are locked. Click the **Run** button to proceed.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38651210260759)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38651210261399)

4\. A dialog will appear that shows your task status and progress. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38651210262935)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38651171750295)

5\. If you locked your virtual cards, the icon in the **State** column will change to a red lock icon. If you unlocked your virtual cards, the icon in the **State** column will change to a blue checkmark.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331603376919)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331603378071)

Bill (Formerly Divvy) Delete
----------------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Cards section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the credit card icon labeled **Cards** when you hover over it.

2\. Select your **Bill (Formerly Divvy)** virtual cards that you want to delete. Click the **Tools** button, select the **Bill (Formerly Divvy) Delete** option, and then click the **Start** button.

3\. When you run the delete tool, your virtual cards will be deleted from the **Bill (Formerly Divvy)** Website. This action is not reversible. A dialog will appear explaining this. If you want to proceed, click the **Run** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38651210265751)

4\. A dialog will appear that shows your task status and progress.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38651171752087)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38651171752215)

5\. Once your virtual cards have been deleted, they will be removed from the Profile Builder tableview UI.
