# Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Add / Remove Phone Number – AYCD

Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Add / Remove Phone Number

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27400308783767-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Add-Remove-Phone-Number/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

October 29, 2024 03:55

Introduction
------------

You can use **Outlook Phone Number** tools to add/remove phone numbers from your Outlook accounts. Your account must have a phone number on it to use regular email forwarding.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To log into your Outlook/Hotmail accounts, you need to add your email credentials to Inbox. [Click here to learn how to add your Outlook accounts to Inbox.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703)
    
*   Microsoft prompts for various forms of verification during login. Inbox can automatically handle TOTP 2FA and recovery email codes. You can add your TOTP 2FA Secret Key and recovery email to your credentials.  
*   The Enable Forwarding tool usually requires that the account be verified via SMS. If you run into failures when using the Enable Forwarding tool, try using the Outlook Add Phone Number tool and then retry. [Click here to learn how to use the Enable Forwarding tool.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/26963363437719)
    
*   You can configure the number of browsers that run concurrently in Settings > Mail | Account Tools. 

Outlook Add Phone Number
------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to use the **Outlook Add Phone Number** tool, then click the Tools button and select the **Outlook Add Phone Number** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461136320023)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461121772439)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461136327959)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461121775767)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Outlook forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461136332311)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. [Click here and navigate to the Captcha Services section of the Inbox Settings guide to learn how to configure automatic captcha solving](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461121779607)

8\. You can automatically move accounts Inbox successfully logged in to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461136340375)

9\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to add phone numbers to your accounts through the selected SMS Task Template. If the tool is successful, the Browser Status will display **Phone Number Added**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461136341527)

Outlook Remove Phone Number
---------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Accounts section of Inbox in Mail. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a book with a person on it is titled **Accounts**.

2\. Select the accounts on which you want to remove the phone number, then click the Tools button and select **Outlook Remove Phone Number**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27400475125527)

3\. Select a Mail Task Template to use. The Mail Task Template is used if your accounts encounter 2FA codes that must be handled via IMAP. You can assign catchall/forwarded emails to your Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461121772439)

4\. You can automatically handle SMS codes with third-party SMS providers or with SMS providers that send codes to your email. The SMS Task Template is used when your accounts encounter SMS verification pages during the login or forwarding flow, or when using other tools. 

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider through their API, you need to create an SMS Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create an SMS Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14795585246231)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461136327959)

*   If you are using SMS from a third-party provider that sends codes to your email, you need to create a Mail Task Template and assign the account credential(s) that receive the SMS codes to the Mail Task Template. [Click here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    
*   After creating your SMS Task Template, select it using the **Edit** button.
*   You can specify the **Phone Number Source Type.** In most cases, you should use the **Use Number From SMS API** option. If you are using an SMS provider that supports requesting SMS from specific phone numbers, use the **Use Assigned Number From Account** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461121775767)

5\. Optionally, you can specify a Custom Password to use if Outlook forces you to change your password during login. Inbox will automatically handle this and replace the existing password in your Inbox credential. If you leave this field blank, Inbox will generate a random 16-character password for each account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461136332311)

6\. Specify the captcha solving mode to use. If you want to solve captchas manually, you can select the **Manual** option. If you want to automatically solve captchas, select the **Automatic** option. [Click here and navigate to the Captcha Services section of the Inbox Settings guide to learn how to configure automatic captcha solving](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14745730512791)

7\. You can configure how many captcha solve attempts are used before failing with the **AutoSolve Maximum Solve Attempts** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461121779607)

8\. You can automatically move accounts Inbox successfully logged in to a different category with the **Move To Category After Success** dropdown. You can type in a custom category name or leave it blank to keep the account in its existing category.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461136340375)

9\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to remove phone numbers from your accounts. If the tool is successful, the Browser Status will display **Phone Number Removed**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/37461136343191)

### Articles in this section

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Create/Import/Export / Import From OneClick/Profile Builder](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Create-Import-Export-Import-From-OneClick-Profile-Builder)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - OAuth2 Automatic / Manual Login (Gmail/Outlook/Hotmail/Yahoo/AOL)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/25709284534295-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-OAuth2-Automatic-Manual-Login-Gmail-Outlook-Hotmail-Yahoo-AOL)

# Inbox - Webhooks - Webhooks - Create / Import / Export AYCD Webhook – AYCD

Inbox - Webhooks - Webhooks - Create / Import / Export AYCD Webhook

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38168231806231-Inbox-Webhooks-Webhooks-Create-Import-Export-AYCD-Webhook/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

February 04, 2026 18:49

Introduction
------------

You can create Webhooks in Inbox to automatically forward incoming webhook messages to Inbox and other webhook addresses. AYCD Webhooks automatically handle rate limits, so you never miss messages. AYCD Webhooks supports forwarding to an unlimited number of Discord webhooks.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   You can scrape data from your incoming webhooks. Some example data you can scrape is email, order, and product information. [You can learn how to scrape data from your incoming webhook messages by clicking here.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38265062518679)
    
*   Inbox must be open for AYCD Webhooks to function. It does not need to be on the same device as the application using AYCD Webhooks.
*   If you reset your Inbox API key in Settings > Inbox API or move your Inbox setup to a new device without restoring from a backup, all of your AYCD webhook URLs will change. 

Create AYCD Webhook
-------------------

1\. Navigate to the Webhooks section of Inbox in Webhooks. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a share button with three dots and 2 lines is titled **Webhooks**.

2\. Click on the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Create** button. The Create Webhook dialog will appear.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38168218507927)

3\. By default, AYCD webhooks are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to create your webhook in a unique category.

4\. Specify a **Webhook ID** for your AYCD webhook. This ID is the webhook's unique ID and cannot be changed after you create it. The ID is visible in the webhook URL. The ID must be between 4 and 64 characters and can only contain lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores.

5. Optionally, you can input any notes or details you want to remember about this webhook in the **Notes** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38168218509591)

6\. In the Forwarding section, you can input the Discord webhooks you want your AYCD webhooks to forward to. Click the **Create** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38169051529239)

7\. Specify a **Nickname** for the forwarding item. 

8\. Specify the **Forward to URL**. You can enter the Discord webhook URL you want your AYCD webhook to forward to in this field.

9\. Specify the **Delay Between Sends**. By default, the delay is set to 2 seconds, and we do not recommend reducing it. Discord webhooks have a rate limit of 30 requests per minute, so a delay of 2 seconds automatically avoids being rate-limited on Discord's end. You can increase this delay as much as you want. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38169038772247)

10\. You can repeat steps 6-9 and add as many webhooks as you want your AYCD Webhooks to forward to. For example, if you want your AYCD webhook to forward to 3 different Discord channels, you would add 3 different webhooks to the Forwarding section.

11\. You can configure a webhook scraper item in the **Scraping** section. To configure the Scraping section when creating a webhook, you need to know how to read JSONPath and create regex patterns. **Instead of configuring your scraper during webhook creation, we recommend configuring a scraper after you receive webhook messages. This method is much easier and does not require any developer knowledge.** [Click here to learn how to configure a scraper on webhook messages.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38265062518679)

12\. In the Options section, you can configure your **Message Auto Expire** behavior. This field determines how long Inbox retains messages before automatically deleting them. We recommend using a more aggressive expiration setting for high-traffic webhooks to avoid running out of application memory. 

13\. You can enable the **Delete Messages After Forward Success** feature to automatically delete messages received by this webhook after Inbox completes all forwarding actions. Webhook messages that fail to forward will not automatically delete. This setting helps manage storage and memory usage. We recommend using this setting for high-traffic webhooks to prevent out-of-memory errors. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38169051532695)

14\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. Your AYCD webhook has been created. You can test your AYCD webhook by selecting it, right-clicking, or clicking the tools button, and then selecting **Send Test Webhook Message**. You can learn more about testing your AYCD webhook by clicking here.**![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38169051535767)**

Import AYCD Webhook
-------------------

1\. Navigate to the Webhooks section of Inbox in Webhooks. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a share button with three dots and 2 lines is titled **Webhooks**.

2\. Click on the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Import** button. The Import Webhook dialog will appear.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38169741734807)

3\. By default, AYCD webhooks are imported into the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to import your webhook into a unique category.

4\. Select the JSON file you want to import, click the blue spreadsheet icon to open file explorer. After selecting the file, click the **Import** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38169755339799)

5\.  Your webhooks will be imported to the Webhooks tableview.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38169755342231)

Export AYCD Webhook
-------------------

1\. Navigate to the Webhooks section of Inbox in Webhooks. When you hover over it, an icon that looks like a share button with three dots and 2 lines is titled **Webhooks**.

2\. Select the AYCD webhooks you want to export, click on the **File** button at the top left of the view, then the **Export** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38169912418583)

3\. The Export Items dialog will appear and ask if you want to proceed with the export. Click the **Yes** button to proceed.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38169912422935)

4\. The Completed - Export Items dialog will appear and ask if you want to open the exported file's location. Click the **Yes** button to open the location where the exported files are stored.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38169867219351)

### Articles in this section

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Create/Import/Export / Import From OneClick/Profile Builder](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14772838711703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Create-Import-Export-Import-From-OneClick-Profile-Builder)

*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - iCloud - Create / Generate / Import / Delete / Export](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/35504921495703-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-iCloud-Create-Generate-Import-Delete-Export)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Yahoo Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29062200244503-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Yahoo-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Generate Microsoft (Outlook/Hotmail) Accounts](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27311400040727-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Generate-Microsoft-Outlook-Hotmail-Accounts)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Schedule Tools / Review Scheduled Tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/28227969764375-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Schedule-Tools-Review-Scheduled-Tools)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Unlock Account](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27040329117847-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Unlock-Account)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook - Change / Verify/Update Recovery Email](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27032547222551-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Change-Verify-Update-Recovery-Email)
    
*   [Inbox - Mail - Accounts - Tools - Outlook Enable Email Forwarding / Disable Email Forwarding / Check Email Forwarding](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/26963363437719-Inbox-Mail-Accounts-Tools-Outlook-Enable-Email-Forwarding-Disable-Email-Forwarding-Check-Email-Forwarding)

# Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - Citi (Generate, Import, Delete) – AYCD

Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - Citi (Generate, Import, Delete)

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38702425613591-Profile-Builder-Cards-Tasks-Citi-Generate-Import-Delete/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



============================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/32171769765655/2021_brand_discord_aycd.png)

**AYCD**

February 28, 2026 00:27

Introduction
------------

You can use Profile Builder to generate, import, delete, and manage Citi virtual cards.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   To manage your virtual cards in Profile Builder, you need an account and a credit/debit card with the selected provider.
*   Citi enforces a limit of 20 virtual cards per source card.
*   Every time you make a transaction on a Citi Virtual Card, the CVV changes. You can run the import tool to update the CVV on your virtual cards.
*   Citi Virtual Cards are locked to a single website. The first website you use the card on is where it's locked.
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles to easily create or update your Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .

Adding Citi Credentials
-----------------------

1\. Navigate over to the Tasks section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the ship's wheel icon labeled **Tasks** when you hover over it.

2\. Click the **File** button at the top left of the view, then click **Create**.

3\. Select **Citi** from the dialog and click the **Next** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331732010519)

4.  By default, virtual card credentials are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your virtual card credentials created in a unique category.

5\. Specify a **Task Name** for the Card Task. The Task Name is how you will identify this task in the tableview ui.

6\. Specify which **Browser Type** you want to use. In almost all cases, you should choose Camo Chrome or Google Chrome. If you are having issues with those browser types, you can try other available browser types.

7\. Optionally, you can save **Notes** to your virtual card credential.  
![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38702408419991)

8\. Input the **Username** (email address) for your Citi account.

9\. Input the **Password** for your Citi account.

10\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38702425612695)

11\. Your virtual card credential will be added to the tableview. You can now use the available Citi tools to generate, import, lock/unlock, and delete virtual cards.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331732011159)

Citi Generate
-------------

1\. Navigate over to the Tasks section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the ship's wheel icon labeled **Tasks** when you hover over it.

2\. Select your Citi virtual card credential. Click the **Tools** button, select the **Citi Generate** option, and then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331732011543)

3\. By default, virtual cards are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your virtual cards created in a unique category.

4\. If you have more than one Source Card specify the **Last 4 Digits of Source Card**.

5\. Specify the number of virtual cards you want to generate with the **Quantity** field.

6\. Once you have configured the dialog, click the **Run** button. Your virtual cards will begin to generate.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38754506068119)

7\. You can click the status of the task to view the Action Log, which includes live details related to the tool you are using.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331738078999)

8\. Profile Builder will save your virtual cards to the **Cards** section in Profile Builder. 

*   You can double-click a virtual card to view its details and add notes. 
*   You can copy information with the two blue spreadsheets icon. 
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .
*   You can export your virtual cards to a CSV with File > Export

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331732012055)

Citi Import
-----------

1\. Navigate over to the Tasks section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the ship's wheel icon labeled **Tasks** when you hover over it.

2\. Select your Citi virtual card credential. Click the **Tools** button, select the **Citi Import** option, and then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331738079895)

3\. The configuration dialog will appear. By default, virtual cards are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to have your virtual cards created in a unique category.

4\. Specify the **Import Quantity Limit,** which is the maximum number of cards to import. If you want to import all your cards, set the quantity to a high value, such as 100000.

5\. Click the **Run** button to start importing your virtual cards.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38754655907351)

6\. You can click the status of the task to view the Action Log, which includes live details related to the tool you are using.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331738078999)

7\. Profile Builder will save your virtual cards to the **Cards** section in Profile Builder. 

*   You can double-click a virtual card to view its details and add notes. 
*   You can copy information with the two blue spreadsheet icon. 
*   You can use tools such as Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles. [Click here to learn how to use the Create new Profiles using Cards or Add Cards to existing Profiles tools](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13325711248151)
    .
*   You can export your virtual cards to a CSV with File > Export.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331732012055)

Citi Delete
-----------

1\. Navigate over to the Cards section of Profile Builder in Cards. This section shows the credit card icon labeled **Cards** when you hover over it.

2\. Select your Citi virtual cards that you want to delete. Click the **Tools** button, select the **Citi Delete** option, and then click the **Start** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/39331738080279)

3\. When you run the delete tool, your virtual cards will be deleted from the Citi Website. This action is not reversible. A dialog will appear explaining this. If you want to proceed, click the **Run** button.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38754678707351)

4\. A dialog will appear that shows your task status and progress.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/38754678708119)

5\. Once your virtual cards have been deleted, they will be removed from the Profile Builder tableview UI.

*   [Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - Capital One (Setup, Generate, Import, Lock, Unlock, Delete)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38489083866647-Profile-Builder-Cards-Tasks-Capital-One-Setup-Generate-Import-Lock-Unlock-Delete)
    
*   [Profile Builder - Cards - Tasks - Citi (Generate, Import, Delete)](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/38702425613591-Profile-Builder-Cards-Tasks-Citi-Generate-Import-Delete)

# Profile Builder - Accounts - Tasks - Shopify - Create / Generate – AYCD

Profile Builder - Accounts - Tasks - Shopify - Create / Generate

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27805648400023-Profile-Builder-Accounts-Tasks-Shopify-Create-Generate/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



==========================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://assets.zendesk.com/hc/assets/default_avatar.png)

**Permanently deleted user**

November 14, 2024 19:39

Introduction
------------

Using Profile Builder, you can generate and manage your Shopify accounts.

Setup & Recommendations
-----------------------

*   If you want to use proxies for your account tasks, you can add them to Profile Builder. [Click here to learn how to add proxies to Profile Builder.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13257962802455)
    
*   If you want to confirm IMAP mail OTP codes automatically, you can use Inbox Mail Task Templates. [Click here to learn how to create an Inbox Mail Task Template.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    

Creating Tasks
--------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Click the **File** button, then either the **Create One Task** or **Create Many Tasks** button.

3\. Select the **Shopify** option in the Choose Account Task Type dialog.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27806622458135)

4\. By default, tasks are created in the **All** Category. You can override this and specify a category name to create your task(s) into a unique category.

5\. You can select various browser types for your tasks. Learn more about each type below.

**Camo Chrome**

*   Camo Chrome is our in-house anti-detect browser. It gives your tasks a unique fingerprint, even from the same device, reducing the risk of account bans and anti-bot detections.

**Google Chrome**

*   Chrome is identical to using Chrome manually on your computer. We do not alter the fingerprint for each Chrome task. 

**Brave**

*   Brave is identical to using Brave manually on your computer. Brave has its own basic anti-fingerprinting solution and can help reduce the risk of anti-bot detections.

**Headless Browsers**

*   Headless browsers are more lightweight compared to their non-headless partners. You can run 2-3 headless browsers for every one non-headless browser.
*   You cannot visibly see what headless browsers are doing and cannot click the eye on your tasks to open the browser.
*   Headless browsers are more prone to anti-bot blocks.

6\. The **Account State** dropdown lets you specify whether the account has already been created. Once you generate an account, it automatically updates.

7\. Optionally, you can add notes to your account.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27806622463639)

8\. If you used the Create One Task option, specify a **Username** for the account task.

9\. If you used the Create One Task option, specify a **Password** for the account task.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27806622466455)

10\. If you used the Create Many Tasks option, you have various ways to specify a Username:

*   You can use a catchall domain to create usernames with the **Username From Catchall** username assign type dropdown.
*   In the **Catchall** field, specify the domain. In the **Number of Account Tasks** field, specify the number of tasks to create.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27806592200471)

*   If you selected profiles, you can use the email address assigned to the profile billing address with the **Username From Billing Address** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27806622475671)

*   If you selected profiles, you can use the email address assigned to the profile shipping address with the **Username From Shipping Address** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27806592209431)

*   You can specify a list of emails to use for your account usernames with the **Username From Provided List** dropdown. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27806622484375)

*   The **Username From Account Tasks** dropdown menu allows you to select your existing account tasks and use the usernames from those tasks. 

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27806622492311)

*   The **Username From Aliases** dropdown menu allows you to select your existing email aliases (from iCloud, for example) and them for the username on your account tasks.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27806592223767)

11\. If you select the Create Many Tasks option, you can specify the password behavior with the **Password Assign Type** dropdown.

*   Select the **Same Password For All Tasks** dropdown if you want to use the same password for all your accounts.
*   You can specify the password you want to use in the **Password** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27806592225943)

*   If you want to use a random password for all of your accounts, you can select the **Unique Password For Each Task** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27806622506391)

12\. You can select **Proxies** to use with your account(s).

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27819184269207)

13\. You can choose how captchas are handled with the **Captcha Action Type** dropdown. 

*   You can select the manual option if you want to solve captchas manually.
*   If you have AutoSolve or a [Captcha](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/captchapass)
     / [Ultimate](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/ultimatepass)
     Pass subscription, you can connect Profile Builder to AutoSolve and solve captchas in OneClick by selecting the **AutoSolve** option. You can solve captchas with third-party solvers or AutoSolve AI in your OneClick solvers. [Click here to learn how to connect AutoSolve with Profile Builder.](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/27813183619479)
    
*   If you have an AutoSolve AI or a [Captcha](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/captchapass)
     / [Ultimate](https://aycd.io/checkout/product/ultimatepass)
     Pass subscription and want Captchas solved within the same browser your tasks are running on, select the **AutoSolve AI** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27819184269591)

14\. You can choose how IMAP Mail OTP codes are handled with the **Mail Action Type** dropdown.

*   You can manually input mail OTP codes using the **M****anual** dropdown. When tasks encounter mail OTP codes, they will wait for you to input the code manually.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27821202545303)

*   Profile Builder can automatically handle IMAP Mail OTP codes with the **Automatic** dropdown.
*   Before utilizing the Automatic option, you need to add your Inbox API key to Settings > Inbox Service.
*   If you are using catchall/forwarded email credentials (which receive your IMAP Mail OTP codes), you need to add them to a Mail Task Template. C[lick here to learn how to create a Mail Task Template](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14821162472855)
    .
*   Specify the name of your Mail Task Template in the **Inbox Task Template Name** section of the Create Task dialog. 
    *   You can specify your mail task template name in **Settings > Inbox Service > Default Mail Task Template** **Name**. Your task template name will automatically prefill when creating tasks.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27821202546199)

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27821216049431)

*   You can configure your tasks to automatically stop when they encounter IMAP Mail OTP codes by selecting the **Stop On Mail Verification** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27821216049943)

15\. Optionally, you can specify where an account is forwarded to in the **Forwards To** field.

16\. Optionally, you can specify an account's recovery email in the **Recovery Email** field.

17\. Optionally, if you are creating one task, you can specify the 2FA (TOTP) Secret Key in the **2FA Secret** **Key** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27821216050583)

18\. Specify the URL for the Shopify store on which you want to create accounts with the **Shopify URL** field.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27821216050839)

19\. After configuring the dialog, click the **Save** button. Your tasks will appear in the tableview in the category you specified in the general tab.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27821216051479)

20\. You can filter which store type is showing in the tableview by clicking the **Task Type** dropdown.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27821216051735)

Generating Shopify Accounts
---------------------------

1\. Navigate to the Tasks section of Accounts. This section looks like a pirate ship steering wheel titled **Tasks** when you hover over the section.

2\. Select the tasks you want to generate, click the **Tools** button, and select the **Shopify - Generate** option. Alternatively, right-click your tasks, click Tools, and select the **Shopify - Generate** option.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/27821262677271)

3\. Click the **Start** button. Your tasks will begin to generate. Once they have successfully generated, the status will say **Generated**.

![](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/article_attachments/28006175450775)

# AutoSolve - Supported Bots – AYCD

AutoSolve - Supported Bots

[Follow](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043237394-AutoSolve-Supported-Bots/subscription.html "Opens a sign-in dialog")



====================================================================================================================================================================

![Avatar](https://aycd.zendesk.com/system/photos/361107019153/1AF391AB-1EF4-4C5F-A5C8-3C1B8FD61B65_1_105_c.jpeg)

**Stealthy**

February 23, 2020 17:00

**AutoSolve** is the latest tool by AYCD, allowing you to connect **OneClick** with supported **bots** to make OneClick a **default Captcha Solver**. By connecting bots to OneClick, it becomes your Captcha solving hub - allowing for captcha challenges to be sent to OneClick and optionally to supported 3rd party solvers like 2Captcha, AntiCaptcha, and CapMonster.

### Bots that Support AutoSolve:

*   [Adept AIO](https://aycd.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360049197853)
